title: AEROSPACE ENGINEERING
summary: CAREER PROFILE: AEROSPACE ENGINEERING
description:
CAREER PROFILE: AEROSPACE ENGINEERING 



Overview:

What is Aerospace Engineering?

 

Aerospace Engineer is one of the branches of engineering.

This career path is advanced in designing aircraft such as jets, gliders and helicopters.

Focuses on the designing of machines and how to build the machines that fly. 





What kind of Job is it, Field or desk?

Aerospace Engineering jobs are responsible for designing aircraft, satellites, spacecraft and missiles.

Basic jobs are design, manufacturing, and testing of aerospace products. 





Roles and responsibilities of a Aerospace Engineering:

The roles and responsibilities of a aerospace engineers are mentioned below:

Designing



Aerospace engineers design, repair and improve air and spacecraft, elements, materials and manufacturing processes with the help of various methods. 



Analysis 



Aerospace engineers analyze the information for the development of the design of the aircrafts. 



Assembling 



Aerospace engineers assemble computer systems, control panels and other various things. 



Preparing Notes 



Aerospace engineers create design proposals, reports and other various technical documents. 



Advisory 





Assists in projects and provides them with their technical advice. 

Benefits of Choosing Aerospace Engineering As A Career Profile:

Practicality:



Studying this will give some practical skills directly to the specific field of industry and employment. 



Salary:



Making airplanes and spacecraft is a big business, and many companies are eager to hire graduates with skills. And there is a high demand for them, so salary packages would also be high. 





Employers:



There are many more choices if you want to pursue a career in manufacturing. Graduates from this course have their pick of employers. 

 

Help change the world: 



All the practical challenges have their benefits. Aerospace engineering is at the forefront of various modern techniques.                                  

How To Become an Aerospace Engineer ?



To become a nurse right after 10th follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete 10th and choose science stream as if you are interested in engineering.

Step-2: Complete 12th in science stream with physics, chemistry and mathematics as their main subjects.

Step-3: Appear for the JEE Main exam if you want to take admission in such as IIT, NIIT, IIIT. 

Step-4: Qualify the entrance exam for this career 

Step-5: obtain B.Tech degree.

Step-6: choose a suitable minor and complete the internship.

Step-7: earn a graduate degree in aerospace engineering.

Step-8: apply for apprenticeship.

Step-9: work in a related job.

Step-10: earn a license. 

 

Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

The age limit for becoming an aerospace engineer depends on the route you choose after 12th. 

Educational qualifications:

A student must have passed 12th in science stream (with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics as main subjects) from any recognized board.

Bachelor of Technology degree of 4 year duration. 

MAE of 3 years. 

Entrance exam for the nursing course is compulsory for admission in this career course. 



Skill requirements:



      To become an Aerospace Engineer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.



Math Skills :



Aerospace engineers need to have mathematical skills such as Trigonometry and calculus, because they need to use them in the designing of the aircraft.



Writing skills:



They need to have good writing and drawing skills to explain their designs clearly in writing and drawing otherwise it may create problem while implementing the design.



Critical-thinking skills:



They need critical thinking skills To conclude why design may not be working and how to fix the design. also to innovate new designs and solutions thinking new everytime is required.



Eye for Detail



Detailing and finishing skills are required as They have to check every single detail while working and they must check it throughly. Because small mistakes can lead to big problems afterwards.



Ability to work in a team and lead a team 



They need to work in team with other departments after making designs and approving them. Therefore they should no how to work in a team and lead the team.



Working  Environment:

Aerospace engineers and technicians work in several places such as laboratories, manufacturing and industrial plants, and offices.

                 

 Individuals who opt for a career as an aerospace engineer generally work in offices, where they have access to computer software and other tools necessary for analyzing projects and designing solutions. Aerospace engineer jobs may vary depending on their skills and experience.

               



Entrance Exams To Become An Aerospace Engineer:

There are different entrance exams available to pursue Aerospace Engineering  as a career option. A student must clear the entrance exam in order to be eligible for the admissions. Below

Overview:

What is Aerospace Engineering?

 

Aerospace Engineer is one of the branches of engineering.

This career path is advanced in designing aircraft such as jets, gliders and helicopters.

Focuses on the designing of machines and how to build the machines that fly. 





What kind of Job is it, Field or desk?

Aerospace Engineering jobs are responsible for designing aircraft, satellites, spacecraft and missiles.

Basic jobs are design, manufacturing, and testing of aerospace products.

The roles and responsibilities of a aerospace engineers are mentioned below:

Designing



Aerospace engineers design, repair and improve air and spacecraft, elements, materials and manufacturing processes with the help of various methods. 



Analysis 



Aerospace engineers analyze the information for the development of the design of the aircrafts. 



Assembling 



Aerospace engineers assemble computer systems, control panels and other various things. 



Preparing Notes 



Aerospace engineers create design proposals, reports and other various technical documents. 



Advisory 





Assists in projects and provides them with their technical advice. 

Benefits of Choosing Aerospace Engineering As A Career Profile:

Practicality:



Studying this will give some practical skills directly to the specific field of industry and employment. 



Salary:



Making airplanes and spacecraft is a big business, and many companies are eager to hire graduates with skills. And there is a high demand for them, so salary packages would also be high. 





Employers:



There are many more choices if you want to pursue a career in manufacturing. Graduates from this course have their pick of employers. 

 

Help change the world: 



All the practical challenges have their benefits. Aerospace engineering is at the forefront of various modern techniques.                                  

How To Become an Aerospace Engineer ?



To become a nurse right after 10th follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete 10th and choose science stream as if you are interested in engineering.

Step-2: Complete 12th in science stream with physics, chemistry and mathematics as their main subjects.

Step-3: Appear for the JEE Main exam if you want to take admission in such as IIT, NIIT, IIIT. 

Step-4: Qualify the entrance exam for this career 

Step-5: obtain B.Tech degree.

Step-6: choose a suitable minor and complete the internship.

Step-7: earn a graduate degree in aerospace engineering.

Step-8: apply for apprenticeship.

Step-9: work in a related job.

Step-10: earn a license. 

 

Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

The age limit for becoming an aerospace engineer depends on the route you choose after 12th. 

Educational qualifications:

A student must have passed 12th in science stream (with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics as main subjects) from any recognized board.

Bachelor of Technology degree of 4 year duration. 

MAE of 3 years. 

Entrance exam for the nursing course is compulsory for admission in this career course. 



Skill requirements:



      To become an Aerospace Engineer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.



Math Skills :



Aerospace engineers need to have mathematical skills such as Trigonometry and calculus, because they need to use them in the designing of the aircraft.



Writing skills:



They need to have good writing and drawing skills to explain their designs clearly in writing and drawing otherwise it may create problem while implementing the design.



Critical-thinking skills:



They need critical thinking skills To conclude why design may not be working and how to fix the design. also to innovate new designs and solutions thinking new everytime is required.



Eye for Detail



Detailing and finishing skills are required as They have to check every single detail while working and they must check it throughly. Because small mistakes can lead to big problems afterwards.



Ability to work in a team and lead a team 



They need to work in team with other departments after making designs and approving them. Therefore they should no how to work in a team and lead the team.



Working  Environment:

Aerospace engineers and technicians work in several places such as laboratories, manufacturing and industrial plants, and offices.

                 

 Individuals who opt for a career as an aerospace engineer generally work in offices, where they have access to computer software and other tools necessary for analyzing projects and designing solutions. Aerospace engineer jobs may vary depending on their skills and experience.

               



Entrance Exams To Become An Aerospace Engineer:

There are different entrance exams available to pursue Aerospace Engineering  as a career option. A student must clear the entrance exam in order to be eligible for the admissions. Below given are some of the entrance exams for aerospace engineer degree:

This course is a type of diploma in Aerospace Engineering. This course lasts for 3 years. Diploma courses in aerospace engineering can be pursued by the students after the 10th and 12th. 



Undergraduate Courses in Aerospace Engineering: 







Students can pursue bachelor of technology in aerospace engineering. The duration of this course would be 4 years. The undergraduate course is concerned with the theoretical, practical knowledge of testing, maintenance and assembling the parts of an aircraft or airplanes. This includes courses:

Top Institutions To Pursue The Aerospace Engineering Courses:

There are numerous good colleges and institutes providing admissions for aerospace engineering  courses. Refer to the list given below to know more.

Types Of Specializations Available For An Aerospace Engineering:

Some of the most popular Aerospace Engineering specializations are listed below. A student can choose the specializations according to his/her interest in the particular field.

Electronic Systems

Aerospace electronic engineers are the one who work in the electronic systems of this field. They usually have to focus on new aerospace technologies. They also wok for improving GPS foe better safety and efficiency to track.

          

Aerospace Propulsion

Aerospace Propulsion engineers works on the designing of engines which are used in rockets , missiles and satellites to accelerate for the launch or flight of them in the space.They need to have a good knowledge of chemistry.

    





Structural Design Engineer

Structural Design Engineers the one who has a field work of designing, development, and testing of aircrafts and other things.They are responsible for making structural designs and engineering specifications after researching and analyzing it. 

     

Instrumental  and Communication

Instrumental and communication Engineer is responsible for the panel systems,gauges, sensors, wiring and other equipments

       

Structural Analysis

Structural analysts in the aerospace engineering takes care of the mechanical requirements, durability and every thing that need to be analyzed about the design and structure made.They do analysis of each of the components and make sure they are in the right way.

Manager, Systems Engineer -Lead Aerospace Engineer to perform astrodynamics analysis and algorithm development to support space- and multi-domain missions.



Top Recruiting Agencies

There are ample opportunities for Aerospace Engineering, in government as well as private sector. Check out the list given below to know the top recruiting agencies.

Demands And Future Scope:





Industry 4.0 will require more numbers of engineers, operators and technicians in Aerospace Engineering.

 Students pursuing Aerospace Engineering courses have opportunities in different domains including research and development of aircrafts; maintenance, monitoring and quality control for handling rocket and rocket launchers.



Top Inspiring Aerospace Engineers:

Here is the list of some of the most celebrated and highly honored Aerospace Engineers :

1. Neil Armstrong:



He was the first person to walk on the moon. He spent over 2 hours outside of the aircraft on that historic day. 



2. Elon Musk:



He founded SpaceX, an aerospace manufacturer and CEO and Lead Designer. He stated his commitment to reducing global warming through sustainable energy. 



3. George Cayley 



He was considered one of the most important people. He is viewed as the first scientific aerial investigator. 

4. Kalpana Chawla



She was the first female of India to go to space. She first flew on the Space Shuttle Columbia. 





5. Ram Narain Agrawal

    

 R N Agrawal is and Indian Aerospace engineer who contributed in the Agni series of surface to surface missiles. He is considered the ‘Father of Agni Series of Missiles’.

title: Admitting Medical Officer
summary: Name of the Career profile: Admitting Medical Officer
description:
Name of the Career profile: Admitting Medical Officer



                                                      



Overview

A medical practitioner linked to the Hospital as an employee, surgeon, specialist, consultant, or agent who authorises the admission of any Eligible Patient for treatment at the Hospital is referred to as an Admitting Medical Officer.

Coordinates actions connected to patient admission to a hospital or other medical facility.



Roles and responsibilities of an Admitting Medical Officer





Receives bookings from physicians, confirms admitting privileges of physicians, interviews patients, and enters patient information into the pre-admissions database.





Explains hospital admission procedures, time of admission, room selection, television and video rentals, telephone availability, mobile phone limits, and addressing queries to patients.



Greets and admits patients by conducting personal interviews;  obtaining signatures on legal consents and insurance forms.



Serves and protects the hospital community by complying to professional standards, hospital rules and procedures, as well as federal, state, and local laws.





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career

You could be a people person who enjoys welcoming and assisting patients and interacting with them.



You could love working in a fast-paced medical atmosphere with new challenges every day.



You may love working with physicians but might not wish to become one but could gain knowledge working with them.



You might be interested in the income packages and job stability that come with medical admission clerk positions.





A basic procedure to become an Admitting Medical Officer



STEP:1  Complete class 10th.Can do a Diploma after the 10th.

                                          OR

STEP:2 Complete your class 12th, subjects like Physics, Chemistry, and Biology will be helpful. 

                                          OR

STEP 3: A bachelor's degree, majors like business, healthcare, and psychology could be helpful.

                                          OR

STEP 4: A master's degree will be beneficial just to enhance your skills.





Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment



             ELIGIBILITY 



              Age Limit- Above 18 years



Education Qualification-Bachelor's degrees are most frequently held by admissions officials. Intermediate degrees come in second place while high school diplomas come in third.



SKILLS REQUIRED

Extensive understanding of medical recordkeeping procedures. As there is a lot of records to keep at hospitals.



 Advanced ability to assure the highest levels of patient care and adherence to healthcare legislation. 



Capability to have improvements in one's field of specialty to perform well in their field.





Outstanding interpersonal, communication, and leadership abilities in dealing with patients, staff, and doctors.





            WORKING ENVIRONMENT

            

They work in a hospital. This may include hospitals, public urgent care centers, and government-sponsored long-term care institutions.

The admitting medical officer's working hours can vary depending on the hospital.

They get Sundays or notified holidays. They get shifts decided whether night or day. 

Entrance exams:



Entrance exam for Bachelor in Arts 



CUET-Central Universities Entrance Test.

NPAT-National Test for Programs After Twelfth



And many more.



Entrance exam for BBA 

IPU CET -Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test.

DU JAT-Delhi University Joint Admission Test



And many more.

Courses description with their expenses:



Courses after 12th-

These are some courses that could be helpful after 12th although not mandatory.

Type of education

Fee structure

Duration of the course

1,64,5000 INR per annum

3 years

20,000 - 2,00,000 INR per year

3 Years





Courses after graduation 

These courses might be helpful but not mandatory

title: ArmyOfficerFile
summary: Who are Army officers
description:
Army Officers 





      



Who are Army officers 



The officer plans the work of the organization assigns tasks to subordinates and sees that the work is accomplished to the highest standard. In short, he/she is a leader.



Overview of the Army Officers 



A high level of integrity and personal conduct is required by officers.

Their judgment and skill can mean the difference between life and death for the soldiers they command.

For them, only three things are the priority and those are DUTY, HONOR, and COUNTRY.



Roles and responsibilities of the Army Officer



 An Army officer's main responsibilities include 

organizing operations



allocating tasks,   

           

successfully communicating with the subordinate staff, as well as commanding,



Training



supervising others 



 keeping track of the welfare and development of new recruits

.



Facilities are given to an Army officer



Group Insurance 

Free Medical  Facilities For Self And Family 

Free ration  

Furnished govt. Accommodation at the best location

Canteen facilities

Mess / Club / Sports Facilities 

60 Days of annual leave

20 Days of casual leave 	

Leave Encashment - Up to 300 days

Pension with all benefits

 

A basic procedure to be in an Indian army



There are various pathways through which a candidate can join the Indian Army. Such as:





STEP:1 Complete class 10th.



STEP:2 after class 12th a candidate can give the following exams: -



STEP:3 Prepare for any of the following: -



National Defence Academy (NDA) Exam conducted by UPSC





                            

                                                      

                                                                 OR



Technical Entry Scheme (TES)





                              



After completing a four-year engineering degree, they are given permanent service in the military with the rank of lieutenant.

Another way to join Army is:



STEP:1 Complete the graduation 



STEP:2 Prepare for the following exam



                                         



Combined Defence Service (CDS): Similar to the NDA, the CDS exam will be commenced twice a year by the UPSC. Candidates appearing for the CDS Exam must have completed their graduation or at least be appearing for the final exam of the degree.



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment



ELIGIBILITY 



Education- Candidates must have completed Class 12 with at least 70% aggregate. The candidate needs to pass their graduation



Age -19 to 25(Unmarried)







SKILLS REQUIRED



the ability to communicate concisely and clearly to their team at ground level.





                  



excellent leadership to guide their team in a well and organized manner. 

                                 





Teamwork skills to maintain proper coordination along with their team.





                          



High level of physical fitness to fight all the unknown ground-level challenges.

                 

                         



WORKING ENVIRONMENT



        •  Work environment varies, depending on a soldier's specialty or area of expertise. The working environment for troops can be very stressful, especially while they are deployed. Long periods of time spent away from home and family, as well as frequently working in hazardous situations, are required of soldiers.



Entrance exam to follow



Combined Defence Services (After graduation)

National Defence Academy(After 12th)

Technical Entry Scheme examination.





Courses description with their expenses:



 



No specific courses are required, but entry is 10+2 Tech Entry and can be applied for after the 12th exam.



The University Entry scheme is for those who want to apply to the Army in their final year of engineering.  Those who have completed BE/B Tech can also take a technical graduate course at the IMA.





Top institutions to get trained for defence









Rashtriya Indian Military College (RIMC) 



Army Cadet College (ACC) 



Indian Military Academy (IMA)   



National Defence Academy (NDA) 











                                              

Pay-Scales as per the experience:



Indian government pays attractive salaries to Army officers ranging from Rs 15,000 to Rs 67,000















POST

PAY SCALE

 Captain -

Rs 15,600 to Rs 39,100

Major -

 Rs 15,600 to 39.100

 Lieutenant Colonel-

Rs 37,400 to Rs 67,000

 Colonel-

 Rs 37,400 to Rs 67.000





On addition they get many benefits and allowances such as Transportation Allowances,

Military Service Pay, Counterinsurgency, Uniform Allowances, Field Area Allowances and more.



Promotions & Upgrades











Career progress in the Indian Army 









 The Lieutenant Phase



The very first thing done by the young officers is to complete the primary branch by taking the basic course.



 The Captain Phase 

The captain is normally in charge of over 100 soldiers. During this phase, you attend the officer advanced course

Canteen facilities

Mess / Club / Sports Facilities 

60 Days of annual leave

20 Days of casual leave 	

Leave Encashment - Up to 300 days

Pension with all benefits

 

A basic procedure to be in an Indian army



There are various pathways through which a candidate can join the Indian Army. Such as:





STEP:1 Complete class 10th.



STEP:2 after class 12th a candidate can give the following exams: -



STEP:3 Prepare for any of the following: -



National Defence Academy (NDA) Exam conducted by UPSC





                            

                                                      

                                                                 OR



Technical Entry Scheme (TES)





                              



After completing a four-year engineering degree, they are given permanent service in the military with the rank of lieutenant.

Another way to join Army is:



STEP:1 Complete the graduation 



STEP:2 Prepare for the following exam



                                         



Combined Defence Service (CDS): Similar to the NDA, the CDS exam will be commenced twice a year by the UPSC. Candidates appearing for the CDS Exam must have completed their graduation or at least be appearing for the final exam of the degree.



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment



ELIGIBILITY 



Education- Candidates must have completed Class 12 with at least 70% aggregate. The candidate needs to pass their graduation



Age -19 to 25(Unmarried)







SKILLS REQUIRED



the ability to communicate concisely and clearly to their team at ground level.





                  



excellent leadership to guide their team in a well and organized manner. 

                                 





Teamwork skills to maintain proper coordination along with their team.





                          



High level of physical fitness to fight all the unknown ground-level challenges.

                 

                         



WORKING ENVIRONMENT



        •  Work environment varies, depending on a soldier's specialty or area of expertise. The working environment for troops can be very stressful, especially while they are deployed. Long periods of time spent away from home and family, as well as frequently working in hazardous situations, are required of soldiers.



Entrance exam to follow



Combined Defence Services (After graduation)

National Defence Academy(After 12th)

Technical Entry Scheme examination.





Courses description with their expenses:



 



No specific courses are required, but entry is 10+2 Tech Entry and can be applied for after the 12th exam.



The University Entry scheme is for those who want to apply to the Army in their final year of engineering.  Those who have completed BE/B Tech can also take a technical graduate course at the IMA.





Top institutions to get trained for defence









Rashtriya Indian Military College (RIMC) 



Army Cadet College (ACC) 



Indian Military Academy (IMA)   



National Defence Academy (NDA) 











                                              

Pay-Scales as per the experience:



Indian government pays attractive salaries to Army officers ranging from Rs 15,000 to Rs 67,000















POST

PAY SCALE

 Captain -

Rs 15,600 to Rs 39,100

Major -

 Rs 15,600 to 39.100

 Lieutenant Colonel-

Rs 37,400 to Rs 67,000

 Colonel-

 Rs 37,400 to Rs 67.000





On addition they get many benefits and allowances such as Transportation Allowances,

Military Service Pay, Counterinsurgency, Uniform Allowances, Field Area Allowances and more.



Promotions & Upgrades











Career progress in the Indian Army 









 The Lieutenant Phase



The very first thing done by the young officers is to complete the primary branch by taking the basic course.



 The Captain Phase 

The captain is normally in charge of over 100 soldiers. During this phase, you attend the officer advanced course.



 The Major Phase 

Here you become a key staff officer in charge of personnel, operations, and intelligence. You are provided with the assignment which let you use previously developed skills.



 The Lieutenant Colonel Phase 



Your job could be that of a battalion commander in charge of hundreds of soldiers, or that of a staff officer in a division or corps. Good performance always deserves a tougher position. Some officers are selected for the Army War College, where they become "experts" in their profession. Promotion to lieutenant colonel takes about 17 years.



The Colonel Phase



During this phase, the military will make the most of your talents. This means being deployed as a brigade commander of thousands of soldiers or as the head of a large staff. Your professional skills and accumulated leadership skills will be put to the test. This is the highest level of responsibility. you are the CEO. Promotion to colonel takes about 22 years.



The General Officers 



Officers demonstrating outstanding leadership and execution ability are appointed as general officers. Chief of the Army. They do nothing but run an army. From division commanders to post-commanders to senior staff positions, general officers are responsible for maintaining an efficient and effective army. Promotion to the general public takes about 25 years.







Top recruiting agencies for Army Officers 







India's armed forces is the only recruiting agency for Army officers. The Indian Armed Forces are the military forces of the Republic of India. It consists of three professional uniformed services: the Indian Army, the Indian Navy, and Indian Air Force. 





Benefits and future scope



After joining the army you need not worry about your job and carrier.



You become a warrior for life, disciplined, and best at doing your tasks.



You are given respect after joining  .and that's the best part.



Your parents are proud of you that you're serving as an officer.



You are given a good salary and perks even during your training period. although the salary during training is less. So you need not worry about your financial status



After becoming an officer you are provided with several facilities..



Now after you get retired you can join any private firm or govt firm and you will be given preference as you belonged to a defense past.



Till the time you get retired you will be near perfectionist and you will be just great.









Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



After knowing so much about the Army career let us be aware of some of our heroes of the Indian Army 



                     







                                                         



                                                       

                                                    





                                                              VIKRAM BATRA

 After completing 19 months of training, he graduated from the IMA on 6 December 1997 and was drafted into the Indian Army as a Lieutenant. He was drafted into the 13th Battalion Jammu and Kashmir Rifles (13 JAK Rif).





                                                    

                                      

                                        

                                                        MANOJ KUMAR PANDEY



Captain Manoj Kumar Pandey  graduated from the National Defense Academy on the 90th course and was a member of Mike Company (Mustang) [7]. Pandey was promoted to Lieutenant, 1st Battalion, 11th Gorkha Rifle Regiment on 7 June 1997.







                                               

Lt Gen Manoj C Pande

                                                    

After graduating from NDA, he joined the Indian Military Academy and was commissioned as an officer. 

He then studied at the Military Technical University, Pune, where he obtained a Bachelor of Technology in Civil Engineering.

                                                           

                                                                     

                                                              

                                                                      Saurabh Singh Shekhawat



He is one of the Indian Army's most decorated officers, with one war-time gallantry award and two peace-time gallantry awards to his name.

                                                                        



                                                                    

                                                              Lt Gen Anaardeep Singh Bhinder

                                                       

LT GEN AMARDEEP SINGH BHINDER, AVSM, VSM, GOC-IN-C, SOUTH WESTERN COMMAND. Lieutenant General Amardeep Singh Bhinder, AVSM, VSM took over the command of South Western Command on 1st Apr 2021.

title: Atomic Physicists
summary: Name of the Career Profile: ATOMIC PHYSICISTS
description:
Name of the Career Profile: ATOMIC PHYSICISTS

                    

                                                       

                                                        



Overview

Atomic physics is a branch of physics that studies atoms and molecular systems present in gas and solids. They research about the atomic energy.

It refers to the study of atomic structures and the interations between the different atoms and studying its reaction.

It also includes studying the behaviour of electrons, protons, light,atoms and molecules.

             



          Roles and Responsibilities

To perform and conduct scientific experiments about the molecular systems and derive some results. 

     





To develop scientific theories and models on how atoms react and the behavior and properties of atoms in different times.



              



To write research papers related to the study they made and take grants for that research papers.



                



To design different scientific equipment according to the requirements and develop them for use.



      





Benefits of choosing this career profile



Atomic physicist is a very reputed and recognized job because his contribution in science research is very high.



       



It is a very highly paid job and the employment growth in this field is increasing.



                  

This field is a government owned field so candidates will get a government job after the studies.

         

It is a job in which you serve your nation by working as a atomic physicist for defense of the country.

       











It has to work according to the projects and research needed by the authority they are working under.

    

                                            







A basic procedure to approach this job



                          Complete class 10







                      Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 50% in science stream with            

                      mathematics, chemistry and physics from any recognized school.

                       

                                              

                      Complete graduation in Physics(B.Sc.) from a recognized college or university.



                                  Candidates can also do master’s program(M.Sc.) in the same field of undergraduate 

                                 from a recognized college



                                  After this they can apply for jobs according to its field.





Eligibility and skills required:



Age limit:

There is no age limit to pursue this course



              Educational Qualifications

The candidate must have passed 12th class in science with mathematics stream from any recognized school.

Candidate must have completed undergraduate degree in B.Sc. in Physics 

Candidates can also do post-graduate in M.Sc. in Physics .









              Skills Requirement

                  

                                               Mathematical skills

Critical thinking









                                                                                                                               Problem Solving                   

Quality control analysis                                               

                                                  

                                                   Programming Language











Mathematical skills:

This job requires an advanced level of mathematical skills and knowledge of applying them. Because this field applies some parts of mathematics which are used with physics during experiment and research.



Communication skills:

Communication with other people in the same field is very necessary because  all have to share relevant information to each other. Also, the  designers and engineers have to communicate their ideas and designs to clients so this skill is required.



Problem Solving:

Atomic Physicists  must have complex problem solving skills because the main task of their job is to solve the scientific problems related to atoms and molecules with the help of computer science, physics and mathematics.



Programming Language

Atomic physicist should have skills of knowing the different programming languages because to analyze the experiments done by them, they need to know the programming language like Java, Python , etc



Quality control analysis

Quality control is the most important thing to keep in mind while doing the experiments and also supervising the other experiments. Hence this skill is required.



 Critical Thinking

Critical thinking is a very crucial skill which is required because to know where to apply what critical thinking is very necessary. As physics is a wide subject, going into depth and understanding and applying the theories is very necessary.



           Work environment

The work environment of atomic physicists is mostly in office if they are not involved in the research and experiments.

              Skills Requirement

                  

                                               Mathematical skills

Critical thinking









                                                                                                                               Problem Solving                   

Quality control analysis                                               

                                                  

                                                   Programming Language











Mathematical skills:

This job requires an advanced level of mathematical skills and knowledge of applying them. Because this field applies some parts of mathematics which are used with physics during experiment and research.



Communication skills:

Communication with other people in the same field is very necessary because  all have to share relevant information to each other. Also, the  designers and engineers have to communicate their ideas and designs to clients so this skill is required.



Problem Solving:

Atomic Physicists  must have complex problem solving skills because the main task of their job is to solve the scientific problems related to atoms and molecules with the help of computer science, physics and mathematics.



Programming Language

Atomic physicist should have skills of knowing the different programming languages because to analyze the experiments done by them, they need to know the programming language like Java, Python , etc



Quality control analysis

 Critical Thinking

           Work environment

The work environment of atomic physicists is mostly in office if they are not involved in the research and experiments. 



            

They also work in the laboratories for experiments and research about the atoms and molecules.

       

They have flexible working hours and have 9 to 5 job time.They may have to do overtime if they are working on some of the important projects or research.

                                         



Entrance exam to become atomic physicist



Some colleges take entrance exams for admission while some offer admission on the basis of merit. So for admission to the entrance exam, one has to make sure that he or she passes with the minimum cutoff. Different colleges have different entrance exams.



Joint Entrance Test (JEE) Mains

Joint Entrance Test (JEE) Advanced

Joint Entrance Screening Test(JEST)





Courses to become atomic physicist



Undergraduate courses

The courses mentioned below can be done right after 12th:



Courses

Post – Graduate Courses:

The courses mentioned below can be done right after graduation:

Top institutions:

Here are the top institutions which have ocean engineer courses available:



St. Stephen’s College, Delhi

Types of specialization available for atomic physicist



Quantum Physicist



They observe and provide the description of the properties and nature at the scale of atoms and subatomic particles. They identify and explain the different structures of different atoms. They find out the behavior of matter and light on the atomic and subatomic scale.



                          



Nuclear physicist

Nuclear physicists study the protons and neutrons at the center of an atom and the interactions that hold them together in space. They mainly study the results of all the interactions taking place between the atoms.

                         



Pay scale of atomic Physicist



Pay scale based on Designation



Designation

Average Pay scale{LPA)

Quantum Physicist

INR 7,00,000

Nuclear Physicist

INR 8,00,000



              



      	Pay Scale on basis of experience

Experience

Average Pay scale (LPA)

0-3 years

7 lakhs

4-9 years

12 lakhs

10-20 years

20 lakhs

20+ years

25 lakhs



               



             



Promotions and upgradations 

The promotion and upgradation in this field is from a physicist to a scientist of senior scientist because they have to do more research and experiments after the promotions.

They can upskill and get upgraded by doing master’s or doctrate in the field. Further studies can help themto get promoted easily.





Top recruiters agencies



Bhabha Atomic Research Centres



  



Indira Gandhi Centre of Atomic Research

                     

Raja Ramanna Centre For Advanced Technology

                   

Variable Energy Cyclotron Centre

      



Atomic Minerals Directorate for Exploration and Research 











Demand and Future Scope

The demand for atomic physicists remains consistent every time because the need of them in the research and experiments is limited and also it is a government owned field. Therefore because of no private interventions jobs are limited.

However, the scope of this career is very high as the one getting a job enjoys a good salary and a lot of growth opportunities. This means the field in itself has a high scope of growth.

                 

Top inspiring Atomic physicist



Homi J. Bhabha

  





Homi J. Bhabha was a famous Indian nuclear Physicist and a professor. He is known as “father of India Nuclear program”. He is known for his work on Cascade  process of Cosmic radiations, theory prediction of nouns etc.







Raja Ramanna

             



Raja  Ramanna was an Indian atomic physicist who contributed so much into the Indian nuclear program and also started various operations like ‘operation shakti’ and ‘operation smiling buddha’

He was awarded Padma shri and Padma Bhushan.



Enrico Fermi



            



Enrico Fermi was an Italian physicist and the creator of the world's first nuclear reactor , the Chicago Pile-1. He is called the “architect of the Nuclear Age”. He was awarded with the Nobel Prize in 1938



Vladimir Fock







Vlandamir Fock  was the Soviet Physicist who worked on the foundations of quantum mechanics   and quantum electrodynamics. He is known for creeping waves, relativistic dynamics etc. 



























Chaitanyamoy Ganguly

               

Chaitanyamoy Ganguly is an Indian  nuclear physicist and former head of Nuclear Fuel Cycle and Material Section of the International Atomic Energy Agency. He is credited with many innovations in this field. He was awarded Padma Shri.

title: Automobile Engineer
summary: Overview of the career profile-
description:
Automobile Engineer 



Overview of the career profile-



What do we understand by the term ‘Automobile Engineer’?

The job of an automobile engineer is to design, create, manufacture and evaluate the functioning of parts of vehicles such as motorbikes, cars, tractors etc. There are different types of engineers depending on the specialisation in one particular field.

What is the role of an ‘Automobile Engineer’?

The role of an engineer is to fulfil the following needs and demands of the association-

  Design

The role of an engineer is to bring forth new designs and suggestions such as new structures, models or functioning methods of instruments, equipment or objects. 



Construct



Operate



Solve Problems related to engineering





Develop and implement the functioning of mechanical equipments





Test the proper operation of mechanisms related to vehicles





Bring forth new ideas and suggestions in the field of technology





   Benefits of choosing this profile as a career





According to Pay Scale India, an automobile engineer can earn anywhere between Rs. 2, 50,000, and Rs. 600,000 per annum including average bonuses and commissions received on the job.

A division head with 15 years’ or more experience can earn a salary ranging from Rs. 25, 00, 000 to Rs. 30, 00, 000 per annum.

Job stability is also one of the advantages that comes with the career of an automobile engineer.

A basic procedure to approach this job: 



Step 1 - The aspirant needs to complete class 10th and class 12th with Science stream with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics as core subjects.



Step 2 - Apply for the admission process into different colleges and universities. 



Step 3 - Proceed by appearing for the entrance examination meant for Automobile Engineering such as IIT JEE, AIEEE, BITSAT, state entrance exams etc.



Step 4 - After the clarification of required documents, the candidate will get admission 

             into the institution of the preference. 



Step 5 - Complete the 4-year long engineering course in the concerned field.



Step 6 - Once the candidate has a required graduation degree and 4 years of experience as a trainee or an intern, one can apply for jobs for the different designations of biomedical engineer. 



What is the eligibility criteria of becoming an Automobile Engineer ?





Age limit- 



There is no specific age limit for becoming an engineer. But there is definitely a certain age limit for writing the entrance examinations of Engineering .

Education qualification-



The candidate must have cleared 10+2 from a recognised board of education with Science stream.

The candidate should have either a Diploma in Engineering or graduation degree in Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) or Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) in Automobile Engineering.

Post graduation in MTech or ME in Engineering is also acceptable.

Skills Required-



 To become an engineer it is very much important for the candidate to be able to have the following set if skills which will help in working efficiently and effectively -

The candidate needs to be aware of all the latest developments and advancements in the field of science and technology 



He or she should have a good hold on communication and should be able to convey his or her ideas in a comprehensive manner.



Problem solving attitude is a win-win scenario for any organisation to look for in their employees.



Brilliant management and leadership skills



The candidate should be well aware of all the aspects of engineering in mechanical, combustion, and fuel technology.



Good mechanical skills and performance.





   Entrance exam to follow the route:



Serial no.

Name of the examination

Examination Conducting Body

1.

All India Engineering Entrance Exams 

Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)

2.

Amrita Engineering Entrance Exam 

Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham

3.

Birla Institute of Technology and Science Admission Test (BITSAT) 

Birla Institute of Technology and Sciences (BITS)

4.

BS Abdur Rahman University Engineering Entrance Examination (BSAUEEE) 

BS Abdur Rahman Crescent Institute of Science and Technology

5.

Engineering, Agricultural and Medical Common Entrance Test (EAMCET) 

Andhra Pradesh/ Telangana State Council of HIgher Education 

6.

Indian Institute of Technology Joint Entrance Exam  (IIT JEE) 

Indian Institute of Technology

8.

Karnataka Common Entrance Test (KCET)

10.

SRM Engineering Entrance Examination

title: BACTERIOLOGIST
summary: Name of career profile: BACTERIOLOGIST
description:
Name of career profile: BACTERIOLOGIST



                                              



Overview

Bacteriologists are usually involved in research of microscopic microbes.

In easy words, they study growth, development and characteristics of bacteria. They also study the effects of the bacteria on the living species of the environment which include plants, humans and animals.

They are also involved in collecting samples, identification, testing and classification of the different species of bacteria.



 

Roles and responsibilities of a Bacteriologist

To observe the behavior of bacteria by doing research in laboratories.

                                   

To Identify the type of bacterial species present in the environment.



To do research about the amount of bacteria present in different things, both living and non- living things (such as food, beverages etc.)



To observe the different effects of the bacteria on other animals.



To do research about how some bacteria can be used for medicines and vaccines.



To classify the different species of bacteria and know that which bacteria has a positive effect and which has a negative effect.

              

  







Benefits of choosing this profile career



The need of bacteriologist is forever very high because the research about this needs to be done consistently. Proper research is very important fo studying this topic.

   

The job opportunities in this field always rises because of opening of new hospitals and laboratories.Also, with going times different diseases and infections are coming so bacteriologist are much needed.

 

There are different branches in this field, therefore one can follow its interest and choose the branch accordingly. Working in the field one is interested in helps to grow more.



This has a very wide scope because of various specialiations and mostly all branches are related in a way.

                         Complete class 10.

                         Complete graduation in biology, biochemistry, cell biology, microbiology, or field related

Types of specialization available for Bacteriologist:



Industrial Microbiology



                             



Industrial bacteriologists work in industries for using microorganisms for products of antibiotics, vaccines, food products etc. They apply Microbiology for deriving these industrial products.



Clinical Microbiology and infectious diseases



                                          



Clinical bacteriologists study the effects of bacteria on different living things. It mainly involves work related to finding out the infections and diseases caused by different bacterial species.

Their tasks include identifying bacterial infections.



Agricultural Microbiology



                                         



This has work related to the infections and diseases caused by bacteria in plants. It also involves identifying the causes of soil degradation and soil fertility because of bacteria. 













Pharmaceutical Microbiology



                                          



This field has work related to developing pharmaceuticals with microorganisms like medicines, vaccines etc.

Environmental Microbiology



                                       



It involves work related to study of how bacteria interact in environment and within itself and what are the effects of this interactions on the environment.

title: BIOCHEMICAL ENGINEER
summary: He researches, develops, documents, and then produces the products that are mainly derived from a combination of organic and lab-made materials which is beneficial to the society and people at large.
description:
BIOCHEMICAL ENGINEER



 

➢    Overview 

He researches, develops, documents, and then produces the products that are mainly derived from a combination of organic and lab-made materials which is beneficial to the society and people at large.

They work with others in process and product development. 

They also makes sure that the results of any research, experiments, and collaborations are properly captured as well as documented.



Roles & responsibilities of a Biochemical Engineer

Analysing and understanding current biomanufacturing processes as well as the techniques.



Ensuring all biochemical processes are in accordance with governmental policies and environmental regulations.



Preparing diagrams and schematics by using CAD software.



Reading new scientific research, preparing project plans, attending different conferences, and networking with other engineers, scientists etc.



➢    Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



A biochemical engineer’s job growth rate is higher than average and they make decent money.



They have a career of opportunities like they can work in pharmaceutical firms, polymer industry, a textile manufacturing company, water treatment plants, chemical plants or in other sectors.



They work mostly indoors





➢    A basic procedure to approach this job:





To become a biochemical engineer, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10.

Step-2:  Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 50% in science from any recognized board to get a seat in a college.

Step-3:  Complete Graduation in biochemical engineering courses [B. Tech. /B.E] which will take 4 years.

Step-4: Candidates can also pursue Master's in biochemical engineering courses [M. Tech./ M.E] which will take 2 years.

Step 5:Students who are further interested in doing research in the field of biochemical engineering courses can take up Doctorate in biochemical engineering courses which will take 3 years in most cases.

Step 6: Students can take up an internship program in order to gain practical knowledge or start working in an industry.



Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

●       There is no age limit.

Educational qualifications:



A student must have passed class 12 in any stream with at least 50% aggregate in science from a recognized board.

Candidates must qualify different entrance exams like JEE Main, JEE Advanced, MHT CET, WBJEE etc. in order to get a seat in college.

Complete Graduation in biochemical engineering courses [B. Tech. /B.E] which will take 4 years.

Candidates will have to score 60% and above in their graduation if they want to pursue Master's in biochemical engineering courses [M. Tech./ M.E] which will take 2 years.

Students who are further interested in doing research in the field of biochemical engineering courses can take up Doctorate in biochemical engineering courses which will take 3 years in most cases.

 Students can take up an internship program in order to gain practical knowledge or start working in an industry.



Skill requirements:

To become a successful biochemical engineer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Good communication and teamwork skills



Good analytical skills





Good creativity skills and technical ability in order to turn designs into products



Good in maths, science and technical knowledge



Gives attention to details



Work environment:



A biochemical engineer mainly works in a laboratory or manufacturing plant floors resolving the complex problems of developing a product with improved quality as well as with efficiency.

Entrance Exams to pursue biochemical engineering courses

Below given are some of the entrance exams to pursue biochemical engineering courses:

Joint Entrance Exam [JEE Main]

Joint Entrance Exam [JEE Advanced]

SRM Joint Engineering Entrance Exam [SRMJEEE]

Vellore Institute of Engineering Entrance Exam [VITEEE]

West Bengal Joint Entrance Exam [WBJEE]

Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering [GATE]

Birla Institute of Technology and Science Admission Test (BITSAT) 

Andhra Pradesh Post Graduate Engineering Common Entrance Test

The courses mentioned below can be pursued right after completing 12:

There are numerous good colleges and institutes providing admissions to courses. 

Indian Institute of Technology [IIT],  Delhi

Indian Institute of Technol

Types Of Specialisations Available For a Biochemical Engineer

Some of the most popular biochemical engineering specialisations are listed below. A student can choose the specialisations according to his/her interest in the particular field.

Tissue Engineer 



They work with manufactured and natural tissues to reproduce tissues required by the different medical patients. 

Metabolic Engineer



They improve or modify the metabolic activity of biological organisms like for example bacteria, yeast or plants in order to increase the production of specific metabolites by using the technology which is involved in genetic engineering . 

Biochemist



They study the chemical and physical principles of living things and of biological processes and also tries to improve our quality of life by understanding living organisms at the molecular level.

Biomedical Engineer



They do research and develop solutions to biological and medical problems.



Pay Scale For a Biochemical Engineer

The statistics given below represent the average pay scale of a biochemical engineer in India:

According to recent statistical records, the employment rate of biochemical engineers is projected to grow 6% from 2020 to 2030, about as fast as the average for all occupations.

title: Beautician
summary: An overview of career a profile:
description:
Beautician 



   An overview of career a profile:

Who is a Beautician ?

The job of a beautician is to provide the clients with the beauty treatments and remedies, such as hair therapy, facial massages or body treatment. And these treatments vary from condition to condition for every client.  

What is the role of a Beautician?

The role and responsibilities of a beautician is to fulfil the following needs and demands of the clients-

Providing beauty related treatments and remedies to the clients as per their demands and requirements



Attend all the important workshops organised by the experts 



Develop new styles and techniques 



Working in groups and collaborating with different agencies



Giving the clients proper treatments and consulting them as per needs



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career

 The benefits of choosing beautician as a career profile is always a plus point as it helps in establishing the learning process and also provides the candidates with a good scope of knowledge and learining. 

Job satisfaction and stability



 The job of  a beautician involves creativity and experimentation. There is always a possibility of growth and development in this career field.

Creativity and Growth



 Makeup line is full of growth and new opportunities for the staff members.

Wider Client Base 



Beauticians are expected to meet new clients on a daily basis. This job requires knowledge on how to handle different types of clients which reduces the stress of facing problems. 

Numerous opportunities 



 As you gain the experience and expertise along the journey, there is a wider range of opportunities available in this field. 



 

   A basic procedure to approach this job:



Step 1 - The aspirant needs to complete class 10th and class 12th from a recognised board of education.

Step 2 - Apply for the admission process into different colleges and universities. 



Step 3 - Proceed by appearing for the entrance examination meant for Beautician courses such as CUCET and IPU CET.



Step 4 - After the clarification of required documents, the candidate will get admission 

              into the institution of the preference. 



Step 5 - Complete the graduation course/diploma courses/certificate courses required for pursuing the profession of a beautician.



Step 6 - After completion of all the required courses, one can start with practical work. 

Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :

Age limit- 



There is no specific age limit for becoming a beautician.



Education qualification-



The candidate must have cleared 10+2 from a recognised board of education.

If the candidate wishes to go into a more professional domain then he or she must have a graduation degree. For example, for becoming a cosmetic dermatologist, one should have passed class 12th with science stream as Physics/ Chemistry and Mathematics/Biology as the core subjects. For graduation, they should at least have the degree of MBBS.

Only two types of entrance exams are available for the beautician course- CUCET and IPU CET.



Skills Required-



 To become a Beautician, it is very much important for the candidate to be able to have the following set of skills-

Friendly and Approachable-



A beautician needs to be quite considerate and polite while handling clients.

Be able to manage time



Since the treatment and appointments consume a good amount of time, thus one should be able to manage time properly.

Brilliant Professional Attitude



There are times when the beauticians are asked to work even on the national holidays or during the festive season, so a good amount of deduction is also a skill to be noted down.



Working Environment-







A beautician is generally required to work in the salon. Sometimes they are also asked to go to the venue as per the clients requirements.

They are also at times asked to work for tv celebrities, important public figures.

  Entrance exam to follow the route:



Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test (IPU CET)

Common University Entrance Test ( CUET)

Attend all the important workshops organised by the experts 



Develop new styles and techniques 



Working in groups and collaborating with different agencies



Giving the clients proper treatments and consulting them as per needs



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career

 The benefits of choosing beautician as a career profile is always a plus point as it helps in establishing the learning process and also provides the candidates with a good scope of knowledge and learining. 

Job satisfaction and stability



 The job of  a beautician involves creativity and experimentation. There is always a possibility of growth and development in this career field.

Creativity and Growth



 Makeup line is full of growth and new opportunities for the staff members.

Wider Client Base 



Beauticians are expected to meet new clients on a daily basis. This job requires knowledge on how to handle different types of clients which reduces the stress of facing problems. 

Numerous opportunities 



 As you gain the experience and expertise along the journey, there is a wider range of opportunities available in this field. 



 

   A basic procedure to approach this job:



Step 1 - The aspirant needs to complete class 10th and class 12th from a recognised board of education.

Step 2 - Apply for the admission process into different colleges and universities. 



Step 3 - Proceed by appearing for the entrance examination meant for Beautician courses such as CUCET and IPU CET.



Step 4 - After the clarification of required documents, the candidate will get admission 

              into the institution of the preference. 



Step 5 - Complete the graduation course/diploma courses/certificate courses required for pursuing the profession of a beautician.



Step 6 - After completion of all the required courses, one can start with practical work. 

Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :

Age limit- 



There is no specific age limit for becoming a beautician.



Education qualification-



The candidate must have cleared 10+2 from a recognised board of education.

If the candidate wishes to go into a more professional domain then he or she must have a graduation degree. For example, for becoming a cosmetic dermatologist, one should have passed class 12th with science stream as Physics/ Chemistry and Mathematics/Biology as the core subjects. For graduation, they should at least have the degree of MBBS.

Only two types of entrance exams are available for the beautician course- CUCET and IPU CET.



Skills Required-



 To become a Beautician, it is very much important for the candidate to be able to have the following set of skills-

Friendly and Approachable-



A beautician needs to be quite considerate and polite while handling clients.

Be able to manage time



Since the treatment and appointments consume a good amount of time, thus one should be able to manage time properly.

Brilliant Professional Attitude



There are times when the beauticians are asked to work even on the national holidays or during the festive season, so a good amount of deduction is also a skill to be noted down.



Working Environment-







A beautician is generally required to work in the salon. Sometimes they are also asked to go to the venue as per the clients requirements.

They are also at times asked to work for tv celebrities, important public figures.

title: Biomedical Engineer
summary: Overview of the career profile-
description:
Biomedical Engineer



Overview of the career profile-



What do we understand by the term ‘Biomedical Engineer’?



The job of a biomedical engineer is to resolve problems in the field of biology using principles and applications of technology. Biomedical Engineers invest their theoretical knowledge into practical use by investing and implementing ideas for the benefit of the society. 



What is the role of a ‘Biomedical Engineer’?



The role of a biomedical engineer is to make use of science and advanced technology in performing complex experiments in favour of human betterment.



The role of a biomedical engineer is to fulfil the following needs and demands-



Designing advanced medical equipments

Maintain and improve the functioning of medical instruments



Creating prostheses



Creating systems and softwares for handicaps



Put into use the practical application of Technology in real life situations



  Benefits of choosing Biomedical Engineering as a career-

Working for the social cause

Biomedical engineers help the society by improvising the existing healthcare facilities.



Higher pay

There is a good amount of income generated by performing the roles and responsibilities of an automobile engineer.



Creativity alongside growth

Bringing forth new and original ideas in the field of development will definitely result in overall growth of the employee.





A perfect blend of engineering and medical sciences

Putting into use different theories and experiments of science and engineering into the healthcare brings out a beneficial blend of both 



Procedure of becoming an Engineer-



Step 1 - The aspirant needs to complete class 10th and class 12th with Science stream.



Step 2 - Apply for the admission process into different colleges and universities.

 

Step 3 - Proceed by appearing for the entrance examination meant for Biomedical Engineering.



Step 4 - After the clarification of required documents and passing the examination, the candidate will get admission into the institution as per the preference. 



Step 5 - Complete the 4-year long biomedical engineering course.



Step 6 - Appear for the entrance exam meant for Biomedical Engineering, i.e. JEE Mains, JEE Advance, VITEEE, BITSAT etc



Step 7 - Once the candidate has a required graduation degree, the candidate can now practise by applying for various internship programs.





What is the eligibility criteria of becoming an engineer ?







Age limit- 

There is no specific age limit for becoming an engineer. But there is definitely a certain age limit for writing the entrance examinations of Engineering .

Education qualification-

The candidate must have cleared 10+2 from a recognised board of education with Science stream.

The candidate must have scored an aggregate of 75% in all the subjects.

The candidate should have either a Diploma in Engineering or a graduation degree in Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) BiomedicalEngineering 

Post graduation in MS (Biomedical Engineering) is also beneficial in becoming a Biomedical Engineer. 



Skills Required-



To become a biomedical engineer it is very much important for the candidate to be able to have the following set if skills which will help in working efficiently and effectively -



Ability to solve and reason the problem



Excellent awareness of surroundings and its requirements



Perceive and visualise



Ability to work in a group 





Working environment for a Biomedical engineer-





Within a hospital, a biomedical engineer may be tasked with using technology to optimise the clinical workflow and enhance healthcare delivery (sometimes called clinical engineering). Or they could also work in rehabilitation, providing tools and devices to assist people who are recovering from (or adjusting to) impairments.



Ways of becoming a Biomedical Engineer-



As we already know that there are different types of engineers depending upon the field of specialisation, there is more than one way of starting your journey in the field of Biomedical Engineering.









 Entrance exam to follow the route-





The following is the list of entrance exams to get admissions into colleges for Graduation-

All India Engineering Entrance Exams (AIEEE)

Bharath University Engineering Entrance Exam

Biotech Consortium India Limited (BCIL) Common Entrance Test

Engineering, Agriculture and Medical Common Entrance Test (EAMCET)

Indian Institute of Technology – Joint Entrance Examination (IIT-JEE)

Jadavpur University Biomedical Engineering Entrance Examination

Sant Longowal Institute of Engineering and Technology Entrance Test (SLIET) (B.E/B.Tech)

VIT Engineering Entrance Examination 

Course description with their expenses-

The candidate can apply for graduation, post graduation or diploma courses in the field of biomedical engineering.

Type of education

Name of the course

Duration of the course

Course fees

Diploma

Diploma in Bioinstrumentation





3 years

INR 32,500

Dipl

title: CHARTERED ACCOUNTANT
summary: Chartered  Accountant
description:
Chartered  Accountant



Overview:

This job profile is meant to manage the financial entities for a company including taxation, auditing, business strategies, and budgeting. 

Chartered accountants provide financial advisory to the clients and manage money related matters for a firm.

A chartered accountant is trained to manage the accounting and taxation related matters. They can work either in the private or in the government sector. There are 3 levels of CA exam to be qualified to become a certified CA. If you think you have determination and dedication towards this career profile then do read the complete post. 



Roles and responsibilities:

The auditor's responsibility is to confirm that publicly released financial statements were produced in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles.



Tax assessment includes financial accounting as a critical component. He prepares tax returns, defends customers in front of tax officials, and gives his clients general tax counsel.



He must provide costing data to the relevant management. In addition to determining appropriate selling prices, a professional Chartered accountant's duties also involve adopting cost-control strategies.



Accountants routinely investigate a company's financial standing, particularly in connection with a firm's purchase or sale, its reconstruction or merger, or the issue of additional shares.



In management accounting, a professional Chartered accountant is in charge of many different activities, such as formulating policies, maintaining daily control, assessing performance, etc.







Benefits of Choosing Chartered Accountancy as a Career Profile:

               

The scope for Chartered Accountancy is much extensive in India as well as in Abroad. Here are some of the benefits of choosing CA as a career option.

Consistent and growing field: chartered accountancy has seen large growth rates through the past years. According to the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS), the employment growth for accountancy will increase by 11% from 2014 to 2024.





Various career choices: being a chartered accountant opens up various career choices. A certified CA can choose to work as a consultant, SAP specialist or set up their own practice.



Unlimited professional growth: CA is one of the most reputed and challenging careers with great professionalism. 



Great pay scale: CA is one of the highest paying jobs in the world. The pay scale of a certified Chartered Accountant is arguably higher than most of the other career profiles.



Being your own boss as you can establish your own company or do a start up as a lot of knowledge you own to be a better entrepreneur.



Basic Procedure to Approach Chartered Accountancy:

       

STEP 1: After class 12, a student may choose any stream to pursue Chartered Accountancy.Sign up for the CA Course as soon as you pass the 12th grade.

STEP 2:(Entry into the Foundation) or upon graduation (Direct entry).

STEP 3: Qualify the first level of the CA course which is CA Foundation course.

STEP 4: Qualify the second level of the CA course which is CA Intermediate Course.

STEP 5: Qualify the third and the last level of the CA course which is CA Final STEPS Course.

STEP 6: Complete a CA article ship of 3 years for the certification.

STEP 7: You have the option of working with a government or private ST business. A certified CA can open a practice.



Eligibilities and Skill Requirements:

Eligibilities to Become a CA:

Age Limit:

There is no age limit for a Chartered Accountancy. 

A candidate can enroll in the CA course soon after passing class 12th.

Educational qualifications:

Minimum educational qualification for a CA course is 12th class.

A candidate applying for the CA must have secured at least 50% aggregate in the qualifying examination if he/she is from the Commerce background.

For the students from Non-commerce background, it is necessary to obtain at least 60% aggregate in the qualifying examination.

Skill Requirements to become a CA:

Analytical thinking as they have to analyze a lot of data and take actions accordingly in their field of work.



Confidentiality so that the organization they are working in their data is not leaked or shared to the rivals.



Market awareness being a chartered accountant requires a lot of market awareness to be up to date in their field.



Good communication skills in an individual will lead to success in the field as they need to communicate to the people in their organization and make them aware about the finances.



Strong problem solving skills as they have to deal with lot of unknown challenges in their field of making sure that their company is not in loss.



            Working Environment for a CA:

           

The range of this area is broad, so you may decide where you want to stay and carry out your duties with all your heart. In other professions, it depends on the sector and you have to relocate to work in a particular nation.

Basic Procedure to Approach Chartered Accountancy:

       

STEP 1: After class 12, a student may choose any stream to pursue Chartered Accountancy.Sign up for the CA Course as soon as you pass the 12th grade.

STEP 4: Qualify the second level of the CA course which is CA Intermediate Course.

STEP 5: Qualify the third and the last level of the CA course which is CA Final STEPS Course.

STEP 6: Complete a CA article ship of 3 years for the certification.

STEP 7: You have the option of working with a government or private ST business. A certified CA can open a practice.



Eligibilities and Skill Requirements:

Eligibilities to Become a CA:

Age Limit:

There is no age limit for a Chartered Accountancy. 

A candidate can enroll in the CA course soon after passing class 12th.

Educational qualifications:

Minimum educational qualification for a CA course is 12th class.

A candidate applying for the CA must have secured at least 50% aggregate in the qualifying examination if he/she is from the Commerce background.

For the students from Non-commerce background, it is necessary to obtain at least 60% aggregate in the qualifying examination.

Skill Requirements to become a CA:

Analytical thinking as they have to analyze a lot of data and take actions accordingly in their field of work.



Confidentiality so that the organization they are working in their data is not leaked or shared to the rivals.



Market awareness being a chartered accountant requires a lot of market awareness to be up to date in their field.



Good communication skills in an individual will lead to success in the field as they need to communicate to the people in their organization and make them aware about the finances.



Strong problem solving skills as they have to deal with lot of unknown challenges in their field of making sure that their company is not in loss.



            Working Environment for a CA:

           

The range of this area is broad, so you may decide where you want to stay and carry out your duties with all your heart. In other professions, it depends on the sector and you have to relocate to work in a particular nation.

Accounting, however, is extremely distinct since it satisfies a universal need. Everyone may gain from the services offered by an accountant, from farmers to the software development companies. When you decide where to live and work, it gives you choice in how much work you produce.







Entrance exams for Chartered Accountancy:

To become a certified CA, one can enroll in the CA course right after pursuing class 12th. There are three levels of entrance exams to be cleared. These entrance exams are according to the level of courses you pursue. Have a look at the details given below to know more about the CA Entrance exams.

LEVEL-1: CA CPT Exam

CA CPT is a level 1 exam that can be qualified only after completing a 4 months CA foundation course.

A student who has completed class 12th is eligible for the CA CPT exam.



LEVEL-2: CA IPCC Exam

CA IPCC Exam is a level 2 exam for admission to CA Intermediate Course.

This exam can only be taken after qualifying the CA CPT Entrance test.



LEVEL-3: CA Articleship

CA Articleship is not an entrance exam, but is a 3 years internship under a chartered accountant. 

Admission to the final Course of CA is provided only after successfully completing the CA articleship.



Courses for Chartered Accountancy
