Specialization under Chartered Accountancy:



Opting Chartered Accountancy as a career option can offer you numerous specializations too. For your reference, we have listed all the specializations for Chartered Accountancy.



Corporate Financing-Corporate finance is the domain of finance that deals with financing sources, capital structures of businesses, managerial activities to raise the firm's value to shareholders, and the tools and analyses used to distribute financial resources.



Management & Corporate Consultancy-Giving organizations advice on how to perform better or in any other manner support the achievement of organizational goals is known as management consulting.



Financial reporting-Financial statements are official records of a person, business, or other entity's financial situation and actions. Structured and simple to comprehend presentations of pertinent financial data are made.



Management Accounting-According to the Corporate Finance Institute, managerial accounting is defined as "identifying, measuring, analyzing, and interpreting accounting information" to help corporate executives make informed financial decisions and effectively manage their day-to-day operations. ” process.



Tax advisor-Tax accountants are financial professionals with training and knowledge of tax accounting and tax law.



Company Liquidator-A liquidator refers to an officer specially appointed to handle the affairs of a company when the company is closed, usually when the company goes bankrupt. The company's assets are sold by the liquidator and the resulting funds are used to pay off the company's liabilities.



GST Expert-The Central and State governments have given the GST expert the authority to do any or all of the following tasks on behalf of a taxable person. These consist of: Submit a new application for registration. Send in a request to modify or cancel your registration.





Auditor-An auditor is a person qualified to examine financial documents, confirm their accuracy, and make sure businesses are following tax regulations.

Kumar Mangalam Birla



The chairman of the Aditya Birla Group, one of India's major international conglomerates, and a billionaire businessman, Kumar Mangalam Birla was born in India on June 14, 1967. He is also a Chartered Accountant and philanthropist. He is also the chairman of the Indian Institute of Management Ahmedabad and the chancellor of the Birla Institute of Technology & Science.

T. N. Manoharan is a chartered accountant and the former chairman of Canara Bank, India's fourth-largest public sector bank. He also served as president of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI).

The Group MD of Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd. is Motilal Oswal (MOFSL). He co-founded the company with co-promoter Raamdeo Agrawal in 1987 and is a Chartered Accountant.

title: CHEMIST
summary: Name of the career profile: CHEMIST
description:
Name of the career profile: CHEMIST



 

➢    Overview 

They research, evaluate, and experiment on the molecular and atomic levels of various substances in order to develop or improve variety of materials or products like metals, pharmaceutical drugs, plastics, cosmetics etc. 

They use different advanced chemicals and specialised equipments like that of reagents, centrifuges, compounds, data analytics software,  pipettes etc.



Roles & responsibilities of a Chemist



Mixing, compounding and testing different solutions, reagents, and chemical agents.





Analysing the results of their experiments.





Presenting their findings to others and thereby collaborating with other professionals, like scientists, engineers, laboratory technicians etc.







Coming up with the necessary hypothesis.





➢    Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



A chemist’s job growth rate is higher than average and they make decent money.



They can pursue a variety of specialisations like agrochemistry, biochemistry, forensic chemistry based on their interests or career goals.

A chemist makes a positive difference in the society.



They have a career of advanced opportunities like chemical engineers, clinical laboratory managers etc.

             

             

➢    A basic procedure to approach this job:

 



To become a chemist, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and choose science stream.

Step-2:  Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 50% in Science stream to get a seat in a college.

Step-3:  Complete Graduation BSc in Chemistry./B.Tech in Chemical Engineering/ B. Pharm or other related fields which will take 3-4 years. 

Step-4: Complete Masters MSc. in Chemistry/M.Pharm/ M.Tech in Chemical Engineering or other related fields which will take 2 years .

Step 5: Candidates then can either go for higher studies or can go for internships in different companies to get a knowledge as well as experience in the real pharmaceutical world. 

Step 6: Candidates can then apply for different job profiles like as Hospital Chemists, Chemist Analyst, Drug Inspector, Administrative Officer, Clinical Research Associate etc.



Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

●       The minimum age limit is 17 years.

Educational qualifications:

Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 50% in the Science stream to get a seat in a college.

Admissions to college are done on a merit basis and entrance exams scores of different colleges like BITSAT, MHTCET, GPAT etc.

Complete Graduation that is BSc in Chemistry./B.Tech in Chemical Engineering/ B. Pharm or other related fields which will take 3-4 years. Candidates can also take up diploma in pharmacy courses which will take 2 years.

Complete Masters that is MSc. in Chemistry/M.Pharm/ M.Tech in Chemical Engineering or other related fields which will take 2 years .

Candidates then can either go for higher studies in order to increase their opportunities to get a job with a higher salary package or can go for internships in different companies to get a knowledge as well as experience in the real pharmaceutical world. 

After completing internship and necessary education, candidates can apply for different job profiles like as Hospital Chemists, Chemist Analyst, Drug Inspector, Administrative Officer, Clinical Research Associate etc.





Skill requirements:



Good communication skills





Good mathematical skills





Good organising skills





Good time management skills





Good analytical skills





Good team working skills





Work environment:

Chemists usually work with both poisonous and explosive materials in pleasant, well-equipped laboratories, plants, offices, or classrooms. 

 



Entrance Exams To Become a Chemist

There are different entrance exams available to become a chemist. Below given are some of the entrance exams which the candidates have to qualify in order to get admitted to a college:



Birla Institute of Technology and Sciences entrance exam [BITSAT]

Maharashtra Health and Technical Common Entrance Test [MHTCET]

Graduate Pharmacy Aptitude Test [GPAT]

Karnataka Common Entrance Test [KCET]

Delhi University Entrance Exam [DUET]

Jawaharlal Nehru University Entrance Exam [JNUEE]

Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test [IPU CET]



Courses To Become a Chemist

●        Undergraduate chemist courses

The courses mentioned below can be pursued right after completing 12: 



Course

Duration

Average Fees

BSc. in Chemistry

4 years

INR 1-9 lakhs

B.Tech. in Biotechnology

INR 1.2-10.5 lakhs

B.Sc. in Biotechnology

INR 50,000-INR 2,00,000 



Postgraduate chemist courses

The courses mentioned below can be pursued after completing graduation:

Course

Types Of Jobs Available For a Chemist

Chemical Engineer

Analytical Chemist

title: CIVIL ENGINEER
summary: CAREER PROFILE: CIVIL ENGINEER
description:
CAREER PROFILE: CIVIL ENGINEER



Overview of this career profile- 

Who is a Civil Engineer?



An engineer having knowledge of building and maintaining infrastructure is known as a civil engineer. Highway, railroad, and airport construction projects all involve civil engineers. 



In the project planning stage, civil engineers may work in office settings, and they may supervise project implementation on construction sites.



What kind of Job is it, Field or desk?

The job of a Civil Engineer is desk as well as field work. An engineer who plans and manages infrastructure projects like highways, dams, and stormwater drainage is known as a civil engineer. Find out the fundamentals of what a civil engineer's life can be like.

Civil engineer have to work for construction and Repair of infrastructure like Roads, Bridges and building.

They provide the networks and links for making transportation possible.





Roles and responsibilities of a Civil Engineer:



The roles and responsibilities of a Civil Engineer depend on the specialization he/she has chosen and their experience. But, the general responsibilities of a Civil Engineer are stated below.

In a safe, timely, and sustainable manner, manage, design, develop, produce, and maintain small-scale through large-scale construction projects.







carry out on-site research and data analysis (maps, reports, tests, drawings and other)







Conduct technical and feasibility research, and create designs that meet technical requirements.Analyze potential risks, resources, and expenses





Give guidance and find innovative solutions to any new issues or deficiencies





overseeing, coaching, and coordinating with a range of stakeholders and to give the final structures and services to the user. 







Track development and create project status reports.







Budget management and equipment/material purchases







Deliver technical files and other relevant technical documentation while adhering to all rules and regulations, including those pertaining to permits, safety, etc.





Benefits Of Choosing Civil Engineering As A Career Profile:

High Earning Potential

An entry-level civil engineering student can typically expect to make between three and four lakhs per year. The figure can increase significantly after a few years of practice. This is only one of the many things that make this area of engineering one of the finest paid job markets in the industry.







Great Growth Prospects

A study area like civil engineering provides chances for early career evaluations. The majority of jobs in the engineering area, whether in India or the USA, are in the civil engineering sector. 

A degree in civil engineering will undoubtedly give your career an edge because job growth in the field is predicted to increase by 11% in the upcoming years.







Networking Opportunities

Working on multiple sites and interacting with various people is required in civil engineering. For civil engineers, the effort pays off since it significantly widens their network. You get to interact with a lot of individuals, such as architects, contractors, and others, which could be quite beneficial in the future.







Field Work

Yes, civil engineers do spend some time in their offices drafting plans, scheduling meetings, and doing other office-related tasks. However, a large portion of their workday is spent outside at job sites where they monitor structures and projects and address problems. 

Civil engineers are constantly on the go due to the nature of their work. Additionally, it is dynamic, which means that every project has a unique set of operational and design constraints. Additionally, engineers get to collaborate closely with employees, architects, and contractors.







How To Become a Civil Engineer?



To become an engineer right after 10th follow the path given below.

Step-1: Decision Making- Making a decision is the most important stage in selecting a career path. Students who want to be civil engineers should make their decision very early so they may start getting ready for the course and its other requirements.

Step-2: Subject Selection: Students aspiring to become civil engineers should put their future admission to a reputable college before everything else. Students taking their class 12th board exams must take Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics in addition to Civil Engineering.

Step-3: Preparing for Entrance Exams: Once passing the 10+2 test, candidates are eligible to take any Entrance Exam. They can enroll in a Civil Engineering course only after they have passed the Entrance Exam. Each student who passes the exam is assigned a specific college where they can begin their graduation path during counseling.

Step-4: Choosing the Right Colleges: You must achieve very high grades in intermediate and entrance exams if you want to be admitted to government colleges. If your grades are low, the only college that will take your grades is a private institution

How To Become a Civil Engineer?



To become an engineer right after 10th follow the path given below.

Step-1: Decision Making- Making a decision is the most important stage in selecting a career path. Students who want to be civil engineers should make their decision very early so they may start getting ready for the course and its other requirements.

Step-2: Subject Selection: Students aspiring to become civil engineers should put their future admission to a reputable college before everything else. Students taking their class 12th board exams must take Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics in addition to Civil Engineering.

Step-3: Preparing for Entrance Exams: Once passing the 10+2 test, candidates are eligible to take any Entrance Exam. They can enroll in a Civil Engineering course only after they have passed the Entrance Exam. Each student who passes the exam is assigned a specific college where they can begin their graduation path during counseling.

Step-4: Choosing the Right Colleges: You must achieve very high grades in intermediate and entrance exams if you want to be admitted to government colleges. If your grades are low, the only college that will take your grades is a private institution. Experts and specialists advise that because government colleges offer the best facilities, admittance there is always preferable.Step-5: complete Graduation in Law

Step-6: After Qualifying Exam: After passing the qualifying exam, your journey really only starts when you show up for the entrance exam and get accepted into the best college. This four-year adventure requires a lot of perseverance and effort to complete. There are various theoretical and practical classes included in the four-year Civil Engineering programme.

Step-7: Internship: All students are required to complete an internship after completing their bachelor's degree programme. Most students are typically provided internships in various organizations as soon as the course is through, but anyone who wants can pursue higher education as well.



 Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:



The age limit for becoming an Engineer completely depends on the route you choose after 12th. 

Educational qualifications:



A student must have passed 12th in any stream with at least 60% aggregate from any government recognized board.

Physics, Chemistry, and Math must all be passed in the 10+2 or equivalent examination from an accredited college.

The JEE Mains, JEE Advanced, KCET, MHCET, and other entrance exams serve as the primary means of admission to this Btech Civil Engineering programme.

Skill requirements:



To become a successful Civil Engineer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Leadership Skills-



Control of the entire project, civil engineers need to be strong leaders and managers. 

From strategy to implementation, coordinate numerous tasks.



Good communication skills-



Able to express concepts succinctly  

Offer instructions without leaving any room for confusion.





Technical skills-



Possess strong mathematics and physical abilities in order to recognise 

Address engineering issues.





Visualizing Skills-



Able to envision and visualize the finished products of the things they are now creating.

Problem solving and analytical skills-

The ability to assess, recognise, and solve complex issues that arise in their line of work is a must for civil engineers.





Critical thinking-





Persuasive skills 

In-depth knowledge of legal terms.



Working Environment- 



The working environment for a civil engineer is full of technology driven activities and projects. The work of an engineer is quite flexible as it depends upon the need and demand of the situation.

The surrounding atmosphere is full of practicality and physical hard work.

There can also be a constant demand for efforts in terms of creativity, data analysis, project management and strategic planning.  

Office-

Although they must create their blueprints in an office environment, civil engineers also need to spend a lot of time on the job site supervising construction. Civil engineers perform their profession in a range of settings. Civil engineers may spend the majority of their time indoors in offices developing plans.

Field Work-

Highway, railroad, and airport construction projects all involve civil engineers. In the project planning stage, civil engineers may work in office settings, and they may supervise project implementation on construction sites. 





Entrance Exams To Become A Civil Engineer:

Types Of Specializations Available For A Civil Engineer:

Some of the most popular specializations are listed below. A student can choose the specializations according to his/her interest in the particular field.

Building engineers



Their responsibility is to oversee the construction process and make sure that it is completed on schedule. 

Construction engineers are frequently responsible for the planning and security of temporary designs during construction.



Geological Engineers



Their responsibility is to make sure that the development of buildings, roads, runways, dams, and other structures centers on how civil engineers' works, such as buildings and tunnels, interact with the earth (including soil and rock).





Highway engineers



They are responsible for planning and organizing parking spaces, one-way systems, and street management. 

Concerns of highway engineers include traffic control and road construction.



Structural engineers



To ensure the strength and durability of large projects like buildings, bridges, or dams, it is their role to plan and assess them. 

They design buildings, bridges, tunnels, and other structures, and they typically serve as consultants to architects or design-build firms.

Promotions And Upgrading-

The civil service, civil engineers can progress from-





First level  promotion - In the beginning, the employee joins any organization at the basic level where he or she works. This post includes titles such as Junior Engineer and Trainee

Senior Engineer - The second level of promotion is when any Junior employee or a trainee becomes a Senior Engineer, the role of this post involves managing the junior staff and maintaining the proper operation of daily activities. 

Supervisor- At the third level of promotion the employee now takes up the role of a supervisor. The role of a supervisor is to educate, sponsor, coach, counselor and direct the employees working under him.

Manager - The final stage of promotion is the post of a Manager, where the employee is asked to hire contractors and build teams, provide clear and concise instructions to engineering teams etc



This kind of promotion also takes place in big businesses.

Assistant Engineer-15 to 20 years from AE(Civil), Group-B Gazetted, Level-7 to EE(Civil), Group-A, Gazetted, Level-11 (only if you are a degree holder, diploma holder who joined the department after 17th October 2012 not eligible to get promoted to the post of EE).

Junior Engineer- A level progression from JE(Civil), Group-B Non-gazetted, Level-6 to AE(Civil), Group-B Gazetted, Level-7 nearly 10 years, on average.

Demands And Future Scope-

As long as there are people, there will be a need to construct buildings, roads, sewer systems, canals, bridges, and dams. Therefore, you can be sure that civil engineers will always have work. 



The market for civil engineering is predicted to increase to USD 11.72 trillion by 2025, according to a recent study report by Global Marketing Insights, Inc., giving civil engineers a wide range of employment options worldwide. work, whether it's now or in the future.



California (38,050), Texas (23,730), Florida (12,720), Pennsylvania (12,650), and New York are the states with the greatest employment rates for civil engineers (13,620).







Top Inspiring Civil Engineers

Here is the list of some of the most celebrated and highly honored Civil Engineers from the world.

Sir Mokshagundam Visvesvaraya

Sir MV Visvesvaraya was and still is a legend in Indian civil engineering. His professional experience included city planning, economics, dams, flood protection systems, and dams, to name a few.





Isambard Kingdom Brunel

He created the SS Great Britain, the largest ship in the world at the time and the first iron ship with propellers to sail the oceans, which was launched in 1843.



 Beatrice Shilling

Before the war, she could frequently be seen competing in motorcycle races at Docklands, where she became only the second woman to reach speeds of over 100 mph.



Elon Musk

Of fact, Elon Musk is much more than just a civil engineer, and his accomplishments appear to grow every year. I could certainly list more titles—engineer, industrial designer, business owner, philanthropist—but I'll stop now. With two BA Honors degrees—one in Economics and the other in Physics—he graduated in 1997.



John Smeaton

In the 18th century, John Smeaton became the first person to describe himself as a civil engineer. He later became renowned as the father of civil engineering. All around the UK, you can still see the results of his labor on bridges, canals, lighthouses, harbors, and mills.

title: COUNSELOR
summary: CAREER PROFILE: COUNSELOR
description:
CAREER PROFILE: COUNSELOR



Overview:

Who is a COUNSELOR?

A skilled person who employs counseling techniques to assist clients in managing and resolving their mental and emotional problems is known as a counselor. 



In addition to formulating treatment programmes and coping mechanisms, their responsibilities also involve listening to patients. They are employed by hospitals, schools, and mental health institutes.

What kind of Job is it, Field or desk?

The job of a counselor's desk work. Your duties will also include listening to clients, asking necessary questions, and developing effective strategies to improve their lives.



Roles and responsibilities of a lawyer:

Evaluating patients through in-depth discussions, interviews, and observations in order to choose the best test or examination.



determining the patient's emotional and mental conditions.



making treatment regimens that are effective and incorporate counseling, medication, or other services.



Creating goals with your patient while working together.



Regularly discussing the treatment plan with your patient can help you find any issues or areas for improvement.



teaching patients how to use healthy coping skills to get them through challenging circumstances.



keeping track of the patient's development and altering the course of treatment as necessary.



Benefits Of Choosing Counselor As A Career Profile:

1. You aim to bring about improvement.



The life of the counselor as well as the lives of others can be improved by a career in counseling. This is very satisfying all by itself.

2. You are looking for a meaningful job and want to change the world.



Everybody has issues that they must learn to deal with in their daily lives, and counselors are an invaluable resource for individuals who require emotional support. 

People frequently choose to consult with a counselor when they are under stress in order to receive direction, support, and assistance. 

You will be taught practical tools to assist real people in the real world while performing important work.

3. You desire career flexibility.



A profession in counseling can be quite versatile. Counseling is a fantastic option for those wanting flexibility in their career because counselors can arrange their clients around their existing obligations or preferred work/life balance. A counselor works an average of slightly over 34 hours per week.

4. The industry has promising career prospects.



The number of counselors employed is predicted to increase significantly, from 15,000 in 2005 to over 25,000 in 2020. (Health Times).

5. Life experience is valued in this field.



Counselors are divided into two main age groups: 25–34 and 45–54, offering a variety of youth and experience.



How To Become a Counselor?



With a job in counseling, you have the chance to assist people every day, and this tool is available to assist you in achieving your career objectives. Our goal is to give you all the knowledge you require to pursue a career as a professional counselor, regardless of where you live, whether you are a practicing professional, looking for a career change, or are following a dream or passion.

How to Be a Counselor: The Steps

study for a master's in counseling-

STEP 1-You must first obtain a master's degree in counseling in order to pursue a successful career in counseling and to be eligible for state licensure or certification. Prior to applying, decide on your counseling preferences and areas of expertise to assist you best prepare for your future job in this fulfilling field.

Complete one hour of practicum or internship.- 

STEP 2-Approximately 600 hours are needed for the internship. Both within and outside of client meetings, students work independently and under close supervision. The clinical site holds counseling interns to the same requirements as other working professionals.

Obtain certification or licensure in counseling-

STEP 3- Most states forbid practicing medicine or taking client health insurance without a license or credential. Counselors must be licensed or certified in order to practice in their state. Proof that a counseling professional has completed state-established qualification standards, such as a minimum level of education and required hours of supervised clinical experience, is provided by a valid state counseling license or certification.

Pick a career in counselling.- 

STEP 4- You can discover counseling career prospects in hospitals, mental health clinics, private offices, drug and alcohol recovery programmes, schools, nursing homes, and government and community agencies with a master's degree and a state license or certification. Who is a COUNSELOR?

A skilled person who employs counseling techniques to assist clients in managing and resolving their mental and emotional problems is known as a counselor. 



In addition to formulating treatment programmes and coping mechanisms, their responsibilities also involve listening to patients. They are employed by hospitals, schools, and mental health institutes.

What kind of Job is it, Field or desk?

The job of a counselor's desk work. Your duties will also include listening to clients, asking necessary questions, and developing effective strategies to improve their lives.



Roles and responsibilities of a lawyer:

Evaluating patients through in-depth discussions, interviews, and observations in order to choose the best test or examination.



determining the patient's emotional and mental conditions.



making treatment regimens that are effective and incorporate counseling, medication, or other services.



Creating goals with your patient while working together.



Regularly discussing the treatment plan with your patient can help you find any issues or areas for improvement.



teaching patients how to use healthy coping skills to get them through challenging circumstances.



keeping track of the patient's development and altering the course of treatment as necessary.



Benefits Of Choosing Counselor As A Career Profile:

1. You aim to bring about improvement.



The life of the counselor as well as the lives of others can be improved by a career in counseling. This is very satisfying all by itself.

2. You are looking for a meaningful job and want to change the world.



Everybody has issues that they must learn to deal with in their daily lives, and counselors are an invaluable resource for individuals who require emotional support. 

People frequently choose to consult with a counselor when they are under stress in order to receive direction, support, and assistance. 

You will be taught practical tools to assist real people in the real world while performing important work.

3. You desire career flexibility.



A profession in counseling can be quite versatile. 5. Life experience is valued in this field.



Counselors are divided into two main age groups: 25–34 and 45–54, offering a variety of youth and experience.



How To Become a Counselor?



With a job in counseling, you have the chance to assist people every day, and this tool is available to assist you in achieving your career objectives. Our goal is to give you all the knowledge you require to pursue a career as a professional counselor, regardless of where you live, whether you are a practicing professional, looking for a career change, or are following a dream or passion.

How to Be a Counselor: The Steps

study for a master's in counseling-

STEP 1-You must first obtain a master's degree in counseling in order to pursue a successful career in counseling and to be eligible for state licensure or certification. Prior to applying, decide on your counseling preferences and areas of expertise to assist you best prepare for your future job in this fulfilling field.

Complete one hour of practicum or internship.- 

STEP 2-Approximately 600 hours are needed for the internship. Both within and outside of client meetings, students work independently and under close supervision. The clinical site holds counseling interns to the same requirements as other working professionals.

Obtain certification or licensure in counseling-

STEP 3- Most states forbid practicing medicine or taking client health insurance without a license or credential. Counselors must be licensed or certified in order to practice in their state. Proof that a counseling professional has completed state-established qualification standards, such as a minimum level of education and required hours of supervised clinical experience, is provided by a valid state counseling license or certification.

Pick a career in counselling.- 

STEP 4- You can discover counseling career prospects in hospitals, mental health clinics, private offices, drug and alcohol recovery programmes, schools, nursing homes, and government and community agencies with a master's degree and a state license or certification. As you work toward your academic and professional objectives, keep coming back to this website for advice and as a resource.

Improve your skills by staying educated-

STEP 5- As their work develops, a competent counselor stays abreast of the times and keeps learning new things. With fresh research and conclusions, the counseling field is always expanding. There might be a novel method to use a counseling strategy to address a previously unresearched mental health issue.

Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

The age limit for becoming a counselor completely depends on the route you choose after 12th. 

Educational qualifications:

A counselor's credibility is increased by having degrees and qualifications that have been certified. In India, one needs to meet the following requirements to work as a counselor:

an undergraduate degree in psychology

Psychology master's degree

courses leading to counseling certification

practical counseling industry experience 

Skill requirements:

To become a successful counselor, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.



Being able to take notes







You can better capture key details throughout the counseling session if you can take organized notes. For in-depth evaluations of the situation or issue with the client, you may always turn to these notes.



Resilience





One of the most important qualities for a counselor to possess when working with clients is patience. Results could not appear for days or even months. Therefore, while choosing candidates for a counselor position, companies favor those who exhibit patience.





Compasion



It's crucial to be able to comprehend the client's issue from their point of view. Counselors that are compassionate can assist a client in resolving their issues fast.



Maintaining confidentiality







An effective counselor must respect confidentiality since they should never disclose a client's personal information without the client's permission.







Inference







A therapist can better comprehend the issues a client is dealing with if they have the ability to extrapolate from what the client is saying. Additionally, you might persuade the client to view their experience more favorably through interpretation.





Reputability





Clients are less likely to divulge their difficulties and private information unless they feel comfortable doing so. As a result, you should be skilled at establishing and upholding trust with your clientele.





Listening





It is among the most crucial abilities for a counselor to possess. Because it includes paying attention to what your customer is saying, listening is more than just paying attention to what you hear.



Environment of work-

School counselors typically work out of their own offices, though this is not always the case. 

Others work in community health centers, hospitals, or have their own private practices. 

Since they must maintain the confidentiality of client information, counselors typically have their own offices.





Entrance Exams To Become A Counselor:

The following are some crucial steps to understanding how to become a counselor. Before accepting students for admission, several colleges have entrance tests. Students must pass their 10+2 with at least a 50% grade in order to be qualified to take the entrance exam.



CUET(Christ University Entrance Test)

Counselor Courses 

Undergraduate in counselling courses



One must finish their bachelor's degree in order to pursue a profession as a counselor. Although there are courses with counseling as a major, few people choose them. In this article, we'll talk about courses in financial and career counseling.



Course

Duration

3 years

3 years



Postgraduate in counselling courses



After earning a bachelor's degree, a student has two choices:

Option 1: He or she could start a career as a counselor. The candidate may also start working with an experienced counselor.

Option 2: The applicant may pursue a master's degree in counseling. The student can pursue a master's degree if they want to specialize in counseling.



Popular counseling master's programme



A two-year degree called the M.A. in Counseling Psychology/Psychology/Applied Psychology allows students to specialize in the subject of counseling.





Top Institutions To Pursue The Counselor Courses:

There are numerous good colleges and institutes providing admissions to this courses. Refer to the list given below to know more.

Top institutions in India

Types Of Specializations Available For A Counselor:

Educator

 From elementary school to college, school counselors or child counselors offer support to pupils. Take time to hear your kids' worries about their academic, emotional, or social issues. One of the responsibilities of a student counselor is to assist students in formulating goals and plans of action. the resolution of disputes between students and teachers. One could work in educational settings including schools, colleges, and universities, assisting students with their academic and personal problems.





Healthcare professional

Healthcare professionals play a crucial role on the treatment team for patients and work in various hospital settings. Therefore, the counselor's main responsibility is case management, which entails assisting the patient's family in locating crucial services following release. They could work in medical facilities, GP offices, community organizations, and occupational healthcare teams.







Social worker

 Another choice is to enter fields like education and social work using your knowledge and counseling abilities. As a counselor, a social worker works with client systems to identify their needs, define their issues, consider potential solutions, and implement intervention techniques in order to build and strengthen those systems' capacity to address issues more successfully.





Clinical psychologist

 Clinical supervision is beneficial for a clinical psychologist or therapist to keep track of their work with clients and to ensure the best possible path for their future professional and personal growth. A collaborative and participatory process that checks client care quality, develops clinical skills, and promotes personal and professional growth is the secret to effective supervision.

title: Cardiologist
summary: Career profile: Cardiologist
description:
Career profile: Cardiologist 



Overview: 



Who is a cardiologist? 

A cardiologist is a doctor who is an expert in taking care of your heart and blood vessels

They can treat or help you in preventing many cardiovascular problems.



They specialize in fields such as abnormal heart rhythms, heart failures or many kinds of heart issues you have since birth. 



What kind of career profile is 



A cardiologist may work autonomously with an office of its choice or for a medical facility. 



The main role of a cardiologist is to help patients in the treatment and diagnosis of cardiac diseases.

They work with patients' primary care doctors and others to develop a practical medical strategy.

In career as cardiologist, individuals work somewhere between eight to nine hours a day. But, it may extend depending on the number of patients one has to attend.





Roles and responsibilities of a cardiologist:



Examine patients and assess their condition.



Explain the risks of prohibited activities to patients.



Give health advice to patients.

.

Diagnose and evaluate patients with heart-related conditions.



Perform surgical procedures on patients with heart-related condition



Measure and track the improvement of patients' health



Command laboratory research



Ensure adherence to relevant laws and regulations.















Benefits for choosing cardiologist as a career profile: 

Respected profession: 



Cardiologists are experts in their field because they have extensive education and training. Because of their highly specialized training, cardiology is a respected profession. 



Non- surgical procedures:

Unlike cardiac surgeons, cardiologists typically only perform non-surgical procedures. Their work includes evaluating patients, ordering and interpreting tests and recommending plans to prevent or treat heart disease.





Variety of work: 





Cardiologists complete a variety of tasks in their job. They may spend part of their day conducting tests for patients, they may perform various patient procedures, such Cardiologists also spend much of their time working with patients to understand their symptoms or concerns so they can make the correct diagnosis. 







High paying salary:



Cardiologists have a  high earning capability, it takes them years of specializing and several degrees to reach where they are, and thus the y have a great demand and earning.

Career advancement: 

 Some cardiologists choose to become medical directors, Others work to become the head of the cardiology department at their facility or hospital. They also choose on becoming faculty members at colleges or universities, where they can help medical students develop the skills to become a cardiologist. 































How to become a cardiologist? 





To become a cardiologist, you must follow the career path given below :



STEP 1 : Complete 10th standard amd choose stream science with physics, biology and chemistry as mandatory subjects



STEP 2 : Pass 12th standard with at least 60% in each of the mentioned subjects



STEP 3 : Appear and qualify the medical entrance test for admission to MBBS (Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery) course.



STEP 4 : Take admission to your chosen course in Cardiology by earning a degree or any such related course.



STEP 5 : Gain hands-on experience in their fields under the supervision of a licensed Cardiologist. 



STEP 6 : Receive a state license that permits you to practice Cardiology for the general public.



Eligibility and skills requirement to become a cardiologist: 



Age limit:









The minimum age limit to be a cardiologist is 17 years. 

No age limit as such. 



Educational qualification:



A candidate must have scored at least 60% aggregate in 12th standard with Physics, chemistry and biology as mandatory subjects.

The minimum educational qualification to become a doctor is an MBBS degree.

Get the admission in PG course which takes to Doctor of Medicine (MD) in general medicine.

After finishing a three-year degree of MD, study the  specialty course of 3-year DM in Cardiology .



Entrance exams: 



A student must clear any medical entrance test in order to be eligible for the MBBS admission.



Skills requirements: 



Communication skills 

As a cardiologist, talking to patients about their health and interactions about their problems is necessary and requires good communication skills





Physical stamina and dexterity 



Cardiologists can be sometimes obliged on working extra hours, good physical stamina and mental 

Dexterity can be a bonus!



Problem solving



With different kinds of patients comes different kinds of problems, in situations like these a problem solving and trick Handling brain is required



Technical knowledge 





Techniques are a must for a cardiologist, with surgeries, working with different reports and datas their technical skills can be tested.





Practical skills

Ensure adherence to relevant laws and regulations.















Benefits for choosing cardiologist as a career profile: 

Respected profession: 



Cardiologists are experts in their field because they have extensive education and training. Because of their highly specialized training, cardiology is a respected profession. 



Non- surgical procedures:

Unlike cardiac surgeons, cardiologists typically only perform non-surgical procedures. Their work includes evaluating patients, ordering and interpreting tests and recommending plans to prevent or treat heart disease.





Variety of work: 





Cardiologists complete a variety of tasks in their job. They may spend part of their day conducting tests for patients, they may perform various patient procedures, such Cardiologists also spend much of their time working with patients to understand their symptoms or concerns so they can make the correct diagnosis. 







High paying salary:



Cardiologists have a  high earning capability, it takes them years of specializing and several degrees to reach where they are, and thus the y have a great demand and earning.

Career advancement: 

 Some cardiologists choose to become medical directors, Others work to become the head of the cardiology department at their facility or hospital. They also choose on becoming faculty members at colleges or universities, where they can help medical students develop the skills to become a cardiologist. 































How to become a cardiologist? 





To become a cardiologist, you must follow the career path given below :



STEP 1 : Complete 10th standard amd choose stream science with physics, biology and chemistry as mandatory subjects



STEP 2 : Pass 12th standard with at least 60% in each of the mentioned subjects



STEP 3 : Appear and qualify the medical entrance test for admission to MBBS (Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery) course.



STEP 4 : Take admission to your chosen course in Cardiology by earning a degree or any such related course.



STEP 5 : Gain hands-on experience in their fields under the supervision of a licensed Cardiologist. 



STEP 6 : Receive a state license that permits you to practice Cardiology for the general public.



Eligibility and skills requirement to become a cardiologist: 



Age limit:









The minimum age limit to be a cardiologist is 17 years. 

No age limit as such. 



Educational qualification:



A candidate must have scored at least 60% aggregate in 12th standard with Physics, chemistry and biology as mandatory subjects.

The minimum educational qualification to become a doctor is an MBBS degree.

Get the admission in PG course which takes to Doctor of Medicine (MD) in general medicine.

After finishing a three-year degree of MD, study the  specialty course of 3-year DM in Cardiology .



Entrance exams: 



A student must clear any medical entrance test in order to be eligible for the MBBS admission.



Skills requirements: 



Communication skills 

As a cardiologist, talking to patients about their health and interactions about their problems is necessary and requires good communication skills





Physical stamina and dexterity 



Cardiologists can be sometimes obliged on working extra hours, good physical stamina and mental 

Dexterity can be a bonus!



Problem solving



With different kinds of patients comes different kinds of problems, in situations like these a problem solving and trick Handling brain is required



Technical knowledge 





Techniques are a must for a cardiologist, with surgeries, working with different reports and datas their technical skills can be tested.





Practical skills 

Cardiologists are bound to perform surgeries, work with hearts practically too, it needs practical ability and hold over patients and their problems.



Working environment:



Cardiologists generally work long and irregular hours and can often travel between different hospitals.They work with the patient's primary care doctor and others to develop a practical medical strategy.

Cardiologists work in clinics and hospitals that are typically fully equipped with the latest technology. Work hours can often reach or exceed 60 hours a week.

Top institutes to pursue cardiologist as a career profile:



There are several good institutes and colleges to pursue courses in medicine. Refer to the list given below for some best cardiology colleges in India as well as abroad.

Types of specialisation available for a cardiologist: 



Clinical cardiologist:



Clinical radiologists can diagnose, advice and confirm heart disease.

If you face any symptoms like angina or abnormal heart rate or even a heart attack clinical cardiologist is best suggested in that case..



Cardio-oncologist:



A cardio-oncologist  provides treatment plans for a whole range of clinical problems faced by cancer patients

They include: Patients who may develop cardiovascular side effects from common chemotherapy regimens including anthracyclines, radiation therapy and novel targeted chemotherapy regimens.



Heart failure specialist:

A heart failure cardiologist typically: Evaluates a patient's medical and heart history, and explains heart failure and its management to patients and their families.

Performs a physical exam including assessing the heart, lungs, and blood vessels.



Cardiac rehabilitation specialist: 



A cardiac rehab helps patients overcome any kind of trauma or operation as well as strengthen bodies as well as heart. 

These specialist work along with the doctors to suggest the best for their patients and give them a better recovery.



Electrophysiologist:



Electrophysiologists generally work at a larger cardiac practice or hospital where they test for, diagnose, and treat abnormal heart rhythms.

They are trained in the use of highly specialized tests, devices, and procedures in order to do so. They also may prescribe medication or make lifestyle recommendations.



Cardio-rheumatologist: 

A  rheumatologist is a doctor specialised in inflammatory diseases.

 They not only treat arthritis but they treat many other conditions as tendon issues, muscle injuries and some rare inherited disorders.





Pay scale for a cardiologist: 

The pay scale of a cardiologist depends on various factors like work experience, specialization, location and employment work. The information given below represents the pay-scale of doctors according to experience and specializations.

Promotions and upgrades of a cardiologist: 



Specialisation

All cardiologists must complete college, medical school and at least three years of residency. At this stage, some cardiologists choose to specialize as a means of separating from the pack and increasing their chances of progressing professionally.



Education 

Cardiologists who wish to teach have the opportunity to take on more senior roles, such as leading cardiology departments in teaching hospitals and medical schools

Medical director 

Medical directors often share the responsibility for leadership in the organization with the other senior administrative officers, such as the CEO and CFO. Cardiologists who are interested in becoming medical directors will  need a second degree,like a master's degree in public health or business administration

Income

 cardiologists can make a lot more by switching specialties or jobs. The average starting income of a cardiologist is somewhere around 15 lakhs per annum

Demand and future scope: 



As a result of the increase in the number of cardiac patients, a number of super-specialty cardiovascular care centres are opening their doors, expanding the reach of cardiologists.

  Heart healthcare specialists are needed in government as well as private hospitals.  As the number of cardiac patients grows, so does the need for cardiologists.

Cardiologists can find job opportunities in private hospitals, government hospitals, military medical services, heart clinics, etc.

title: Dermatologist
summary: An overview of career profile:
description:
Dermatologist



An overview of career profile:

Dermatologists are therapeutic physicians who focus on skin well-being. They observe patients with skin conditions and outline the evolution of dermatological handlings.

Their job charge comprises inspecting the patients, carrying out grafts, smears of the affected skin and executing laser treatments on the affected areas.



Roles and responsibilities:

Analyze the results from diagnostic skin tests - Based on the apprehension, some tests are advised to know more about the disease.



Provide treatments based on tests - As the results suggest the disease, certain specific treatment procedures are started.



Inform on different treatment options and solutions - It is seen if the treatment is working fine. If not, alternate treatments are advised.



Guidance on skin well-being advices and top practices - Regular updates are given regarding the process being followed



Recommend appropriate products - As a precaution, different products are advised which do not alter the treatment.



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career

Dermatologists can treat up to 3,000 skin diseases - A variety of diseases can harm the skin.



Dermatology patients can be of any age



Dermatologists can decide to work in private practice or public hospitals - There is freedom to work anywhere.



They can also deliver training for general medical practitioners, teach at a university or run clinical trials in a research lab





A basic procedure to approach this job:

For becoming a dermatologist, the procedure will be-

STEP-1: Complete 10th standard and opt science stream with Physics, Chemistry and Biology as subjects in 11th standard.

STEP-2: Pass 12th standard with at least 60% marks in all of the above-mentioned subjects.

STEP-3: Qualify the medical entrance test for admission to MBBS (Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery) course i.e., appear in National Eligibility cum Entrance Test-Undergraduate or NEET in India to study undergraduate medical courses in government or private medical colleges in India. 

STEP-4: Complete the MBBS course.

STEP-5: After completion of the MBBS degree course, the candidates have to do MD (Dermatology). For this, MBBS students need to appear for Post graduate medical entrance tests conducted by NEET as well as various states and colleges. Some institutes, however, provide admissions on the basis of marks obtained in the MBBS course and work experience of the candidates.

STEP-6: The Dermatologists, after completing the MD (Dermatology) course and getting the registration from Medical Council of India, can get chances in reputed government and private hospitals in India. They can open their private practice also.



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment:

Skills Required - 

Detailed knowledge of several dermatological approaches

Should know the methods to perform the treatment.



Capability to analyze complex information

Should know the facts clearly, so that difficult situations can be handles and decisions can be made.



Active communication skills and the capability to successfully clarify clinical terms to patients

Should be able to communicate clearly to the patients.



Strong and sympathetic character

Should be kind to the patient and should be able to consider that the patient is likely to be scared for the process.



National medical license



Degree in Medical Science



Age Limit

No specific age limit

Education Qualification

5 and a half years MBBS degree followed by 3 years of MD Dermatology course.

Working environment

Working environment is very good. They work in well-lighted, AC offices, and are usually assisted by clerical and nursing staff. 

Have a very high level of social interaction. They communicate with medical staff and patients by telephone, e-mail, letters, memos, and in person daily.

Are very responsible for the health and safety of patients.

Repeat the same physical and mental tasks.

Make nearly all their decisions and set their daily tasks and goals independently.

Entrance exam to follow the route:

National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET)

All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS)

Jawaharlal Institute of Postgraduate Medical Education and Research (JIPMER) [For specific university]

Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education and Research (PGIMER) [For specific university]

Course description with their expenses: 

In dermatology, courses at certificate level, diploma level, bachelor and master level are available. Most sought dermatology courses are listed below:



Course

Duration

Fees of the Course 

Level of Education 

Diploma in Dermatology

2 years

INR 50,000 to 2 lakhs

Diploma

Bachelor of Science in Dermatology

3 years

INR 2-8 Lakhs

Graduation

Post Graduate Diploma in Dermatology, Venereology & Leprosy

2 years

INR 3-12 Lakhs

Post Graduation

Master of Science in Dermatology, Venereology & Leprology

2 years

INR 30,000 to 15 Lakh

Type of specialisation available under this job profiles:

Dermatopathology - Dual subspecialty of dermatology and pathology. In this, the study of dermal cells is emphasized at a microscopic and molecular level.





Dermato Immunology - Studies skin as an organ of immunity. Numerous areas have distinct considerations, like photo-immunology, inflammatory diseases, probably autoimmune skin diseases, and lastly the immunology of microbial skin diseases.





Mohs surgery - It is a detailed and effective medical method used for the treatment of common skin cancers and certain rare forms.





Tele dermatology - Denotes several features of dermatology delivered by means of contemporary information and communications technology. It might be functional to dermatological education.



Pediatric dermatology - Specialization in analysing and treating children, including new-borns and infants. They treat a variety of pediatric skin conditions using the modern accessible treatment approaches.



Cosmetic dermatology - Uses treatments to improve the appearance of the skin. It contains procedures to even fine lines and wrinkles or intensify facial volume etc.



Promotion and Upgrades:

New Fillers - Recent developments in cosmetic surgery present safer and more effective products and procedures that are now used for plastic surgery and other procedures. For example, dermatologists now-a-days largely use safer fillers that are formed of hyaluronic acid which lasts longer and almost has non-existent allergic effects.

Scar-less Lifts - With Alcor micro-coring, scarring is not an issue. This novel non-invasive procedure uses hollow needles to remove 6,000 to 12,000 “microcores” or 8% of the skin’s volume in one time. The result is smoother and tighter skin.

RAP to Get Rid of Cellulite - A novel non-invasive treatment, uses “Rapid Acoustic Pulse” or RAP to pass high-energy sound waves through the skin. This physically hampers the fibrous bands, which then releases the skin, making it look smooth.

Demands & Future Scope 

As a Dermatology aspirant, you can expect an excellent future provided you focus on the subject as it is quite rigorous and tough at times and there are no shortcuts.

You have to invest in dedicated years to become a Dermatologist.

But once you pass years of hard work and grinding to become a skin doctor, then it is all about how you treat your patients.

Dermatology being a speciality, Dermatologists are one of the highest-paid people among doctors in the world.

Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:

Dr Chytra V Anand

Cosmetic Dermatologist & Founder, Kosmoderma Skin, Hair & Body Clinic.Some of the services provided by the doctor are: Skin Rejuvenation,Body Sculpting,Laser Hair Removal - Face,Non Surgical Face Sculpting and Skin Care etc.



Dr Alekya Singapore

Dermatologist & Cosmetologist, Founder, The Skin Sensé, Skin and Hair Clinics. Did fellowship in cosmetology and is working in clinical dermatology and cosmetology. She is extremely well at analysing cases and treating them appropriately.





Dr Sonia Tekchandani

Dermatologist & Co-Founder, Tender Skin International. She is trained in Beauty & Wellness & is a supporter of ‘Women Empowerment’. It is her belief that training women in clinical cosmetology allows them to start their own business.



Ms. Lalita Arya

Skincare & Haircare Expert & Vice President, Dermapuritys. Her understanding and knowledge of the beauty industry is a testament to her ability and hard work.



Dr Nidhi Gupta

Cosmetic Dermatologist & Founder, Aloe&Me. Forward-thinker, always looking to better skin health and skincare and coaching people to take pride in their appearance.

title: Endocrinologists
summary: Career Profile : Endocrinologists
description:
Career Profile : Endocrinologists 



          



Overview 

Who is an Endocrinologist? 





Endocrinologists are doctors who have a certified specialized degree on hormones and glands related problems. 



They are responsible for managing the endocrine conditions including Lipid disorders, thyroid dysfunction and diabetes mellitus. 





           





They focus on the metabolism in the human's body and monitor the biochemical processes and its changes. 





What kind of career profile is it ? 





The job of an endocrinologist  is encompassed by scientific reasoning and the primary focus of this job is  Adrenals, glands and bone metabolism. 

       

This job profile has a number of challenges as they also need to work in teams with doctors and surgeons and also sometimes manage manual tests. 



An Endocrinologist has to work for hours continuously.  During emergency situations they even need to make case studies, questionnaires and reports. 







Roles and Responsibilities of an Endocrinologist ? 



The roles and responsibilities of an endocrinologist depend on the types of research on which he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





The endocrinologists manage patients' medical histories and monitor hormonal changes and metabolism imbalances. 



            

             







They manage research and conducts the study of the endocrine system and it's disorders. 



               



They make regular checks which include bones, parathyroids, pituitary gland and reproductive glands. 





                



They provide advice to patients that includes changes in the diet plans and medications. 





                



They also monitor cancer of endocrine glands, overactive or underactive thyroid and parathyroid gland. 



            





Benefits of Choosing Endocrinologist as a Career Profile:



High Employment rates and job opportunities

 

             

             



There is a high employment rate as endocrinologists can work in different types of places such as in research labs, hospitals, and health care clinics. They can also create their own chambers independently. 

 



Variations in Job Timings



        



            



There are full-time and part-time jobs  for endocrinologists and can also work independently.  Usually, in India an endocrinologist needs to  work for 40 hours a week. 





Opportunity to work in teams





           





Endocrinologists get the opportunity to work in teams including doctors, scientists and researchers which not only improves the quality of work but also this unique way teaches new techniques of work. 



Highly paid jobs





           



There are various types of specializations available for an endocrinologist and is one of the highly paid jobs.  In India, the average salary of an endocrinologist is INR 36,000. 





Highly challenging job





            



If you love to struggle and solve challenges and hurdles everyday, you can choose to become an endocrinologist as it encompasses scientific knowledge and practical abilities. 







How to become an Endocrinologist? 



If you want to become an endocrinologist you need to follow the steps provided below after Class 10. 



STEP-1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to choose science stream for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2: Students need to obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12 board examinations in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different institutions in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in Endocrinology. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree for 5 years. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for post graduation or an internship. 



STEP - 6 : You  can register yourself as a professional doctor and get a medical license. 







Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become an Endocrinologist: 





   Age Limit : 



The minimum age to apply as an endocrinologist is 18 years. 



As per the rules, there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications : 





A student must secure  50 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Endocrinology. 



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course . 





Entrance Exams : 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify NEET (National Entrance Examination Test

AIIMS-EE (All India Institute of Medicine and Sciences Undergraduate Entrance Examination)

JIPMER-EE (Jawaharlal Institute of Postgraduate Medical Education & Research Entrance Examination)  in order to be eligible as an endocrinologist. 



Working Environment: 



Endocrinologists work in various kinds of

There are various types of specializations available for an endocrinologist and is one of the highly paid jobs.  In India, the average salary of an endocrinologist is INR 36,000. 





Highly challenging job





            



If you love to struggle and solve challenges and hurdles everyday, you can choose to become an endocrinologist as it encompasses scientific knowledge and practical abilities. 







How to become an Endocrinologist? 



If you want to become an endocrinologist you need to follow the steps provided below after Class 10. 



STEP-1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to choose science stream for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2: Students need to obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12 board examinations in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different institutions in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in Endocrinology. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree for 5 years. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for post graduation or an internship. 



STEP - 6 : You  can register yourself as a professional doctor and get a medical license. 







Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become an Endocrinologist: 





   Age Limit : 



The minimum age to apply as an endocrinologist is 18 years. 



As per the rules, there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications : 





A student must secure  50 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Endocrinology. 



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course . 





Entrance Exams : 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify NEET (National Entrance Examination Test

AIIMS-EE (All India Institute of Medicine and Sciences Undergraduate Entrance Examination)

JIPMER-EE (Jawaharlal Institute of Postgraduate Medical Education & Research Entrance Examination)  in order to be eligible as an endocrinologist. 



Working Environment: 



Endocrinologists work in various kinds of places and also work with different teams. 



Endocrinologists usually need to work for longer hours and work in places like laboratories, hospitals, research centers, nursery homes and health care clinics. 



They research and investigate the endocrine system, glands and metabolism disorders. 



They, being a doctor, work part time as well as full time and also work for longer hours during a crisis or a medical emergency. 





Skills Requirements 



Good Communication Skills



               





Communication skills are the most essential need in today's world with the advancement of social media. Endocrinologists need to focus on their communication skills and improve them as they need to speak to their patients and also provide reports to the hospitals. 

 





Good leadership



       

           



Leadership quality is also needed as it empowers people and creates a different path. Endocrinologists need to possess good leadership quality and share their vision especially while participating in government awareness campaigns. 





Good listening

 

   

  

Listening skills help to restore information in the memory and speak wisely. Endocrinologists need to listen carefully and possess good listening skills which will help them in communication. 



Excellent practical knowledge



             



Practical knowledge is required in a real life working environment as it not only entwines reality but also improves working techniques. Endocrinologists being doctors need to possess excellent practical knowledge and experience in order to examine a patient's problems. 







Time management skills

   

                



 

Time management skills teaches the way of leading life and easy techniques to finish work early. Time management skills build the power of quick and right decision making and empowers people. 





Entrance Exam To Become an Endocrinologist 



A student needs to clear entrance exams conducted by universities and institutions in order to get admission in top colleges. Here is a list of all the entrance examinations that will help students to become an endocrinologist.

Types of Specializations available for an Endocrinologist: 



Diabetologists

       

       



Diabetologists manage diabetic retinopathy,  and diabetic neuropathy and concentrate on his treatment of diabetes. 



They also support high and low blood sugar levels and pressure of the patients with the help of diet plans and medications . 





Pediatric Endocrinologists 

 



             



Pediatric Endocrinologists concentrate on adolescence and growth related problems. 



They are specialized as a child doctor and mainly focuses on children's well being and their hormonal problems. 





Endocrine oncologist 





                    



Endocrine oncologists focus on hormone-producing glands such as the thyroid, parathyroid, pituitary and adrenal gland.



They also treat and cure problems like 

endocrine tumors of the gastrointestinal tract and pancreas. 



Neuroendocrinologist 





                     



Neuroendocrinologists concentrate on hormone production by neurons, the sensitivity of neurons for hormones. 



They treat, manage and cure bidirectional interactions between neurons and endocrine glands. 



Reproductive endocrinologists 





                     



Reproductive endocrinologists treat, reduce in the testicle area and abnormal breast growth. 



They concentrate on problems with sexual function and sperm count. 





Pay Scale For Endocrinologist

Promotions And Upgrading Of An Endocrinologist 

 

Endocrinologists have various scopes in different fields and follow a hierarchy.  There are different levels of endocrinologists which are as follows: 





LEVEL-1: Interns



    



Interns are the recent graduates of Endocrinology.. Internships provide individuals the space to compete in a real working environment. 







LEVEL - 2 : Assistant Endocrinologist 



   

      





An assistant endocrinologist is a person who is slowly stepping into the real world of jobs and competitiveness. He or she deals with patient's diagnoses and patient related problems. He or she works under a senior's supervision and also assists a senior doctor.





LEVEL - 3 : Senior Endocrinologist 





     









Senior Endocrinologists have enough experience as they have worked for the longest time in various aspects. They are able to implement new techniques, manage the patients case history and also are involved in surgeries.

Being an endocrinologist student, they can not only provide knowledge of medicine  in the world of academia but also design and develop many new treatments and surgical procedures. 

After the pandemic and medical emergencies, endocrinologists  are in great demand as they are able to analyze and solve people's metabolism and glands related problems. 

The endocrinologists are able to work as educators and educate many students across the globe.

Career Profile : Metallurgical Engineer 



     



Overview



Who is a Metallurgical Engineer 





Metallurgical Engineers manage the industrial works and are specialized in metals. 



Metallurgical Engineers are responsible for extracting and refining metals and also provide new techniques of using metals in factories. 











They are responsible for conducting various tests and research about metals and their sustainability. 





What kind of career profile is it ? 





The job of a metallurgical engineer is encompassed by scientific reasoning and the primary focus of this job is designing and operating the industrial products and other metals. 

       

This job profile has a number of challenges as they also need to work in teams with scientists and also sometimes need to make new properties and durability by combining different types of metals together. 



A metallurgical engineer has to work for hours continuously. They need to analyse and provide case studies and reports. 







Roles and Responsibilities of a Metallurgical Engineer 



The roles and responsibilities of a metallurgical engineer depend on the types of research on which he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows 







The metallurgical engineers specialize in different kinds of metals like steel and also discuss the latest Inventions and discoveries of ores and metals in the world. 





    



They do  research and implement unique techniques of metallurgical analysis along with collecting samples and case studies. 









They are also responsible for computer modelling and also manages the quality of the metals that have been manufactured and extracted. 



      



They also sometimes use X-rays to study the structure of metal and checks the quality of the metals that are used in transport systems and health care clinics. 





  



Benefits of Choosing Metallurgical Engineer as a Career Profile: 





Opportunity to work outdoors







A Metallurgical Engineer needs to visit sites and ores of metals in order to collect samples of the extracted materials. If you want to develop the quality of metals and reduce corrosion of metals, you can choose to become a metallurgical engineer. 



Opportunity to invent new things 







A metallurgical engineer has the ability to invent new procedures from the extracted metals. They are specialized in metallurgy and by mixing different metals they are able to develop the quality of metals in the factories and industries. 



Working overtime 







A metallurgical engineer can work in various research centers and laboratories and sometimes they research independently. They need to discover new procedures and make reports. Their work schedules are sometimes flexible and sometimes work overtime and learn new techniques.





Highly paid jobs







There are different aspects of metallurgy and there is an exposure of jobs of metallurgy not only in the factories of the towns but also in other laboratories. 



How to become a Metallurgical Engineer? 



If you want to become a metallurgical engineer you need to follow the steps provided below after Class 10. 



STEP-1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to choose science stream for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2: Students need to obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12 board examinations in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different institutions in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in metallurgy. In some cases, students are chosen  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for  post graduation.







Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become a Metallurgical Engineer: 





   Age Limit : 



The minimum age to apply as a metallurgical engineer is 18 years. 



As per the rules, there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications : 





A student must secure  50 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Metallurgical Engineering. 



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course. 





Entrance Exams : 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify JEE Main or JEE Advanced  in order to be eligible as a metallurgical engineer.

They work part time as well as full time and also work for longer hours during the extraction of metals and other elements.

Listening skills not only helps to solve problems and visualize things but also helps to know the intricate details of problem causing parts in a machinery in factories.

Being an engineer, mathematical skills is also important though it is not directly related but it helps to solve various statistical data problems easily.

Time management skills are also important as it helps to create a clock of its own and is an aim setter. It creates time limits and helps  to finish work

Good Practical skills
