title: Ayurvedic Physician
summary: Career Profile: Ayurvedic Physician
description:
Career Profile: Ayurvedic Physician



                    





Overview





Who is an Ayurvedic Physician? 





Ayurvedic Physicians is also a form of doctors who are trained in natural things and provide medications which are extracted from different plants.



They are trained in the oldest form of Ayurveda and usually diagnosed  with ancient techniques. 



       

              







They, with the help of scientific techniques and research, make and sell tonics. 





What kind of career profile is it ? 





This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with Physicians, doctors and medical practitioners.





An Ayurvedic physician has to work for hours continuously. 



They need to test and research while making medicines from the plants and trees.







Roles and Responsibilities of an Ayurvedic physician? 



The roles and responsibilities of an Ayurvedic physician are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





The Ayurvedic physicians analyse and diagnose patients by checking the pulse rate and patient's medical history. 



               





They sell tonics and 'bhasms' and prepare them by extracting the juice of the plants. 



              



They can manage, treat and cure diabetes, tuberculosis, nervous or mental disorders. 



                 





They research and perform tests in their own way.



               











Benefits of Choosing Ayurvedic physicians as a Career Profile : 





High employment rates and job opportunities 



                  



There is a high employment rate as ayurvedic physicians can work in different types of places such as Government Health Departments, National and Global Medical Research and  Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Industries. They are demanded not only in India but across the globe. 





Variations in Job Timings



              



There are  permanent and contractual jobs along with full-time and part-time jobs for ayurvedic physicians that help in analyzing complex cases. They can also open their own chamber and work independently. 

 



Work outdoors 



  

           



Ayurvedic physicians need to work outdoors as they need to search for various trees and plants with the help of which they produce medicines. They need to visit various sites and forests in search of plants. 





New challenges 

 

          





Ayurvedic physicians not only analyze a patient's history but also need to research and create development in the field of Ayurveda.  If you have good interactive skills and want to analyze natural goods, you can choose to become an ayurvedic physician.







How To Become an Ayurvedic Physician ? 





To become an ayurvedic physician you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard: 



STEP -1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to take science  for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Sanskrit. Some universities sometimes prefer students with the subject Sanskrit. 





STEP - 2 : Students need to study in a recognized institution and also obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12  in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Students need to  appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different colleges  in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in Ayurvedic medicine  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree..



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters. 





Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become an Ayurvedic physician : 





   Age Limit :  





  The minimum age to apply as an Ayurvedic physician is 17 years. 



As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications: 





A student must secure  50-60 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Ayurvedic medicine..

Career Profile: Ayurvedic Physician



                    





Overview





Who is an Ayurvedic Physician? 





Ayurvedic Physicians is also a form of doctors who are trained in natural things and provide medications which are extracted from different plants.



They are trained in the oldest form of Ayurveda and usually diagnosed  with ancient techniques. 



       

              







They, with the help of scientific techniques and research, make and sell tonics. 





What kind of career profile is it ? 





This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with Physicians, doctors and medical practitioners.





An Ayurvedic physician has to work for hours continuously. 



They need to test and research while making medicines from the plants and trees.







Roles and Responsibilities of an Ayurvedic physician? 



The roles and responsibilities of an Ayurvedic physician are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





The Ayurvedic physicians analyse and diagnose patients by checking the pulse rate and patient's medical history. 



               





They sell tonics and 'bhasms' and prepare them by extracting the juice of the plants. 



              



They can manage, treat and cure diabetes, tuberculosis, nervous or mental disorders. 



                 





They research and perform tests in their own way.



               











Benefits of Choosing Ayurvedic physicians as a Career Profile : 





High employment rates and job opportunities 



                  



There is a high employment rate as ayurvedic physicians can work in different types of places such as Government Health Departments, National and Global Medical Research and  Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Industries. They are demanded not only in India but across the globe. 





Variations in Job Timings



              



There are  permanent and contractual jobs along with full-time and part-time jobs for ayurvedic physicians that help in analyzing complex cases. They can also open their own chamber and work independently. 

 



Work outdoors 



  

           



Ayurvedic physicians need to work outdoors as they need to search for various trees and plants with the help of which they produce medicines. They need to visit various sites and forests in search of plants. 





New challenges 

 

          





Ayurvedic physicians not only analyze a patient's history but also need to research and create development in the field of Ayurveda.  If you have good interactive skills and want to analyze natural goods, you can choose to become an ayurvedic physician.







How To Become an Ayurvedic Physician ? 





To become an ayurvedic physician you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard: 



STEP -1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to take science  for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Sanskrit. Some universities sometimes prefer students with the subject Sanskrit. 





STEP - 2 : Students need to study in a recognized institution and also obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12  in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Students need to  appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different colleges  in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in Ayurvedic medicine  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree..



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters. 





Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become an Ayurvedic physician : 





   Age Limit :  





  The minimum age to apply as an Ayurvedic physician is 17 years. 



As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications: 





A student must secure  50-60 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Ayurvedic medicine..



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course. 





Entrance Exams: 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify in BAMS (Bachelor's of Ayurvedic Medicine and Surgery) in order to be eligible as an ayurvedic physician. 



Working Environment: 



Ayurvedic physicians work  in various kinds of places like Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Industries, Government Health Departments National and Global Medical Research Centers. 





They also  research various trees and plants and extract the juice of their leaves in order to make medicines. 







Skills Requirements: 





Excellent Communication Skills



            



Communication is the art of living which not only helps to transfer information but also aids the speakers to become interactive. Ayurvedic physicians need to have communication skills as it is the only way of diagnosis. 





Excellent listening skills



           



Possessing good listening skills is a perfect way of living life as it not only solves problems but also helps to learn the intricate details of a patient's medical history.





Time management





            



Time management skills involve not only time saving but proper planning and setting various aims. An Ayurvedic physician has to work hard with the capacity to work hard within the stipulated time. 





Practical knowledge



             





Practical skills  create the ability to solve problems. An Ayurvedic physician needs to be practical in order to diagnose and cure patients.

Career Profile: Colon and Rectal Surgeons 





          





       Overview 



Who is a Colon and Rectal Surgeon? 



A colon and rectal surgeon is a doctor who monitors and treats various diseases of the colon, rectum, anal canal and small intestine. 



A colon and rectal surgeon encompasses scientific knowledge and is specialized in colon related issues including anorectal and colorectal conditions. 





         





Colon and Rectal Surgeons being physicians focus on the lower digestive tracts of people and detect tumors or inflammation within the digestive system. 







What kind of career profile is it ? 





The job of a colon and rectal surgeon is encompassed by scientific training and the primary focus of this job is treating surgical and non-surgical colon problems. 

       

This job profile has a number of challenges as they also need to work in teams with doctors and sometimes need to even manage and monitor various tests manually. 



A  colon and rectal surgeon has to work for hours continuously.  During emergency situations or an epidemic like cholera, they even need to work for more hours and treat various problems like Anal Fissure and Rectal Prolapse. 





Roles and Responsibilities of a Colon and Rectal Surgeon? 



The roles and responsibilities of a colon and rectal surgeon depend on the patient's history on which he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





The colon and rectal surgeons manage and examine colon screening examinations and treat problems with the help of surgeries. 





           



They need to work in teams with the doctors and set up sterile fields and perform colorectal surgery. 



              





They need to make reports and perform certain tests including colonoscopy and laparoscopy. 



             

    



They need to concentrate on the medical history of the patients and also maintain patient's records. 





          



Benefits of Choosing Colon and Rectal Surgeon as a Career Profile:



High employment rates and job opportunities 





           





There is a high employment rate as the colon and rectal surgeons can work in different types of places such as in research labs, hospitals, Health care clinics, laparoscopy labs etc. 

 



Variations in Job Timings





       





There are full-time and part-time jobs for colon and rectal surgeons and this makes it easier to concentrate on new advanced technology and patient's case history.  Usually, in India a surgeon needs to  work for 40-45 hours a week. 





Opportunity to work as a Doctor 



             



Being a colon and rectal surgeon, creates a gateway to work as a surgeon. If you are planning to step into the world of medicine and become a doctor, you can choose to become a colon and rectal surgeon and fulfill your wishes. 





Working Overtime 



           



Colon and Rectal Surgeons can work independently, in laboratories and in many hospitals. They sometimes need to prepare case studies and make reports. This facility aids in learning new things and will also provide incentives for working overtime. 





How to become a Colon and Rectal Surgeon? 



If you want to become a colon and rectal surgeon, you need to follow the steps provided below after Class 10. 



STEP-1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to choose science stream for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2: Students need to obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12 board examinations in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different institutions in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in  general surgery. In some cases, students are chosen based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree for 5 years. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree apply for a specialization and qualify the entrance test. 



STEP - 6 : Attend the 1 year specialization course on colon and rectal surgery. 





Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become a Colon and Rectal Surgeon : 





   Age Limit : 



The minimum age to apply as a colon and rectal surgeon is 18 years. 



As per the rules, there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications : 





A student must secure  50 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in General Surgery. 



A candidate needs to complete a specialized course. 





Entrance Exams : 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify NEET PG or AIIMS PG  in order to be eligible as a colon and rectal surgeon. 



Working Environment : 



Colon and rectal surgeons work in various kinds of places and also work with different teams.

Time management teaches us to create a schedule and set time limits. Time management skills helps people to fly high up and gain rewards and accolades.

Being a doctor, computer skills are also important as doctors are not only needed to interact with patients but also make detailed statistical and database analysis. 





Mathematical skills

Career Profile: Geneticist







Overview 



Who is a Geneticist?





A Geneticist is specialized in genetics and heredity, mutation and the roles of genes. 





They are medical professionals who monitor and manage the inheritance of traits and concentrate on molecules and organisms. 







                



They create research, analyze and make reports of their recent detailed analysis. 





What kind of career profile is it ? 





The job of a geneticist is encompassed by scientific reasoning and the primary focus of this job is to study heredity traits and also providing statistical data. 

       

This job profile has a number of challenges as they also need to work in teams with doctors and scientists and also sometimes make case studies and questionnaires rounds. 



A geneticist has to work for hours continuously.  During emergency situations they even need cell production and mutation. 



They need to detect genotypes and monitor patients' medical history. 







Roles and Responsibilities of a Geneticist? 



The roles and responsibilities of a geneticist depend on the types of research on which he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





Geneticists manage and perform research and experiments in the laboratories. 







                       



They sometimes extract the DNA and analyse the hereditary traits performing research on the DNA. 



                     



They sometimes monitor and make statistical data to analyse the laboratory results and make reports. 



                                                                        



They investigate genetic disorders and problems and provide solutions to these problems. 



                                        

         

They sometimes write journals and advise people based on genes and genetic disorders. 



                       

        .





Benefits of Choosing Geneticist as a Career Profile:





High Employment rates and job opportunities

They create research, analyze and make reports of their recent detailed analysis. 





What kind of career profile is it ? 





The job of a geneticist is encompassed by scientific reasoning and the primary focus of this job is to study heredity traits and also providing statistical data. 

       

This job profile has a number of challenges as they also need to work in teams with doctors and scientists and also sometimes make case studies and questionnaires rounds. 



A geneticist has to work for hours continuously.  During emergency situations they even need cell production and mutation. 



They need to detect genotypes and monitor patients' medical history.

Geneticists manage and perform research and experiments in the laboratories. 







                       



They sometimes extract the DNA and analyse the hereditary traits performing research on the DNA. 



                     



They sometimes monitor and make statistical data to analyse the laboratory results and make reports. 



                                                                        



They investigate genetic disorders and problems and provide solutions to these problems. 



                                        

         

They sometimes write journals and advise people based on genes and genetic disorders. 



                       

        .

Career Profile: Neurologists 



          

                

                   



Overview



Who is a Neurologist? 





Neurologists are doctors specialized in the human brain and nervous system of human beings.





Neurologists diagnose and treat patients with headaches, migraine, brain tumors and sleep disorders. 



              . 







Neurologists know the intricate details of the central nervous system and the brain perform neurological tests and manage the disorders caused in the spinal cord. 







What kind of career profile is it ? 





The job of a neurologist is encompassed by scientific reasoning and the primary focus of this job is providing relief to the patients suffering from pain and nerve disorders.

       

This job profile has a number of challenges as they also need to work in teams with doctors and surgeons and also advise medications and exercises to the patients. 



A neurologist has to work for hours continuously. They need to regularly check patients' medical history and also counsel patients on neurological disorders. 



Roles and Responsibilities of a Neurologist?



The roles and responsibilities of a neurologist depend on the types of research on which he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows 





The neurologists play an active role in neuroscience research activities. 



               



The Neurologists perform tasks like electroencephalography, electromyography, and nerve conduction velocity tests.

    

              



They also provide reports and diagnose and perform treatments like sleep disorders, neuroimmunology, neuro-oncology, behavioral neurology, and neurogenetics.



                

 

They also manage the cognitive side effects of treatment and medication.



                 



They sometimes research to find out the advanced techniques of treatments. 



              





Benefits of Choosing Neurologists as a Career Profile:





High employment rates and job opportunities 



            



There is a high employment rate as neurologists can work in different types of places such as in research labs, hospitals and health care clinics. 







Variations in Job Timings





There are full-time and part-time jobs  for neurologists and this makes it easier to concentrate and treat patients easily with advanced and modern techniques. 

 Usually, in India a neurologist needs to  work for 40-45 hours a week. 



Highly paid jobs 



     



There are various types of specializations available for a neurologist and is one of the highly paid jobs.  In India, the average salary of a  neurologist is INR 45,000. 



Opportunity to take part in Government campaigns



                  



Neurologists and other professionals get the opportunity of participating in government camps and free campaigns. This participation helps to add a lot in the nation's health policies and helps to learn various new procedures of diagnosis.

  The minimum age to apply as a neurologist is 18 years. 



As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India.

A student must secure  50-60 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Surgery or Neurology

title: Medical Technologist

description:
Career profile: Medical Technologist





Overview: 

Who is a medical technologist?

A medical technologist is a Health Care worker who is highly trained and skilled and is responsible for performing complex tests that help physicians identify and treat diseases.

These technicians are also responsible for conducting X-rays, USG, MRI Scans and various other medical procedures.



What kind of career profile is it?



The job of a medical technologist involves mostly lab work. They need to collect specimens from patients, store reports and maintain the database of the patient’s diagnostic history and then communicate the reports to physicians. Based upon the job role, they may also need to work in diagnostic centres where they can perform scans, ultrasonography etc. 

This job profile has various challenges where they need to collect samples, manage instrumentation, remain updated about new methods of testing, analyse collected data and provide work assistance to other team members as well.

Career profile: Medical Technologist





Overview: 

Who is a medical technologist?

A medical technologist is a Health Care worker who is highly trained and skilled and is responsible for performing complex tests that help physicians identify and treat diseases.

These technicians are also responsible for conducting X-rays, USG, MRI Scans and various other medical procedures.



What kind of career profile is it?



The job of a medical technologist involves mostly lab work. They need to collect specimens from patients, store reports and maintain the database of the patient’s diagnostic history and then communicate the reports to physicians. Based upon the job role, they may also need to work in diagnostic centres where they can perform scans, ultrasonography etc. 

This job profile has various challenges where they need to collect samples, manage instrumentation, remain updated about new methods of testing, analyse collected data and provide work assistance to other team members as well.

Since this job is closely related to a medical field, medical technologists are also expected to deliver in critical conditions where the time needed to produce results is relatively short.

Roles and Responsibilities of a medical technologist: 

The roles and responsibilities of a medical technologist depend on the organisation he works with. But, a general overview of the Roles and Responsibilities of a medical technologist is given below. 

Validate the authenticity of the documents submitted by patients.



Diagnose, troubleshoot and repair any issues with laboratory equipment.



Efficiently operate highly computerised machines and collaborate with team members to generate reports.



Examine the specimens of the body collected from patients’ samples using standardised lab equipment.





Conduct various tests to find if there are any abnormalities in the samples.



In case of any abnormalities found, check for the indication of diseases and their type.



Generate on-time and accurate report so that the physician can start treatment as early as possible.



Work closely with physicians and assist them in diagnosing, treating and preventing diseases.







Benefits Of Choosing Medical Technologist as A Career Profile:

Lower Competition:



Since this is a new field, there is not much competition in the field and one has much higher chances of a successful career if he/she has good theoretical and practical knowledge in the field.

High paying salary:





Medical technologist jobs pay higher than most other jobs in government as well as private sector.



Job security:





There are various private and government sectors where you can start your career as a medical technologist. 





Good chance of Career growth:



As you increase your experience, you will get good opportunities to grow your career as a medical technologist. For example: Initially you may work with small laboratories and as you gain experience you can work with government research centres.



Honourable Position:



People working in the medical sector are highly honoured in the community. The job of a medical technologist provides this benefit.





How To Become a Medical technologist?



To become a medical technologist, you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard.

STEP-1: Complete 10th standard and choose science stream with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Mathematics as mandatory subjects in 11th standard.

STEP-2: Pass 12th standard with at least 60% marks in each of the above-mentioned subjects.

STEP-3: Apply for a B.Sc. (Bachelor of Science) Course in medical technology(preferred), Biology, Pharmaceutical etc.

STEP-4: Complete the BSc course.

STEP-5: After completing BSc, join a certification program in medical technology.

STEP-6: After completing certification, find employment.

STEP-7: You can continue your education further. If you opt for a masters in medical technology, you can get jobs such as specialist where your salary will increase.

Eligibility And Skills Requirement to Become a medical technologist:

Age limit:

The minimum age limit to apply for this job profile is 17 years.



Educational qualifications:

A candidate must have scored at least 55% aggregate in 12th standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology, Mathematics as mandatory subjects.

The minimum educational qualification to become a medical technologist is a BSc degree.

You can pursue higher studies (MSc, P.H.D) after BSc.

Entrance exams:

Entrance exams depend on the university you are going to take admission in. Some universities may provide direct admission as well after 10+2.

Working Environment:

Working environment for a medical technologist is mostly lab work. They work in hospitals, private labs, clinics etc.

Medical technologists also work in Government Centres where they get a good chance to use their skills to find results from a public survey and contribute to advanced medical treatment.

Skills requirement:

Good communication skills:



 Medical technologists need to efficiently communicate the results so that accurate reports can be generated to send to the physician.



Ability to work for long hours:



Since medical technologist jobs involve maximum lab work, often conducting tests can take a good amount of time. Especially in cases where the result needs to be updated within a short period of time, medical technologists may have to work for long hours to find the inferences.



Effective decision making:



Based on a medical technologist’s findings, the supervisor concerned will take necessary steps to prepare the detailed report. Hence, you need to be an effective and responsible decision maker to be in this job role.





In depth knowledge of medical technology and the respective field



As this job involves much research work, aspirants must have in-depth knowledge of the subject. They must also keep themselves updated about new findings in the field.





Good data analysis skills



After collecting sample data, medical technologists need to conduct tests and analyse it to find results. Hence, they need good data analysis skills. A good knowledge of statistics is preferable.



Excellent practical skills

             



















Using and maintaining lab equipment effectively requires good practical skills. Also collecting the right samples, figuring out areas for research need practical skills which you can refine with experience.





Ability to keep oneself always updated about new discoveries in the field





You need to remain updated with new discoveries in the topic that you are going to research on.



Remain flexible: 



Sometimes there can be  situations where you need to work at night to observe or collect samples for e.g.: in critical conditions.





























Good mechanical skills:



As lab equipment is costly, you must have efficient skills to use and manage them to generate accurate results.



Time management skills:





You need to have excellent time management skills to schedule time for research work and generate detailed reports of your findings as well.





Entrance Exam to Become a medical technologist:



There is no common entrance exam to pursue UG medical technology, Biology or MLT in India. But universities may conduct their own entrance exams for students wishing to pursue a BSc Degree.





AIIMS UG ( All India Institute of Medical Sciences Undergraduate)

CBT (Computer Based Test and then interview)

Top Institutions to Pursue medical technologist as A Career Profile:

There are many colleges that offer degrees in medical technology in India. To become a medical technologist, students can pursue graduation in relevant courses and then apply for certification courses in medical technology.

Top colleges in India:

Top Colleges abroad:

Types of Specialisations available for a medical technologist:

Laboratory Technician:



Laboratory technicians collect and process specimens from patients in a hospital or private medical diagnostic laboratory. These samples usually include samples of  skin and body fluids. They operate under the supervision of a laboratory technologist and use  techniques that help medical professionals to identify diseases, prepare remedies, and assess the efficacy. 

Operation Theatre Technician: 



Operation theatre technicians help health care practitioners in patient care. They prepare the operation theatre, maintain sterilisation of the instruments, hygiene, and support nurses, surgeons with various procedures.

Ophthalmic Medical Technician:



 An ophthalmic medical technician is responsible for maintaining the equipment, performing tests, monitoring etc. They conduct diagnostic tests, maintain various eye equipment, and explain principles relevant to eye care to patients.

Perfusionist: 



Perfusionists are highly trained specialists who are a part of the heart surgical team. They are mainly responsible for working in cardiovascular surgeries, blood conservation, long-term care in case of respiratory failure, assisting in treatment of cancerous tumours, and in organ transplants.

Researcher:



To enter this field, you need to complete higher education (preferably P.H.D) in medical technology. A researcher works on finding new and useful inventions in his domain as well as publishing research papers.



  



Pay Scale for medical technologist:

The pay scale of a medical technologist depends on various factors like work experience, specialisation, location etc. The information given below represents the pay-scale of medical technologists according to experience and specialisation.

Promotions And Upgrading of a medical technologist:

The career of a medical technologist lacks promotional structure. But an overview is given here:

LEVEL-1: Interns/ Trainee

Interns are recent graduates of the BSc course and are currently trained under medical technology certification programs.

They practise medical technology under an experienced senior in their work laboratories.

LEVEL-2: Practising medical technologist

They have some experience or are MSc graduates who work in laboratories, hospitals, and clinics. 

They are responsible for conducting tests, managing the equipment they use and also for working closely with physicians in hospital or lab settings.

LEVEL-3: Senior medical technologist

They have a good level of experience and are responsible for providing assistance, managing expeditions and training junior researchers, medical technicians etc.



Top Recruiting Domains for medical technologist:

Here are the domains where medical technologists can work:

Government Agencies

Research Organisations

Hospitals

Private Pathological Labs

Medical and Diagnostic Labs



Top recruiting Agencies for medical technologists:

Johns Hopkins Medicine

             

Mayo Clinic











Dr. Lal Pathlabs













Exact Sciences



AIIMS Delhi



Apollo Clinic



SRL Diagnostics



Thyrocare Technologies:



Fortis Healthcare



Stability Healthcare





Demand And Future Scope: 



Since the world is advancing and life is becoming fast-paced, many diseases are affecting the population. Hence, there is a high demand for skilled professionals in medical technology who can operate instruments, generate highly accurate reports and provide assistance to physicians.

With the increase of illnesses in humans, medical technologists are required to detect these in their early and treatable stage so that good medical care can be provided to the ailing patients. Employment of these technicians is expected to grow by 11 percent from 2020 to 2030.

Top Inspiring medical technologists:

Louis Pasteur:ForMemRS 



Louis Pasteur was a French chemist and microbiologist. He is renowned for his discoveries like the principles of vaccination, microbial fermentation etc. The process of Pasteurisation was named after him. 

Karl Landsteiner:



An Austrian-born American biologist, physician, immunologist, Karl Landsteiner is known for distinguishing the main blood groups and developing the modern system of classification of blood groups and also the presence of agglutinins in blood.



Frederick Banting:KBE MC FRS FRSC 



Sir Frederick Grant Banting was the co-discoverer of insulin and its therapeutic potential. He was a Canadian medical scientist, physician and Nobel laureate and received the Nobel Prize in Medicine along with  John Macleod.



Johannes Peter Muller:



Johannes Peter Müller was a German physiologist, ichthyologist, and herpetologist. The paramesonephric duct was named after him.



Joseph Murray:



Joseph Edward Murray , an American plastic surgeon , is known for performing the first successful human kidney transplant on identical twins.

Generate on-time and accurate report so that the physician can start treatment as early as possible.

Top Inspiring Cytologists:

Edmund Beecher Wilson: P.H.D:

Thomas Hunt Morgan: P.H.D:







An American evolutionary biologist, geneticist, and science author, Thomas Hunt Morgan who won the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine in 1933 for his discoveries that explained the role of chromosomes in heredity. 



Matthias Schleiden And Theodor Schwann: 







The co-founders of the cell theory-Matthias Jacob Schleiden was a botanist and Theodor Schwann was a physiologist.  They both worked in the laboratory of Johannes Müller, who was a famous zoologist. They collaborated and in 1839, co-founded the cell theory.



Albert Claude: Doctor of Medicine:





Albert Claude was a cell biologist and medical doctor. The Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine was awarded to him in 1974 along with Christian de Duve and George Emil Palade.

Career profile: Analytical Chemists 





Overview 

Who is an Analytical Chemist? 



An analytical chemist manages laboratory research and invent instruments used for analytical purposes. 



They create product development and create many kinds of drug components.



           

They analyze samples with  electro-chromatography, high performance liquid chromatography and spectroscopy. 





What kind of career profile is it? 



The job of an analytical chemist consists of scientific research and making new instruments. 



This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with Physicians, scientists and researchers. 



An Analytical Chemist has to work for hours continuously.





Roles and Responsibilities of an Analytical Chemist? 



The roles and responsibilities of an analytical chemist are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





They analyze samples and create drugs and medicines. They need to visit, collect samples from various sites and locations and analyse them inside a laboratory. 





                



They manage and use ion chromatography, electrochromatography, liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (LC-MS) and spectroscopy. 





                     



They review , record and write reports. They are required to concentrate not only on invention of equipment but also in creating statistical data, databases and record various invention techniques. 



                    





They collect various samples and perform tests and analysis with scientific formulas in laboratories. 



                





Benefits of Choosing Analytical Chemist as a Career Profile : 





Variations in Job Timings



                    



There are  permanent and contractual jobs along with full-time and part-time jobs for analytical chemists. This helps in creating new instruments. 





Working outdoors





                    



Analytical chemists  sometimes need to visit the sites  and collect various samples for tests. They need to interact with various people, do case studies and questionnaires. If you have good interactive skills, you can choose to become an analytical chemist. 





New challenges



                      





Analytical Chemists need to work on complex and difficult projects in the field of  and medicine.  They even need to work for longer hours and also work overtime during an emergency. If  you are fond of new challenges and puzzles and love to solve them with patience and effort. You can choose to become an analytical chemist.

Analytical chemist jobs are dynamic and versatile as they not only analyze and research and can also provide medicinal specifications. 





Highly creative



                                





Analytical chemists need to possess the quality of creation and patience.  They need creative abilities along with their scientific knowledge to create medicines and drugs.

To become an analytical chemist you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard:

STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters.

As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India.

description:
Career profile: Anesthesiologists 







Overview 



Who is an Anesthesiologist ? 



An Anesthesiologist is trained in medicine and manages anesthesia during a surgery. 



An Anesthesiologist is a doctor who takes care of a patient's pain and provides drugs and medication to them. 





      



An Anesthesiologist monitors and manages anesthesia assistants and nurse anesthetists while providing anesthesia to people. 









What kind of career profile is it? 

The job of an anesthesiologist is encompassed by scientific knowledge and the primary focus of this job is analysing and managing medical procedures. 

       

This job profile has a number of challenges as they also need to work in teams with medical doctors and surgeons and take care during anaesthesia of minor surgery. 



An anesthesiologist has to work for hours continuously.  During emergency situations they even need to take care of general anaesthesia and also lead the anesthesia care team. 





Roles and Responsibilities of an Anesthesiologist 





The roles and responsibilities of an anesthesiologist depend on the types of research on which he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





The anesthesiologists  help in providing anesthesia and monitor the medical history and vital signs of the patients. 



        





They are responsible for providing general , sedative, regional and local anesthesia. 



         





They also manage the medical files and provide reports to the doctors. 



         





They also carry out assessments in critical care units, provide relief during an emergency and provide training on topics like pain management.



       

     

 

Benefits of Choosing An Anesthesiologist As a Career Profile





High employment rates and job opportunities 



     



There is a high employment rate as anesthesiologists can work in different types of places such as in research labs, health care clinics and hospitals. 



Flexible Hours 

  

       

 

They can work full time and part

The anesthesiologists are able to work independently and also in time and provide medication to the patients. 





Working Overtime   



          



The anesthesiologists sometimes need to work overtime and exceed their work hours. They sometimes need to case atia and analyse patient's medical history and work overtime during complex projects and emergency situations.







Opportunity to work in teams

  

        



The anesthesiologists get the opportunity to work with other doctors and physicians and are able to learn more things in the world of medicine and surgery. 



 They get the exposure to interact with surgeons and thus they gain experience learning new things.







How to become an Anesthesiologist? 



If you want to become an anesthesiologist you need to follow the steps provided below after Class 10. 



STEP-1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to choose science stream for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2: Students need to obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12 board examinations in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different institutions in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in Medicine.  In some cases, students are chosen  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree and get a Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work as a junior anesthesiologist. 







Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become an Anesthesiologist 





   Age Limit : 



The minimum age to apply as an anesthesiologist is 18 years. 



As per the rules, there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications : 





A student must secure  50 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Medicine or Surgery. 



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course preferably in Medicine. 





Entrance Exams : 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET)  in order to be eligible as an anesthesiologist. 



Working Environment : 



An anesthesiologist works in various kinds of places and also works with different doctors and physicians. An anesthesiologist usually needs to work for longer hours and work in places like  hospitals, research centers and health care clinics. 



They provide pain  relief to the patients and also cure the injuries along with providing anesthesia. 



Anesthesiologists work part time as well as full time and also work for longer hours during a crisis or a medical emergency.

Overview 



Who is an Applied Mathematician ? 



An applied mathematician is trained in physics, engineering, computer science, medicine and uses theories while solving practical problems.



An applied mathematician uses modelling techniques and solves problems in various government institutions and business organisations. 



                 



An Applied mathematician use computational methods and research on practical mathematical studies. 



What kind of career profile is it ? 



The job of an applied mathematician consists of scientific research of mathematical concepts and the primary focus of this job is solving practical problems.  



This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with mathematicians, educators and researchers. 



 Due to the advancement of technology and sciences, an applied mathematician has to work continuously for longer hours and provide more information about mathematics and other related information. 







Roles and Responsibilities of an Applied Mathematician? 



The roles and responsibilities of an applied mathematician are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates on. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 



Applied mathematicians work with scientists and engineers and teach them about mathematical concepts. 



                         



They manage and develop computer software applications using mathematical models. 

  



                

            









They give business decisions and produce reports and other statistical data. 



                                          



They become educators and teach students applied mathematics as a subject. 



                   



    

They create computer models and other new products while researching theories.

There is a high employment rate as applied mathematicians  can work in different types of places such as Banks, insurance companies, investment and securities firms and other government institutions. 





Variations in Job Timings

Career profile: Astrophysicists 

         

                     



Overview 





Who is an Astrophysicist ? 





Astrophysicists focus on stars, planets, galaxies and discover new things in the universe. 



Astrophysicists are specialized in space science and concentrate on the evolution of stars and galaxies. 



                                             

    

They  study the components of celestial objects and also focus on electromagnetism, statistical mechanics and molecular physics. 





What kind of career profile is it ? 



The job of an astrophysicist consists of discovery and analysis of celestial bodies, stars and galaxies. 





This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with scientists. 





An Astrophysicist has to work for hours continuously. 



 During the launch of rockets and spacecraft  they need to analyze data and work for longer hours. 





Roles and Responsibilities of an Astrophysicist? 



The roles and responsibilities of an astrophysicist are of various kinds and depend on what he focuses on.  An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





Astrophysicists research theoretical models and study about galaxies, stars and celestial bodies. 



                    



They use telescopes in order to study radiation which is emitted by the planets, stars and galaxies. 



                  



They utilize computer programs in order to study and analyse practical and theoretical data. 

     

                  



They create reports and provide it to me Head of the institution. 



                  



They study complex projects like stellar dynamics, stellar evolution and galaxy formation. 



                      



They can also work as an educator and provide knowledge to astronomy students.

High employment rates and job opportunities 

 

                  



There is a high employment rate as astrophysicists  can work in different types of places such as laboratories, research centres and universities.

Astrophysicists analyse and research on planets, galaxies and stars.  If you are fond of space and galaxies  and want to make new developments, you can choose this career option. 



Work in teams



                



Astrophysicists sometimes need to work with other laboratory scientists and robotics engineers. If you have good interactive skills and want to analyze the structure of galaxies,  you can choose to become an astrophysicist .

Astrophysicists  work on complex and difficult projects in the field of space and discover new things in galaxies. They even need to work for longer hours during the launch of rockets. STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree.



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters.

  The minimum age to apply as an astrophysicist is 18 years. 



As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India.

Career profile: Gynecologist





            



 Overview 



Who is a Gynecologist? 



Gynecologists are a part of medical doctors and treat gynecological and female related problems. 



Gynecologists manage and treat cases related to pregnancy and birth control. 







                





They concentrate on a female's well-being and provide medication to women. 







What kind of career profile is it ? 



The job of a gynecologist focuses on the problems related to the female's reproductive system and implements new  preventive measures.





This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with doctors and surgeons. 





A gynecologist has to work for hours continuously and also monitor a surgery. 



They also need to participate and provide advice in healthcare decisions.



Roles and Responsibilities of a Gynecologist? 



The roles and responsibilities of a gynecologist are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 







Gynecologists monitor and treat complex cases related to pregnancy and menopause. 



            



They treat female patients who are affected with breast cancer, fissures and other problems. 



              



They advise the child birth process to women and also provide medications. 



              



They provide postpartum care for  mothers.



              



They also perform various tests and minor surgeries. 



             



They also sometimes create awareness campaigns and advise women about hygiene and other birth related issues. 





              





Benefits of Choosing Gynecologist as a Career Profile : 



High employment rates and job opportunities 



               



There is a high employment rate as gynecologists  can work in different types of places such as Government Health Departments, Private Hospitals and NGOs. 





Variations in Job Timings



              



There are  permanent and contractual jobs along with full-time and part-time jobs for gynecologists that help to eradicate women's diseases. 







Opportunity to take part in Health Policies 





               





Gynecologists take part in the healthcare policies and discuss women's problems and hygiene. If  you want to take part in people's welfare and want to eradicate female problems, you can choose this career option. 



Working Outdoors



           



Gynecologists have to sometimes work outdoors and visit government camps. They need to create free campaigns organized by the government and advise women about hygiene and medications of health. 







How To Become a Gynecologist? 





To become a gynecologist you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard: 



STEP -1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to take science  for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2 : Students need to study in a recognized institution and also obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12  in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Students need to  appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different colleges  in order to get admission for a MBBS. In some cases, students are chosen by the colleges  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters..







Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become a Gynecologist 





   Age Limit :  





  The minimum age to apply as a gynecologist is 18 years. 



As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications: 





A student must secure  50-60 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a M.B.B.S. 



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course. 





Entrance Exams: 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify in-



AIIMS-EE: All India Institute of Medicine and Sciences Undergraduate Entrance Examination, NIMHANS-EE: National Institute of Mental Health and Neuro-Sciences Entrance Examination, SCTIMST-EE: Sree Chitra Tribunal Institute for Medical Sciences and Technology Entrance Examination in order to be eligible as a gynecologist. 





Working Environment:



Gynecologists work in various kinds of places like Private Hospitals, healthcare clinics, Government Health Departments National and Global Medical Research Centers, NGOs. 



They also work with different teams of doctors and nurses. 



Gynecologists usually need to work for 50 hours to research and dissolve female problems. 





They also  advise and treat cases of cancer, reproduction, birth control and fertility rates. They also need to make strategies and take prevention measures.





Gynecologists  being medical professionals work

Career profile: Internists



                  



Overview



Who is an Internist ? 





An Internists specializes in internal medicines and diseases and treats patients facing problems in internal organ systems. 



They diagnose surgical and non-surgical cases and cures bacterial and viral infections.





                



They also manage and cure acute problems like hypertension, heart disease. .





What kind of career profile is it ? 



The job of an internist consists of curing injuries of the internal organs and the primary focus of this job is providing medications and therapies to people. 





This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with doctors, nurses and scientists. 





 During an emergency they need to even visit government camps and awareness campaigns and diagnose people. 





Roles and Responsibilities of an internist? 



The roles and responsibilities of an internist are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates upon. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





Internists manage and cure diabetes,  hypertension,cholesterol, heart disease, kidney issues, endocrine issues, blood disorders. 



                  





They create a treatment plan according to the patient's symptoms. 



                   



They sometimes perform and manage tests like  x-rays and EKGs. 



                  



They monitor nurses and also sometimes provide diet plans to the patients. 



                     



They perform minute and major surgeries of the internal organs.

High employment rates and job opportunities 

   



                 





There is a high employment rate as interns can work in different types of places such as National and Global Medical Research Centers, Private Hospitals and health care clinics.

There are  permanent and contractual jobs along with full-time and part-time jobs for internists that helps to eradicate internal problems and acute issues.

Work outdoors



                



Internists sometimes need to visit the government hospitals and rural areas in order to diagnose and cure internal injuries.  If you have good interactive skills and want to diminish long term problems you can choose to become an internist.

Internists need to work on complex and difficult projects in the field of diseases and medicine.  They need to concentrate on adult long term problems and work for longer hours during an emergency. Career profile: Cell Biologist 



                   



Overview



Who is a cell biologist? 



Cell Biologists study, manage and investigate the processes and codes of cells.



They concentrate on the molecules in humans, microorganisms, plants, and animals and it's complex laboratory research.





                            



They develop and maintain cells in a laboratory. 





What kind of career profile is it ? 



The job of a cell biologist consists of scientific research of cells and it's growth. 



This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with Physicians, scientists and medical practitioners.



A Cell Biologist to work for hours continuously. 







Roles and Responsibilities of a Cell Biologist? 



The roles and responsibilities of a cell biologist are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





They manage, design molecular and cellular laboratory experiments. 



                        



They perform experiments with deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) sequencing, cloning and extraction, ribonucleic acid (RNA) purification, or gel electrophoresis.

 

                          



They experiment with various instruments like gas chromatographs and high-pressure liquid chromatography, electrophoresis units, thermocyclers, fluorescence activated cell sorters, and phosphor-imagers.



                   





They also research on monitor cells and sometimes work as an educator. 



                     





Benefits of Choosing Cell Biologist as a Career Profile : 





High opportunity and jobs 





                    



There is a high employment rate as epidemiologists can work in different types of places such as Government health departments, Researcher centers, health care clinics and hospitals. 





Variations in Job Timings





                   



There are  permanent and contractual jobs along with full-time and part-time jobs for cell biologists that help in analyzing the cell and its intricate details. 

 Usually, in India a cell biologist needs to  work for 40-50 hours a week. 





New challenges 



                      





Cell Biologists need to work on complex and difficult projects  and cells. They even need to work for longer hours and also work overtime during an emergency. If  you are fond of new challenges  and love to solve them with patience..You can choose to become a Cell Biologist. 





How To Become a Cell Biologist: 





To become a cell biologist, you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard: 



STEP -1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to take science  for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2 : Students need to study in a recognized institution and also obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12  in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Students need to  appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different colleges  in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in Biotechnology. In some cases, students are chosen by the colleges  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree and get a B.Sc. degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters. 



STEP - 6 : Students can also join an internship and get exposed to a real working environment.

  The minimum age to apply as a cell biologist is 18 years. 



As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 



Educational Qualifications:



A student must secure  50-60 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Biotechnology. 



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course. 



Entrance Exams: 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify  in All India Biotechnology Entrance Examination in order to be eligible as a cell biologist. 

 

Working Environment: 



Cell biologists  work in various kinds of places like Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Industries, Government Health Departments National and Global Medical Research Centers..



They also work with different teams of scientists and physicists. 



Epidemiologists usually need to work for 40 hours to research and investigate the cells and it's detailed analysis. 



Skills Requirements: 





Good Communication Skills





                



Communication solves problems and helps to transfer information. Good communication skills cater to seeking a good position in a company.



Listening skills

Career profile: Detective 



                                 



Overview 





Who is a Detective? 



Detectives are professional investigators who discover and investigate criminal cases. 





They are also known as specialist investigators and helps in disclosing cases like domestic violence, robbery and murder. 





                       



They sometimes work with the police department and witness a crime scene and research in solving a case. 





What kind of career profile is it ? 



The job of a detective  consists of research on different crimes and robbery and the primary focus of this job is disclosing the real truth. 





This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with police officers, forensic department and crime branch officers.



A detective has to work for hours continuously. They need to disguise themselves and research certain facts and incidents. 



 

Roles and Responsibilities of a Detective? 



The roles and responsibilities of a detective are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates on. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





Detectives disguise themselves and work outdoors in search of information.



             



They solve complex criminal cases with the help of their detailed analysis and raids. 

                   



 They manage and conduct interviews and orientation of criminals and victims. 



                        



They produce reports and participate in firearms and other equipment programs



                



They help the police  to raid and arrest criminals.

    

               





Benefits of Choosing Detective as a Career Profile : 





Variations in Job Timings



             







There are  permanent and contractual jobs along with full-time and part-time jobs for detectives that help in analyzing and disclosing the criminal cases. 

 Usually, in India a physicist needs to  work for 60 hours a week. 



Opportunity to open an own office



               





Detectives analyze and investigate criminal cases, incidents and  take part in raids and clicking pictures of the criminal.   If you are fond of investigation and research, and want to open your own chamber,  you can choose this career option. 





Working outdoors



           



Detectives need to visit the incident site and create a deep study on things. They need to collect and gather different samples and pictures for analysis. They interact with various people and do case studies and questionnaires. If you have good interactive skills and want to investigate and solve cases, you can choose to become a detective. 





New Challenges and working overtime



             





Detectives  need to work on complex and difficult projects in case of cybercrime and murders. They even need to work for longer hours and also work overtime during an emergency. If  you are fond of new challenges and puzzles and love to solve them with patience and hard work, then choose to become a detective.

STEP - 2 : Students need to study in a recognized institution and also obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12 . 



STEP - 3 : Students need to  appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different colleges  in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in Bachelor Of Arts in Criminology and Forensics or Bachelor Of Science in Forensic Science





STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree and get a degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters in Master of Arts in Criminology and Forensics or Master of Science in Forensic Science

  The minimum age to apply as a detective is 18 years. 



As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India.

They sometimes concentrate on criminal science investigation and discover the suspects and criminals with the help of reports..







Police detective



          



The police detectives are trained in managing complex cases and also help the police to raid and create arrests of the criminals. 





They create questionnaires and case studies and also produce reports and other valid documents in front of the court. 





Forensic detective



         



Forensic detectives blend their scientific knowledge and employ their scientific reasoning and analyse the criminal cases. 





They sometimes work outdoors and collect the samples for tests and investigation.





Private detective





            



Private detectives work independently and fearlessly take interviews of the suspects and victims. 



They work in their own office and gather information with the help of various disguises and work with analytical thinking.





Narcotics Detective 







                   



 Narcotics Detectives investigate and discover people who are addicted to drugs and other related products that cause intoxication. 



They arrest people who sell drugs and participate in the implementation of the narcotics laws and regulations..

An assistant detective steps from the micro cosmic world into the world of competition and reality..He or she deals with investigation, research, reports and helps to solve the problems and cases.



LEVEL : 2 - Senior Detective 





            



Senior detectives are experienced people and  they have worked outdoors and witnessed many criminal scenes. They are able to identify and disclose the real cause of the crime.

Detectives can become police officers if they become fond of investigation and research. They can apply for a post of police officer and after appearing and qualifying the internal exams they can become a police officer.

title: Forensic Scientist
summary: Career profile: Forensic Scientist
description:
Career profile: Forensic Scientist



Overview: 



Who is a forensic scientist?

Forensic scientists are responsible for applying science during criminal investigation. They are responsible for collecting, preserving and analysing scientific evidence during the course of the investigation. This field includes various domains like DNA analysis, bloodstain pattern analysis, fingerprint analysis, Pathology, hair and fibre analysis, examination of ballistics etc. 

What kind of career profile is it?

The job of a forensic scientist involves mostly lab work. They need to collect specimens from patients, conduct tests and then communicate the reports to legal authorities in concern.

This job profile has various challenges where they need to visit the crime scene to collect samples, manage instrumentation, remain updated about new methods of testing, analyse collected data and provide assistance  with legal complexities to the officers as well.

Forensic scientists are also expected to deliver in critical conditions where the time needed to produce results is relatively short.

Roles and Responsibilities of a forensic scientist: 

The roles and responsibilities of a forensic scientist depend on the organisation he works with. But, a general overview of the Roles and Responsibilities of a forensic scientist is given below. 

Gather the evidence and samples from the scenes of crimes:



Quite a good amount of your work time may go into travelling to crime scenes to collect evidence. You must know to identify the right evidence that can help your analysis.



 Verify the authenticity of the related documents:



Many times, suspects or victims may try to submit fake documents, biometric data etc. Hence, you should have a keen eye to verify them before your analysis.



Test the fluids,if, found any, for use of drugs and other compounds:



Many crime activities may involve use of illicit drugs, toxins etc. Hence, you must know how to test the samples to check their usage.



Prepare and use your reports and findings to give and defend evidence in the Court:

 

You are responsible for giving and defending evidence in the Court on behalf of the investigation team. Hence, you must know to use your findings wisely.



Recover data:



You must know how to recover the data from electronics found at the crime scene or that belong to the parties involved for e.g: mobiles, laptops, PCs, etc. These can be used as important evidence.



Use analytical techniques:



In Forensic Science, various analytical techniques like electron microscopy, DNA profiling etc are used upon the samples collected to generate reports. You must be proficient in these.



Be unbiased and remain professional throughout your career:



You must remain unbiased. You must not support a suspect or a victim with wrong evidence. You also need to handle your emotions very well.





Benefits Of Choosing forensic scientist as A Career Profile:

Contribute to public safety:



With your work, you will contribute to public safety and help maintain law and order. Hence, being a Forensic Scientist is a much honourable position that offers job satisfaction as well.



Exciting and detailed work:



This job role is exciting and needs professionals with an eye for detail. They must be able to find much detailed inferences.



Numerous career opportunities:



There are multiple career opportunities where you can specialise as a forensic scientist. From being a forensic pathologist, anthropologist, medical expert to a forensic science expert, this domain has multiple sub domains where you will definitely find your interest.



High paying salary:





Forensic scientist jobs pay higher than most other jobs in government as well as private sector. These jobs are in-demand and the scope is wide as well.



Job security:





There are various private and government sectors where you can start your career as a forensic scientist. These jobs provide good job security. 





Good chance of Career growth:



As you increase your experience, you will get good opportunities to grow your career as a Forensic Scientist. For example: Initially you may work with small legal firms and as you gain experience you can work with government  centres.



How To Become a Forensic scientist?



To become a forensic scientist, you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard.

STEP-1: Complete 10th standard and choose science stream with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Mathematics as mandatory subjects in 11th standard.

STEP-2: Pass 12th standard with at least 50% marks in each of the above-mentioned subjects.

STEP-3: Apply for a B.Sc. (Bachelor of Science) Course in forensic science(preferred)

STEP-4: Complete the BSc course.

STEP-5: After completing BSc, apply for MSc in forensic science.

STEP-6: If you don’t want to do MSc, you can go for a PG Diploma in forensic science.

STEP-7: You can find employment or  continue your education further by opting
