Career profile: Forensic Scientist



Overview: 



Who is a forensic scientist?

Forensic scientists are responsible for applying science during criminal investigation. They are responsible for collecting, preserving and analysing scientific evidence during the course of the investigation. This field includes various domains like DNA analysis, bloodstain pattern analysis, fingerprint analysis, Pathology, hair and fibre analysis, examination of ballistics etc. 

What kind of career profile is it?

The job of a forensic scientist involves mostly lab work. They need to collect specimens from patients, conduct tests and then communicate the reports to legal authorities in concern.

This job profile has various challenges where they need to visit the crime scene to collect samples, manage instrumentation, remain updated about new methods of testing, analyse collected data and provide assistance  with legal complexities to the officers as well.

Forensic scientists are also expected to deliver in critical conditions where the time needed to produce results is relatively short.

Roles and Responsibilities of a forensic scientist: 

The roles and responsibilities of a forensic scientist depend on the organisation he works with. But, a general overview of the Roles and Responsibilities of a forensic scientist is given below. 

Gather the evidence and samples from the scenes of crimes:



Quite a good amount of your work time may go into travelling to crime scenes to collect evidence. You must know to identify the right evidence that can help your analysis.



 Verify the authenticity of the related documents:



Many times, suspects or victims may try to submit fake documents, biometric data etc. Hence, you should have a keen eye to verify them before your analysis.



Test the fluids,if, found any, for use of drugs and other compounds:



Many crime activities may involve use of illicit drugs, toxins etc. Hence, you must know how to test the samples to check their usage.



Prepare and use your reports and findings to give and defend evidence in the Court:

 

You are responsible for giving and defending evidence in the Court on behalf of the investigation team. Hence, you must know to use your findings wisely.



Recover data:



You must know how to recover the data from electronics found at the crime scene or that belong to the parties involved for e.g: mobiles, laptops, PCs, etc. These can be used as important evidence.



Use analytical techniques:



In Forensic Science, various analytical techniques like electron microscopy, DNA profiling etc are used upon the samples collected to generate reports. You must be proficient in these.



Be unbiased and remain professional throughout your career:



You must remain unbiased. You must not support a suspect or a victim with wrong evidence. You also need to handle your emotions very well.





Benefits Of Choosing forensic scientist as A Career Profile:

Contribute to public safety:



With your work, you will contribute to public safety and help maintain law and order. Hence, being a Forensic Scientist is a much honourable position that offers job satisfaction as well.



Exciting and detailed work:



This job role is exciting and needs professionals with an eye for detail. They must be able to find much detailed inferences.



Numerous career opportunities:



There are multiple career opportunities where you can specialise as a forensic scientist. From being a forensic pathologist, anthropologist, medical expert to a forensic science expert, this domain has multiple sub domains where you will definitely find your interest.



High paying salary:





Forensic scientist jobs pay higher than most other jobs in government as well as private sector. These jobs are in-demand and the scope is wide as well.



Job security:





There are various private and government sectors where you can start your career as a forensic scientist. These jobs provide good job security. 





Good chance of Career growth:



As you increase your experience, you will get good opportunities to grow your career as a Forensic Scientist. For example: Initially you may work with small legal firms and as you gain experience you can work with government  centres.



How To Become a Forensic scientist?



To become a forensic scientist, you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard.

STEP-1: Complete 10th standard and choose science stream with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Mathematics as mandatory subjects in 11th standard.

STEP-2: Pass 12th standard with at least 50% marks in each of the above-mentioned subjects.

STEP-3: Apply for a B.Sc. (Bachelor of Science) Course in forensic science(preferred)

STEP-4: Complete the BSc course.

STEP-5: After completing BSc, apply for MSc in forensic science.

STEP-6: If you don’t want to do MSc, you can go for a PG Diploma in forensic science.

STEP-7: You can find employment or  continue your education further by opting for a Doctorate or M.Phil in Forensic Science.

Eligibility And Skills Requirement to Become a forensic scientist:

Age limit:

The minimum age limit to apply for this job profile is 17 years.



Educational qualifications:

A candidate must have scored at least 50% aggregate in 12th standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology, Mathematics as mandatory subjects.

The minimum educational qualification to become a forensic scientist is a BSc degree with a diploma.

You can pursue higher studies (MSc, P.H.D) after BSc.

Entrance exams:

Entrance exams depend on the university you are going to take admission in. Some universities may provide direct admission as well after 10+2.

Working Environment:

Working environment for a forensic scientist is mostly lab work. They also need to travel to crime scenes for sample collection. They mainly work for government agencies, legal and private firms etc.

Forensic Scientists must give a minimum 40 hours a week for work. They must be ready to work in shifts as the investigation demands. Their work environment will include visits at the crime scenes to collect evidence and also working with advanced equipment in the laboratory to analyse those evidences.



Skills requirement:

Good communication skills:



Forensic scientists need to efficiently communicate the results to the police, investigation experts and in legal courts as well so that the criminals can be identified with proper evidence.



Ability to work for long hours:



Since forensic scientist jobs involve maximum lab work, often conducting tests can take a good amount of time. Especially in cases where the result needs to be updated within a short period of time, forensic scientists may have to work for long hours to find the inferences.



Effective decision making:



Based on a forensic scientist’s findings, the investigation officer will implement other legal processes. Hence, you need to be an effective and responsible decision maker to be in this job role.





In depth knowledge of forensic science and the respective field



As this job involves much research work, aspirants must have in-depth knowledge of the subject. They must also keep themselves updated about new findings in the field.





Good data analysis skills



After collecting sample data, forensic scientists need to conduct tests and analyze it to find results. Hence, they need good data analysis skills. A good knowledge of statistics is preferable.









Excellent practical skills

             



















Using and maintaining lab equipment effectively requires good practical skills. Also collecting the right samples, figuring out areas for research need practical skills which you can refine with experience.





Ability to keep oneself always updated about new discoveries in the field





You need to remain updated with new discoveries in the field that you are working upon.



Remain flexible: 



Sometimes there can be  situations where you need to work at night or odd hours to observe or collect samples for e.g.: in critical conditions.



Good mechanical skills:



As lab equipment is costly, you must have efficient skills to use and manage them to generate accurate results.



Time management skills:





You must have good time management skills to schedule time for research work and generate detailed reports of your findings as well.

Attention to detail:





You must pay attention to detail to identify minute evidence as well which can prove effective in your analysis.



Legal Know-how:



As you will be working with a dynamic team that helps identify criminals, maintain law and order, you must also have good knowledge of laws and legal complexities. This is also required to cite references while providing evidence in court.



Entrance Exam to Become a forensic scientist:

The entrance exams to pursue Forensic Science in India are:



Name of Entrance Exam

Conducting Body

AIFSET (All India Forensic Science Entrance Test ) (UG)

Vivekananda Global University Jaipur; Invertis University Bareilly; APG Shimla University.

University Grants Commission





Top Courses to Become a forensic scientist:

The list given below has top courses to become a forensic scientist:

Top Institutions to Pursue forensic scientist as A Career Profile:

The top institutions that provide courses in forensic sciences are mentioned below:

Top colleges in India: 

FS Indian Education Department, Pune

Top Colleges abroad:

UC Davis

University of Oxford

University of Dundee

Auckland University of Technology

University of Technology Sydney

Swinburne University of Technology

Deakin University

University of Amsterdam

National University of Singapore



Types of Specialisations available for a forensic scientist:

Forensic Pathologists: 



Forensic pathologists are responsible for finding the cause and the timing of deaths and in police investigations by analysing the samples from crime scenes.

Forensic Anthropologists:



Forensic Anthropologist are responsible for identifying individuals for cases like murders or disasters like plane crashes, explosions, fires, etc. They help in knowing the biometric data like sex, age, stature etc of the dead persons.

Clinical Forensic Medicine Experts:



They examine the injuries of the victims and the criminals . They are responsible for finding conclusions like the time of the injury, the weapons used and the amount of injury caused etc.



Forensic Serology Experts: 



They  analyse the blood groups, blood samples, body fluids, perform DNA profiling that help in identifying the victims and the criminals of the crime.



Toxicologist:



These professionals have qualifications in chemistry or biochemistry . They work in police departments and detect the illicit drugs, poisons, toxins and their levels, etc. in the body.



Forensic Expert/ Scientists:



Forensic scientists work as part of teams that are responsible for collecting evidences. They also work with the police and investigators by visiting the crime scene to get major crime evidence. They are also responsible for managing the forensic department in the investigation.



Pay Scale for forensic scientist:

The pay scale of a forensic scientist depends on various factors like work experience, specialisation, location etc. The information given below represents the pay-scale of forensic scientists according to experience.

Pay scale of a forensic scientist according to work experience :



 Work experience in years

Average Pay Scale [LPA]

1-3 years

4-6 years

Pay scale of a forensic scientist according to designation:

Pay Scale as per Specialisation:

Forensic pathologist

5.5

Forensic anthropologist

7.4

Clinical forensic medicine expert

9.7







Promotions And Upgrading of a forensic scientist:

The career of a forensic scientist lacks promotional structure. But an overview is given here:

LEVEL-1: Interns/ Trainee

They are under trainee programs where they are taught about investigation techniques from practical cases and are allowed to practise under expert supervision.

LEVEL-2: Practising forensic scientist

They have a basic experience and travel with a team for sample collection, perform analysis, generate reports etc. They convey their findings to senior Forensic experts.

LEVEL-3: Senior forensic scientist

They are experts with a high level of work experience (6+ years) and hence are responsible for supervising, maintaining accuracy of the results and providing evidence in the courts.

Top Recruiting Domains for forensic scientist:

Here are the domains where forensic scientists can work:

Central Govt. Forensic Sciences Labs

Quality Control Bureau

Defence/Army

Police Department

Private Detective Agencies

Hospitals

Law Firms

Universities



Top recruiting Agencies for forensic scientists:

Quest Diagnostics



Eurofins Scientific



SPEX Forensics



CBI ( Central Bureau Of Investigation India)



National Institute of Criminal and Forensic Science (India)



Directorate of Forensic Science Services (India)



Central Forensic Science Laboratory (India)



Demand And Future Scope: 



Demand: 

As crime activities are increasing nowadays, there is a high demand for forensic sciences experts who can bring the law violators to court judgement. The increasing rates of bombings, murders, kidnappings, cyber crimes etc require highly accurate forensic experts to use their knowledge for community safety and maintenance of law and order. The forensics industry is projected to grow by 27% in the next 5-10 years. 

Scope:

The government agencies such as the Central Bureau of Investigation or the Information Bureau hire many forensic science graduates. Legal firms and private investigators also hire the experts to strengthen their investigations and cases. Forensic Science Experts can also go for academics as a teacher, professor or trainer.

Top Inspiring forensic scientists:

Edmond Locard: Doctorate in Medicine



A French criminologist also known as the "Sherlock Holmes of France", Dr. Edmond Locard formulated the basic principle of forensic science: "Every contact leaves a trace" which is also known as Locard's exchange principle.

Clea Koff: MSc.



A forensic anthropologist and author, Clea Koff was the Co-coordinator of the Anthropology Laboratory of the UN Committee on Missing Persons in Cyprus . She founded the Missing Persons Identification Resource Centre to develop forensic profiles that can help the identification of unidentified bodies.

Keith Simpson:FRCPath



An English forensic pathologist, Cedric Keith Simpson was the Professor of Forensic Medicine in the University of London . He was also a Lecturer in Forensic Medicine at the University of Oxford. He jointly founded The Association of Forensic Medicine and also served as its President.

Alphonse Bertillon: No Higher Education



Alphonse Bertillon , a French police officer and biometrics researcher , applied the technique of anthropometry for law enforcement . He created an identification system which was based on physical measurements. Before this method was invented, criminals could only be identified by name or photograph.  Hence, he contributed the first scientific method of anthropometry in forensic sciences.

Michael Baden: MD



Michael M. Baden, an American physician and board-certified forensic pathologist, is known for his work in  investigating high-profile deaths. He was the Head of HBO’s Autopsy. He was also the chief medical examiner of the City of New York from 1978 to 1979.

title: Hematologist
summary: Career profile: Hematologist
description:
Career profile: Hematologist 







Overview



A hematologist is a doctor who has additional training in diseases of the blood, bone marrow, and lymphatic system.

A hematologist is a kind of specialist who has some expertise in examining, diagnosing, and treating

Growth factor treatment and also immunotherapy 

They do cancer treatments, including chemotherapy and biological therapies

Bone marrow transplant and stem cell donations

 

Roles and responsibilities of a Hematologist









Prescribing medications to their patients -evaluate and clearly define the patient's problem, specify the therapeutic goal, choose the appropriate drug therapy, initiate therapy with sufficient details, and consider nonpharmacologic therapies





Analyzing blood samples and identifying white or red blood cell abnormalities- A blood smear examines platelets for irregularities and can aid in the diagnosis of contaminations, iron deficiency, and other blood problems.







Treating diseases such as anemia, hemophilia, or leukemia- Blood disease treatments and prognosis differ depending on the blood condition and its severity.







Studying lab tests, CAT scans, and MRIs for a more accurate diagnosis.-Diagnostic imaging techniques aid in narrowing the causes of an injury or illness and ensuring an accurate diagnosis. X-rays, computed tomography (CT) scans, and magnetic resonance imaging are examples of these techniques (MRI).







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career 



Choosing a hematology specialty opens up many career opportunities, both academic and non-academic.







Hematology is a rapidly evolving specialty







Advances in diagnosis and treatment have made it an intellectually challenging and rewarding career choice.







A basic procedure to become a Hematologist



             

      STEP:1 After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology



       STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English as a     

                        compulsory subjects so that you can become eligible for MBBS.



       STEP:3 Complete your MBBS.



       STEP:4 Pursue MD in pathology.



       STEP:5 DM in Hematology to specialize in a field.



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment



           ELIGIBILITY 



             Age Limit-



The candidate must be of minimum age limit is 17 years to give the medical entrance exam

And a maximum age of 25 years.          



             Education Qualification-

 

Candidates must possess an MBBS degree .

Followed by an MD course

After doing a Masters one could do DM in Hematology



SKILLS REQUIRED



There are several skills required that a person should have to be a successful Hematologist as mentioned below:



High clinical expertise-Clinical knowledge covers both the practitioner's own experience and the general fundamental abilities required for clinical practice.







Intellectual to deal with changes as they may get into unknown challenges during their career.



             



Strong teamwork and great power of analysis to work with doctors and nurses and hence get better results and succeed in operations.

                 



       





Patient and supportive and emotionally strong as they may face critical cases where they need to have the patience to deal with the situation and need to be emotionally strong to handle the critical situations  



          







WORKING ENVIRONMENT







Hematologists may work in private settings, hospitals, healthcare environments, or research facilities.

They employ tools and gear utilized in the examination of blood and plasma.

They must wear safety equipment like gloves, a mask, and closed-toed shoes.





Entrance exam to follow the route:



Scorecards from specific national level common entrances which are accepted by many institutions,such as



National Entrance cum Eligibility Test( NEET)

All India Institute of Medical Sciences.  (AIIMS)

Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education and Research ( PGIMER)etc

2-3 Years

title: Mathematical Physicists
summary: Career profile: Mathematical Physicists
description:
Career profile: Mathematical Physicists 



                 



Overview 





Who is a Mathematical Physicist? 





Mathematical physicists are specialized in  mathematics and create development in mathematical methods. 



They create, perform and manage complex calculations  and also participate in writing journals. 



                       



They provide theories and observations  in mathematical terms . 





What kind of career profile is it ? 



The job of a mathematical physicist  focuses on analysis and modeling, software development and theoretical physics research. 





This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with scientists and university lecturers. 





A mathematical physicist has to work for hours continuously. 



 

Being an educator, they need to analyze data and even work on mathematical theories. 





Roles and Responsibilities of a Mathematical Physicist? 



The roles and responsibilities of a mathematical physicist are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates on. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 



Mathematical physicists study and analyze data with the help of mathematical equations. 



                     



They create mathematical modeling and research on mathematical theories. 



                                       



They solve practical problems with the help of applied mathematical theories. 



                         











Benefits of Choosing Mathematical Physicist as a Career Profile : 





High Employment rates and job opportunities 

 

                          





There is a high employment rate as scientific research and development services firms, laboratories and universities. 





Variations in Job Timings



                      





There are  permanent and contractual jobs along with full-time and part-time jobs for mathematical physicists that help in analyzing the structure of complex mathematical concepts.  Usually, in India a mathematical physicist needs to  work for 40-50 hours a week. 





Opportunity to develop mathematical concepts

  

                  



Mathematical physicists analyze and investigate the theory of mathematical equations and its structure. If you want to take part in further development in mathematics, you can choose this career option. 



Highly creative



                    



Mathematical physicists  need to possess the quality of creation and creative abilities. They need creative abilities along with their scientific knowledge to create mathematical developments. 



Working Overtime



              



A mathematical physicist can work in various areas  and sometimes they research new techniques independently. They need to discover new procedures and make reports. Their work schedules are sometimes flexible and sometimes work overtime and learn new techniques.









How To Become a Mathematical Physicist? 





To become a mathematical physicist you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard: 



STEP -1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to take science  for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2 : Students need to study in a recognized institution and also obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12  in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Students need to  appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different colleges  in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in Mathematics or Physics. In some cases, students are chosen by the colleges  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters in Physics. You need to complete a PhD. in Physics or Mathematics. 







Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become a Mathematical Physicist 





   Age Limit :  





  The minimum age to apply as a mathematical physicist is 18 years. 



As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications: 





A student must secure  50-60 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Mathematics or Physics. 



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course.



You need to complete a PhD. program in Physics or Mathematics.  





Entrance Exams



A candidate needs to appear and qualify in

Jawaharlal Nehru University Entrance Examination

 Banaras Hindu University Postgraduate Entrance Test

Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering 

to become a mathematical physicist. 



Working Environment 



Mathematical physicists work in various kinds of places like  scientific research and development services firms. 



They also work with different teams of scientists. 



Mathematical phy

Skills Requirements: 



Computer skills 





                     



Computer skills are the basic need since the evolution of technologies.  Computers provide good performance and provide error free work. Computer skills are a key to success and provide several opportunities. 





Time Management skills 



                   



Time management saves  time and helps to create a proper planning and daily schedule.  A mathematical physicist has to work hard and work for longer hours. 



 Decision maker



                 



Decisions play an active role and during research and mathematical modeling it's important to make major decisions and actions. 





Practical Knowledge 



                      



Practical skills  improve performance and create the capability to solve problems. It opens the real competitive environment of the world which helps the person to learn more things.

Types of Specializations available for a Mathematical Physicist:



Astrophysicist: 





                 



Astrophysicists are specialized in astronomy and focus on the space and the structure of the galaxies. 

They study about the evolution of the universe and stars. 





Quantum Physicist:



                    





Quantum physicists focus on the radiation of the atomic scale and deal with the nature of matter and radiation. 

They study about the behavior of matter and their interactions. 





Particle Physicist: 





                





Particle Physicists focus on protons, electrons and neutrons. 

They study particles produced by radiative and high energy scattering processes.  





Solid State Physicist : 



                  



Solid State Physicists study the various properties of bulk matter through methods such as quantum mechanics, crystallography. 

They focus on the mechanical and thermal behavior of solids. 



Plasma Physicist:



                  



Plasma Physicist deals with the properties and applications of plasma. 

They focus on scientific theories and research and also provide reports of the plasma.

A junior researcher researches, investigates and sometimes assists the senior scientists and researchers. They create reports, records, theories and work on mathematical development. 





LEVEL 2 - Senior Researcher



                       



A Senior researcher is experienced and works on complex projects like mathematical equations and mathematical modeling.

title: Statistician
summary: Career profile: Statistician
description:
Career profile: Statistician



Overview: 

Who is a Statistician?

A Statistician is a professional who deals with data. He is responsible for analysing data related to a single domain from various sources by applying various statistical methods on it to draw inferences.

What kind of career profile is it?

The work of a statistician involves mostly theoretical work. But nowadays in most statistician job roles , they are expected to have a good hands-on knowledge on coding and using softwares like R, SPSS, MS- Excel etc and hence, these jobs involve more digital work.

Roles and Responsibilities of a Statistician: 

The roles and responsibilities of a Statistician depend on the organisation he works with. But, a general overview of the Roles and Responsibilities of a Statistician is given below:

A much important part of a statistician’s job is to identify and collect authentic and trusted data for analysis.



Next, the statistician is responsible for analysing the data and finding trends, seasonal fluctuations, relationships etc.



Also, the statistician must communicate to stakeholders and the clients to understand the business needs.



Statisticians must interpret their findings and help in decision making for the client.



A statistician must also know how to design questions for surveys, polls etc.





Benefits Of Choosing Statistician as A Career Profile:

High chances of a Successful Career:



There is a good chance of success in the field and one can excel in his/her career if he/she has good theoretical and practical knowledge in the field.



Job security:





There are various private and government sectors where you can start your career as a Statistician. These organisations provide good job security. 



Flexibility:



As you become an experienced Statistician, you will have good flexibility in your job. You will get the chance to choose your suitable work shift and work settings.



 Good chance of Career growth:





As you increase your experience, you will get good opportunities to grow your career as a Statistician. For example: Initially you may work with small firms and as you gain experience you can work with government centres, research institutions etc.



Remote-working options:



Statisticians are allowed to work remotely as well as in offices. This job requires quite less travelling and provides a great work-life balance.





How To Become a Statistician?



To become a Statistician, you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard.

STEP-1: Complete 10th standard and choose your stream wisely with Mathematics and Statistics or both as mandatory subjects in 11th standard. The other subjects that can be chosen include Economics, Computer Application or Computer Science etc.

STEP-2: Pass 12th standard with at least 60% marks in the above-mentioned subjects.

STEP-3: Pursue BSc in Statistics. You can also pursue alternate courses like BSc Data Science, BSc Applied Mathematics etc.

STEP-4: Complete the BSc course.

STEP-5: Find employment.

STEP-6: However, it is advised that you complete MSc in Statistics before applying as a Statistician as most firms demand the degree. Also, this job requires a good and in-depth knowledge of statistics.

Eligibility And Skills Requirement to Become a Statistician:

Age limit:

The minimum age limit to apply for this job profile is 17 years.



Educational qualifications:

A candidate must have scored at least 50% aggregate in 12th standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology, Mathematics as mandatory subjects.

The minimum educational qualification to become a Statistician is a BSc Degree.

You can pursue higher studies (MSc, PHD) after the certification course..

Entrance exams:

To get admission in this stream in reputed colleges you must clear their entrance exam, interview or the cut-off marks.

The most popular UG exam for a degree in Statistics is Indian Statistical Institute’s UG Entrance exam. In the case of Masters, IIT JAM MS ( Indian Institute of Technology Joint Admission Test for Masters Mathematical Statistics) is one of the main entrance exams.

Working Environment:

Statisticians also work in an office environment . They are also allowed to work from home based on the policies of the organisation. Sometimes, they may need to travel to check the reliability of the data sources.

Statisticians generally work five days a week from 9 am to 5pm. On an average, they spend 35-40 hours a week on the job. This job is a low-stress job with a good work–life balance and high rates of career growth.

Skills requirement:

Good communication skills:



Statisticians must know how to convey their findings easily to people or clients with non-statistical background. 



Effective decision making:



Decision making is very important in this job role. Statisticians must decide the sources of data, identify the outliers and also know which exploratory and analytical method fits best for the data.



Analytical Thi

Mathematical ability, in-depth knowledge of the subject and computer literacy:

Time management skills:





You must have good time management skills to schedule time for work, supervision and research.



Entrance Exam to Become a Statistician:

To pursue graduation in Statistics from the best colleges in India there are a few entrance exams conducted on the national level. However, many government institutes or private colleges may provide merit-based admission on the basis of 12th result or may conduct their own tests. Some entrance exams are:

There are many Institutes in India as well as abroad that provide courses in Statistics. Statistics is a diverse field and hence, after completing the degree courses, students can opt for certification in special branches. 

Top colleges in India: 

Indian Statistical Institute, New Delhi

Indian Statistical Institute, Bangalore

Indian Statistical Institute, Kolkata

Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay

Aligarh Muslim University: Department of Statistics and Operations Research, Aligarh

Chennai Mathematical Institute

University of Delhi, New Delhi

Kalyani University, West Bengal



Top Colleges abroad:

University of Stanford, USA

Harvard University, USA

University of Michigan, USA

University of Cambridge, UK

National University of Singapore, Singapore

Massachusetts Institute of Technology, USA

University of Oxford, UK



Types of Specialisations available for a Statistician:

The various specialisations or designations available for a Statistician are:

Data Analyst:



Data Analysts are responsible for interpreting the data and converting it into useful information to meet business objectives. Data Analysts collect  the information from various sources and use their skills to identify patterns and trends in the market.



Biostatistician:



Biostatisticians  analyse the data and statistics of living things by collecting live samples from medical research centres. They use statistics tools to analyse the design and implement the research processes.

Econometrician:



Econometricians perform analysis on data related to employment, supply, demand, inflation, company profits, and other important economic topics. They support the research by developing the needed computer configurations for system analysis.

Mathematician:



Mathematicians use advanced mathematical processes and techniques to analyse the data. Mathematicians solve real time problems through their scientific approach.

Quality Control Analyst:



Quality control analysts ensure that a company’s products meet the required standards. They check the finished goods and the materials based upon which either reject or accept them.

Statistical Investigator :



They draft documents, prepare survey report requirements, perform data entry, tabulate, model and compile existing data into reports .

Data Scientist:



Data Scientists are professionals with good knowledge of statistics, linear algebra, calculus, computer science, data analytics, machine learning and artificial intelligence. They generate insights from data and build software models for the business based on the story the data says.



Pay Scale for Statistician:



The pay scale of a Statistician depends on various factors like work experience, specialisation, location etc. The information given below represents the pay-scale of Statisticians according to experience.

Promotions And Upgrading of a Statistician:

The career of a Statistician lacks promotional structure. But an overview is given here:

LEVEL-1: Interns/ Trainee

Interns are the freshers or recent graduates from statistics or relevant courses.

They are mostly assigned jobs like data collection, tabulation, data entry and analysis and are mentored by experienced senior Statisticians.

LEVEL-2: Statistician

They have some experience and mostly perform analysis on data and generate reports.

They coordinate with the clients and the team and can also be assigned project manager roles.

LEVEL-3: Senior Statistician



They have a good level of experience and are responsible for supervising the team, collaborating with clients, reviewing results, managing operations and using their expertise in business decision-making.



Top Recruiting Domains for Statistician:

Demand And Future Scope: 



In today’s fast-paced world, data is termed as the new ‘oil’. Hence ,Statistics, as a study of the data, is indispensable and is used to  improve economic developments, determine price of items, perform time series analysis, demand analysis, check production control, increase business development, growth, analysis etc.Hence, statistician jobs are in high demand nowadays.

The employment for statistician jobs is projected to grow about 31 percent from 2021 to 2031. This growth projection rate is much higher than the average for most other occupations. 

Top Inspiring Statisticians:

Prasanta Chandra Mahalanobis: 



The founder of Indian Statistical Institute , Sir Mahalanobis was a statistician and a scientist. He computed the Mahalanobis distance and is honoured globally as a pioneer figure in statistical research.

R.A Fisher: FRS 



Sir Ronald Aylmer Fisher ,a British polymath and a mathematician, statistician, geneticist, and professor is well known for his work in statistics. He has been described by Anders Hald as  "a genius who almost single-handedly created the foundations for modern statistical science."

C.R Rao:FRS



Sir C.R Rao is a mathematician and statistician and is currently a professor in Pennsylvania State University. His most famous contributions include Cramer-Rao Theorem, Rao-Blackwell Theorem which are related to the quality of estimators.

Harald Cramer:PHD



Harald Cramér was a Swedish mathematician and statistician who specialised in mathematical statistics and probabilistic number theory. He has been described by John Kingman as "one of the giants of statistical theory".

Neyman:



Jerzy Neyman was a Polish mathematician and statistician . He was the first to introduce the concept of a confidence interval into statistical hypothesis testing . Along with Egon Pearson, he co-revised Ronald Fisher's null hypothesis testing.

title: phlebotomist
summary: Career profile: Phlebotomist
description:
Career profile: Phlebotomist





Overview: 

Who is a phlebotomist?

A Phlebotomist is a Health Care worker who is responsible for performing phlebotomy in healthcare laboratories.  In short, they are responsible for drawing blood from patients, managing the equipment used and helping in conducting tests and blood transfusions.



What kind of career profile is it?

The job of a phlebotomist involves mostly lab work. They need to collect specimens from patients, store reports and maintain the database of blood transfusion, the patient’s diagnostic history and then communicate the reports to pathologists.

This job profile has various challenges where they need to collect samples, manage instrumentation, maintain a database, ensure patient’s safety during transfusion, maintain hygiene and sanitation  and provide work assistance to pathologists as well.

Since this job is closely related to a medical field, phlebotomists are also expected to deliver in critical conditions where the time needed to produce results is relatively short.

Roles and Responsibilities of a phlebotomist: 

The roles and responsibilities of a phlebotomist depend on the organisation he works with. But, a general overview of the Roles and Responsibilities of a phlebotomist is given below. 

Use their knowledge and experience to take blood samples from patients.



Label the blood samples correctly before sending it for tests .



Review the needs of the patients and their medical history before drawing blood.



Select the right vein, fingers or arm, the syringe, slides etc needed before drawing blood.



Prepare the specimens for transportation if required.



Match the specimens of blood to determine the blood group and also conduct small tests.



Supply the notes of the patient’s diagnostic history to the pathologist , physician and other healthcare professionals.



Maintain and keep the record of the patient's name and medical history , note the volume of blood drawn and the diagnostic findings in the test.



Clean and maintain the  laboratory equipment used in drawing and testing of the blood specimen.



Help the pathologist to correctly analyse the blood specimen and find the results.







Benefits Of Choosing phlebotomist as A Career Profile:

Lower Competition:



There is not much competition in the field and one has much higher chances of a successful career if he/she has good theoretical and practical knowledge in the field.



Job security:





There are various private and government sectors where you can start your career as a phlebotomist. 



Flexibility:



As you become a certified phlebotomist, you will have good flexibility in your job. You will get the chance to choose your suitable work shift and work settings.



 Impact:





As a phlebotomist, you are ensuring safe blood transfusion and testing which will help to save a patient’s life. You may also be working on collecting blood in blood donation camps and hence, helping to contribute to the greater good of the community. 





Good chance of Career growth:





As you increase your experience, you will get good opportunities to grow your career as a phlebotomist. For example: Initially you may work with small laboratories and as you gain experience you can work with government centres, apply for certification in nursing etc.





How To Become a phlebotomist?



To become a phlebotomist, you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard.

STEP-1: Complete 10th standard and choose science stream with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Mathematics as mandatory subjects in 11th standard.

STEP-2: Pass 12th standard with at least 60% marks in each of the above-mentioned subjects.

STEP-3: Apply for a certification course in MLT(Medical Laboratory Technology) or in Phlebotomy.

STEP-4: Complete the certification course.

STEP-5: After completing the certification course, you can work as a certified phlebotomist.

STEP-6: You can continue your education further. If you opt for a BSc in MLT, you can get exposure to more jobs in your domain.

Eligibility And Skills Requirement to Become a phlebotomist:

Age limit:

The minimum age limit to apply for this job profile is 17 years.



Educational qualifications:

A candidate must have scored at least 50% aggregate in 12th standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology, Mathematics as mandatory subjects.

The minimum educational qualification to become a phlebotomist is a certification course(CMLT).

You can pursue higher studies (BSc) after the certification course..

Entrance exams:

There is no entrance exam to pursue a certification course or a diploma course in phlebotomy. The only requirement is a basic understanding of Science, English and Mathematics. Some training courses may ask you for a driving licence as well.

Working Environment:

Working environment for a phlebotomist is mostly lab work. They work in hospitals, private labs, clinics etc.

Phlebotomists also work in Government Centr

Career profile: Phlebotomist





Overview: 

Who is a phlebotomist?

A Phlebotomist is a Health Care worker who is responsible for performing phlebotomy in healthcare laboratories.  In short, they are responsible for drawing blood from patients, managing the equipment used and helping in conducting tests and blood transfusions.



What kind of career profile is it?

The job of a phlebotomist involves mostly lab work. They need to collect specimens from patients, store reports and maintain the database of blood transfusion, the patient’s diagnostic history and then communicate the reports to pathologists.

This job profile has various challenges where they need to collect samples, manage instrumentation, maintain a database, ensure patient’s safety during transfusion, maintain hygiene and sanitation  and provide work assistance to pathologists as well.

Since this job is closely related to a medical field, phlebotomists are also expected to deliver in critical conditions where the time needed to produce results is relatively short.

Roles and Responsibilities of a phlebotomist: 

The roles and responsibilities of a phlebotomist depend on the organisation he works with. But, a general overview of the Roles and Responsibilities of a phlebotomist is given below. 

Use their knowledge and experience to take blood samples from patients.



Label the blood samples correctly before sending it for tests .



Review the needs of the patients and their medical history before drawing blood.



Select the right vein, fingers or arm, the syringe, slides etc needed before drawing blood.



Prepare the specimens for transportation if required.



Match the specimens of blood to determine the blood group and also conduct small tests.



Supply the notes of the patient’s diagnostic history to the pathologist , physician and other healthcare professionals.



Maintain and keep the record of the patient's name and medical history , note the volume of blood drawn and the diagnostic findings in the test.



Clean and maintain the  laboratory equipment used in drawing and testing of the blood specimen.



Help the pathologist to correctly analyse the blood specimen and find the results.







Benefits Of Choosing phlebotomist as A Career Profile:

Lower Competition:



There is not much competition in the field and one has much higher chances of a successful career if he/she has good theoretical and practical knowledge in the field.



Job security:





There are various private and government sectors where you can start your career as a phlebotomist. 



Flexibility:



As you become a certified phlebotomist, you will have good flexibility in your job. You will get the chance to choose your suitable work shift and work settings.



 Impact:





As a phlebotomist, you are ensuring safe blood transfusion and testing which will help to save a patient’s life. You may also be working on collecting blood in blood donation camps and hence, helping to contribute to the greater good of the community. 





Good chance of Career growth:





As you increase your experience, you will get good opportunities to grow your career as a phlebotomist. For example: Initially you may work with small laboratories and as you gain experience you can work with government centres, apply for certification in nursing etc.





How To Become a phlebotomist?



To become a phlebotomist, you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard.

STEP-1: Complete 10th standard and choose science stream with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Mathematics as mandatory subjects in 11th standard.

STEP-2: Pass 12th standard with at least 60% marks in each of the above-mentioned subjects.

STEP-3: Apply for a certification course in MLT(Medical Laboratory Technology) or in Phlebotomy.

STEP-4: Complete the certification course.

STEP-5: After completing the certification course, you can work as a certified phlebotomist.

STEP-6: You can continue your education further. If you opt for a BSc in MLT, you can get exposure to more jobs in your domain.

Eligibility And Skills Requirement to Become a phlebotomist:

Age limit:

The minimum age limit to apply for this job profile is 17 years.



Educational qualifications:

A candidate must have scored at least 50% aggregate in 12th standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology, Mathematics as mandatory subjects.

The minimum educational qualification to become a phlebotomist is a certification course(CMLT).

You can pursue higher studies (BSc) after the certification course..

Entrance exams:

There is no entrance exam to pursue a certification course or a diploma course in phlebotomy. The only requirement is a basic understanding of Science, English and Mathematics. Some training courses may ask you for a driving licence as well.

Working Environment:

Working environment for a phlebotomist is mostly lab work. They work in hospitals, private labs, clinics etc.

Phlebotomists also work in Government Centres  where they get a good chance to use their skills to find results from a public survey and contribute to advanced medical treatment.

Skills requirement:

Good communication skills:



Phlebotomists need to efficiently communicate the diagnostic history of the patients, their blood groups to pathologists so that accurate reports can be generated to send to the physician.

Kind and patient:



You must be kind and patient as there can be many patients who may feel nervous or reluctant to give blood. You must have a patient and kind approach to deal with them.



Ability to work for long hours:



Since phlebotomist jobs involve maximum lab work, often drawing blood, conducting tests for determining blood group, labelling and preparing equipment before and after the transfusion can take a good amount of time. Especially in cases where the results of the blood test are needed by the physician as early as possible, phlebotomists may have to work for long hours.



Effective decision making:



You must know which vein or finger you will take the blood from, the equipment used, substitutions in case needed etc. Hence, you need to be an effective and responsible decision maker to be in this job role.





Excellent practical skills

             



















Using and maintaining lab equipment effectively requires good practical skills. Also collecting the right samples  needs practical skills which you can refine with experience.



Remain flexible: 



Sometimes there can be  situations where you need to work at night to observe or collect samples for e.g.: in critical conditions.



Good mechanical skills:



As lab equipment is costly, you must have efficient skills to use and manage them to generate accurate results.



Time management skills:





You need to have excellent time management skills to schedule time for lab work, labelling, maintaining databases etc.



Entrance Exam to Become a phlebotomist:



There is no entrance exam to pursue a certification course in Phlebotomy in India. However if you opt for BSc in MLT then the following exams can be given to get admission in reputed institutes: 



AIIMS( All India Institute of Medical Sciences)  UG

CBT (Computer Based Test and then interview)

 Tamil Nadu Professional Courses Entrance Examination (TNPCEE) 





Top Courses to Become a phlebotomist:

The list given below has top courses to become a phlebotomist:

Top Institutions to Pursue phlebotomist as A Career Profile:

There are very few colleges that offer degrees in Cytology in India. However, many colleges offer degrees in MLT, Biology etc. To become a phlebotomist, students can pursue certification courses in phlebotomy and then go for graduation in MLT or Paramedical courses and vice-versa.

Top colleges in India: 

For certification in Phlebotomy , the top centres are:

Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

Virohan Institute

DPMI (Delhi Paramedical and Management Institute) Kalyani Paramedic College

IGNOU (Indira Gandhi National Open University)

National Skill Development Corporation

VIVO Healthcare



The colleges that offer BSc in MLT and Paramedical are:

AIIMS Delhi (MLT)



CMC Vellore, Vellore (MLT)



Institute of Paramedical Science and Management (Paramedical)





Top Colleges abroad:

St.Lawrence College, Canada

University of South Australia, Australia

University of North Texas, USA



Institutes offering certification courses in Phlebotomy:

Chicago School of Phlebotomy

National Phlebotomy Association, Maryland

American National University

Heart to Heart Healthcare Training

Silver Oak University



Types of Specialisations available for a phlebotomist:

Phlebotomy Supervisor:





They are responsible for planning work schedules of the employees, ensuring accuracy of the blood labels, ensuring work completion before deadline etc. A graduate degree is required for this managerial position.



Medical Laboratory technician:



They are responsible for collecting and analysing blood samples to determine medical conditions. An associate degree is required for this post.



Registered Nurse:



To become a nurse after a certified phlebotomist, you must complete a course in nursing. As a nurse, your main duty will be to take care of patients and provide assistance to surgeons, doctors etc.



Physician Assistant:



Similar to a nurse, a PA helps the physician in observing the patient’s health and assisting in treatment planning. This post requires a master’s degree.



Pay Scale for phlebotomist:



The pay scale of a phlebotomist depends on various factors like work experience, specialisation, location etc. The information given below represents the pay-scale of phlebotomists according to experience.

Pay scale of a phlebotomist according to work experience and designation:

Post/Designation according to work experience in years

Average Pay Scale [LPA]

Intern/Entry Level(0-1 year)

0.2

Practising phlebotomist(2-6 years)

2.2

Phlebotomy Specialist(6+years)

3.3





Pay Scale as per Specialisation:

Specialisation

Average Salary [LPA]

Phlebotomy Supervisor

3.6

Medical Laboratory Technician 

3.8

Registered Nurse

4

Physician Assistant

6.4



Promotions And Upgrading of a phlebotomist:

The career of a phlebotomist lacks promotional structure. But an overview is given here:

LEVEL-1: Interns/ Trainee

Interns are those trainees who have completed phlebotomy certification programs recently.

They practise phlebotomy under an experienced senior in their work laboratories.

LEVEL-2: Practising phlebotomist

They have some experience and work in laboratories, hospitals, and clinics. 

They are responsible for conducting tests, managing the equipment they use and also for working closely with pathologists in the lab.

LEVEL-3: Phlebotomy Specialist

They have a good level of experience and are responsible for supervising the team, managing travel routines, database and training interns.



Top Recruiting Domains for phlebotomist:

Here are the domains where phlebotomists can work:

Government Agencies

NGOs

Hospitals

Private Pathological Labs

Medical and Diagnostic Labs

Top recruiting Agencies for phlebotomists:

Johns Hopkins Medicine

           

Mayo Clinic





Dr. Lal Pathlabs





Apollo Clinic



SRL Diagnostics



CSL Plasma



American Red Cross







Demand And Future Scope: 



The field of Phlebotomy is projected to grow 23% between now and 2028. There are many job openings in various work environments like hospitals, medical labs, and clinics.

Phlebotomy technicians are in high demand, and that demand . This is because a lot of locations (such as hospitals which remain open 24 hours) require around three shifts of phlebotomists on staff. 

Top Inspiring phlebotomists:

Hippocrates: Ancient  Greek Physician



Known as the ‘Father of Phlebotomy’, Hippocrates was the first to introduce the concept of phlebotomy and its use. He also introduced various concepts of medicine to the world.



Jahangir Moini: MD



Dr. Moini is a professor and researcher specialising in the field of Phlebotomy. He published many medical textbooks,one of which named "Phlebotomy: Principle and Practice," has become one of the most-used textbooks by college students. 

Thomas S. Kickler: MD



Dr. Kickler, the Director of Phlebotomy Services at Johns Hopkins University, has good experience in blood pathology and haematology. He has authored over 150 peer-reviewed articles. One of his jointly authored ground-breaking research papers includes "Effect of Repeated Whole Blood Donations on Serum Immunoreactive Erythropoietin Levels in Autologous Donors."



Susan Phelan: P.H.D



Dr. Phelan , an  eminent publisher, writer, and lecturer whose book "The Phlebotomy Techniques Curriculum Guide,"  is used by college students all over the world. She is an active member in various organisations like the ASCP and the National Accrediting Agency for Clinical Laboratory Services (NAACLS). 

 

Marjorie Di Lorenzo :BS, MT(ASCP)SH



Marjorie Di Lorenzo is an acclaimed professor, civic leader, and author in the Phlebotomy domain who teaches at the Nebraska Methodist College . She is also the Phlebotomy Technician Coordinator of the college. Some of her most influential Phlebotomy textbooks include "The Phlebotomy Textbook," "Blood Collection: A Short Course”. "Phlebotomy Notes," etc.

title: Career profile Budget Manager
summary: Name of the career profile: Budget Manager
description:
Name of the career profile: Budget Manager







An overview of career a profile:



The budget manager is responsible for ensuring that the money designated for various government programmes is properly spent. 

The position is most frequently filled by someone with a background in finance, however depending on the legislative environment and the size of the office, it may be advantageous to have some experience working on legislation or managing projects. 

Budget managers are expected to help the workplace as a whole remain within budgets while also putting in a lot of overtime managing their particular projects.



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Accepts managerial responsibilities for the City's Budget Division's designated activities, operations, and services.







Ensures that divisional goals are realised through supervising, contributing to, and/or maintaining divisional goals, objectives, policies, and procedures.





Aanages, supervises, and takes part in creating the annual budget for the city.







Oversees the delivery of monthly budget reports and analyses revenue and expenditure deviations from the established budget.







Implements budget policies and procedures, monitors them, evaluates them, and, when appropriate, provides recommendations.







Gives recommendations based on research, analysis, and evaluation of difficult budget and financial management concerns.







Creates and delivers reports, documents, or communications to relevant committees, groups, or boards.

.



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Since members of the management team often earn more than nonmanagerial employees, this is one of the main advantages of becoming a Budget manager.







Budget managers take pleasure in having an impact on how their firm functions, including developing and enhancing the corporate culture.







You may learn how to spend less and save more money by creating a budget and sticking to it.









Personnel decisions must be made by budget managers. While some may perceive this as a challenge, others see it as an opportunity to develop a powerful, cohesive, and cooperative team.





Working as a budget manager gives you the chance to help your staff members advance in their careers.







When you take on accountabilities, you have the chance to determine what leadership style works best for you and those you are leading.







A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a Budget Manager, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 in any stream maintaining the aggregate of 60% or above.

Step-2: Earn a 4 year bachelor's degree in  Accounting, Business  or Finance

Step-3: Earn a Master’s Degree in in  Accounting, Business or Finance.

Step-4: Gain some more experience in Accounting and Finance by doing some internship or training.

Step-5: Start .

Eligibility and skills requirements:

     Age Limit

There is no age limit for being a Budget Manager

You should pursue a degree and should have relevant experience for same

     Educational qualifications

In grades 10 and 12, candidates can choose any stream but should maintain the aggregate of 60% and above. 

They ought to have finished 4 years of undergraduate degree in Accounting, Business  or Finance

They ought to have finished their Master’s Degree in Accounting, Business  or Finance.

They should pass the certification exam like CPA, MBA, PMP

Finish your Training or Internship.



     Entrance exams:



To get admission in this stream in reputed colleges you must clear their entrance exam such as ISI-AT (Indian Standard Institute) exam , CUEE (Centurion University Entrance Examination) , interview or the cut-off marks.

     Skill requirements



To become a successful Budget Manager, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.





Auditing



Examining financial records to spot flaws or errors and fixing them is the process of auditing.

Data Management



Budget managers frequently work with a lot of data on transactions and budgets. They can handle and safeguard the company's information by having a solid understanding of how data functions.

Presentation skills



As a budget manager, you might inform CEOs and other firm employees on the quarterly budget and annual financial analyses.

Communication Skills



On a typical day, a budget manager collaborates with a wide range of experts, including accountants, financial analysts, and other managers.

Data Analysis



When preparing future budgets, financial data from the past is frequently analysed by budget managers.

Work environment



Being a budget manager is a decent career choice for people who cannot perform physically demanding jobs because the work is rarely physically taxing. It is required of budget managers to work in an office environment on the company's property. 

Budget managers typically put in 35 to 40 hours each week. Depending on the approaching deadlines and d

Being a budget manager is a decent career choice for people who cannot perform physically demanding jobs because the work is rarely physically taxing. It is required of budget managers to work in an office environment on the company's property. 

Budget managers typically put in 35 to 40 hours each week. Depending on the approaching deadlines and duties allotted to them, they can be required to work beyond hours.

Budget Managers follow a hierarchy of roles who are posted at different levels. They start from Controller and are promoted according to their experience in that particular field.

title: Career profile Civil Rights Lawyer
summary: Name of the career profile: Civil Rights Lawyer
description:
Name of the career profile: Civil Rights Lawyer







An overview of career a profile:



A civil rights lawyer often handles issues involving discrimination against individuals who belong to a protected group. 

Lawyers for civil rights also take on disputes involving private rights, such the right to free expression. 

typically have a strong sense of patriotism and desire to guarantee that everyone has access to the judicial system.



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Civil rights attorneys draft paperwork, confer with clients, represent clients in court, and negotiate case settlements.







Civil rights attorneys frequently devote the majority of their days to case preparation or courtroom presentation.







A civil rights lawyer's preparation for a case may include gathering cases and legal precedent relevant to their client's situation, speaking with their client, and writing arguments or legal documents.







 Civil rights attorneys travel to their clients, sometimes even visiting them in jail, as well as to the courtroom when a judge hears their case.







Understanding laws and judgements for individuals, businesses, and organisations is part of a civil rights lawyer's profession.





Legal appeals are filed by a civil rights attorney in both the federal and state courts of appeals.









A civil lawyer deals with civil problems' non-criminal aspects. He or she deals with grievances, resolves arguments, and ends disagreements between parties.

.



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Civil Rights Lawyers represent private clients in court, and they establish a close working relationship with each one of them during that time.





They assist people with obtaining their legal claims, and careers like this provide the highest level of professional satisfaction because you are, in a sense, giving back to society.







One of the main benefits of being a civil rights attorney is the high pay. They receive the highest salaries of any legal practitioners in the sector.









The daily tasks of a civil rights attorney are varied, which is not conceivable in the majority of other legal specialties.





The ability to manage cases is arguably one of the benefits of being a civil rights attorney.







A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a Civil Rights Lawyer, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete your 10+2 in any stream with an aggregate of 50% of marks from a recognised board..

Step-2: Prepare for the entrance exam like CLAT (Common Law Admission Test), ILSAT (Law School Admission Test), AILET (All India Law Entrance Test) in order to get admission in college.

Step-3: Earn a degree in Bachelors of Arts- Bachelor of Legislative Law)



Step-4: Earn a Master’s Degree in Bachelor of Legislative Law

Step-5: Step-5: Start your Training or Internship

Eligibility and skills requirements:



     Age Limit

There is no age limit for being a Civil Rights Lawyer.

You should pursue a degree and should have certain experience.

     Educational qualifications

Candidates must complete their 10+2 in any stream with an aggregate of 50% of marks from a recognised board.

Candidates must pass the entrance exams like CLAT (Common Law Admission Test) or ILSAT (Law School Admission Test) in order to pursue their degree.

They ought to have finished their undergraduate degree in Bachelor of Legislative Law.

They ought to have finished their Master’s Degree inBachelor of Legislative Law.

Finish your Training or Internship.





      Entrance exams:



To get admission in this stream in reputed colleges you must clear their entrance exam, interview or the cut-off marks.



     Skill requirements



To become a successful Civil Rights Lawyer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Communication Skills



A civil lawyer needs to be good at communicating. He or she must put together legal paperwork. In court, a civil lawyer represents clients. He or she needs to be articulate in order to counsel clients and persuasively advocate on their behalf.

Problem Solving Skills



A civil attorney must be able to evaluate the complexity of the issue by considering relevant legal facts. He or she needs to have backup plans in case the original plan fails.

Negotiation



A civil lawyer needs to be able to negotiate. He or she must have the ability to unite people or work toward rapprochement.

Persuasive Skills



A civil lawyer must be able to influence others to act on advice or suggestions made by them and change their minds.

Social perceptiveness



A job as a civil lawyer requires strong social perception. He or she must be able to comprehend or anticipate the causes of someone else's response as well as potential future reactions.

Work environment



A civil attorney practises either in court or an office setting. He or she can conduct business from home. He or she typically serves business clients, law firms, and private indiv

To get admission in this stream in reputed colleges you must clear their entrance exam, interview or the cut-off marks.

Communication Skills

Problem Solving Skills



A civil attorney must be able to evaluate the complexity of the issue by considering relevant legal facts. He or she needs to have backup plans in case the original plan fails.

Negotiation



A civil lawyer needs to be able to negotiate. He or she must have the ability to unite people or work toward rapprochement.

Persuasive Skills



A civil lawyer must be able to influence others to act on advice or suggestions made by them and change their minds.

Social perceptiveness



A job as a civil lawyer requires strong social perception. He or she must be able to comprehend or anticipate the causes of someone else's response as well as potential future reactions.

Work environment



A civil attorney practises either in court or an office setting. He or she can conduct business from home. He or she typically serves business clients, law firms, and private individuals.



Working under constant time constraints to enable the timely completion of documentation work and other activities that entail research, review, and case studies is a must for a career as a civil lawyer.



Most civil attorneys put in much longer than 40 hours each day. People who work for law firms must put in long hours to conduct research, examine, and document legal documents or contracts.



A civil lawyer's working hours may change depending on the organisation's operating schedule. Most civil attorneys put in more than 40 hours a week.





Entrance exam to follow the route:



There are different entrance exams available to pursue Civil Rights Lawyer as a career option. Below given are some of the entrance exams for Civil Rights Lawyer courses.

Type of specialisation available under this job profiles:

Some of the most popular Civil Rights Lawyer specialisations are listed below. A student can choose the specialisations according to his/her interest in the particular field.

Divorce Lawyer



A divorce attorney is a civil lawyer who deals with issues involving disagreements between couples. He or she is in charge of the equitable distribution of assets and liabilities. The duration of child custody and support is decided by a divorce attorney.

Family Lawyer



A family lawyer is a civil law specialist that handles issues involving family conflicts such child custody, divorce, alimony, abuse, and family divisions and successions. He or she assists clients in finding solutions to problems pertaining to family matters.

Personal injury Lawyer



A personal injury attorney's main goal is to ensure that the injured party receives justice and compensation. To this end, they file legal complaints, provide legal counsel, prepare legal paperwork, and represent their clients in court. They see to it that the losses suffered by their customer are compensated.

Intellectual property lawyer



Cases involving intellectual and creative innovations and effort are handled by an intellectual property attorney. A lawyer that specialises in intellectual law concentrates on issues including copyright, patents, trademarks, industrial design rights, duplication, and others.

After obtaining sufficient experience, a Civil Right lawyer may apply for the position of judge. A Supreme Court or high court judge can be promoted from being a professional advocate.

The advocate required ten years of legal experience before being appointed as a high court judge.

title: Career profile Clinical Psychologist
summary: Name of the career profile: Clinical Psychologist
description:
Name of the career profile: Clinical Psychologist





An overview of career a profile:



Clinical psychologists are licensed specialists with specialised training in psychometrics who can offer direct services to individuals seeking assistance with mental or behavioural difficulties or psychological measurement.

 They carry out psychotherapy, diagnose mental diseases, develop treatment strategies, and give and analyse cognitive and personality tests..



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Lead biweekly interdisciplinary rounds, supervise MSW social work interns, and organise suitable psychiatric and rehabilitation discharges..







They diagnose and treat patients for a variety of different mental problems either one-on-one or in a group setting.







Work with a group of social workers, therapists, and psychiatrists to create individualised treatment programmes for veterans of war.







Clinical psychologists individually design their treatment programmes for each patient because everyone has a unique set of issues and benefits most from a certain type of therapy.







Build programme initiatives are concentrated on scheduling clinical rotations and ensuring APA criteria are met is the major role of Clinical Psychologist.







Creating behaviour modification programmes after determining the client's issue







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Between 2012 and 2022, there will be a projected 23% growth in the number of clinical psychology positions. There is widespread concern that there won't be enough suitable applicants to fill these posts..





Working with people of different ages, circumstances, and cultures is possible for clinical psychologists..









The variety of the work, which includes conducting psychotherapy for an hour in the morning and performing tests or evaluations, appeals to many clinical psychologists.







Adaptable timetable is a major benefit for Clinical Psychologists, especially those who have opened their own private practice, can frequently work a highly flexible schedule and visit patients..





You will have the chance to take on new challenges, support others in developing as people, and discover new aspects of yourself.







A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a Clinical Psychologist, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 in any stream like arts, science or commerce.

Step-2: Earn a bachelor's degree in Clinical Psychology.

Step-3: Earn a Master’s Degree in Clinical Psychology.

Step-4: Complete your doctorate in Clinical Psychology (Ph.D. or Psy.D)

Step-5: Complete your internship.

Step-6: Complete Clinical Training and get Licensed

Eligibility and skills requirements:

     Age Limit

There is no age limit for being a Clinical Psychologist.

You should pursue a degree and should have completed your internship in order to get licensed.

     Educational qualifications

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Science, Mathematics, Biology,  physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished their undergraduate degree in Clinical Psychology.

They ought to have finished their Master’s Degree in Clinical Psychology.

Candidate should have done doctorate in Clinical Psychology (Ph.D. or Psy.D)

Finished their internship and should have received licensure.



     Skill requirements



To become a successful Clinical Psychologist, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Communication



When working with patients who are not of sound mind, effective communication skills are essential. To avoid creating the wrong impressions, you must be able to speak clearly, clarify your observations, and thoroughly explain your findings..

Researching



Regardless of your expertise, conducting research will be a significant component of your psychological career. A competent psychologist will be aware of what to look for and where to look for the solutions.

Listening Skills



A good psychologist must be able to listen to their patients because many patients find relief by simply being able to talk to someone about their difficulties..

Problem-Solving



Since it is the responsibility of psychologists to resolve the difficulties of their patients, problem-solving is another essential skill set. Having a backup approach in case one answer doesn't work is an important aspect of problem-solving.

Ethics



When dealing with other people's lives, psychologists assume a great level of responsibility since they have the power to affect their patients' thoughts at a moment when they are most vulnerable.

Patience



When working with patients, it could take a while before you see the desired success..

Work environment



Many clinical psychologists have their own office and schedule and work in private practice. Schools and universities, clinics, hospitals, centres for rehabilitation, and community and mental health services are some more common locations of emp

Type of specialisation available under this job profiles:

Some of the most popular Clinical Psychologist specialisations are listed below. A student can choose the specialisations according to his/her interest in the particular field.

Forensic psychologist



A forensic psychologist is skilled at applying psychological concepts to legal cases. They collaborate closely with attorneys and law enforcement personnel to comprehend the psychological effects of a case. Interviews are conducted with suspects, prisoners, and victims to better understand their behaviours and mental health..

Clinical child psychologist



A professional psychologist who focuses on studying children's cognitive, social, and emotional development is known as a clinical child psychologist. Child psychologists typically examine development from the time of conception through puberty.

Clinical health psychologist



Licensure allows a clinical health psychologist to work directly with patients. These experts work with people one-on-one to teach them how to change their lifestyle and behaviours to improve their physical health.

Clinical neuropsychologists



Clinical neuropsychologists treat neurological patients who are frequently referred to them by neurologists, other doctors, other psychologists, or social service organisations.

Geropsychologist





The optimal mental health care strategy is developed in consultation with the relatives and caretakers of the clients by geropsychologists, who typically work in nursing or retirement facilities.

Promotion and upgradation of this career profile:



Clinical Psychologists follow a hierarchy of roles who are posted at different levels. They start from Intern and are promoted according to their experience in that particular field. 



Level 1: Clinical Supervisor

A clinical supervisor in charge of supervising and evaluating psychologists and other practitioners who work at a hospital.



Level 2: Assistant Professor

At universities or colleges, assistant professors are entry-level academic staff members who support full-time professors.



Level 3: Case Manager

Case managers operate under the premise that when a person achieves their ideal level of wellbeing and capacity, everyone wins.



Level 4: Clinical Coordinator

A clinical coordinator is in charge of planning or managing a healthcare facility's daily operations. As a clinical coordinator, you might supervise personnel, communicate with other departments, go to meetings, and make sure patients receive proper treatment in designated units.



Level 5: Clinical Case Manager

Clinical case managers support patient care by facilitating it. They visit with patients to determine their needs and determine their current state of health.



Level 6: Therapist and Program Manager

The Therapist & Program Manager often oversees and leads the organisation's physical therapy division. They oversee and coordinate the initiatives, goals, and policies of physical therapy services.



Top recruiting agencies for this profile

The top companies which hire Clinical Psychologist are:

Aspen Medical Staffing



Sri Aurobindo Society
