Demands & Future Scope 







The Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) forecasts a faster-than-average 14% increase in job opportunities for psychologists between 2016 and 2026.

 Furthermore, there were 166,600 psychologists as of 2016, and there will likely be 23,000 more employment openings in this industry between 2016 and 2026.

The BLS anticipates that the demand for psychologists will increase significantly in the next few years, which bodes well for their employment prospects.



Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Carl Ransom Rogers







American psychologist Carl Ransom Rogers was one of the pioneers of the humanistic school of psychology. Many people credit Rogers as one of the pioneers of psychotherapy research.



Albert Bandura 

 

Albert Bandura was a social clinical psychologist who is credited with the development of the self-efficacy concept, the social learning theory, and a well-known experiment involving a Bobo doll.

Jean Piaget



A genetic epistemologist and psychologist from Switzerland, Jean Piaget. He is well known for his theory of cognitive development, which examined how children's minds grow throughout childhood.

Martha Bernal



For a number of reasons, Bernal is a particularly important person in clinical psychology. First off, her rise to fame happened at a period when women and people of colour were intentionally excluded from psychology practice and psychology study..

B.F. Skinner



In psychology circles, Skinner continues to be seen as a key person who is maybe as beloved as he is reviled. His use of operant conditioning has made the most significant contributions to clinical psychology.

title: Career profile Design Architect
summary: Name of the career profile: Design Architect
description:
Name of the career profile: Design Architect







An overview of career a profile:



A Design Architect works under the direction of a Senior Architect to plan and design buildings and other structures.

They create different designs or plans using computer design applications.



     Roles & responsibilities of a this career profile:

Supervise the planning and management of numerous infrastructure repair projects for Verizon Telecommunications in NYC.



Create two-dimensional plans and merchandising concepts that are accurate, comprehensive, and precise.



  

Use Revit to carry out individual tasks, such as pre- and post-design work for projects and construction papers.



Work together and coordinate with consulting electrical, structural, and MEP experts.





Offer customers solutions that satisfy their standards for design, image, productivity, and quality to deliver outstanding customer service.







Leading and guiding cross-functional teams and staff members and giving direction and leadership to cross-functional teams.





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



A good pay, flexibility, and excellent learning opportunities are benefits of working as a design architect.







Working with many clients and having many teammates are advantages of becoming a design architect.







Numerous travel opportunities are there for design architects to keep up with the most recent advances, it is required to visit museums, Asian complexes, and other architectural wonders.







For many people, the idea of not working overtime may sound fantastical. However, it is a fact for architects.







Architects do not have any physical labour to do. Your briefcase is the heaviest object you will ever lift. Unlike engineers, you can relax and spare your back.





A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a Design Architect, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects Arts or Commerce.

Step-2: Prepare for Entrance Exams such as UGC-NET (University Grants Commission National Eligibility Test) or GATE (Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering).

Step-3: Earn a bachelor’s degree in Design Architect from any reputed college 

Step-4:  Consider getting a master’s degree in Architect.

Step-5: Complete your Diploma in Architecture.

Step-6: Start your internship.

Eligibility and Skill Requirements



     Age Limit



There is no upper Age Limit for being a Design Architect.

There is no maximum age limit for being a Design Architect you just need relative skills and certifications.

     Educational qualifications

The candidates should have studied Arts or Commerce in their 10+12.



Candidates should have finished their  three years of a bachelor's programme in art or architecture.

They must have successfully finished a postgraduate degree, typically an MSc in Architectural Design with a chosen specialisation.

Skill requirements



To become a successful Design Architect, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.



Construction Documents



            



Construction contracts issued by the hiring party serve as the basis for the design and blueprints of a construction site. As part of the project design protocol, they are both written and graphic in nature.



Design Development



          

In numerous industries, the term "design development" is used to describe a variety of jobs and abilities.

Teamwork Skills: 







To finish their tasks, design architects frequently collaborate in groups. They must therefore be able to cooperate with others and comprehend how their teammates function.



Time-management Skills







Design Architects must prioritise their tasks and manage their time well in order to meet deadlines and produce high-quality work.



Communication Skills







 Design architects consult with other architects and senior managers to produce fresh concepts.

Project Management Skills



Working with the project management team and staff to create efficient mitigation plans.

Work environment



The surroundings and working conditions of an architect are generally extremely pleasant. Except for site visits, all of your work will be done indoors in a contemporary office. 



Design architects don’t need to perform physical labour as construction workers do, so you'll probably spend the most of your time on a computer.



Entrance exam to follow the route:



There are different entrance exams available to pursue Design Architect as a career option. Below given are some of the entrance exams for this course.



Joint Entrance Screening Test (JEST)

UGC-NET (University Grants Commission National Eligibility Test)

GATE (Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering)

IIT JAM (Joint Admission Test)

	

CSIR UGC NET (Council of Scientific and Industrial Research University Grants Commission National Eligibility Test.)





Course description with their expenses: 



Undergraduate Design Architect courses

The courses mention

Type of specialisation available under this job profiles:



Some of the most popular Design Architect specialisations are listed below. A student can choose the specialisations according to his/her interest in the particular field.

Project Architect:

The primary responsibility of a project architect is to direct and coordinate the planning and design of construction projects. In order to grasp the requirements of the projects and what needs to be done, he or she coordinates with the client.



Project Manager:

The success of projects depends on their project managers. Project Managers make sure the job is finished on schedule and within a set budget, they take on tasks that involve planning and supervising each project.



Senior Architect:

Senior architects are professionals with a wealth of industry knowledge who can act as managers, mentors, and advisors. They create and analyse different structural designs.



Senior Designer:

A senior designer oversees the work of designers creating visuals that meet market demands while taking into account marketing strategies and company objectives.



Design Manager: 

Successfully engages potential clients, and they frequently apply their creative sensibilities to promote clever innovations. The design manager at the organisation is the only one who is more inventive and imaginative.



Consultant & Architect:

Architect consultants, also referred to as consultant architects, are qualified individuals who focus on managing and creating architectural projects.

Promotion and upgradation of this career profile:



Design Architects follow a hierarchy of roles who are posted at different levels. The Design Architect start from Intern Architect and are promoted according to their experience in that particular field. 



             Level-1: Intern Architect

Does the necessary labour in design documentation and contract administration as directed by an architectural assistant, project manager, or architect. 

Does anything and everything as required by a senior member of the team.



             Level-2: Architectural Assistant



Working under the direction and supervision of an architect or project manager.

Helps with contract administration and design documentation.



Level-3: Project Manager

                   

Contract paperwork and administrative tasks as requested by the Architect or Senior Architects are the main responsibilities of the position.      

         

             Level-4: Junior Design Architect



Focuses on a project's design elements.

Interior design is a possible component of design work.



             Level-5: senior Design Architect



The concept design and design development of a project are the only emphasis of the position.

Gives the team's junior architectural designers design instructions.



            Level-6: Senior Architect

The person in charge of carrying out the specific design or building tasks.

Oversees meetings with the general contractors and design consultants.

Frank Lloyd Wright 







Over 1000 buildings were created by the well-known American architect Frank Lloyd Wright during the course of his long career.



Mies Van der Rohe 





He has received numerous honours, including the Royal Gold Medal from the Royal Institute of British Architects and the AIA Gold Medal.



Nari Gandhi







When it comes to structural designs, Nari Gandhi has a realistic, tactile, artisanal sort of eye. His immediate reaction to the location's particulars persisted throughout.



BV Doshi 





One of India's greatest architects is B V Doshi. the first person from India to be awarded the renowned Pritzker Architecture Prize, as well as the Padma Shri and Padma Bhushan awards.



Sheila Sri Prakash







Among Indian architects, Sheila Sri Prakash is a veteran with a variety of skills. She was the first Indian woman in the nation to open her own architectural firm.

title: Career profile Experimental Physicist
summary: Name of the career profile: Experimental Physicist
description:
Name of the career profile: Experimental Physicist







An overview of career a profile:

Scientists who research and comprehend the physical universe using the scientific method are known as experimental physicists.

They conduct experiments, examine data, and interpret their findings to create novel discoveries about the nature of matter, energy, space, and time.

Experimental physicists may concentrate on a particular branch of physics, such as astrophysics or quantum mechanics.



     Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Testing the new materials to see if they can be used in various applications, such transistors or solar panels.





Carrying out research in fields like computer engineering, nuclear technology, or particle physics



Work on radiation exposure levels in various environments to assist guard against overexposure of workers



Keeping meticulous records of their work, observing and documenting data throughout the research.



Carrying out studies in controlled locations like labs or clean rooms to study how materials or organisms behave under various circumstances.







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Pros of being an experimental physicist include a good salary, flexibility, and the chance to learn in many different fields.







Perk of becoming a physicist is that, in addition to working in research, they are in high demand in the corporate world.







Being well-versed in the experimental aspects of your field. This can help one create an experiment that is more focused.





A new subfield or sub-sub-field being created. This subfield occasionally sits at the nexus of several other fields and is multidisciplinary.







Graduates in physics can obtain jobs in the medical industry and scientific research with ease.











A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become an Experimental Physicist, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry Biology or Mathematics.

Step-2: Prepare for Entrance Exams such as UGC-NET (University Grants Commission National Eligibility Test), GATE (Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering), or IIT JAM (Joint Admission Test). 

Step-3: Earn a bachelor’s degree in physics from any reputed college.

Step-4:  Consider getting a master’s degree in physics.

Step-5: Complete your PhD in physics to obtain the most prestigious positions

Step-6: Pursue an internship.

Eligibility and skills requirements:



     Age Limit

There is no upper Age Limit for being an Experimental Physicist.  

As per the order by the Supreme Court, there is no maximum age limit for being an Experimental Physicist.

     Educational qualifications

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied mathematics, physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished at least three years of undergraduate study in mathematics, physics, or applied physics.

They ought to have finished a postgraduate degree, often an MSc in Physics with a chosen speciality.

Some job requirements call for a doctorate in physics (M.Phil./Ph.D.).



     Skill requirements



 To become a successful Experimental Physicist, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.



Technical skills



Technical expertise is used by experimental physicists in the execution of experiments and data analysis. They employ technical expertise to run sophisticated machinery and software and to resolve technological problems.

Analytical Skills



Experimental physicists need analytical skills because they utilise them to assess data and draw conclusions about their experiments.

Problem-solving Skills



Experimental physicists find innovative solutions to challenging issues. To make sure the technology functions as intended, they employ their problem-solving abilities to pinpoint problems, create solutions, and test those solutions.

Teamwork Skills:





 

To finish their assignments, experimental physicists frequently collaborate in groups. They must therefore be able to collaborate with others and comprehend how their teammates think. 

 

Time-management Skills



Physicists must prioritise their tasks and use time management strategies to produce high-quality work while meeting deadlines when conducting research.



Communication Skills:



To develop novel concepts and experiments, experimental physicists collaborate with other physicists, engineers, and technicians.



Work environment





Work environments for experimental physicists include academic institutions, government research facilities, and commercial businesses.



They normally work regular hours in well-equipped offices and laboratories, however occasionally they may need to work odd hours to finish studies or go to conferences. 



They might travel to work with other physicists or go to conferences.





Entrance exam to follow the route:



There are different entrance exams available to pursue Experimental Physicist as a career option. Be

Type of specialisation available under this job profiles:



Some of the most popular Experimental Physicist specialisations are listed below. A student can choose the specialisations according to his/her interest in the particular field.

Nuclear Physicists 



The scientists that require the most precise scientific investigations are typically physicists. They acquire the best training, design, plan, carry out, and recognise the revolutionary experiments at the core of the NP programme.

Condensed Matter Physicists 



                    



Condensed Matter Physics is another field of research that focuses on understanding the physical characteristics of condensed matter (solids, liquids, etc.). In statistical physics, they examine phase transitions as well as the distribution of energy and states.



Atomic Physicists 



               





The interaction of matter with either light or merely matter generally worries people. They are classified further into optical, molecular, and atomic physics.



Optical Physicists 







Light is the main subject of optical physics. Everything revolves around light: its character, its behaviours, its relationships to other forms of energy, and its interactions with matter. 



Particle Physicists



           



Astrophysicists work on the very large scale of physics, whereas particle physicists work on an extremely small scale.

A research physicist's duties include investigating physical occurrences, creating hypotheses based on observation and experimentation, and coming up with ways to put physical laws and theories into practice.

title: Career profile Family Lawyer
summary: Name of the career profile: Family Lawyer
description:
Name of the career profile: Family Lawyer







An overview of career a profile:



Any family-related issue must be settled with a family lawyer's assistance. In most cases, when family disputes develop, family lawyers serve as mediators. 

Writing prenuptial agreements to safeguard a person's financial interests before marriage, providing advice on the justifications for impeachment or civil union separation, and creating separation agreements are all tasks that family lawyers are responsible for.



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Family lawyers are responsible for supervising and resolving legal problems that develop between family members.







They coordinate and record all court-related paperwork, as well as oversee and direct mediation meetings.





Family law specialists deal with non-criminal matters. They are in charge of handling grievances, settling disputes, and settling family disputes.







Family lawyers offer their clients advice on how to proceed in their personal matters as well as legal rights and obligations.







Thorough analysis is one of the most important duties of family lawyers. They review earlier iterations of the statute and earlier legal rulings.







Several sources, including law libraries, public records, electronic databases, and the internet, are used by family lawyers while conducting research.







They keep customers informed about their issues on a daily basis and attend trials and court sessions..

.



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Family lawyers are constantly in high demand. Given the rise in marital issues in India, they have little trouble finding clients.





This profession has the potential to be very fulfilling, both financially and emotionally, as well as in terms of reputation and peer acceptance.









One of the main benefits of being a Family Lawyer is the high pay. They receive the highest salaries of any legal practitioners in the sector.







When attorneys assist their clients during trying times, they reap the benefits of satisfied clients.





The ability to manage personal cases in person is one of the benefits of being a Family Lawyer.





A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a Family Lawyer, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete your 10+2 in any stream with an aggregate of 50% of marks from a recognised board..

Step-2: Prepare for the entrance exam like CLAT (Common Law Admission Test), ILSAT (Law School Admission Test), AILET (All India Law Entrance Test) in order to get admission in college.

Step-3: Earn a degree in Bachelors of Arts- Bachelor of Legislative Law)



Step-4: Earn a Master’s Degree in Bachelor of Legislative Law

Step-5: Step-5: Start your Training or Internship

Eligibility and skills requirements:



     Age Limit

There is no age limit for being a Family Lawyer Lawyer.

You should pursue a degree and should have certain experience.

     Educational qualifications

Candidates must complete their 10+2 in any stream with an aggregate of 50% of marks from a recognised board.

Candidates must pass the entrance exams like CLAT (Common Law Admission Test) or ILSAT (Law School Admission Test) in order to pursue their degree.

They ought to have finished their undergraduate degree in Bachelor of Legislative Law.

They ought to have finished their Master’s Degree inBachelor of Legislative Law.

Finish your Training or Internship.



      Entrance exams:

To get admission in this stream in reputed colleges you must clear their entrance exam, interview or the cut-off marks.

To become a successful Family Lawyer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Research Skills



Family lawyers must conduct research on pertinent material. Every case requires a thorough understanding of the facts in order to be resolved. Family lawyers must conduct basic and in-depth research on their clients and their requirements in order to establish legal strategies.

People Skills



Family lawyers need to be professional, likeable, and able to read people. This enables family lawyers to assess the jury's reactions and the reliability of the witnesses.

Analytical Skills



Family lawyers need to be professional, likeable, and able to read people. This enables family lawyers to assess the jury's reactions and the reliability of the witnesses.

Communication Skills



Family lawyers should practise effective communication because it is essential to the expansion and operation of enterprises.s.

A job as a Family Lawyer requires strong social perception. He or she must be able to comprehend or anticipate the causes of someone else's response as well as potential future reactions.

Some of the most popular Family Lawyer specialisations are listed below. A student can choose the specialisations according to his/her interest in the particular field.

Civil Lawyers



Civil lawyers deal with non-criminal conflicts, primarily involving property and court actions. On behalf of private firms, civil lawyers are primarily in charge of resolving legal issues and controversies..

Criminal Lawyer



Criminal defence attorneys investigate and evaluate a case to ascertain the possibility of a favourable result and develop a plan to defend their clients in court..

Corporate Lawyer



Corporate attorneys make assurance that a corporation's or organisation's financial activities are legitimate. Additionally, corporate attorneys must educate their clients on their fundamental rights and responsibilities, including those related to the duties and tasks of the company's officers.

Legal Advisor



A legal advisor serves both the public and private sectors of the economy. The work of a legal counsellor entails a great deal of accountability, stress, and enthusiasm.

Divorce Lawyer





A divorce attorney is a civil lawyer who deals with issues involving disagreements between couples. He or she is in charge of the equitable distribution of assets and liabilities. The duration of child custody and support is decided by a divorce attorney.

title: Career profile Field technicians
summary: Name of the career profile: Field technicians
description:
Name of the career profile: Field technicians







An overview of career a profile:



A field technician is in charge of doing on-site maintenance, checks, and repairs on a company's electrical products or equipment. 

This may include heavy machinery, computers, heating and cooling systems, security systems, and more. Field technicians may perform on-the-job duties at manufacturing facilities, factories, or construction sites..



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Control network performance, capacity, and reliability while working with RF engineers to define rules.







Acquired the ability to swiftly troubleshoot laptops and identify the parts required for repairs..







At the homes of the clients, offer desktop assistance and fix network communication problems.







Experience with imaging new systems, testing SCCM for migration, and rebuilding existing desktops and laptops.







Install NEC POS systems and networking hardware in new stores.





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



As customer needs change, you'll never get bored at work because there are never any boring moments.





The bulk of the time that field service technicians are employed, they engage directly with clients. It can be quite fulfilling to be able to see the individuals you are helping in person and understand how you are benefiting them.









Field service technicians have a lot of opportunity for learning because they must be creative and multitask in order to succeed at their position.







Speaking about training, several businesses provide completely funded courses to help technicians get competent for the position.









The path to a long and extremely gratifying career begins with becoming a field service technician.







A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a Field technician, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics.

Step-2: Prepare for the Entrance Exams such as JEE Main (Joint Entrance Exam), JEE Advanced (Joint Entrance Exam).

Step-3: Earn a 4 year of bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology or in any equivalent stream related to computer technology.

Step-5: Start your training.

Eligibility and skills requirements:



     Age Limit

There is no age limit for being a Field Technician..

You should pursue a degree and should have relevant skills and work experience.

     Educational qualifications

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Mathematics, physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished 4 years of undergraduate degree in Bachelor of Technology in streams such as Computer Science, Information Technology etc.

They ought to have finished 3 years of Master’s Degree in Master of Technology in any field.



     Skill requirements



 To become a successful Field technician, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Customer Service



Responding to questions, fixing problems, and delivering excellent service are all part of the process of providing assistance to all current and potential clients.

Equipment Test



A mechanical instrument or apparatus used to generate signals and obtain feedback from an electronic device being tested is called test equipment..

Knowledge about hand tools



Utilising testers and all required hand tools, repairing mechanical and electrical equipment and restoring or replacing the structures' defective or damaged elements with hand tools.

.

Technical Support



The services offered to customers by any hardware or software company are known as technical support or tech support. They help customers with technical problems with their products or services.

Preventive Maintenance



Performed corrective and preventative maintenance for mechanical, electrical, and hydraulic systems as a freelance field service technician.

Data Gathering



Data collection refers to the analysis and gathering of all required data. It aids in conducting research and keeping track of crucial information.

Work environment



A field technician is responsible for on-site servicing, diagnostics, and repairs for the electrical equipment or products of a corporation. 

The field technician often works at the client's designated location, maintaining and repairing the network, hardware, software, and other telecom and IT-related setups. 

Hours and pay of field Technicians are determined by the employee's location, employer, and any overtime they work.



Entrance exam to follow the route:



It is a good idea to obtain well-known field technician professional certifications like the Master Certified Electronics Technician (CETma), EPA Amusement Operators Safety Certification (EPA), or Emergency Medical Technician (EMT) in order to thrive in field technician professions .



If you want to pursue a degree then there are different entrance exams available to pursue Field technician as a career option. Bel

Field systems technician

Lead technician





Lead technicians are technicians who are in charge of a department's or project's technical requirements. They are typically long-term employees, and being acquainted with the technical and technological procedures, software, and hardware of the organisation would be a huge advantage in their work..

Promotion and upgradation of this career profile:



Field technicians  follow a hierarchy of roles who are posted at different levels. They start from Field Service Technician and are promoted according to their experience in that particular field. 



Level 1: Field Service Technician

Field service technicians may work for a variety of businesses and industries. Their primary responsibility is to examine and resolve equipment problems that arise on-site at various facilities.



Level 2: Field Engineer

An individual in this position is in charge of maintaining and fixing machinery. They troubleshoot mechanical and construction problems using their knowledge of engineering concepts.



Level 3: Technical Support Specialist

They deal with hardware issues and other technological issues. In order to guarantee consumer happiness, they provide assistance with a variety of technological problems.



Level 4: Project Engineer

A project engineer is in charge of organising and carrying out the project. Together with the business management, they evaluate proposals and establish project objectives..



Level 5: Maintenance Supervisor

Serving as the principal conduit for the communication of information within a facility, maintenance supervisors act as a link between the rest of the maintenance team and top management.



Level 6: Operational Supervisor

Operations Supervisor keep an eye on the performance of staff members and procedures in a range of departments, including IT, sales, production, finance, and customer service.

Demands & Future Scope 







Between 2020 and 2021, the demand for Field technicians doubled, rising from 136,503 to 258,000. Demand surpassed supply by over three to one last year; this year, the ratio is projected to be five to one. 

According to the Bureau of Labor Statistics, field technician positions are predicted to rise by 4% between 2022 and 2028, which is classified as "as fast as average."



Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Nikola Tesla







Mastermind inventor Nikola Tesla created various revolutionary inventions. He was a technician who contributed to history with roughly 300 patents.



Claude Shannon

 

Electrical engineer, mathematician, and cryptographer all describe Claude Shannon. His publication of the essay A Mathematical Theory of Communication, which launched the area of information theory, is credited to him.

Alexander Grahmbell



Technician, scientist, and inventor, Alexander Graham Bell. It is said that he created the first working telephone. He is also recognised with helping start AT&T, the nation's largest telephone company, which has been operating successfully since 1885.

Thomas Edison



Thomas Edison is known as America's greatest inventor, and he leaves behind a lasting legacy. Despite not being the original inventor of the incandescent light bulb, he was the first to contribute to making it economically feasible..

Mihajlo Pupin



Technician, Mathematical physics lecturer Mihajlo Idvorski Pupin works in the recently established Electrical Engineering Department. His role as a teacher eventually led him to get interested in studying electromagnetic phenomena.

title: Career profile Geneticist
summary: Name of the career profile: Geneticist
description:
Name of the career profile: Geneticist







An overview of career a profile:

Geneticists are specialists who research the interactions, evolution, and duplication of genes in plants, animals, and people.

A geneticist may expect to make between INR 3 LPA and INR 8 LPA annually, based on their level of specialty and experience.

A geneticist is supposed to research genetic material in laboratories. The examination of cells at the genomic level makes use of cutting-edge instruments and technologies. These people are specialists in putting their knowledge of research and development to use.



     Roles & responsibilities of a Geneticist

Geneticists generally carry out lab research and tests in laboratories.



Extracting DNA or carrying out demonstrative tests and interpreting, assessing, or defending genetic research evidence



  

Understanding, assessing, or supporting genetic lab results and analysing general data to make judgments and publish research





Geneticists make publication of discoveries, for instance at conferences or in scholarly journals. 





Guiding or organising the efforts of various geneticists, academics, experts, or biometricians who are working on genetics research projects

.



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Perks of being a Geneticist are satisfying salary, flexibility, learning opportunity, people-orientation, and the most important exploring various things about organisms. 







Geneticist is considered as one of the most secure jobs and they can shift or work in their specialisation field from any laboratory in the world.







The Positive impact of the career of having a Geneticist in the industry will not only help in developing new things about genes of plants and living organisms.







Earning unique experience by identifying, managing risks, and gaining a vivid set of knowledge will help you to grow in this career.



Graduates in genetics can easily find employment in the medical field and scientific research.











A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a Geneticist, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry Biology or Mathematics.

Step-2: Prepare for Entrance Exam like GSAT, DUET. These exams are conducted for Admission in Genetics Courses.

Step-3:  Select correct College for Geneticist.

Step-4: Complete your college which will take 3-4 years.

Step 5: Pursue an internship.

Eligibility and skills requirements:



     Age Limit

There is no upper Age Limit for being a Geneticist.  

As per the order by the Supreme Court, there is no maximum age limit for being a Geneticist.



     Educational qualifications

A Bachelor's degree in genetics is needed for entry-level positions as a geneticist, whereas higher positions call for a Master's degree or an M.Tech with a focus on genetic engineering.

It typically takes 3–4 years to complete a bachelor's degree.

You must pass English proficiency tests like the International English Language Testing System (IELTS), Pearson Test of English (PTE), or Test of English as Foreign Language (TOEFL) to work as a geneticist abroad.

Along with your degree, transcripts, resume, and required examinations, you also need to submit a Letter of Recommendation.



Skill requirements



 To become a successful geneticist, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.







Analytical skills: One should have excellent mathematical, deductive and inductive reasoning skills.



Critical-thinking Skills: Geneticists make decisions based on their judgement and logic after conducting research.



Problem-solving Skills: Geneticists use experiments and data analysis to solve challenging scientific problems.



Interpersonal Skills: Numerous geneticists lead interdisciplinary teams toward a shared objective, act as team captains, and encourage and direct their members.



Time-management Skills: When conducting research, geneticists must adhere to deadlines, so they must prioritise their activities and manage their time effectively while still generating high-quality work.



Mathematic Skills: These specialists must comprehend statistics, calculus, and general maths since they frequently use complicated formulae and equations.



Communication Skills: Verbal and written communication are crucial for geneticists since they must effectively interact with their team members, publish reports, produce research papers, and deliver presentations.





Work environment





A geneticist is supposed to examine genetic material in laboratories. 

The examination of cells at the genomic level makes use of cutting-edge instruments and technologies. 

Geneticists are specialists in putting their knowledge of research and development to use.

Geneticist's duties may include analysing DNA, synthesising proteins, learning about biological processes, and identifying nutrients in tissues. 



Entrance exam to follow the route:

Type of specialisation available under this job profiles:



Some of the most popular geneticist specialisations are listed below. A student can choose the specialisations according to his/her interest in the particular field.

Genetic Counsellor



A specialist who counsels families, communities, and individuals about genetic illnesses and discusses disease treatment choices with them is known as a genetic counsellor. They educate children regarding the dangers of an illness.

Plant Breeder/Geneticist





A plant breeder is someone who investigates the traits of seeds and attempts to enhance the traits of plants. They use their understanding of genetics to cross plants. They assist the crops and plants stay healthy and protect them from insects and pests.

Research Scientist 



They are the experts in charge of gathering scientific data and setting up experiments to carry out scientific research in carefully monitored settings. There are specialisations available in a variety of fields, including microbiology, genetics, cancer studies, neuroscience, stem cell research, and others.

Clinical Scientist



Scientists who study clinical immunology examine the immune system to gain insight into illnesses, diseases, and the effects of any foreign substances on the body. They are skilled in caring for patients with immunologic problems, digestive system disorders, asthma, allergic patients, and gastrointestinal illnesses.

Promotion and upgradation of this career profile:



Geneticists follow a hierarchy of  roles who are posted at different levels. The Geneticist starts from Graduate Assistant and are promoted according to their experience in that particular field. In general there are 10 levels of Geneticist, given below with detailed explanation.





             Level-1 : Graduate Assistant



 A graduate assistant assists departments, students, or instructors with a variety of tasks.

They plan research strategies, carry out experiments, and perform analyses.





              Level-2 : Fellow



A professional who seeks experience or expertise in a community of peers is referred to as a fellow.



A fellow's responsibilities may include taking part in experiments, coming up with research ideas, organising high-level meetings involving undergraduates, graduate students, and Phds, and developing procedures.

           

           Level-3 : Laboratory Technician

                   

A laboratory technician is in charge of creating laboratory tests and running experiments to confirm ideas.

               

            Level-4: Scientific Technician.



 By taking care of the practical details, a scientific technician supports research and contributes to the creation of new processes and products.

        

           Level-5: Forensic Science Technician



By incorporating scientific knowledge into the investigation, a forensic science technician aids criminal investigators in eliminating suspects.



Technicians in forensic science gather information, evaluate it, and interact with law enforcement.



        Level-6: Biologist



A biologist is a specialist who examines plant and animal life to understand how various creatures interact with one another and with their environment.



They document their findings and share them with other scientists so that they can explore potential steps to conserve the environment or a particular species.



        Level-7: Academic Researcher



A scholar who conducts research in a particular field of study may also produce reports, studies, or papers on policy.



       Level-8: Biomedical Engineers



Apparatus and procedures are created by a biomedical engineer. To identify medical conditions, biomedical engineers build devices and artificial organs.

       

       Level-9: Medical Scientist



A medical scientist is in charge of carrying out research to learn more about human ailments and discover treatments.



Additionally, they research cell structure and look for ways to avert illnesses.



      Level-10: Pharmacologist



A pharmacologist researches how medications or chemicals affect people, plants, and animals.



Pharmacologists study how chemicals affect cells and comprehend how medications are dissolved, absorbed, and distributed in the body.

title: Career profile Homeopathic Doctor
summary: Name of the career profile: Homoeopathic Doctor
description:
Name of the career profile: Homoeopathic Doctor







An overview of career a profile:



A homoeopathic doctor is a type of medical professional who treats illnesses with natural remedies. What a chemical is capable of causing, it is also capable of treating, according to the main tenet of this therapy.

A homoeopathic doctor, often known as a homoeopath, is a physician who uses the homoeopathic method of treatment, which was created more than 200 years ago by a German doctor named Samuel Hahnemann and is still used on every continent today.   

  

Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Conducting first patient consultations to obtain a thorough clinical history. They would be questioned extensively about their personality, diet, lifestyle, health issues, and way of life.







Writing prescriptions for homoeopathic medicines to treat a variety of illnesses. From serious conditions like the common cold or a sore throat to degenerative conditions like arthritis and asthma







Keeping tabs on patients' recuperation times. Homeopathy, in contrast to allopathy, does not promise quick fixes.







Taking care of administrative and marketing responsibilities. Most homoeopathic physicians work for themselves..



Conducting lectures and presentations to promote homoeopathy for organisations, communities, and health professionals.







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Pros of being a Homoeopathic Doctor include a good salary, flexibility, and the chance to learn in many different fields.







There is a high percentage of employment rates of graduates practicing medicine. In the UK about 99 percent of graduates were hired within the past six months.







There are various private and government sectors where you can start your career as a Homoeopathic doctor. You can even open your clinic to practise medicine on your own.





The job profile of a Homoeopathic doctor is full of respect and one of the most renowned job profiles in the world.







Homoeopathic Doctors are never out of employment, there are various specialisations of a doctor to choose from.











A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a Homoeopathic Doctor, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology.

Step-2: Prepare for the Medical College Admission Test (MCAT)

Step-3: Earn a bachelor’s degree in Homeopathy Medicine & Surgery (BHMS).

Step-4:  Complete your Diploma in Homeopathy Medicine & Surgery (DHMS).

Step-5: Pursue an internship Training.

Eligibility and skills requirements:



     Age Limit

The age limit for both the male & female for a homoeopathic doctor is not more than 35 years 

     Educational qualifications

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Biology, physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished 4 years of undergraduate degree in Homeopathy Medicine & Surgery

They ought to have finished 3 years of Diploma in Homeopathy Medicine & Surgery (DHMS).

Some job requirements call for a doctorate in physics (M.Phil./Ph.D.).



     Skill requirements



 To become a successful Homoeopathic Doctor, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.



Technical skills



You must be able to use excel spreadsheets, label-making software, medical software, information retrieval software, and accounting software.

Analytical Skills



They should be able to articulate complex concepts and identify the advantages and disadvantages of potential solutions using logic and reasoning.

Problem-solving Skills



Despite advances in science, there isn't always an immediate fix for a medical issue. You must be able to choose different therapy options to address such issues.

Teamwork Skills:





 

Paying close attention to what others are saying, taking your time to understand what has been said, asking appropriate questions, and avoiding unnecessary interruptions

 

Time-management Skills



In order to complete their work on time and with good quality, doctors must prioritise their responsibilities and employ time management techniques.

Communication Skills:



You must be able to carefully listen to your patients' responses while also posing pertinent inquiries about their lifestyle and medical background.

Work environment





A homoeopathic doctor's practice might get quite busy.

A typical day for a homoeopathic doctor would include gathering medical histories, arranging for diagnostic testing, creating treatment plans, and reviewing the patients' medical files. It will resemble the medical offices of any other doctor.



Entrance exam to follow the route:



There are different entrance exams available to pursue Homoeopathic Doctor as a career option. Below given are some of the entrance exams for Homoeopathic Doctor courses.



NEET 2021 (National Eligibility Cum Entrance Test)

BVP CET (Bharati Vidyapeeth Common Entrance Test)

IPU CET 2021 (Indraprastha University Common En

providing lifestyle guidance on matters including nutrition, exercise, and mental health



             Level-2: Public Health Specialist 



A public health expert is a medical practitioner who conducts research on diseases and illnesses and works with epidemiologists and medical professionals to identify and stop the spread of disease.



Level-3:  Pharmacist

                   

Preparing and distributing medications on doctors' orders.

Keeping track of patients' pharmacological treatments, offering advice on actions, and warning patients of any potential ill effects

         

             Level-4: Lecturer/ Professor



A lecturer's primary duty is to instruct students at the undergraduate and graduate levels in subjects that are assigned by the department head and periodically reviewed by him or her



             Level-5: Homoeopath

Evaluating patients and creating a thorough case history with them that includes symptoms and details of their psychological, emotional, and physical health

keeping thorough clinical records and notes for each patient.

Top recruiting agencies for this profile

The top companies which hire Homoeopathic Doctors are:

Boiron



Heel



Homeocan Inc



Nelsons



GMP Laboratories of America 



Hylands



Willmar Schwabe GmbH & Co. KG





Demands & Future Scope 







Homoeopathy is regarded as the second-largest medical system in India. Promising opportunities exist in this area. At a variety of public and private homoeopathic hospitals, you can become a medical officer or doctor. As an alternative, you may start your own medical facility. After gaining some experience, you can work as a research fellow or a college instructor and receive funding for your future studies from the medical field.



Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



J. Ellis Barker







James Ellis Barker was a British journalist, historian, and homoeopath. In addition, Barker promoted the use of alternative cancer therapies and the notion that chronic poisoning and vitamin deficiency lead to autointoxication, which in turn causes cancer.



Clemens Maria Franz





Clemens Maria Franz was an attorney, civil official for the Dutch and Prussian governments, agriculturalist, botanist, doctor, and a forerunner in the field of homoeopathy. He received a Knight in the Légion d'honneur decoration.



Samuel Cockburn







Early in his medical career, Samuel Cockburn, a Scottish doctor with standard training for the period, was convinced by the ideas of homoeopathy.



John Franklin Gray





John Franklin Gray was a pioneer in the field of homoeopathy and one of its first practitioners in the United States. He was also an educator and doctor. Additionally, he is acknowledged as a significant medical reformer.



Hahnemann, Mélanie





The first licenced female physician in history was a French woman named Marie Mélanie d'Hervilly Gohier Hahnemann.

title: Career profile Industrial Organizational Psychologist
summary: Name of the career profile: Industrial-Organizational Psychologist
description:
Name of the career profile: Industrial-Organizational Psychologist







An overview of career a profile:



The field of industrial-organisational psychology (I-O) focuses on the analysis of behaviour in the workplace. 

People who work in these fields apply psychological concepts to things like human resources, employee development, marketing, and sales.

I-O psychologists commonly use research in product testing, personnel placement, and workplace productivity enhancement.



     Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Create software using SAS and other PC tools to aid in the administration, analysis, and support of ongoing processes.



They are responsible to independently develop and deliver plans to locate civilian leaders and close the communication gap between civilian and military employees.

  

Offer the inmate population general clinical care as well as supervision oversight over a residential drug programme, a non-residential drug programme, etc.



Conducting new candidate screening. This entails doing a variety of evaluations to determine whether they would be a good fit for the position.







Evaluating the necessity of and carrying out training and motivation programmes.







Developing strategies with other departments to increase organisational effectiveness







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



I/O psychologists frequently receive competitive pay and benefits.







There is a lot of possibility for advancement and specialisation because the sector is so diverse and wide-ranging.







The majority of I/O psychologists consider their work to be very gratifying. The efficiency and productivity of the business are directly impacted when you assist employees in being more productive, contented, and fulfilled.







The more experience and knowledge you have, as in many careers, the more autonomy and flexibility you will have in this field.







Theoretically, there is a demand for I/O psychologists everywhere there are enterprises, hence there are employment accessible everywhere.







A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become an Industrial-Organizational Psychologist, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects like Science, Mathematics, Biology,  physics, and chemistry.

Step-2: Earn a 4 year bachelor's degree in psychology or in a related field.



Step-3: Earn a Master’s Degree in Psychology.

Step-4: Complete your doctorate in Industrial-Psychology.

Step-5: Start your Training..

Eligibility and Skill Requirements



     Age Limit



There is no upper Age Limit for being an Industrial-Organizational Psychologist.  

There is no maximum age limit for being an Industrial-Organizational Psychologist, you just need to complete your degree and have relative experience.

     Educational qualifications

The candidates should have studied Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics in their 10+12.

Candidates with a bachelor's degree in chemistry, preferably with biology and/or mathematics as complementing disciplines, or an equivalent degree, and an overall grade point average of at least 50% are eligible to apply.

Candidates with a bachelor's degree from a recognised university or board with Chemistry as a supplemental topic and the same overall score are also eligible for the programme.

Institutions that require applicants to take an entrance exam may consider both the results of that exam and prior academic achievements.



Skill requirements



To become a successful Industrial-Organizational Psychologist, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.



Analytical skills



            



The process of acquiring data from multiple sources and then analysing it to draw a logical conclusion that is advantageous to the company's operations requires analytical skills.



Technological Skills



          

Technological skills are helpful because I/O psychologists will need to use spreadsheet, presentation, and human resources tools, as well as analytical or scientific software..

Integrity 







Integrity includes being honest and having high moral standards are required in the field of Industrial Organizational Psychologist.

Time-management Skills







To fulfil deadlines and generate high-quality work, IO Psychologists must effectively prioritise their duties and manage their time.



Communication Skills







Your ability to clearly communicate your thoughts, opinions, and ideas to those around you is demonstrated by your communication abilities.

Problem Solving Skills



In order to develop and assess possibilities and find solutions, I/O psychologists must identify complicated challenges and review pertinent information.

Work environment



Industrial organisational psychologists typically have a solid sense of work security. You'll most likely be on a contract when working in lower jobs, which gives you security.



Industrial organisational psychologists typically operate in off

title: Career profile Law Reporter
summary: Name of the career profile: Law Reporter
description:
Name of the career profile: Law Reporter







An overview of career a profile:



Transcribing spoken or recorded speech into written form is the responsibility of a Law Reporter. In order to create an official transcript of court hearings, descriptions, and other proceedings, the majority of Law Reporter utilise a stenotype (keyboard machine that records) or steno mask. 

Stenographer is another name for a Law Reporter.



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Acquire the organisational skills, adaptability, database research, and communication skills needed to gather the knowledge required to complete daily responsibilities.







Transcripts of sworn interviews, administrative hearings, and depositions must be provided verbatim.







Speak to a senior lawyer who specialises in civil litigation and bankruptcy.







Medical malpractice, product liability, personal injury, and divorce litigation transcripts should be reported, edited, and produced electronically.







Provide superior court judges and attorneys with both electronic and printed trial transcripts.





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Court reporting is the right profession for you if you want to be able to choose how you spend your time at work. Law Reporter who work on a freelance basis can create their own timetable and modify it as necessary. It enables real work-life balance.







Job stability is a major benefit of being a law reporter. The NCRA predicts a shortage of Law Reporter by 2018 due to retirees, and the court reporting industry is expected to develop significantly over the next ten years.







A new adventure every day. Every day brings with it a fresh adventure, new acquaintances, and fresh tales. 







In as little as two years, obtain a degree. One of the best degrees you can obtain in as short as two years is a court reporting degree or certificate.







Professional Law Reporters can find work all throughout the world. The world is your oyster, and accurate record keeping is always necessary.







A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a law Reporter, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects like Arts, Commerce and Science.

Step-2: Complete your Associate’s Degree in Court Reporting  or another related program..

Step-3: Earn a Master’s Degree in Court Reporting or in any other programs (it is not mandatory)

Step-4: Pass Certification exam CVR (Certified Verbatim Reporter’s examination) or Registered Professional Reporter (RPR) certification.

Step-5: Complete your relevant training or Internship

Eligibility and skills requirements:



     Age Limit

For being a Law Reporter your  age should be 18 or more than 18 years.

You should pursue a degree and should have pass the CVR (Certified Verbatim Reporter’s examination) or Registered Professional Reporter (RPR) certification

     Educational qualifications

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Arts , Commerce, or Science.

They ought to have finished an associate degree in court reporting, or another related program..

They ought to have finished their Master’s Degree in court reporting (optional).

Candidates should pass the certification exam in order to pursue internship or training.

Finish your training or Internship.



     Skill requirements



To become a successful law Reporter, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Communication



Law Reporter must be proficient in both written and spoken communication because they frequently engage with everyone from judges to criminal defendants.

Punctuality



Law reporter should be punctual since it's normally forbidden for court proceedings to continue without a Law Reporter present

Listening Skills



To accurately record what is being said, a Law Reporter needs to hear very well. It is advised that a Law Reporter not wear a hearing aid for corrective purposes.

Clerical skill



Despite the fact that testimony is transcribed using a stenograph machine, written transcripts can still be created using specialised software.

Professional Appearance



Whether working in a courthouse, law office, or in private practice, Law Reporter are normally expected to present themselves professionally.

Work environment



Law reporters are frequently found in legislatures or courts. Many also work as freelance journalists and are occasionally hired on-demand by law firms or businesses to cover pretrial depositions and other events.

There are numerous courthouses and offices that require a Law Reporter to travel. However, some CART (communication access real-time translation) and broadcast captioning service providers operate remotely from either their home or a centralised office.

Those who record events and write up transcripts for court proceedings often work full-time as a Law Reporter. Reporters who work freelance have more freedom in determining their work hours.





Entrance exam to follo

Type of specialisation available under this job profiles:

Some of the most popular Law Reporter specialisations are listed below. A student can choose the specialisations according to his/her interest in the particular field.

Broadcast Captioner



For live television programmes, broadcast captioners give captions so that deaf and hard-of-hearing viewers can readily read what is being said.

CART Reporters



Communication Access Realtime Translation (CART), commonly referred to as real-time or live-event captioning, offers the deaf and hearing-impaired live, individualised, in-person captioning services.

Data Entry Specialist



Since a trained stenotype operator can type at least twice as many words per minute as an experienced typist, qualified Law Reporters may employ their expertise in rapid data entry for businesses.

Judicial transcriptionists 



Legal or judicial transcriptionists record speeches that are transcribed for them by attorneys while correcting for grammar and clarity. They may record client meetings, letters to trial participants, or directions for wills and written contracts when working with attorneys.

Scopist’s 



A scopist's responsibility is to edit Law Reporters stereotyped transcripts into understandable, readable English.

Law Reporter follow a hierarchy of roles who are posted at different levels. They start from Legal Assistant and are promoted according to their experience in that particular field. 



Level 1: Legal Assistant

As a legal assistant, you will spend a large portion of your day carrying out activities for the benefit of a lawyer.



Level 2: Executive Assistant

The high-level assistance that many businesses require to develop and prosper is provided by executive assistants. They manage all requests for information that cross their desk, do research, and examine statistics reports.



Level 3: Legal Secretary

A legal secretary supports attorneys by assisting with document preparation and organisation. They have received training and possess in-depth understanding of legal procedures and case paperwork.



Level 4: Office Manager

The daily duties of everyone in the office, as well as overseeing a small administrative staff, are all part of the office manager's job description.

Jane Rose

Janice S



As a self-employed Law reporter, Janice S. Baker started her court reporting career in 1983. She relocated to Clayton County Superior Court in 1985 and worked there for fifteen years under the supervision of two justices of the superior court.

Rich  Baker



In 2009, Rich joined Janice Baker & Associates, and in 2011, he passed the court reporting exam. From 2012 to 2018, he served as Judge Michael Garrett's assistant at Clayton County State Court. Rich was an active-duty Army officer before he became a Law reporter.

title: Career profile Neuropsychologist
summary: Name of the career profile: Neuropsychologist
description:
Name of the career profile: Neuropsychologist







An overview of career a profile:



Neuropsychologists focus on understanding the functioning of the brain and treating brain illnesses. They put their education to use by researching, diagnosing, and treating mental health conditions. 

Working with people who have experienced mood or behaviour changes, or who have sustained brain injuries, is something that professionals in this sector can do.



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Disorders of the neurological system are studied and treated by neuropsychologists. After a sickness or accident, they assess patients and do tests to ascertain how their brains function..







Neuropsychologists may also design additional patient treatments such as cognitive rehabilitation treatment.







Neuropsychologists collaborate closely with medical professionals and neurologists when assessing patients.







Assessment tests are used by neuropsychologists to gauge brain activity. These examinations can evaluate one's cognitive, problem-solving, and memory skills.







Neuropsychologists treat patients with strokes, memory loss problems, or learning difficulties depending on their area of expertise.

.



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



One advantage of a neuropsychology job is its diversity, which enables you to concentrate on various neuropsychology themes, practise, or research.





Numerous patients trusted neuropsychologists with their life, and this is reflected in their annual salary.









Neuropsychology is a demanding and fulfilling field of work. Neuropsychologists often create and carry out a treatment plan intended to enhance the quality of life for their patients after diagnosing a disease.







The wide variety of patients you will work with is another major advantage of a career in neuropsychology.





Investigating how a neurological condition has impacted someone's cognitive performance requires a lot of interesting investigative work.







A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a Neuropsychologist, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects like Science, Mathematics, Biology,  physics, and chemistry.

Step-2: Earn a 4 year bachelor's degree in psychology, biology, pre-medicine, or another related program..

Step-3: Earn a Master’s Degree in Neuropsychology

Step-4: Complete your doctorate in neuropsychology (Ph.D. or Psy.D)

Step-5: Start your Training.

Eligibility and skills requirements:



     Age Limit

There is no age limit for being a Neuropsychologist.

You should pursue a degree and should have pass the EPPP exams

     Educational qualifications

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Science, Mathematics, Biology,  physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished 4 years of undergraduate degree in psychology, biology, pre-medicine, or another related program..

They ought to have finished there Master’s Degree in Neuropsychology

Earn a doctorate in neuropsychology (Ph.D. or Psy.D)

Finish your postdoctoral fellowship



     Skill requirements



To become a successful Neuropsychologist, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Communication



Effective communication is crucial for a variety of situations, including giving instructions to a patient or outlining an idea to a coworker. This applies to both writing ideas clearly and speaking about them clearly.

Researching



The area of neuropsychology is driven by research. This implies that the majority of neuropsychologists must be capable of designing and managing research investigations. To effectively comprehend and evaluate data from other researchers, even those who don't undertake much research will need research abilities..

Critical Thinking



Critical thinking abilities and the capacity to adopt novel ways to problems or topics are prerequisites for being able to comprehend and analyse data as well as develop and carry out research. Critical thinking is helpful for connecting newly found concepts to real-world applications.

Problem-Solving



Problem-solving abilities are crucial for neuropsychologists, whether they're diagnosing a patient or looking into the origins of a neurological condition in a group of people..

Observing Power



Neuropsychologists research how the brain and behaviour interact. Naturally, they need to be very good observers to be able to discern certain human gestures, movements, and other acts as well as to study and evaluate research data and findings..

Work environment



Neuropsychology specialists are not restricted to medical facilities. Work environments for cognitive neuropsychologists include research labs at universities, governmental institutions, and commercial businesses like hospitals. 

Clinical and non-clinical neuropsychologists may work in hospitals as well as schools, private clinics, and rehabilitation facilities. They can also move to work in clini

Neuropsychologists focus on understanding the functioning of the brain and treating brain illnesses. They put their education to use by researching, diagnosing, and treating mental health conditions.

Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Neuropsychologists may also design additional patient treatments such as cognitive rehabilitation treatment.







Neuropsychologists collaborate closely with medical professionals and neurologists when assessing patients.







Assessment tests are used by neuropsychologists to gauge brain activity. These examinations can evaluate one's cognitive, problem-solving, and memory skills.







Neuropsychologists treat patients with strokes, memory loss problems, or learning difficulties depending on their area of expertise.

.



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



One advantage of a neuropsychology job is its diversity, which enables you to concentrate on various neuropsychology themes, practise, or research.





Numerous patients trusted neuropsychologists with their life, and this is reflected in their annual salary.









Neuropsychology is a demanding and fulfilling field of work. Neuropsychologists often create and carry out a treatment plan intended to enhance the quality of life for their patients after diagnosing a disease.







The wide variety of patients you will work with is another major advantage of a career in neuropsychology.





Investigating how a neurological condition has impacted someone's cognitive performance requires a lot of interesting investigative work.







A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a Neuropsychologist, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects like Science, Mathematics, Biology,  physics, and chemistry.

Step-2: Earn a 4 year bachelor's degree in psychology, biology, pre-medicine, or another related program..

Step-3: Earn a Master’s Degree in Neuropsychology

Step-4: Complete your doctorate in neuropsychology (Ph.D. or Psy.D)

Step-5: Start your Training.

Eligibility and skills requirements:



     Age Limit

There is no age limit for being a Neuropsychologist.

You should pursue a degree and should have pass the EPPP exams

     Educational qualifications

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Science, Mathematics, Biology,  physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished 4 years of undergraduate degree in psychology, biology, pre-medicine, or another related program..

They ought to have finished there Master’s Degree in Neuropsychology

Earn a doctorate in neuropsychology (Ph.D. or Psy.D)

Finish your postdoctoral fellowship



     Skill requirements



To become a successful Neuropsychologist, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Communication



Effective communication is crucial for a variety of situations, including giving instructions to a patient or outlining an idea to a coworker. This applies to both writing ideas clearly and speaking about them clearly.

Researching



The area of neuropsychology is driven by research. This implies that the majority of neuropsychologists must be capable of designing and managing research investigations. To effectively comprehend and evaluate data from other researchers, even those who don't undertake much research will need research abilities..

Critical Thinking



Critical thinking abilities and the capacity to adopt novel ways to problems or topics are prerequisites for being able to comprehend and analyse data as well as develop and carry out research. Critical thinking is helpful for connecting newly found concepts to real-world applications.

Problem-Solving



Problem-solving abilities are crucial for neuropsychologists, whether they're diagnosing a patient or looking into the origins of a neurological condition in a group of people..

Observing Power



Neuropsychologists research how the brain and behaviour interact. Naturally, they need to be very good observers to be able to discern certain human gestures, movements, and other acts as well as to study and evaluate research data and findings..

Work environment



Neuropsychology specialists are not restricted to medical facilities. Work environments for cognitive neuropsychologists include research labs at universities, governmental institutions, and commercial businesses like hospitals. 

Clinical and non-clinical neuropsychologists may work in hospitals as well as schools, private clinics, and rehabilitation facilities. They can also move to work in clinical management, clinical education, and policy planning.

Type of specialisation available under this job profiles:

Some of the most popular Software Development Project Manager specialisations are listed below. A student can choose the specialisations according to his/her interest in the particular field.

Clinical Neuropsychologists



They have received specialised training to manage the evaluation of the human brain. If a brain issue is discovered, they correct the symptoms and work to prevent them. They even use technologies like computerised tomography (CT Scanning), magnetic resonance imaging (MRI), and PET to identify mental illnesses and traumas (Positron Emission Tomography).

Forensic Neuropsychologists



They are experts who have received training to help resolve legal problems. These professionals are also called as witnesses in cases of personal injury. The need for forensic neuropsychologists in the legal sector is rapidly increasing in India..

Cognitive Neuropsychologists



These experts primarily concentrate on the relationship between the brain and mental processes like memory, attention, perception, body language, planning, and others. These specialists offer counselling services to patients and have direct contact with them..

Paediatric neuropsychologists



Paediatric neuropsychologists are qualified to distinguish between the development of a newborn's brain and a child's behaviour. Experts in this area specialise in evaluating youngsters for brain injury and functional impairments..

Neuropsychology professors





In a university context, neuropsychology professors carry out research in the field. Despite similarities, neuropsychology professors work in academia, whereas researchers could be employed by non-academic organisations..

Promotion and upgradation of this career profile:



Neuropsychologists follow a hierarchy of roles who are posted at different levels. They start from Neuropsychologist Intern and are promoted according to their experience in that particular field. 



Level 1: Neuropsychologist

Many project managers start out in non-management positions and advance to project manager as they take on more duties.



Level 2: Clinical Psychologist

Individuals or families can receive mental health care from clinical psychologists..



Level 3: Clinical Supervisor

You might oversee the work of clinical staff members with less experience in your capacity as a clinical supervisor. In order to develop their abilities and uphold a high degree of morality and professionalism, you can also find, hire, train, and coach clinical staff..



Level 4: Nursing Director

A nursing director's responsibility is to supervise a nursing team's activities. They are responsible for activities like creating patient care policies, simplifying the patient admission process, and managing the departmental budget in addition to crafting strategic plans for the nursing department..



Level 5: Clinical Services Director

All of a hospital's administrative and operational duties fall under the purview of clinical services directors. Their primary objective is to ensure that patients receive the finest care available.



Level 6: Medical Director

In order to ensure that all patients receive high-quality care and that staff are treated fairly, patients, medical staff, and other important stakeholders in a healthcare facility expect operations to run as smoothly as possible..

UnitedHealth Group

UC San Francisco







UCHealth





Demands & Future Scope 







Through 2024, there will be a greater need for neuropsychologists than there is currently, due to the ageing population and the resulting rise in demand for mental health services.

However, the need for professionals in the field of neuropsychology could increase more quickly over the course of the following 10 to 20 years due to rising interest in the brain and how it functions as well as rapidly advancing imaging technologies.



Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Sigmund Freud







S. G. Freud was One of the most influential thinkers of the 20th century was this Jewish Austrian physician and neurologist. His concept of the unconscious is still recognised as one of psychology's most influential turning points in addition to being the originator of psychoanalysis.



Steven Paul Novella

 

American clinical neurologist Steven Paul Novella is an associate professor at Yale University School of Medicine. As the host of the podcast The Sceptics' Guide to the Universe and the president of the New England Sceptical Society, Novella is most recognised for his engagement in the sceptical movement..

Paul Broca



Modern neuropsychology is credited with having been founded by Paul Broca. Tan, his well-known case study, had experienced a stroke. Tan could only create a few syllables at a time and struggled to form coherent phrases, yet he was still able to fully comprehend what was being said to him.

Christof Koch Christof Koch 



Theodore Koch Christof Koch is a neurophysiologist and computational neuroscientist who is of German and American descent. He is well recognised for his research on the neurological foundation of consciousness. He serves as the Allen Institute for Brain Science's president and principal scientist..

Miguel Ângelo Laporta Nicolelis 



Miguel Angelo Laporta Nicolelis, a Brazilian scientist and physician who teaches neuroscience at Duke University's Duke School of Medicine, is well renowned for his groundbreaking work on brain-machine interface technology..
