title: Career profile Organic Chemist
summary: Name of the career profile: Organic Chemist
description:
Name of the career profile: Organic Chemist







An overview of career a profile:



Organic chemists research the composition, characteristics, and reactivity of carbon-containing compounds.



They also create novel organic materials with distinct characteristics and uses. These substances have then been employed to create a variety of commercial goods, including plastics and pharmaceuticals.



     Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Organic chemists study the composition, properties, and reactivity of substances that include carbon.



They also produce novel organic compounds with unique properties and uses.



  

Carrying out lab analyses to pinpoint the source of manufacturing and production issues



Use analytical methods to find various biological components and chemicals in food matrices.







Take part in clinical and pre-clinical drug trials and related regulatory processes.







Perform wet chemical testing and analysis on samples that customers ask you to look at.







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



There are permanent and contractual jobs along with full-time and part-time jobs for Organic chemists.







The job of an Organic Chemist is one of the highest paying jobs in the world. A well experienced Chemist in India can earn up to INR 20 Lakhs per year.







There is a high percentage of employment rates of graduates practising medicine. In the UK about 99 percent of graduates were hired within the past six months.







Organic Chemist are never out of employment, there are various specialisations of Chemist to choose from.







The job profile of any Chemist is full of respect and one of the most renowned job profiles in the world.







A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become an Organic Chemist, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects including Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics.

Step-2: The student should have completed their graduation with BSc in Chemistry or any other related discipline

Step-3:  Students who want to enrol in this postgraduate programme overseas must take an English proficiency exam such the IELTS (International English Language Testing System), TOEFL (Test of English as a Foreign Language), or equivalent.

Step-4: Start your internship.

Eligibility and Skill Requirements



     Age Limit



There is no upper Age Limit for being an Organic Chemist.  

There is no maximum age limit for being an Organic Chemist you just need to complete your degree and have relative skills.

     Educational qualifications

The candidates should have studied Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics in there 10+12.

Candidates with a bachelor's degree in chemistry, preferably with biology and/or mathematics as complementing disciplines, or an equivalent degree, and an overall grade point average of at least 50% are eligible to apply.

Candidates with a bachelor's degree from a recognised university or board with Chemistry as a supplemental topic and the same overall score are also eligible for the programme.

Institutions that require applicants to take an entrance exam may consider both the results of that exam and prior academic achievements.



Skill requirements



To become a successful Organic Chemist, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Scientific and Analytical skills



            



Analytical abilities are needed for the process of gathering data from many sources, analysing it, and then coming to a logical conclusion that is useful to the operations of the firm.



Mathematical Skills



          

The ability to solve for the unknown, visualise data, and do basic addition and subtraction are all examples of maths talents that are useful in the business.

Organisational skills: 







Organic Chemists usually work in teams to complete their research. Therefore, they must be able to work well with others and have an understanding of how their teammates operate.

Time-management Skills







To fulfil deadlines and generate high-quality work, Organic Chemists must effectively prioritise their duties and manage their time.



Communication Skills







Your communication skills show your capacity to express your ideas, opinions, and thoughts to others around you in a clear and concise manner.

Problem Solving Skills



Supervised general upkeep and operational issue resolution for lab equipment hence problem-solving skills helps in lab work.

Work environment



In addition to spending a lot of time in the lab, organic chemists also read and write about their research in the scientific literature.

 Some organic chemists use modelling and simulation tools to conduct their work on computers. Colleagues and meeting attendees include organic chemists.

The working environment of an organic chemist is typically hygienic, well-lit, secure, and pleasant. Spend some time at the workstation and the lab bench.

Type of specialisation available under this job profiles:



Some of the most popular Organic Chemist specialisations are listed below. A student can choose the specialisations according to his/her interest in the particular field.

Organic Chemistry Professor 



The task of training students in the field through lectures, presentations, and other methods falls to an organic chemistry professor. Professors of chemistry may also carry out research investigations and publish the findings.

Forensic Analyst 



A bachelor's in organic chemistry is required for jobs in forensic science. A forensic analyst searches for contaminants including poisons and chemicals in samples of water, soil, people, and plants.

Environmental Chemist 



An environmental chemist researches how human activity in the environment affects chemical processes. Examples of potential operations include new construction, mining, and waste disposal, notably for hazardous materials and prescription drugs. For this project, knowledge of organic chemistry is required.

Textile Chemist 



In the natural and synthetic textile industries, organic chemists are crucial. They investigate novel treatments for unfinished natural materials like wool, cotton, silk, and bamboo to produce fabrics that are more aesthetically pleasing and long-lasting, as well as the chemical makeup of the dyes used to colour them.

Food Scientist 



The nutritional content, flavour, appearance, preservation, and packaging of foods for people and animals are all things that food scientists work to enhance. Government agencies like NASA and the food and agriculture industries employ food scientists.

Pharmacologist 



A pharmacologist is in charge of creating and assessing products in the pharmaceutical sector. They investigate a variety of substances, hunt for ways to enhance already-made products, carry out pharmacological trials, and write research articles.

             Level-2: Food Scientist



To better understand how to make food safe for consumption and nourishment for people, a food scientist performs research and experiments to discover how a food develops, degrades, and nourishes.

A forensic analyst is in charge of investigating crime scenes to help gather proof that could lead to the conviction of suspects.

         

             Level-4: Environmental Scientist

Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Carolyn Ruth Bertozzi

Eric Scerri



Eric R. Scerri is a Maltese-born scientist, author, and philosopher of science. In addition to being a lecturer at the University of California, Los Angeles, he founded and serves as editor-in-chief of the international peer-reviewed magazine Foundations of Chemistry, which covers chemical education and the history and philosophy of chemistry.



Ada Yonath



Israeli Chemist Ada E. Yonath is well recognised for her ground-breaking studies on the structure of ribosomes.



Jean-Pierre sauvage



French coordination chemist Jean-Pierre Sauvage works at Strasbourg University. In 1967, he received his diploma from the National School of Chemistry of Strasbourg, currently known as the ECPM Strasbourg.



Fraser Stoddart



Sir James Fraser is a British-American Chemist who serves as the Board of Trustees Professor of Chemistry at North western University in the United States and is also the director of the Stoddart Mechanostereo Stereochemistry Group.

title: Career profile Petroleum and Petrochemical Engineer
summary: Name of the career profile: Petroleum and Petrochemical Engineer
description:
Name of the career profile: Petroleum and Petrochemical Engineer







An overview of career a profile:

A petroleum engineer is in charge of developing the techniques intended to enhance the extraction of oil and gas from subsurface resources.

They create terminals for the exploration and extraction of oil and natural gas.   



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Decision Making



Making decisions involves choosing the best option available in a certain situation. Additionally, you have to act sanely in this situation: acknowledge a variety of choices







Product Development



Product development is the process of taking a product from its initial concept through its eventual market launch and beyond.







Monitoring



Petroleum engineers spend a lot of time on the job site analysing and keeping an eye on system equipment, such as gauges, dials, and other indications, to make sure the machine is operating at its best.









Quality Control



Petroleum engineers often do quality control analyses to identify anomalies and to foresee potential problems.







Documentation



Petroleum engineers write scientific reports for workers in management and research. People examine innovations for the manufacture, design, or upkeep of machinery or systems.







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



There is a great need for petroleum engineers and plenty of opportunities for advancement and development.







Petroleum engineers receive compensation for travel to a variety of destinations, making it a fascinating career path.







Petroleum engineers have fulfilling careers since they contribute to the smooth operation of numerous societies.







Considering petroleum engineers can work on a range of projects, there is a tone of variation.







There are numerous specialities available to Petroleum Engineers, thus they can never be unemployed.









A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become Petroleum and Petrochemical Engineer, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics.

Step-2: Prepare for the Entrance Exams such as JEE Main (Joint Entrance Exam), JEE Advanced (Joint Entrance Exam), UPSEE (Uttar Pradesh State Entrance Exam)

Step-3: Earn a bachelor’s degree in B.E. (Bachelor of Engineering) Petroleum Engineering or B.Sc. (Bachelor of Science) Petroleum Engineering.

Step-4:  Complete your post-graduation in M.Sc. (Master of Science) Petroleum Engineering or any other equivalent

Step-5: Pursue an internship Training.

Eligibility and skills requirements:



     Age Limit

There is no age limit for being a Petrochemical Engineer

The individual needs to be a citizen of India.

     Educational qualifications

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Mathematics, physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished 4 years of undergraduate degree in Bachelor of Petroleum Engineering 

They ought to have finished 3 years of Master’s Degree in (Master of Science) Petroleum Engineering.





     Skill requirements



To become a successful Petroleum and Petrochemical Engineer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.



Technical skills



Considering that petroleum engineers must apply calculus principles and other complex concepts for analysis, design, and troubleshooting

Analytical Skills



Petroleum engineers need to be able to gather and understand a lot of technical data and information.

Problem-solving Skills



Petroleum engineers must see issues and act promptly to fix them. Hence, problem solving skills are required for this.

Creativity:





 

As petroleum engineers must be able to create innovative designs to extract oil and gas from each new drill site, which is unique.

Interpersonal Skills



As they will have to collaborate with numerous other experts (such as engineers, field workers, and clients), To make sure the project is safe and successful, they must be able to communicate effectively with others.

Communication Skills:



You must be able to carefully listen to your clients' responses while also posing clients inquiries about their lifestyle and engineer background.

Work environment





Petrochemical engineers routinely travel to places as well as to meet with other engineers, team members, and clients due to the remote locations of oil fields.

 Moreover, depending on the size of the companies' operations, your journey may possibly take you across the entire world.



Entrance exam to follow the route:



There are different entrance exams available to pursue Petroleum and Petrochemical Engineering as a career option. Below given are some of the entrance exams for Petroleum and Petrochemical Engineer courses.



JEE (Joint Entrance Exam) (Advanced)

JEE (Joint Entrance Exam) Main 

Uttar Pradesh Technical University State Entrance Exam (UPSEE)

Lovely Professional University Entrance Exam

Academy of Maritime Education and

Type of specialisation available under this job profiles:

Some of the most popular Petroleum and Petrochemical Engineer specialisations are listed below. A student can choose the specialisations according to his/her interest in the particular field.

Natural Gas Engineer





One of the branches of petroleum engineering is natural gas engineering. The knowledge to develop a natural gas resource belongs to natural gas engineers.

Drilling Engineer



It is common knowledge that a drilling engineer is responsible for the oil well's drilling. The majority of drilling-related operations are also developed, planned, and overseen by a drilling engineer.

Production Engineer





All of the machinery used to drill the oil well is under the supervision of a production engineer. The production engineer is responsible for organising and maintaining the drilling machinery. Additionally, they choose surface machinery that separates the created fluids (oil, natural gas, and water).

Reservoir Engineer





The assessment of pockets of oil and gas is the responsibility of a reservoir engineer. The reservoir engineer is in charge of figuring out how much oil and gas is available and whether the location is suitable for digging a well.

Promotion and upgradation of this career profile:







Petroleum and Petrochemical Engineer follows a hierarchy of roles who are posted at different levels. They start from Junior Petroleum and Petrochemical Engineer and are promoted according to their experience in that particular field. 



Level 1: Junior Engineer: 

Petroleum engineers join as junior engineers at the entrance level. Typically, senior engineers or engineering managers are in charge of them. 

An engineer is capable of distinguishing between theoretical and practical knowledge at this level.

Level 2: Senior Engineer: 

Senior engineers are promoted from junior engineers after getting some practical experience and developing their theoretical understanding. 

Typically, autonomous tasks with more latitude are assigned to them.

Level 3: Engineering Managers: 

Senior engineers may eventually move to the position of engineering manager, overseeing a team of technicians and engineers and managing large projects. This position is crucial for directing a team to accomplish the objectives of the organisation.

Level 4: Sales Engineers: 

After attaining the necessary experience for the position, some engineers can also transition into the sales team. 

They explain the technical aspects of the product to customers using their engineering knowledge, and they assist with product planning, installation, and use.

Petroleum engineers' employment is anticipated to expand by 15% between 2016 and 2026, which is substantially faster than the average for all occupations. However, due to the modest size of the occupation, the rapid expansion will only create 4,900 new positions over the next ten years.

Jr. FREng is an American chemical engineer, scientist, businessman, inventor, and one of the twelve Institute Professors at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT).

title: Career profile Software Development Project Manager
summary: Name of the career profile: Software Development Project Manager
description:
Name of the career profile: Software Development Project Manager







An overview of career a profile:

The logistical parts of project management are under the control of a software development project manager.

They consult with many parties and carefully prepare the project. Project managers for software development also use their interpersonal abilities to motivate the team to achieve its objectives.



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Arrange for and oversee UAT support, data setup and migration, defect tracking using an ALM tool, and fix releases.







Oversee the creation of an internet portal with a full dynamic content base for member administration and profiling.







Complete database logical design, verification, and validation for oil tube production line's production managing software.







Oversee technical integration of massive relational databases, architecture, and systems for product fulfilment; directly answer to CTO.







Implement and uphold QA rules and development standards.



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Unlimited potential



Being able to create software for any industry in almost any situation is the finest part about being a software engineer.



Extremely profitable pay







For decades, the software business has expanded at an incredible rate, and there are no indicators that this growth will stop.



Regional independence



More and more businesses are accepting of remote employment, particularly since the COVID-19 pandemic. While some do have certain restrictions, you can still find something that will grant you the freedom you seek.





Ongoing education process



Software is intimately tied to technology, which means that it will continue to advance as technology does. As technology develops, you will learn new talents on a daily basis, which will enhance your capabilities and increase your versatility.



Great Demand





Students studying information technology and computer science have a wide range of prospects because of the technology sector's rapid expansion.





A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become Software Development Project Manager, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics.

Step-2: Prepare for the Entrance Exams such as JEE Main (Joint Entrance Exam), JEE Advanced (Joint Entrance Exam).

Step-3: Earn a 4 year of bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology or in any equivalent stream.

Step-5: Sit in College Campus for job opportunities or apply Off Campus.

Eligibility and skills requirements:



     Age Limit

There is no age limit for being a Software Development Project Manager.

You should pursue a degree and should have relevant skills.

     Educational qualifications

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Mathematics, physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished 4 years of undergraduate degree in Bachelor of Technology in streams such as Computer Science, Information Technology etc.

They ought to have finished 3 years of Master’s Degree in Master of Technology in any field.



     Skill requirements



 To become a successful Software Developer Project Manager, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Project Management



During the design, implementation, and testing of IT systems, including product installation and support, shown well-developed project management skills.

Software Development



The process through which programmers generate computer programmes is known as software development. A framework for creating applications that satisfy both user and technology needs is the software development life cycle (SDLC).

Web Services



Software that is accessible via the internet and makes use of a particular messaging platform is known as a web service. A type of service that is not restricted to a particular programming language or operating system is a web service.

Software Development Life Cycle



Software Development Life Cycle, or SDLC for short, is also known as Application Development Life Cycle in the world of software engineering.

Business Process



A business process is a collection of operations carried out by employees in order to produce a good product and offer it to clients.

Database



A database is a grouping of information and data that makes it simple to see, access, and manage. Databases can store a tonne of data and save a tonne of time.

Work environment















When compared to the ordinary career, working as a software engineer often requires higher levels of intelligence. This implies that it is necessary for software engineers to actively pursue fresh knowledge in their field and find solutions to challenging issues.

They generally work on their own computer system and they can work from anywhere, there is no need to go outside the home or office in order to complete tasks.



Entrance exam to follow the

title: Career profile template ACCOUNTANT .
summary: Career profile : Accountant
description:
Career profile : Accountant



 		





An overview of career as an accountant-

Who is an accountant:

An accountant is a person who keeps financial records on behalf of a firm, updates management on business performance, and generates financial statements.



Roles & responsibilities of an Accountant:



An accountant has to :



Manage all accounting transactions



An accountant is required to take into account all the transactions whether it be related to revenue or expenditure.



Prepare budget forecasts



This helps the business in funding the activities the company is looking forward to intake.It also prepares the owner with any kind of unexpected contingency.

Publish financial statements in time

The most crucial part of the job is to prepare the trading and profit and loss a/c and balance sheet on time. 



Reconcile accounts payable and receivable



Reconciliation helps in better management of the data which prevents the company from any kind of fraud.

Compute taxes and prepare tax returns

After the implementation of Goods & services tax and also Income tax return filling has become a mandatory job to perform as an accountant.The deductions under ITR is to be availed which requires proper awareness.



Report on the company’s financial health and liquidity

This report helps in building up the creditworthiness and goodwill of the company to which an accountant is associated with.

	



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career

Accountants build a better understanding of finances:

Finances are better managed when invested. An accountant becomes well versed with the market conditions in the ever-changing world scenario which helps him not only in building knowledge but also money.





The pay range for accountants is competitive  

With growing experience and skills an accountant can get into any private or public sector at a blink of an eye. Various international courses and passing of entrance exams may land an accountant into well-paying jobs anywhere in the world.





Can become an entrepreneur   

Start-up India,Skill India and digital India missions have provided major opportunities for budding entrepreneurs to build a market of their choiceBeing an accountant helps in getting investments as one has well equipped knowledge both theoretically and practically of how things work.



Recession proof job i.e. always in demand	 

           Keeping in mind the recent unprecedented crisis of COVID 19 the recession was not brought to this industry rather growth was seen.When people started to work from home this led to a reduction of expenses on part of the company. The accountant was still needed to maintain the accounts of the company to run well and hence,is a recession proof job.





Do not necessarily need a degree

The know-how of accounts and how to manage the finances is all that is required to become an accountant.A degree will only add on but will not be statutory.













A basic procedure to approach this job:





For becoming an accountant the procedure will be-



STEP 1:Complete 10th  and 12th  standard in any stream (Preferably accounts to be chosen as a subject)





STEP2:For admission to undergraduate courses in accounting, minimum educational qualification is 10+2 passed with commerce subjects like Accounts, Economics and Mathematics or pass entrance based exam.





STEP 3: For further certifications apply for exams conducted by ICAI(Institute of Chartered accountants of India) or give entrances conducted by various other private and government institutes.





STEP 4: Get into a company or business firm and start practicing the intended work.



Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



Eligibility criteria:

Age limit:

18 years to 27 years. 

The extendible age limit could be 40 years depending upon the basis of contract and requirement of the company.

Educational qualification:

Qualification: 10+2

 Stream: Commerce

 Total fees: Varies with the type of institute ranging from Rupees 20,000 to Rupees 2,95,000

Skills requirements:

Good written and verbal communication



Arrangement and focus on detail



Talents in analysis and problem-solving



Management of time



Systems research



Deductive and mathematical thinking



Critically analyzing



Active education



Legal expertise



Working environment:

Some accountants and auditors work from home, but most of them are office-based.

Operate independently for a large portion of the time

Accountants and auditors occasionally collaborate in teams. 

Auditors and accountants may make trips to their clients' offices.

Entrance exam to follow the route:





CUET (Common university entrance test)

Institute of Cost Accountant of India (ICAI)

The Institute of Chartered Financial Analysts of India (ICFAI)

Manage all accounting transactions

The following are the posts offered:

Finance Managers Accounting professionals who work as finance managers are in charge of a company's or organization's financial health.

Financial Controllers:

A senior-level executive known as a financial controller is in charge of accounting and supervises the creation of financial reports like balance sheets and income statements.

Financial Advisors:

A financial advisor is a specialist who offers knowledge for clients' financial, investment, and personal decision-making.

Directors (Finance):

Financial activity oversight, revenue reporting, accounting staff training, budgeting, fund distribution to departments, risk management, policy implementation, and process improvement are all responsibilities of the finance director.

Certified Public Accountant:

A label used to identify public accountants with US practice permits.

Chief Financial Officer:

The official title of the individual in charge of overseeing the business's financial operations and strategy.

Chartered Management Accountant:

Prepare, create, and analyse financial data for the management team of an organisation to utilise in making educated decisions that will support stability, growth, and profitability in the long run.

Company Secretary:

A business's efficient administration is the responsibility of the company secretary, who also ensures that the board of directors' decisions are carried out and that all legal and regulatory obligations are met.

Type of specialization available under this job profile:



Tax accountants

Tax accountants are often defined as accountants who have a focus on the laws, rules, and regulations governing the preparation and calculation of federal, state, and municipal taxes. Tax accountants offer both people and corporations a variety of tax-related services, which may include preparing and filing tax returns.

Cost accountants

Its responsibility is to accurately record all business expenses so that they can be used to enhance management.

Payroll clerks

The job of a payroll clerk is to process employee paychecks by gathering their information and timesheets.

Inventory accountants

The area of accounting that deals with estimating asset values and recording changes in inventories.

Billing clerks

A billing clerk is a specialist who is in charge of producing bills and credit memos, maintaining customer records, and reminding clients to make payments.

Collection clerks

The collections clerk is responsible for using a range of customer location strategies, collection techniques, legal claims, and the selective use of outside collection firms to recover the maximum amount of past-due money from customers.                  .

The payscale of an accountant depends on various factors like work experience,certifications,specialization,location and employment work.

An accountant can further get certifications in the following to get an edge over the profession:

Certified public accountant (CPA)

A label used to identify public accountants with US practice permits.

Certified management accountant (CMA)

Manages accounts for government,private businesses or public enterprises.

A bachelor's degree or an equivalent professional certification, along with two years of work experience in management accounting or financial management, are requirements for CMA certification. Additionally, CMA candidates must pass a two-part test.

Certified Internal Auditor (CIA)

The Institute of Internal Auditor awards the only globally recognised professional qualification for internal auditors.

Chartered financial analyst(CFA)

The certification, which is commonly regarded as the pinnacle of professional development in investment management, is given out by the CFA Institute.

Demands & Future Scope 







The demand for accountants will grow more than ever keeping in mind the geo political scenario all over the world.The future of this profession is bright keeping in mind the upgradation and advent of technologies.

Nearly 3 lakh chartered accountants (CAs) function as the financial advisors in a nation with 125 crore residents and 6.8 crore taxpayers in 2017–18. There are just 2.82 lakh CAs in India as of April 2018, and only 1.25 lakh of those members practise full-time, making up approximately. 44% of the whole power.





Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Kumar Mangalam Birla: 



Mr. Kumar Mangalam Birla is the Chairman of the Indian multinational Aditya Birla Group, which operates in 36 countries across six continents. He is a Chartered Accountant and holds an MBA degree from the London Business School.

Suresh Prabhu



Suresh Prabhu was the Government of India's Minister of Commerce, Industry, and Civil Aviation. He has previously managed a number of ministries including Railways, Environment, Power, and Heavy Industries.



Rakesh Jhunjhunwala



Rakesh Jhunjhunwala, a stock market legend and one of the wealthiest people in India, is also referred to as the "Warren Buffet of India." He has long been regarded as a trading master and an icon in the stock market.

Tony Fernandes





 He is the founder of Tune Air Sdn. Bhd. From Malaysia, who introduced the first budget no frills airline,Air Asia.



Naina Lal Kidwai



In the nation, Naina Lal Kidwai is a well-known chartered accountant. She was the Country Head of HSBC India and a Group General Manager. The Federation of Indian Chambers of Commerce and Industry was also led by her (FICCI).

title: Chemical Engineer
summary: An overview of career a profile-
description:
Chemical Engineer

 

An overview of career a profile-

What do we understand by the term ‘Chemical Engineer’?

The job of a chemical  engineer is to resolve problems in the field of chemical sciences  using principles and applications of theories of chemistry. 

Chemical Engineers invest their theoretical knowledge into practical use by investing and implementing ideas for the benefits of the society. 



What is the role of a ‘Chemical Engineer’?

The role of a chemical engineer is to make use of science and advanced technology in performing complex experiments in favour of human betterment.



Research

An engineer is required to do research and findings related to the concerned field about the new inventions and launches of the different gadgets and equipment. Before implementing the idea, an engineer is required to do the research about the same in all means, 



Develop

The development of the technical and chemical department lies in the hands of the engineer, it is important to cope up with the requirements and advancement of society on a daily basis, only then will any organisation grow. 







Management

Without proper management, there can be no efficiency and effective functioning, which is why it is also the job of senior executive engineers to keep a watch on the activities of the staff as per the demand of the organisation. 



Analyzation 



This role involve analysing the collected data samples and producing results 









 Benefits of choosing this profile as a career

Working for the social cause

Chemical engineers help society by reducing the production of chemicals and its remains.



Higher pay

There is a good amount of income generated by performing the roles and responsibilities of an automobile engineer.



Creativity alongside growth

Bringing forth new and original ideas in the field of development will definitely result in overall growth of the employee.





A perfect blend of engineering and medical sciences

Putting into use different theories and experiments of science and engineering into the healthcare brings out a beneficial blend of both 



   A basic procedure to approach this job:



Step 1 - The aspirant needs to complete class 10th and class 12th with Science stream.



Step 2 - Apply for the admission process into different colleges and universities.

 

Step 3 - Proceed by appearing for the entrance examination meant for Chemical Engineering.



Step 4 - Appear for the entrance exam meant for chemical engineering , i.e. MH CET, JEE Main, BITSAT, VITEEE, KIITEE and COMEDK UGET. Complete the 3-year long chemical engineering course.



Step 5 -  After the clarification of required documents and passing the examination, the candidate will get admission into the institution as per the preference. 



Step 6 - Complete the 3-year long graduation course in microbiology.



Step 7 - Once the candidate has a required graduation degree, the candidate can now practise by applying for various internship programs. 

   Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :

 

Age limit- 

In order to be eligible for pursuing chemical engineering, it is important for the candidate to be of the age group - 17 years to 22 years for getting admissions into the graduation courses.

Education qualification-

The candidate must have cleared 10+2 from a recognised board of education with Science stream.

The candidate must have scored an aggregate of 50%  in all the subjects.

The candidate should have either a Diploma in Chemical Engineering or a graduation degree in Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) Chemical Engineering. The candidate can also pursue B.Tech in Chemical Engineering.

Post graduation in MS (Chemical Engineering) is also beneficial in becoming a Chemical Engineer. 

Skills Required-



To become a Chemical Engineer, it is very much important for the candidate to be able to have the following set if skills which will help in working efficiently and effectively -

Good knowledge of concerned subject-



Chemistry as a subject is full of possibilities and experimentation, thus having a good hold on the subject and its implications is very essential.



Ability to solve and reason the problem-



Problem solving attitude is a win-win scenario for any organisation to look for in their employees.



Good communication skills-



He or she should have a good hold on communication and should be able to convey his or her ideas in a comprehensive manner.



Analytical Skills-



Analysing and processing the data in a productive manner helps in resolving the task at the earliest.



Working Environment -



Chemical engineers work mostly in offices or laboratories. They may spend time at industrial plants, refineries, and other locations, where they monitor or direct operations or solve onsite problems. Nearly all chemical engineers work full time.

 Entrance exam to follow the route:



 The following is the list of entrance exams to get admissions into colleges

title: Chief Medical Officer
summary: Name of the Career profile: Chief Medical Officer [CMO]
description:
Name of the Career profile: Chief Medical Officer [CMO]







Overview 

A CMO is a senior government official who runs the medical department of a government hospital. Posts are occupied by senior physicians who act as consultants, direct and supervise medical professionals, physician groups, and research public health issues.

Oversee doctors and manage healthcare programs at medical facilities. Attend meetings with hospital boards and management to discuss improvements, policies, procedures, or other hospital issues.



Roles and responsibilities of an Chief Medical Officer 

A chief medical officer directs and oversees medical activities and the professionals who carry them out on behalf of the entity for which he or she works, such as a hospital or the government.



Chief medical officers are in charge of creating new strategies and initiating projects .They also do research on current clinical and patient care trends in order to improve existing procedures.





Chief medical officers  effectively manage their medical team and give internal organisational assistance.



They seek to design innovative tactics that will increase patient happiness, medical outreach, development, strategic and operational direction, and so on, while also maintaining medical facility profitability.





     ➢ Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



High-paying jobs-Chief Medical officers get high payment as they achieve this position after many achievements and good work in their field.



Job stability- Their job is not going anywhere because hospitals do need someone qualified for their management and examination

.

Continuous education- Being in a medical field is a great opportunity to always learn.As there are always unknown challenges which are also great lessons.



Gain respect- Being on such a high post is a great respect and also  you and your suggestion are valued by the people.



A basic procedure to become a Chief Medical Officer



       STEP:1 After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology



       STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English as a     

        compulsory subjects so that you can become eligible for MBBS.



       STEP:3 Complete your MBBS.



       STEP:4 Get a Doctorate.



  STEP:5 Obtain a practice licence from the appropriate state government. Work at a hospital, either        public or private. You will be promoted to the position of CMO with time and excellent achievement.

       

 STEP 6:(Optional)Master of health administration (MHA) or Master of business administration   (MBA) advantageous



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment



          ELIGIBILITY 



         Age Limit-  Above 18 years old to be a Chief Medical officer o



Education Qualification- A candidate should have a Medical degree.Valid medical license to   practice.





         SKILLS REQUIRED

full_content:
Name of the Career profile: Chief Medical Officer [CMO]







Overview 

A CMO is a senior government official who runs the medical department of a government hospital. Posts are occupied by senior physicians who act as consultants, direct and supervise medical professionals, physician groups, and research public health issues.

Oversee doctors and manage healthcare programs at medical facilities. Attend meetings with hospital boards and management to discuss improvements, policies, procedures, or other hospital issues.



Roles and responsibilities of an Chief Medical Officer 

A chief medical officer directs and oversees medical activities and the professionals who carry them out on behalf of the entity for which he or she works, such as a hospital or the government.



Chief medical officers are in charge of creating new strategies and initiating projects .They also do research on current clinical and patient care trends in order to improve existing procedures.





Chief medical officers  effectively manage their medical team and give internal organisational assistance.



They seek to design innovative tactics that will increase patient happiness, medical outreach, development, strategic and operational direction, and so on, while also maintaining medical facility profitability.





     ➢ Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



High-paying jobs-Chief Medical officers get high payment as they achieve this position after many achievements and good work in their field.



Job stability- Their job is not going anywhere because hospitals do need someone qualified for their management and examination

.

Continuous education- Being in a medical field is a great opportunity to always learn.As there are always unknown challenges which are also great lessons.



Gain respect- Being on such a high post is a great respect and also  you and your suggestion are valued by the people.



A basic procedure to become a Chief Medical Officer



       STEP:1 After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology



       STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English as a     

        compulsory subjects so that you can become eligible for MBBS.



       STEP:3 Complete your MBBS.



       STEP:4 Get a Doctorate.



  STEP:5 Obtain a practice licence from the appropriate state government. Work at a hospital, either        public or private. You will be promoted to the position of CMO with time and excellent achievement.

       

 STEP 6:(Optional)Master of health administration (MHA) or Master of business administration   (MBA) advantageous



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment



          ELIGIBILITY 



         Age Limit-  Above 18 years old to be a Chief Medical officer o



Education Qualification- A candidate should have a Medical degree.Valid medical license to   practice.





         SKILLS REQUIRED

Communication abilities- speak respectfully with patients, staff and junior doctors so that they can feel connected to the Chief Medical Officer.



leadership abilities- In order to lead a team of doctors and medical personnel or taff they need to have leadership skills .



Decision-making abilities -They are required as they need to make swift decisions for the benefit of the hospital and the patients.



Management abilities -In order to run the hospital in a better way they should also have great management skills to make the hospital or healthcare a successful brand.





              WORKING ENVIRONMENT



Chief medical officers can work in almost any hospital. This may include everything from hospitals to public urgent care centres and government-sponsored long-term care institutions.

Demands and Future Scope 







Due to the pandemic, businesses that provide services to persons and consumers as well as to them were forced to prioritise their customers' health and well-being. The function of the chief medical officer (CMO)  has long been crucial in managing patient safety for businesses in the healthcare and life sciences sectors. 

 Many  firms have expanded the function of their CMO to include contributing to strategy, interacting with regulators, and influencing internal rules and culture in response to the COVID-19 epidemic.

From 2016 to 2021, Whitty held the positions of Director of the National Institute for Health and Care Research (NIHR) and Chief Scientific Advisor (CSA) to the Department of Health and Social Care. From 2017 to 2018, he also served as the government's acting chief scientific adviser.



Gregor Smith



 Smith, a medical school graduate from the University of Glasgow, practised general medicine in Larkhall. Prior to being named Deputy Chief Medical Officer for Scotland in 2015, he was the Director for Primary Care at NHS Lanarkshire.

title: Company Secretary
summary: Name of the career profile: COMPANY SECRETARY
description:
Name of the career profile: COMPANY SECRETARY



Overview:







Who is a Company Secretary ?



Company Secretary is a career profile which works in any company whose job is to keep the legal affairs, Accounts  and administration in order.

Company Secretary is a member of ICSI ( Institute of Company Secretaries of India).

A company secretary is the one who ensures that the decision taken by the board of directors is according to the requirements of statutory and regulatory norms.





Roles and responsibilities of a Company Secretary -



Advising Company on legal disputes is one of the main roles and duties of a CS . They have to take care of  all the legal parts of the company. They provide suggestions and solutions in company cases according to the laws under companies act.



              



To advise the company on their legal and financial risks. They advise the board of directors on what to do and what not to do. This helps them to make decisions with keeping in mind the legal things and laws.



             



Do Company Compliance with Statutory regulations. This means that they need to incorporate the legal restrictions in the company's statutory regulations so that there does not occur any problem in future regarding the violation of laws.



              



 Make arrangements for a meeting for the Promoters. Promoters are the one who gives an idea for starting a business. So he/she is a very important part of a company and also there are so many agreements with the promoters so CS needs to arrange meetings with them for decision making



              



 Prepare a Statutory report and file the copy of such report with the registrar of the Company. This is required for the registration of the company and this needs to be very accurate so this is all done by the company secretary appointed by the company.



           



 To take the required permission from the board and various government bodies. This includes taking permission for different grants and preparing documents related to and also for launching any new product, proper documentation with all the information is prepared by the CS.

              

To give guidance about the Tax and other financial things. Here the CA and CS work together for the betterment and financial stability of the company

             





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career -





 Help to better understand Company law.

CS helps the company to have knowledge about the legal formalities and law they need to abide too. They make them aware about the rules and regulations they need to follow. Therefore, CS help them to prevent legal problems

.

             



 No age restriction

There is no age restriction for giving exams or anything so this is the biggest advantage of being an CS or trying to be an CS.



             







It helps to earn higher income

It is a financially rewarding job and they enjoy a high salary. They can live a good lifestyle with the salary they get. Also, they get other benefits from the company. 

             



Provide respect and value in society.





The job gives you immense respect and value in the society because first, It is too hard to become a Company Secretary so it automatically becomes a respected job.Second the value and the work is very much needed to make a better society to live.





 Higher job satisfaction



 

After working so hard and showing dedication towards the goal , becoming a Company secretary gives a lot of job satisfaction and also the work they do is legal work so there is no dishonest or illegal thing in it. Also the salary they get is satisfying.



              



Connection with Higher Authorities.

The company secretary has to work mainly with the board of directors and other higher authorities. Therefore, the status of a Company Secretary is very high.



            





How to become a Company Secretary -









Step -1 Complete class 10th and choose any stream after it but commerce stream gives more benefits to this job.



For doing CS there are no colleges.



There is 2 route for doing CS- 







First is after 12th and second is after graduation . Those who did their graduation there is no need to give CSEET.



Step 2- Complete class 12th and register yourself for CSEET from ICSI website. (icsi.edu.in)

  

Step 3- After Clearing Cseet or graduation  do register yourself for Cs Executive .



Step 4- After Clearing Cs Executive  you have to register yourself For CS professional.



Step 5- After clearing Cs Executive you have an option to appear for internship or you have an option to appear for internship under any practicing company after clearing all the levels.



Step 6 - Internship period is 21 months .



After completing all the processes you become a Company Secretary .





Eligibility



To become a company secretary one should have completed class 12th in any stream from a recognized school.

                

Age Requirement -

Minimum ag

Step -1 Complete class 10th and choose any stream after it but commerce stream gives more benefits to this job.



For doing CS there are no colleges.



There is 2 route for doing CS- 







First is after 12th and second is after graduation . Those who did their graduation there is no need to give CSEET.



Step 2- Complete class 12th and register yourself for CSEET from ICSI website. (icsi.edu.in)

  

Step 3- After Clearing Cseet or graduation  do register yourself for Cs Executive .



Step 4- After Clearing Cs Executive  you have to register yourself For CS professional.



Step 5- After clearing Cs Executive you have an option to appear for internship or you have an option to appear for internship under any practicing company after clearing all the levels.



Step 6 - Internship period is 21 months .



After completing all the processes you become a Company Secretary .





Eligibility



To become a company secretary one should have completed class 12th in any stream from a recognized school.

                

Age Requirement -

Minimum age required for doing Cs is 17 years and there is no maximum age restriction. 

  



Qualities and Skill requirements:







 Command Over languages.

Company secretaries should have command over languages, especially English, because they have to communicate with higher authorities and clients. Therefore good communication skills are required.



              





 Knowledge of Company law.

Company Secretary should have knowledge of all the company laws because it is their main work and specially they should have the knowledge of the Companies Act. They should remember everything about it.

 





General Knowledge

They should have general knowledge related to their field specifically. Like which laws were there and are any amendments in it etc.

            



 Knowledge of Accounting and Taxation.

Company secretaries need to have basic knowledge of taxation and accounting because they need to keep some of the records of taxation.



            





 Organizational Skill 

Company Secretary needs to have organizational skills because they need to do so many things at a time and organizing different documents with the task to be done is required. Otherwise, it may create a problem for them.

             



Stock Market Knowledge

Company secretaries should have knowledge of the stocks and stock markets. They have to advise the company on what to do with stocks and how to manage their finances in general. Also, if the company is registered in the stock market, everyday analysis and knowledge is required. 

            







Working environment



The working environment of the Company secretary is in the office or the cabin provided by the company.They have to work with the board of directors, the clients and Chartered Accountants for legal and financial management.

                                

They have work load on the basis of the time period or the requirements of the work they have to complete,





Entrance Exam to become a Company Secretary



The entrance exam of the company secretary is based on the different levels called Foundation, Executive and Professional.



Level of examination

Company Secretary Executive Entrance Test(CSEET)

Company Secretary Executive

Company Secretary Professional

And the fees are only exam fees , the coaching fees are different.





Top institutes for company secretary

Types of Specializations available for Company Secretary



Banking Law and Practice



In this the company secretary works and guides the banks about their laws and legal things they need to abide by. Basically, they specialize in the laws of the banking sector or financial institutions and work in that sector.





Insurance Law and Practice



The company secretary specializes in the laws of the insurance companies. For example, there are guidelines related to claims and risk management of the Insurance company, the company secretary helps to understand all those laws.They then work in insurance sector.



            



Intellectual Property Rights Law and Practice



In this the company secretary specializes in the aws related to intellectual property rights. They work in a rights management company to make rules and regulations and policies of the company according to it.







International Business Laws and Practice



In this the company secretary specializes in the aws related to international business. They work as an import - export company to make rules and regulations and policies of the company according to it.

title: Computational Physicist
summary: Name of the Career Profile : COMPUTATIONAL PHYSICISTS
description:
Name of the Career Profile : COMPUTATIONAL PHYSICISTS

                    

                                                       

                                                        



Overview

Computational physicists are one who study scientific problems which are solved with the help of Computational skills.

It combines mainly physics, computer science and mathematics for deriving solutions for scientific problems.

This field uses different programming languages of computer science to solve the scientific equations and analyze the data.



     



   Roles and Responsibilities

To conduct experiments related to the field of mathematics and physics. It includes doing research with the existing theories.

      



To use computer models to redevelop the quantitative theories of physics and use computer science with physics.

      



To create new algorithms for new upgraded technology and make tasks easier or automatic.

     

     



To write research reports related to the field by collecting data and samples and analyzing them with the help of a computer.

                 

                 



To use different programming language of computer science like Python, R etc for solving all mathematical, physical and statistical problems.



               





Benefits of choosing this career profile



This career has a lot of job opportunities because this is an emerging career and we know that the future is in technology so this brings many job vacancies.

                       



The study of this field is acceptable all over the world. So if one pursue computational physics in India he or she can get a job anywhere in the world on the basis of performance.

               



Anyone having interest in this three subjects, mathematics, Physics and computer can do best in this field and make a good career.



                        



It is a highly paid and reputed job in India. And there is high growth in this career because of its wide scope.



                 



A basic procedure to approach this job



                         

                        Complete class 10







                      Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 50% in science stream with            

                      mathematics, chemistry and physics from any recognized school.

                       

                                              

                      Complete graduation in Physics, Mathematics, or Computer( B.Sc)from a                      



                     recognized  College.



                                  Candidates can also do master’s program(B.Sc) in the same field of undergraduate        

                                 from a recognized college



                                  After this they can apply for jobs according to their field.





Eligibility and skills required:



Age limit:

There is no age limit to pursue this course



              Educational Qualifications

The candidate must have passed 12th class in science with mathematics stream from any recognized school.

Candidates must have completed undergraduate degree in B.Sc. in Physics or Mathematics or related fields.

Candidates can also do post-graduate in M.Sc. in physics or mathematics.

MATLAB

Algorithms and Applied mathematics

This job requires an advanced level of mathematical skills and knowledge of applying them. Because they have to make algorithms , therefore knowledge of probability, statistics and all is necessary.



Communication skills:

Communication with other people in the same field is very necessary because  all have to share relevant information to each other. Also, the designers and engineers have to communicate their ideas and designs to clients so this skill is required.

Computational physicists must have problem solving skills because the main task of their job is to solve scientific problems with the help of computer science, physics and mathematics.



Algorithm and Applied math

Knowing how to develop algorithms is an important skill because it is used in problem solving and in making new techniques. Applied mathematics knowledge includes differentiation, probability etc.

They should also have knowledge about softwares like UNIX, MATLAB, etc.

Computational physicists usually work in offices with computers and with all the software.

Name of the Career Profile : COMPUTER SCIENTIST

                    

                                              

                                                        



Overview

Computer scientists are the ones who research, examine and analyze how computers work like how they store information and how they use them.

As the name suggests in itself a scientist, it means researching new things about computers or discovering something new about the things related to computers. For example, new software in computers or new programming languages etc.

Basically, their work includes research, experiment and applying their knowledge for developing new computers.



                        



          Roles and Responsibilities

To create and revise new algorithms and computer programming languages or codes.

                    



To research and design new computer systems for upgradation.



                 



To improve the efficiency and speed of computers by designing new software and repairing the existing hardware .



                                         

To work with computer engineers and software developers to make new technologies.

  

                                      

To educate other professionals in the field of training or by joining in any university.





                                   





Benefits of choosing this career profile

Every business and industry needs new technologies for them so computer science is one of those career profiles which will be the future. So, there is a great need for computer scientists.



                



The career profile of a computer scientist is financially rewarding and has many perks coming along with it.

                         



There is a sense of job security in this field and the opportunities are also very high.



                               

It is a technical career but if one has interest in research and technology then this is the best career for them because this is the best combination for the future of the world.

                          Complete class 10







                      Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 50% in science stream with

They should also have knowledge about software like UNIX, MATLAB, etc.

title: Copy of OBSTETRICIAN
summary: Name of the Career Profile:Obstetrician
description:
Name of the Career Profile:Obstetrician



 

➢    Overview 

An obstetrician or a doctor specialises in pregnancy, childbirth, and also woman's reproductive system. 

They also take care of their patients throughout their pregnancy, and also gives necessary follow-up care.



Roles & responsibilities of an Obstetrician

       

Monitoring the health of their patients and their babies with a lot of care because they are very sensitive.



Advising patients about required medications, diet, exercises etc so that they could look after them and take care of them in a better way



Answering the questions which are asked by patients regarding pregnancy and the baby to make them aware.



Evaluating baby’s growth, size etc by performing different tests and make sure that baby’s health is fit and fine.



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



An obstetrician’s job growth rate is high as they are in huge demand. The employment rate of obstetricians thus as pointed out as pointed out by US Bureau of Labor Statistics Is expected to to grow by 10.1% between the year 2020 and 2030.



They earn a decent amount of money. The average base salary of an obstetrician in India is Rs 31 lakhs per year.

            

They have a career of opportunities like for example they can become a gynaecologist, reproductive endocrinologists etc. 





They make an impact on the lives of many women by taking care of their patients throughout their pregnancy, and also giving necessary follow-up care.



A basic procedure to apply for this job:

 

To become an obstetrician, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10.

Step-2:  Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 50% in science stream from any recognized board to get a seat in a college.

Step-3:  Complete Graduation in medical field from the recognized university by Medical Council of India (MCI).

Step-4: Apply for jobs or complete Masters degree in medical field

Step 5-Candidates can either go for PHD courses for further research or apply for jobs.

Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

              Age limit:

●       There is no such age limit for an individual to go for the obstetrician as a career.

              Educational qualifications:

●    A student must have passed class 12 in science stream with at least 50% aggregate from any recognized board.



Qualify entrance exams like NEET, AIIMS CET  etc in order to get a seat in college.



Candidates must complete undergraduate courses in the medical field from the recognized university by Medical Council of India (MCI) like for example MBBS which will take 5.5 years or BSC. in Gynaecology and Obstetrics which will take 3 years.





Complete can apply for jobs or pursue Masters [M.S. in Obstetrics and Gynaecology] , after securing 50% in MBBS. This course will take 2 years.

Candidates can either go for PHD courses for further research or apply for jobs.

              Skill requirements:

To become an obstetrician,  a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities:

Good communication skills in order to advise patients on required medications, diet, exercises and also to answer the questions which are asked by patients regarding pregnancy and the baby.





Good listening skills in order to understand their patient’s problems and then deal with them effectively.



Good problem solving skills in order to analyse issues of their patients carefully and then provide treatment plans for the correct diagnosis.  



Good analytical skills in order to critically analyse issues of their patients. 







              Work environment:

        

Obstetricians work in hospitals, clinics, surgical centres etc, taking care of their patients throughout their pregnancy, and also giving  necessary follow-up care.





Entrance Exams in order to pursue career as a Obstetrician

Below given are some of the top entrance exams to pursue career obstetrician

National Eligibility Entrance Test [NEET]

Amrita Institute of Medical Sciences Common Entrance Test [AIIMS CET]

All India Pre Medical Test [AIPMT]

Jawaharlal Institute of Postgraduate Medical Education and Research (JIPMER) Entrance Test



Courses To Become an Obstetrician

●        Undergraduate courses to become an Obstetrician

The course mentioned below can be pursued right after completing 12:

INR  50,000 to 5,00,000.

Types Of Specialisations Available For a Obstetrician

Some of the most popular obstetric specialisations are listed below. 

Gynaecolgist

Reproductive endocrinologist

Gynaecological Surgeon

Gynaecological Oncologist



These doctors treat cancerous tissues of their patients by performing surgery, prescribing therapy, and  other medicines.

Obstetricians have various scopes in different fields and follow a hierarchy.  There are different levels of quality assurance engineers which are as follows: 





LEVEL-1: Interns









Interns signify that they are recent graduates of obstetric courses in order to pursue a career as an obstetrician. Internships  elevate the understanding of an individual and create a real life working environment for the individuals. 







LEVEL - 2 : Junior Obstetrician







They work both for experience and job promotion. They assist the senior obstetrician in order to monitor the health of their patients and their babies, writing reports etc. 



LEVEL - 3 : Senior Obstetrician







They give managerial tasks to the junior obstetricians along with other tasks that include taking  care of their patients throughout their pregnancy, advising patients about required medications, diet, exercises, monitoring the health of their patients and their babies etc.

title: Corporate Lawyer
summary: Name of the career profile: Corporate Lawyer
description:
Name of the career profile: Corporate Lawyer





An overview of career a profile:



Corporate lawyers are legal practitioners who specialise in corporate law.

Corporate lawyers are involved with firms and corporations including checking the legality of all documents .

They also represent the company in case of any legal disputes and guide the company to avoid any problems. 



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Representation of clients in court

Corporate lawyers are responsible in case their firm gets into any legal disputes such as the merger or dissolution of companies. They are also responsible for defending their clients in case of any lawsuits such as accusations by other partners or third partners.







Drafting documents 

A practice common to all branches of law, a corporate lawyer is responsible for the creation of the necessary documentation regarding any activities related to their domain. This includes lawsuits and merger and disposition letters. 





Raising equity

They are responsible for raising money for operations in a firm by suggesting which shares to sell, if any sacking of employees should be done and any changes in the past structure. 







Legal Research 

Since business and corporate law can undergo massive changes depending on the policies of the government it's the job of the corporate lawyer to research these laws to make sure that the company is adhering to the policies without breaking any legal guidelines.







Developing Company policy

Corporate lawyers advise the various branches of the company how to operate while keeping profits high and costs low. They also give advice on how to treat employees and if they are breaking any rules.







Protecting the company from risks

Corporate lawyers are also protecting the intellectual and legal rights and properties of a firm from risks such as fraud, poaching , copyright violations,etc. 







Helping during the dissolution of companies

When a company decides to break away or a divorce happens, corporate lawyers help in determining the settlement amount and what portion of it is applicable to be taxed. 







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Stability in career

Corporate law is one of the most stable branches of law. This is due to their nature as permanent employees and intimate association with the corporate. This means that as long as the company stays afloat , a corporate lawyer gets job security.





Many different job opportunities

There are a variety of different job roles available under corporate law. This is due to the fact that corporate law covers various different niches of business and the corporate world. This means there are a lot of different roles available. 







Collaborative Nature 

Corporate lawyers generally have to work with a variety of different people ranging from employees, shareholders,board of directors. In addition to this they also work closely with the finance and legal department of the company. Those who want variety at work it's perfect.





A very good package 

Corporate lawyers are subjective to get lucrative salary packages, because they are highly in demand by providing their services to a company which is entirely dependent on them. another reason is the

shortage of people in this job profile. 





Developing leadership skills

Since in this job profile you'll be working with the top brass of the company and your involvement in the progress of a company, you'll have a chance to develop great leadership skills. This also builds your confidence by knowing your decisions have lasting results. 





Fast paced work

Corporate world is a very fast paced world and thus your work as a corporate lawyer will be full of exciting and fast work too which adds to a very thrilling experience. This is ideal for people who don't want a boring work environment. 



A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a corporate lawyer follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 in any stream like arts, science or commerce.

Step-2: Complete an integrated course in Law such as BA.LLB or B.Com LLB. 

Step 3: Alternatively you can complete a 2 year diploma in corporate law and then complete your bachelor's in law

Step-4: Appear for the Bar exam so that you can start your practice as an advocate. 

Step-5: Now you can start private practice or apply at law firms.

Eligibility and skills requirements:

Age Limit

The age limit set by the Bar council of India is 45 years. 

You should pursue a degree in order to get licensed.



Educational qualifications 

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Science, Mathematics, Biology,  physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished their undergraduate degree in corporate law

Finished their internship and should have received their licence.



 Skill requirements

To become a successful corporate Lawyer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.





Business

An overview of career a profile:



Corporate lawyers are legal practitioners who specialise in corporate law.

Corporate lawyers are involved with firms and corporations including checking the legality of all documents .

They also represent the company in case of any legal disputes and guide the company to avoid any problems. 



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Representation of clients in court

Corporate lawyers are responsible in case their firm gets into any legal disputes such as the merger or dissolution of companies. They are also responsible for defending their clients in case of any lawsuits such as accusations by other partners or third partners.







Drafting documents 

A practice common to all branches of law, a corporate lawyer is responsible for the creation of the necessary documentation regarding any activities related to their domain. This includes lawsuits and merger and disposition letters. 





Raising equity

They are responsible for raising money for operations in a firm by suggesting which shares to sell, if any sacking of employees should be done and any changes in the past structure. 







Legal Research 

Since business and corporate law can undergo massive changes depending on the policies of the government it's the job of the corporate lawyer to research these laws to make sure that the company is adhering to the policies without breaking any legal guidelines.

Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Stability in career

Corporate law is one of the most stable branches of law. This is due to their nature as permanent employees and intimate association with the corporate. This means that as long as the company stays afloat , a corporate lawyer gets job security.

shortage of people in this job profile. 





Developing leadership skills

Since in this job profile you'll be working with the top brass of the company and your involvement in the progress of a company, you'll have a chance to develop great leadership skills. This also builds your confidence by knowing your decisions have lasting results. 





Fast paced work

Corporate world is a very fast paced world and thus your work as a corporate lawyer will be full of exciting and fast work too which adds to a very thrilling experience. This is ideal for people who don't want a boring work environment. 



A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a corporate lawyer follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 in any stream like arts, science or commerce.

Step-2: Complete an integrated course in Law such as BA.LLB or B.Com LLB. 

Step 3: Alternatively you can complete a 2 year diploma in corporate law and then complete your bachelor's in law

Step-4: Appear for the Bar exam so that you can start your practice as an advocate. 

Step-5: Now you can start private practice or apply at law firms.

Eligibility and skills requirements:

Age Limit

The age limit set by the Bar council of India is 45 years. 

You should pursue a degree in order to get licensed.



Educational qualifications 

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Science, Mathematics, Biology,  physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished their undergraduate degree in corporate law

Finished their internship and should have received their licence.



 Skill requirements

To become a successful corporate Lawyer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.





Business Awareness



It goes without saying, anyone who wants to become a corporate lawyer ought to have excellent business awareness since all of their work will revolve around corporate policies, public image and profits and costs. Due to this they should be aware how a corporation works and how a good one is maintained. 

Negotiation skills



You'll have to be very good at negotiations since you'll be dealing with a variety of cases that are based on money such as legal settlements. This is because no company wants legal issues and if possible always solve any lawsuits out of court with monetary or other means. 

Understanding of company policies



A corporate lawyer is responsible for many major decisions that are made such as handling of documentation, maintaining the public image of the company, etc. To make sure all these duties are effectively done, the lawyer should know the company policies without fail so as to base companies decisions on that. 

Great communication skills



Just like any other branch of law, a corporate lawyer should be able to communicate the facts and their views effectively to other parties. They should also be able to persuade the others to think about their proposals as well as show assertiveness while presenting their side of the argument 

Legal and business ethics



As a lawyer you'll be many times be involved in legal issues many times where ethics might be ignored got a bigger cut off profit. In such situations you should be responsible for keeping business and legal ethics safe from exploitation. 

Through legal knowledge



A corporate lawyer needs to be totally aware of the various laws and should be completely accustomed to legal procedures. They should also be up to date with any law changes 

Work environment



Most corporate lawyers have a very flexible work environment which generally only demands them to go to office when they have a case or a client requires their services.

While a lawyer can be subject to much travel depending on getting clues for their cases, generally these cases are far and few between and they don't have to travel much.

Annual board meetings and any such events where decisions regarding the company takes place, a corporate lawyer has to be present. . 



Entrance exam to follow the route:



There are different entrance exams available to pursue corporate law as a career option. Below given are some of the entrance exams:

AIBE (All India Bar Exam)
