Type of specialisation available under this job profiles:

Some of the most specialisations available under corporate law are:

Legal Advisor



The role of a legal advisor is to assist managers and directors through the process of making company laws while also helping them navigate through the legal front of their corporation such as lawsuits, etc. 

Legal administrator





A legal administrator generally works at a branch level and is responsible for maintaining smooth operation of the branch and to oversee that all government and employment policies are being followed correctly. In case of any law changes, they change the company policies according to that. 

Corporate events associate



The responsibility of a Corporate associate is mostly only during corporate events. They are responsible to make sure that no legal troubles are created during these events and in case of any disputes, they try to resolve them by negotiation or settlements, failing which a lawsuit is lodged. 



Oath commissioners



An oath commissioner is responsible for maintaining the affidavits of a corporation. They are appointed by the chief justice and they make sure that all the oaths taken are fair and true by corresponding information that proves the corporation's claim. 



Judge



A lawyer who has passed the bar council exam can further try to become a judge by giving the examination for it. A judge presides over cases related to corporate lawsuits in the civil court. 

Solicitor 



A solicitor is basically the chief lawyer of a corporation and is the one who generally represents the defendant in the court.

Promotion and upgradation of this career profile:



Generally most corporate  lawyers start as interns in law firms. In that position, the truth under senior attorneys. Once they gain sufficient experience, they become partners.

Once becoming partners they work on private cases where they get interns of their own. 

In corporate settings the lawyers first become associates, then advisors and solicitors.

Finally they've a chance to become a chief counsel.

Due to the growing number of job opportunities and the rise in popularity, it's recommended to join this job profile. 



Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:..

Bahram Vakil



Bahram N. Vakil, one of the founding partners of AZB & Partners, is a renowned restructuring, infrastructure, and project finance lawyer in India and has been recognised as a top project finance lawyer by the majority of international magazines for over ten years.

Ajay Bahl



Ajay Bahl is a senior partner at AZB & Partners at the Delhi office.He has been connected to a number of significant, ground-breaking tax judgements, including a recently published case involving a US satellite business in which the Indo-US Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement was carefully examined and set apart from domestic law.

Zia is a leading corporate lawyer in India and a founding partner of AZB & Partners. Zia is a corporate attorney and entrepreneur from India. She is regarded as an expert in the fields of securities law, project financing, private equity, and corporate merger and acquisition law.



Shardul Suresh Shroff

Cyril Shroff

Engineering Physicist provides the skills and experience needed to new technology and engineering projects.

Engineering Physicist supplemented with technical experiences as well as protected time for technical work terms.

Engineering Physicists are the ones who work in office settings and labs.

Electronics and computer technologies:



They develop electronics and computer technologies such as designing amplifiers, logic circuits and detectors.  



Lasers and optics 



They develop prototype systems related to lasers and optics such as nonlinear optics, photonics. 



Measures the environmental sciences 



They  measure the techniques related to environmental sciences such as pollution detection and control, and climatology. 



Instrumentation and control systems:

Top Inspiring Engineering Physicist:

title: ENT copy copy
summary: Career profile: ENT specialist
description:
Career profile: ENT specialist



Overview:

Who is an E.N.T specialist?



E.N.T stands for ear, nose and throat and an ENT specialist specialises in all these fields.





 An ENT treats a person’s smell or taste problems, sleeping issues, hearing, balance disorders and treats everything related to neck, head and ears.



What kind of career profile is an ENT specialist?



An otolaryngologist or an ENT specialist is a surgeon who treats diseases affecting the senses. 

They also treat problems such as swallowing and breathing. 

They might also treat head and neck tumours. 



Roles and responsibilities of an ENT specialist:



Examining

The main responsibility of an ENT specialist is to examine and diagnose the problem or disease the patient is going through in order to suggest the best possible treatment or medications for it.



Performing surgery

Career profile: ENT specialist



Overview:

Who is an E.N.T specialist?



E.N.T stands for ear, nose and throat and an ENT specialist specialises in all these fields.





 An ENT treats a person’s smell or taste problems, sleeping issues, hearing, balance disorders and treats everything related to neck, head and ears.



What kind of career profile is an ENT specialist?



An otolaryngologist or an ENT specialist is a surgeon who treats diseases affecting the senses. 

They also treat problems such as swallowing and breathing. 

They might also treat head and neck tumours. 



Roles and responsibilities of an ENT specialist:



Examining

The main responsibility of an ENT specialist is to examine and diagnose the problem or disease the patient is going through in order to suggest the best possible treatment or medications for it.



Performing surgery



Sometimes the patients might not feel better by just medications and in situations like these the ENT surgeons have to perform surgeries to make the situation better.



Surgical assistance 



These specialists have to give surgical assistance and advice and support their staff or juniors in their surgeries.



Treating patients



Treating patients with throat, nose, ear or any kind of neck troubles or many more kinds of diseases and disorders are their one of the main roles.









Prescribing suited medications















Examining patients and suggesting to them the best medication for the problem they’re facing so they get as better as possible and as soon as possible.





Benefits for choosing ENT specialist as a career profile:



High paying salary





With such a lengthy and tough way of studying ENT specialists earn a very good amount of money, their pay scale is more than most of the professions and serves them very lavishly. 



Respected career



As a doctor, they treat many patients and make their lives better in very even ways. Their career and degree deserve equal appreciation because they undergo so many years of education and work for so many extra hours for the general public, this makes their profile respected and honoured.



Always in demand



Unfortunately people can stop never falling sick and their job remains intact forever. This way they can never run out of jobs, we need doctors at every point of our lives and that always keeps them in demand and never off the market.



Chance to help people 





Being an ENT specialist you come across many patients and get the opportunity to treat them and make them believe this world is a better place to live. Your life contributes to caring for patients from all the age groups and helping them get better.



Variety of work and career options:



Being an ENT specialist brings you a variety of work and a variety of people to serve, this brings enough career options to switch or continue and educate people with your precious knowledge.











How to become an ENT specialist:



To become, you must follow the career path given below :

STEP 1: A candidate should choose biology, chemistry and physics as their major subjects after they complete their 10th boards.

STEP 2: A candidate should finish their 12th degree with a minimum of 50% with physics, chemistry and biology as their mandatory subjects.

STEP 3: After that the candidate needs to qualify entrance exams such as NEET (National Eligibility cum Entrance Test) and many more entrance exams on a university level to get into a college of their preference. 

STEP 4: After this the candidate needs to finish their bachelor’s degree in the major’s bachelor’s degree such as MBBS (Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery) or BSc (Bachelor of Science). 

STEP 5: After getting the bachelor’s the candidate can even start his working career directly or choose to do his post bachelor’s degree, MSc (Master of Science) into any desired specialisation.

STEP 6: After this the candidate can officially get a license and start working for the general public.



Eligibility and skills requirement to become an ENT specialist:



Age limit:



The minimum age to be an ENT specialist is 16.

There is no maximum age to become an ENT specialist as such.





















Educational qualification:







A candidate must have a 12th degree with physics, chemistry, biology as their mandatory subjects with a minimum of 50%.

They need a Bachelor’s MBBS or BSc degree to qualify for their post bachelor’s degree.

After the Undergraduate course the candidate needs a MSc degree in the specialisation of their own choice.

Candidates can later decide if they want to do doctorate degrees such as PhD in their selected field.



Skills requirements:



Communication skills



An ENT specialist requires strong communication skills. They need to listen to their patients when they describe symptoms and associated issues. 





Teamwork



An ENT expert is supposed to work in teams. ENT technicians are required to provide medical assistance to other surgeons while performing surgeries that relate to their area of specialty. 



Excellent hand-eye coordination 



These doctors have to perform a lot of surgeries and they need to have a very tight hold over their hand-eye skills in order to ace their profession.



Stamina and ability to work



ENT specialists can also be working for long hours without breaks, their body needs to cooperate with their work life and should have enough potential and stamina to work without resting in emergencies. 





Time management 



They should be able to maintain their tiring work life with their personal life in order to not exhaust themselves mentally as well as physically and have a happy work life and a personal life.









Working environment :



The majority of ear, nose and throat specialists are employed in private clinics or offices, usually assisted by nurses and administrative staff, the working environment is quite comfortable.

Self-employed professionals have a great deal of flexibility and can organize their own schedules, while those working in an organisation have much less control over their schedules.

Ear, nose, and throat specialists work long, uneven hours. Travel is a necessary part of that occupation. They work more than 35 hours a week.

Types of specialisation available for an ENT specialist: 



Ophthalmologist



An ophthalmologist is a medical professional who possesses an MD (Doctor of Medicine) degree in eye and vision care. They are trained to give eye tests, examining and treating eye or vision problems.

These doctors may perform surgeries but they don’t prescribe glasses or lenses to the patients.



Oncologist 





An Oncologist is a doctor who specialises in giving the treatment of cancer using chemotherapy and other surgical methods. 

These types of ENT technicians may specialize in any of the three major fields - medical, surgical and radiation.



Rhinologist





A rhinologist is a professional surgical specialist who has completed his or her training in rhinology and skull base surgery.

They have advanced training in the treatment of complex cases of the nasal chamber and sinuses.



Laryngologist



Laryngologist has a specialty in treating diseases, diseases and wounds of the larynx. 

A voice box or larynx is located in the front of the neck. It consists of vocal chords that are responsible for generating sound and swallowing.



Lecturer

Lecturers are also known as subject matter experts that are designed to develop and deliver study material using a wide range of platforms and methods.

It basically includes developing lesson plans, course material, curriculum, and conducting ENT field research.

They have a lot of career options even if they don’t want to work or perform surgeries, they can be professors or lecturers and share their knowledge and expand the field's growth.

Walsh is well appreciated by his patients for his good work, he’s one of the best ENT surgeons in the world. He works at western health care where he takes care of the multidisciplinary head and Neck Cancer clinic.

CAREER PROFILE: ELECTRONICS AND TELECOMMUNICATION ENGINEER



Overview:

Who is an ELECTRONIC AND COMMUNICATION ENGINEER?

Electronics Engineering is one of the largest branches of engineering. It has made available the equipment like Television, Radio, computers, telephones etc.Electronics is the most important part of every industry because it is used in every industry. 

An electronics engineer has to design and innovate /develop new electronic equipment which can be used also for further communication. Basically they build communication systems.

They can get work in every industry because communication systems are needed in every industry. Their work also includes developing and designing broadcasts.



What kind of Job is it, Field or desk?

The job of an ETC Engineer is desk work. They have to take care of the operations, designing and structures of the electronic and telecommunication systems. So they mainly have computer based work. Sometimes they have field work at the time of implementation of the design.

                 



Roles and responsibilities of a ELECTRONIC AND COMMUNICATION ENGINEER:

The roles and responsibilities of an ETC Engineer depend on the specialization he/she has chosen. But, the general responsibilities of an ETC Engineer are stated below.

To design, review , test and repair the software or hardware of the systems. They mainly have to take care that all the means of communication in the system is working properly.

They have to coordinate and work with other engineers, higher authorities and managers to do things according to the requirements of the customers or clients. Also they have to work with other branches of engineering like mechanical for completing the project.





To ensure that all tools and products adhere to health and safety standards. The quality or the standards of safety. That they are not of cheap quality. Because if they don't check then it may affect them in future.

Creating efficient testing, maintenance, and quality control procedures is another work they have to do. This means that they have to make the procedure or step by step guide on how the whole process of any work is tobe done.

Roles and responsibilities of a ELECTRONIC AND COMMUNICATION ENGINEER:

The roles and responsibilities of an ETC Engineer depend on the specialization he/she has chosen. But, the general responsibilities of an ETC Engineer are stated below.

To design, review , test and repair the software or hardware of the systems. They mainly have to take care that all the means of communication in the system is working properly.

They have to coordinate and work with other engineers, higher authorities and managers to do things according to the requirements of the customers or clients. Also they have to work with other branches of engineering like mechanical for completing the project.





To ensure that all tools and products adhere to health and safety standards. The quality or the standards of safety. That they are not of cheap quality. Because if they don't check then it may affect them in future.



To give suggestions for process enhancement after observing current procedures. For example, if some communication systems are made then they have to check them if there are any suggestions they have to give them.





Creating efficient testing, maintenance, and quality control procedures is another work they have to do. This means that they have to make the procedure or step by step guide on how the whole process of any work is tobe done.









Presenting at conferences and, if necessary, representing the company did one the duties of STC engineer. They present what they have done in their project and also convince clients if necessary.





Benefits Of Choosing ETC ENGINEER As A Career Profile:

Lots of Career Opportunities

                   

Knowing that even entry-level electronics engineers are always welcomed in large companies may help you relax. They are looking for applicants with a new perspective and creative ideas. Therefore, they won't be too concerned about your experience. Many businesses that deal with electronic devices are accepting applications.



Innovation



You won't get bored working in this fascinating field. Companies release more and more tools to assist individuals automate repetitive jobs every year or even every day. The same is true with emerging technologies, which are always undergoing updates and alterations.







A Huge Demand

              

There are numerous places where you can refer to work as an electronic engineer, regardless of where you live. 

Don't overlook the enormous need for foreign expertise either. Such a professional can help you open up the world because they will welcome you to participate in internships or just tour the globe in an effort to share your opinions on technology in many nations at once.



High Starting Salaries

                

Pay for electronics engineers can start at $57K annually, which is a significant benefit even if you lack experience. Of course, you should also keep in mind that there will be a lot of pressure and obligations. However, these experts claim that they are enthusiasts of ongoing difficulties and are aware of what they are getting into.



Largest Branch

   

It shouldn't come as a surprise that electronics engineers are among the best in the world. They are revered in every nation and use technology and inventions to run it. Steve Wozniak and Steve Jobs, the minds behind the Apple firms and the products that have been on everyone's lips up until this point, are a good example.u Join the Elite.



How To Become a ETC Engineer?



To become an engineer right after 10th follow the path given below-

Step-1: Subject Selection: Students aspiring to become ETC engineers should put their future admission to a reputable college before everything else. Students taking their class 12th board exams must take Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics in addition to ETC Engineering.

Step-2: Preparing for Entrance Exams: Once passing the 10+2 test, candidates are eligible to take any Entrance Exam. They can enroll in a ETC Engineering course only after they have passed the Entrance Exam. Each student who passes the exam is assigned a specific college where they can begin their graduation path during counseling.

Step-4: Choosing the Right Colleges: You must achieve very high grades in intermediate and entrance exams if you want to be admitted to government colleges. If your grades are low, the only college that will take your grades is a private institution. 

Step-6: After Qualifying Exam: This four-year adventure requires a lot of perseverance and effort to complete. There are various theoretical and practical classes included in the four-year ETC Engineering programme. 

Step 7: A bachelor's degree in electrical engineering, electronics engineering, or electrical engineering technology is required for aspirant electrical and electronics engineers to enter the field. Programs include in-class, lab, and field research. Digital systems design, differential equations, and electrical circuit theory are all included in the courses. 

Step-8: Internship: All students are required to complete an internship after completing their bachelor's degree programme. Most students are typically provided internships in various organizations as soon as the course is through, but anyone who wants can pursue higher education as well.

Step 9: Some colleges offer 5-year programmes where students can earn both a bachelor's and a master's degree. An engineer with a graduate degree can work in research and development or as a lecturer at various universities.



Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:



The age limit for becoming an ETC Engineer completely depends on the route you choose after 12th. 

Educational qualifications:



A student must have passed 12th in any stream with at least 60% aggregate from any government recognized board.

Physics, Chemistry, and Math must all be passed in the 10+2 or equivalent examination from an accredited college.

The JEE Mains, JEE Advanced, KCET, MHCET, and other entrance exams serve as the primary means of admission to this Btech ETC Engineering programme.

Skill requirements:



To become a successful ETC Engineer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.



Concentration 



Complex electrical systems, electronic devices, and components are designed and developed by electrical and electronics engineers. 

When carrying out these activities, they must be able to keep track of numerous design components and technical specifications.

Electrical and electronics engineers must have the initiative to use their knowledge in new ways on each project they work on. 

They also need to pursue ongoing education to stay abreast of technological advancements.





Interpersonal capabilities 



To ensure that their plans are properly carried out during the manufacturing process, electrical and electronics engineers must be able to collaborate with others. 

This partnership entails keeping an eye on the technicians and coming up with solutions as issues emerge.





Math abilities



Calculus and other sophisticated math concepts must be mastered by electrical and electronics engineers in order to evaluate, develop, and troubleshoot machinery.





Speech abilities



 Engineering technicians and other professionals collaborate closely with electrical and electronics experts. 

They must be able to clearly communicate their designs and justifications as well as deliver directions during the creation and development of products. 

They also have to educate clients who lack technical knowledge about difficult situations.





Writing prowess 



The technical papers that electrical and electronics engineers create for the equipment they design include maintenance manuals, operating instructions, parts lists, product proposals, and documentation outlining design methodologie.

Promotions And Upgrading

The promotions of the post of a mechanical engineer can be understood by the following flow chart. One can begin from being a trainee to becoming a Manager of the organization he or she is associated with.



First level  promotion - In the beginning, the employee joins any organisation at the basic level where he or she works. This post includes titles such as Junior Engineer and Trainee

Senior Engineer - The second level of promotion is when any Junior employee or a trainee becomes a Senior Engineer, the role of this post involves managing the junior staff and maintaining the proper operation of daily activities. 

Supervisor- At the third level of promotion the employee now takes up the role of a supervisor. The role of a supervisor is to educate, sponsor, coach, counselor and direct the employees working under him.

Manager - The final stage of promotion is the post of a Manager, where the employee is asked to hire contractors and build teams, provide clear and concise instructions to engineering teams etc.



The following are the posts of different electronic engineers allotted on the basis of performance and knowledge. 

1. Engineer in electronics



They are the ones who combine technological and mathematical skills. An electronics engineer's primary responsibilities include designing, developing, testing, installing, and maintaining various machinery frameworks and telecom systems.

2. Software Developer



They examine the entire IT system architect. The best solutions for a company's computer system requirements are implemented by software analysts. Designing, testing, and evaluating standards and methodologies that meet the business requirements of either their organization or a client is their responsibility.

3. Engineer in Electronic Design



They are the experts in charge of the digital transformation. They create, produce, test, make, and maintain both commonplace and sophisticated electrical equipment. The pursuit of superior expertise in research, design, and circuit modeling is another goal of electronics design engineers.

4. Test Engineer on the Field



Several departments work together with a field test engineer to make sure a plan is in place for a smooth product launch. Throughout the final product testing, they recognise any issues and provide them with high-quality remedies. Additionally, they keep a careful eye on the processes involved in product design, installation, and implementation.

5. Desktop Support Specialist



They are experts at troubleshooting. After thoroughly inspecting the software for security and system flaws, a desktop support consultant will troubleshoot numerous user issues.

6. Engineer for network planning



They control and characterize network planning and delivery tools. In order to specify needs and incorporate them into network architecture, Network Planning Engineer coordinates with the Customer Support Team.

7. Engineer, Communications



Conducting research, curating, producing, and collaborating on the creation of communication devices are the main responsibilities of a communication engineer. They are also in charge of managing and monitoring the team's performance.

8. Customer support specialist



They serve as the company's public face. They support clients through the onboarding process, troubleshoot network problems, configure operating systems, identify and fix internal technical problems with a company's product or service, and assist clients with installation and update problems as necessary.

title: Ecologist
summary: Name of career Profile: Ecologist
description:
Name of career Profile: Ecologist





An overview of the career profile:



An Ecologist is one who studies the intertwined relationships between animals, plants, microbes and humans while they interact with their physical environment and understanding the intricacies in the associating relations. 

An ecologist has varying amounts of work in both the indoors and the outdoors which include studying microorganisms and tissue samples in their laboratories while they might also be required to conduct field trips to collect new samples. 

Ecologists study the effects of construction activities on ecosystems and see how damages can be avoided.













Roles and Responsibilities of an ecologist 



Writing environmental impact studies. 



Generally since ecologists know about how human activities influence an ecosystem, 

it's their job to research the effects of housing and farming projects and their impact on a particular ecosystem in order to preserve nature.



Researching on topics and then publishing research papers on these topics followed by presentations of the research results at conferences.













Ecologists are also involved in heavy research on various topics ranging from preservation of endangered species to conservation efforts to increasing and decreasing population of humans and the other inhabitants of any area.



Helping the conservation efforts of various endangered species. 





Ecologists are thoroughly connected with the population density of various species of any ecosystem and hence they know all about the various practices that can be put to use to conservation efforts of endangered species like Black rhino.



Writing in depth reports which tells them about the varying effects of deterioration or progress an ecosystem is going through. 



Ecologists are like the caretakers and overseers of nature. Since they keep track of all the animals, vegetation and other factors of an ecosystem, they also give detailed survey reports to conservation officials and forestry departments on how to conserve nature. 





Maintenance of tools and instruments used in field experiments. 



Since ecologists are well trained in all the field work and maintenance work, they can easily maintain the tools and implements necessary to maintain a laboratory or those needed for a field research. 



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Flexible working hours which involves working at their own ease of convenience without any visible deadlines. 





Mostly all field work and research is done in the summer and spring season due to which mostly the ecologists are only expected to work for only half the year.

















Ecologists earn an average of 5lpa as freshers with those associated with government or corporate/housing firms earning as high as 25 lpa salaries. Thus it's a very lucrative field.







 







If you like to view new sceneries and visit new places, then the career prospects of an ecologist is one for you since you'll be required to visit multiple places for conducting surveys and collecting samples. 







Ecology is a very passion driven field so you'll never be bored of your work since the job of an ecologist brings new opportunities and with them new topics of interest.





















There is a shortage of ecologists as a whole so job opportunities once you get your permit/certificate as an ecologist will be abundant. 









Ecology is also a very well respected field, given importance by both science oriented people as well as those in the city development and government sectors.





A basic procedure to approach this job:





Complete class 10th with minimum 60% marks.

Next you've to complete 12th class or 10+2 with the subjects Physics, chemistry, biology with at least 50% in all of the subjects mentioned. 

With this you can take admission in B.Sc with biology and environmental studies as their core subjects.

After completion of their bachelor's degree, you can study further in the form of a master's degree or M.Sc to learn even more.

After completion, you can look for field or research jobs in various sectors such as ecologists in the corporate sector, those hired by the government for development projects or you can opt for placements if your college/University provides that option. 



Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



Age Limit

There is no age limit in order to become an ecologist 



Educational Qualifications

A student must have passed class 12 in any stream with at least 50% aggregate from any government-recognized board or private institution.

Candidates must complete a 3-year undergraduate course in B.Sc in Environmental studies or biology from any recognized institute.

There is no certification exam for an ecologist and you can try to be employed as one with even a 12th class diploma but it's recommended to continue higher studies for more career opportunities.



Skill Requirements 

To bec
full_content:
Name of career Profile: Ecologist





An overview of the career profile:



An Ecologist is one who studies the intertwined relationships between animals, plants, microbes and humans while they interact with their physical environment and understanding the intricacies in the associating relations. 

An ecologist has varying amounts of work in both the indoors and the outdoors which include studying microorganisms and tissue samples in their laboratories while they might also be required to conduct field trips to collect new samples. 

Ecologists study the effects of construction activities on ecosystems and see how damages can be avoided.













Roles and Responsibilities of an ecologist 



Writing environmental impact studies. 



Generally since ecologists know about how human activities influence an ecosystem, 

it's their job to research the effects of housing and farming projects and their impact on a particular ecosystem in order to preserve nature.



Researching on topics and then publishing research papers on these topics followed by presentations of the research results at conferences.













Ecologists are also involved in heavy research on various topics ranging from preservation of endangered species to conservation efforts to increasing and decreasing population of humans and the other inhabitants of any area.



Helping the conservation efforts of various endangered species. 





Ecologists are thoroughly connected with the population density of various species of any ecosystem and hence they know all about the various practices that can be put to use to conservation efforts of endangered species like Black rhino.



Writing in depth reports which tells them about the varying effects of deterioration or progress an ecosystem is going through. 



Ecologists are like the caretakers and overseers of nature. Since they keep track of all the animals, vegetation and other factors of an ecosystem, they also give detailed survey reports to conservation officials and forestry departments on how to conserve nature. 





Maintenance of tools and instruments used in field experiments. 



Since ecologists are well trained in all the field work and maintenance work, they can easily maintain the tools and implements necessary to maintain a laboratory or those needed for a field research. 



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Flexible working hours which involves working at their own ease of convenience without any visible deadlines. 





Mostly all field work and research is done in the summer and spring season due to which mostly the ecologists are only expected to work for only half the year.

















Ecologists earn an average of 5lpa as freshers with those associated with government or corporate/housing firms earning as high as 25 lpa salaries. Thus it's a very lucrative field.







 







If you like to view new sceneries and visit new places, then the career prospects of an ecologist is one for you since you'll be required to visit multiple places for conducting surveys and collecting samples. 







Ecology is a very passion driven field so you'll never be bored of your work since the job of an ecologist brings new opportunities and with them new topics of interest.





















There is a shortage of ecologists as a whole so job opportunities once you get your permit/certificate as an ecologist will be abundant. 









Ecology is also a very well respected field, given importance by both science oriented people as well as those in the city development and government sectors.





A basic procedure to approach this job:





Complete class 10th with minimum 60% marks.

Next you've to complete 12th class or 10+2 with the subjects Physics, chemistry, biology with at least 50% in all of the subjects mentioned. 

With this you can take admission in B.Sc with biology and environmental studies as their core subjects.

After completion of their bachelor's degree, you can study further in the form of a master's degree or M.Sc to learn even more.

After completion, you can look for field or research jobs in various sectors such as ecologists in the corporate sector, those hired by the government for development projects or you can opt for placements if your college/University provides that option. 



Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



Age Limit

There is no age limit in order to become an ecologist 



Educational Qualifications

A student must have passed class 12 in any stream with at least 50% aggregate from any government-recognized board or private institution.

Candidates must complete a 3-year undergraduate course in B.Sc in Environmental studies or biology from any recognized institute.

There is no certification exam for an ecologist and you can try to be employed as one with even a 12th class diploma but it's recommended to continue higher studies for more career opportunities.



Skill Requirements 

To become a successful ecologist one must have the following skill-sets:



     Good knowledge of field ecology and supplementary skills



Ecologists are expected to carry out surveys in various parts of the country in order to collect samples and research on various new species and understand the condition of a particular ecosystem by direct observation. 



Thorough understanding of lab techniques used for research



Ecologists are required to research lab samples in order to better understand microbes and other similar organisms and their effect on humans. For this they need excellent lab skills. 



People management skills



Since ecologists are involved with various housing firms and agricultural activities as their consultant, they are expected to be great leaders and got mediators in order to let people know about the scope of sustainable development and persuade people to act differently.



Leadership skills in order to lead a team regularly or in times of urgency to achieve results.



They should be great at leading people in order to better influence people 



Time management skills to complete work in allotted time.



       Ecologists may manage several projects at the same time. They develop proposals and may create individual projects based on their expertise. For this they need to preserve time. 



Organizational skills to help in corporate settings.



Many times ecologists are employed in the corporate sector, so they should be able to voice their views on any particular thing to their employers and colleagues. For this they need to be good orators and great analysts. 



Analytics and model making skills. 



Ecologists are required to be good at using various designing software in order to better represent their research findings to other researchers and the general public in order to show the extent of human activity. 



Maturity, perseverance and self respect. 



Ecologists are sometimes involved in a very delicate field of work where emotional outbursts will result in complete mishaps, so they need to be patient and extremely calm and collected in order to work towards their goal without any flaring. 



Working conditions 



 Ecologists generally work in laboratories and research centers depending on whether they specialize in field work or studying the samples. 

Generally ecology involves working with professionals of various fields and helping them to maintain the ecological balance. 

This can be in normal corporate conditions or they can be hired by government organizations such as wildlife or forestry departments. 

They also have the chance to travel a lot so they’re not confined to any one working place. 



Entrance exam to follow the route:



There are different entrance exams available to pursue Ecology as a career option. Below given are some of the entrance exams for getting into a college/University to pursue the respective courses:



JNU entrance exam

Delhi University entrance exam

Anna University entrance exam

Banaras Hindu University entrance exam,

BIT entrance exam

CUSAT entrance exam

TANCET entrance exam

Gauhati University Entrance exam

Courses after class 12th



A bachelor's degree in science with any of the following courses will be enough to be qualified enough in order to become an ecologist.

Type of specialization available under this job profiles : 



The following are some of the most popular specializations that ecologists settle with. These are both in high demand and also offer good salaries. These are: 



Conservation Scientist 

The primary focus of conservation scientists is on natural resources and achieving a balance between using resources responsibly and protecting them for present-day populations as well as the health of the planet.











Environmental Scientist

Environmental science encompasses a wide range of professions, from studying and dealing with contaminated land and water to monitoring air quality. Through research and instruction in the natural sciences, an environmental scientist protects both human health and the environment.







Wildlife Biologist

A wildlife biologist conducts study on the wildlife population, as well as on how it affects the environment and threatened species. Wildlife biologists’ main duty is to do study on the biology, behavior, and habitats of a wide variety of wild species and uncultivated ecosystems.





Agricultural Ecologist

The responsibility of an agricultural Ecologist is to find better ways of feeding the population without causing greater threat to the environment. In other words sustainable farming and animal husbandry and rearing is the job of an Ecologist. 







Administration and Teaching

The most promising long-term work possibilities are provided by a Ph.D. in ecology or a closely related environmental science. These ecologists could work as professional environmental consultants for private consulting firms or lecture at the university level.





Forestry ecologist 

Among the many tasks a forester performs are ecological restoration, timber harvesting, and protected area management. They keep an eye on continuing forest activities like hunting, gathering raw materials, outdoor leisure, conservation, and aesthetics.







Botanist







A highly qualified scientist who specialises in studying plant life and creatures is a botanist. Their study of more than 300,000 plant species falls within their very broad area of botany.

The statistics given below represents the average pay-scale of an Ecologist in India:

Promotions & upgradings:



Generally just like any other career profile, with experience comes greater packages and increased responsibilities.

Mostly higher studies like a master's degree or a doctorate results in being eligible for these posts. 

Every 2-3 years Ecologists experience a salary hike of 12-20% with other benefits.

Those with an experience of 10+years can apply for the government sector where they can get registered by the government of India as an official ecologist.

Demands & Future Scope 



Making a career as an ecologist is an increasingly lucrative one in today's world.

Since people have become increasingly aware of the importance of ecological balance, they are trying their hardest to conserve the habitats and practice sustainable farming and land development.

That's why, a  job as an ecologist is currently available in a wide range of professions that are connected to the environment.

Many industries need ecologists, including agriculture, forestry, environmental pollution reduction, regional development, and many more.

Plus once you get in the industry, you'll only continue to learn more and get more experience, this is subject to better salaries and more amenities and perks. 



Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Jadav Payeng 





Jadav Payeng, who hails from the state of Assam, is also referred to as the “forest man of India.”  His work as an environmentalist consists of independently building a 550 acre man-made forest.



Sumaira Abdulali 



Sumaira Abdulali has been working to protect the environment since 2002. She was born into a family of environmentalists. She has made significant contributions to two environmental problems for more than ten years: sand mining and noise pollution. Her non-profit organization, Awaaz Foundation, was instrumental in putting silent zones and safe zone restrictions in place during festivals as well as in addressing the problem of sand dredging, which has a detrimental effect on the bio-system. 



Salim Ali 



Ali was a biologist and ornithologist who was often referred to as the “Birdman of India.” He made an environmentalist contribution by performing meticulous bird surveys in India. He played a very important role in founding of many national parks and sanctuaries which resulted in hundreds of species of birds to be classified as protected species. His achievements include the silent hill valley park. 



Sunderlal Bahuguna 



He was the founder of the Chipko movement and an Indian environmentalist. He campaigned for the protection of Himalayan forests. He started out fighting in 1970 as a part of the Chipko movement and then led the anti-Tehri Dam movement from the 1980s until the beginning of 2004.



Sohrab Pirojsha Godrej 



Godrej was one of the premier ecologists and conservationists of India, who fought for the conservation of many forests and other nature groves. He even became the President of

Indian branch of world wildlife fund and was awarded a lot of degrees.

title: Engineer
summary: Overview of the career profile-
description:
Engineer 





Overview of the career profile-



What do we understand by the term ‘Engineer’?



The job of an engineer is to resolve problems in the field of science and technology. There are different types of engineers depending on the specialisation in one particular field. Being an engineer by profession means that one gets to deal with manufacturing, designing and testing complex technical equipment and at the same time putting theoretical knowledge of engineering into the practical use of daily life. 



What is the role of an ‘Engineer’?



The role of an engineer is to fulfil the following needs and demands of the association-



Research

An engineer is required to do research and findings related to the concerned field about the new inventions and launches of the different gadgets and equipment. Before implementing the idea, an engineer is required to do the research about the same in all means, 



Develop

The development of the technical department lies in the hands of the engineer, it is important to cope up with the requirements and advancement of society on a daily basis, only then will any organisation grow. 



        

 Design 

The role of an engineer is to bring forth new designs and suggestions such as new structures, models or functioning methods of instruments, equipments or objects. 



Construct 

For designing, planning and constructing different public properties or any specific structure, there are Construction Engineers who specialise in this particular category of construction. 



Produce-

Assembling and putting together an idea into the structure of a model is one of the many roles of an engineer. 



Operate

Operating involves keeping in check the correct operation of all the machines and instruments which are put into use,  



Management

Without proper management, there can be no efficiency and effective functioning, which is why it is also the job of senior executive engineers to keep a watch on the activities of the staff as per the demand of the organisation. 



Benefits of choosing Engineer as a career-

Job Stability

 

An engineer gets a definite place for work which results in job stability depending upon the quality of his or her work. 

Higher income-

 

Be it a Chemical Engineer or Automobile engineer, there is an ample amount of income generation which comes along with different perks and benefits. 

Transferable job opportunities



Based on the quality of the work, an engineer has the liberty to work as per the convenience and there are opportunities available such as transferring from one place to another as per needs. 

Work around the world



Getting employed across the world is also one of the many benefits of being an engineer. As per the demand of manpower, an engineer can be called upon anywhere. 

Beneficial impacts on the surroundings and the society 



Engineers such as Biomedical Engineers are now no less than a doctor, as they create and bring into action various medical instruments in the medical sciences which are beneficial for the humans such as prosthesis, walking canes, crutches etc. 



Procedure of becoming an Engineer-







Step 1 - The aspirant needs to complete class 10th and class 12th with Science stream.



Step 2 - Apply for the admission process into different colleges and universities. 



Step 3 - Proceed by appearing for the entrance examination meant for Engineering.



Step 4 - After the clarification of required documents, the candidate will get admission 

             into the institution of the preference. 



Step 5 - Complete the 4-year long engineering course in the concerned field.



Step 6 - Appear for the NCEES FE exam. (National Council of Examiners for Engineering and     Surveying) 



Step 7 - Once the candidate has a required graduation degree and 4 years of experience as a trainee, he or she can apply for PE Exam



Step 8 - The aspirant can now apply for the job of an Engineer in a concerned field. 





What is the eligibility criteria of becoming an engineer ?



Age limit- 



There is no specific age limit for becoming an engineer. But there is definitely a certain age limit for writing the entrance examinations of Engineering .



Education qualification-



The candidate must have cleared 10+2 from a recognised board of education with Science stream.

The candidate should have either a Diploma in Engineering or graduation degree in Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) or Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech)

Post graduation in MTech or ME in Engineering is also acceptable.



Skills Required-



 To become an engineer it is very much important for the candidate to be able to have the following set if skills which will help in working efficiently and effectively -



The candidate needs to be aware of all the latest developments and advancements in the field of science and technology 



He or she should have a good hold on communication and should be able to convey his or her ideas in a comprehensive

 Design 

The role of an engineer is to bring forth new designs and suggestions such as new structures, models or functioning methods of instruments, equipments or objects. 



Construct 

For designing, planning and constructing different public properties or any specific structure, there are Construction Engineers who specialise in this particular category of construction. 



Produce-

Assembling and putting together an idea into the structure of a model is one of the many roles of an engineer. 



Operate

Operating involves keeping in check the correct operation of all the machines and instruments which are put into use,  



Management

Without proper management, there can be no efficiency and effective functioning, which is why it is also the job of senior executive engineers to keep a watch on the activities of the staff as per the demand of the organisation. 



Benefits of choosing Engineer as a career-

Job Stability

 

An engineer gets a definite place for work which results in job stability depending upon the quality of his or her work. 

Higher income-

 

Be it a Chemical Engineer or Automobile engineer, there is an ample amount of income generation which comes along with different perks and benefits. 

Transferable job opportunities



Based on the quality of the work, an engineer has the liberty to work as per the convenience and there are opportunities available such as transferring from one place to another as per needs. 

Work around the world



Getting employed across the world is also one of the many benefits of being an engineer. As per the demand of manpower, an engineer can be called upon anywhere. 

Beneficial impacts on the surroundings and the society 



Engineers such as Biomedical Engineers are now no less than a doctor, as they create and bring into action various medical instruments in the medical sciences which are beneficial for the humans such as prosthesis, walking canes, crutches etc. 



Procedure of becoming an Engineer-







Step 1 - The aspirant needs to complete class 10th and class 12th with Science stream.



Step 2 - Apply for the admission process into different colleges and universities. 



Step 3 - Proceed by appearing for the entrance examination meant for Engineering.



Step 4 - After the clarification of required documents, the candidate will get admission 

             into the institution of the preference. 



Step 5 - Complete the 4-year long engineering course in the concerned field.



Step 6 - Appear for the NCEES FE exam. (National Council of Examiners for Engineering and     Surveying) 



Step 7 - Once the candidate has a required graduation degree and 4 years of experience as a trainee, he or she can apply for PE Exam



Step 8 - The aspirant can now apply for the job of an Engineer in a concerned field. 





What is the eligibility criteria of becoming an engineer ?



Age limit- 



There is no specific age limit for becoming an engineer. But there is definitely a certain age limit for writing the entrance examinations of Engineering .



Education qualification-



The candidate must have cleared 10+2 from a recognised board of education with Science stream.

The candidate should have either a Diploma in Engineering or graduation degree in Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) or Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech)

Post graduation in MTech or ME in Engineering is also acceptable.



Skills Required-



 To become an engineer it is very much important for the candidate to be able to have the following set if skills which will help in working efficiently and effectively -



The candidate needs to be aware of all the latest developments and advancements in the field of science and technology 



He or she should have a good hold on communication and should be able to convey his or her ideas in a comprehensive manner.



Problem solving attitude is a win-win scenario for any organisation to look for in their employees.



Brilliant management and leadership skills





Working Environment- 

The working environment for an engineer is full of technology driven activities and projects. The work of an engineer is quite flexible as it depends upon the need and demand of the situation.

.

Ways of becoming an engineer-







As we already know that there are different types of engineers depending upon the field of specialisation, there is more than one way of starting your journey in the field of engineering.

title: Engineering Technician
summary: Name of the Career profile: Engineering technician
description:
Name of the Career profile: Engineering technician 



                             

Overview

An engineering technician is a professional who has been trained in the skills and procedures associated with a certain sector of technology and has practical knowledge of the technical ideas involved.

An engineering technician collaborates with a competent team of engineers and technologists to guarantee that all machinery utilised to bring a design to life is functional and operational.



Roles and responsibilities of an Engineering Technician:



Collaborating with engineers to develop, create, and test solutions to a wide range of technological difficulties.



Examining and improving current designs and prototypes by conducting research, data collection, and daily report writing



Analysing processes and making recommendations for improvement. Keeping track of records and aiding with presentations.



Attending conferences and other opportunities for learning and growth to always be updated in their field.





Benefits of choosing Engineering Technician as a career:



Confirms your status as a professional in your industry because you deal with practical knowledge of your subject.



Increased career and pay opportunities as the world is modernising so the need for technicians is a must.



Increased power inside your company as the work is incomplete without your assistance.



Opportunities to meet prominent and inspiring individuals in their fields and to work with them and gain knowledge.





A basic procedure to become an Engineering Technician:

STEP:1 Complete class 10th.



STEP:2 Complete class 12th with subjects Physics chemistry and maths.

                                                       OR

Complete Diploma in related fields.



STEP 3: Complete Bachelor's Degree in related fields. Such as BSc in Physics or a related field.



STEP 4: (Optional) Complete Masters in Related field.





Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment:



            ELIGIBILITY 



             Age Limit-



The candidate must be of a minimum age limit of 17 years to give the entrance exam.

      

             Education Qualification-

 

Bachelor's degree in their related field or Diploma after 10th.



Skills Required

Excellent detail-oriented and organisational abilities to work with engineers or while working in labs.



Should have a practical approach towards everything as they need to deal with the practicals during their working hours.



Excellent understanding of health and safety so that could work by keeping all the precautions in mind.



Ability to communicate while being cool and professional in a fast-paced workplace.





WORKING ENVIRONMENT

            

Indoors, frequently in labs, environmental engineering technologists and technicians may work. They also operate outside, sometimes in isolated areas.

Mostly it depends on the specialisation a person adopted if Mechanical then industries or Civil then at construction sites if computer science then IT industries and so on.



Entrance exams:

Assam Polytechnic admission Test

Some of the specialised technicians are:

The mechanical engineering technician course makes assumptions about how to put together or create processes, markets, and job trends that are ready for the industry.



 Electrical Engineering Technician







These technicians get training  in electrical machines, power transmission, power systems, electronics, circuit networks, motors and generators, electronic appliances, microprocessors, electromagnetism, sensors, and other related areas. 



 Electronics and Communication Engineering Technician







This field technician is concerned with the development, production, and upkeep of electronic devices and their associated machinery.



Automobile Engineering Technician 





These technicians are relevant to the development, production, and usage of buses, trucks, motorcycles, and their corresponding engineering subsystems.



 Aeronautical Engineering Technician





These technicians deal with designing, producing, maintaining, servicing, and testing the aircraft, its parts, and numerous other parts.





             There are many more specialisations which you can follow as per your interest.

Demands and Future Scope:



Engineering technician positions are on the upswing.  Engineering technicians are expected to rise at a pace of 1-3 percent, according to the organisation. That number is expected to rise as more sectors rely on technology and research to ensure their success.



Top engineers:

Kumar Bhattacharya





Engineer, educator, and government advisor Kumar Bhattacharyya, also known as Lord Bhattacharyya, CBE, FREng, FRS, is Anglo-Indian. He was born in Dhaka on June 6, 1940. Bhattacharyya received his Mechanical Engineering degree from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Kharagpur.

Franklin Chang Diaz





On May 5, 2012, Chang Diaz was honoured with induction into the NASA Astronaut Hall of Fame during a ceremony held at the Kennedy Space Center Visitor Complex. Additionally, he has received several awards in Costa Rica for his professional and scientific achievements, including being proclaimed an Honor Citizen by the national senate.











Satish Dhawan







In India, he is regarded as the founder of experimental fluid dynamics research. Dhawan was born on September 25, 1920, in Srinagar. He earned a Bachelor of Science in Physics and Mathematics as well as a Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering from the University of Punjab in Lahore, now Pakistan.



 E. Sreedharan





He is referred to as India's Metro Man. Having left the Indian Engineering Service, he (IES). He has significantly improved India's public transportation system. He attended Basel Evangelical Mission Higher Secondary School for his secondary studies.











Nagavara Ramarao Narayana Murthy







He is an Indian IT industrialist and the co-founder of Infosys. Murthy was born on 20 August 1946 in Kolar District Karnataka. He graduated from the National Institute of Engineering with a degree in Electrical Engineering.

title: Environmental Lawyer
summary: Name of the Career profile: Environmental Lawyer
description:
Name of the Career profile: Environmental Lawyer

                                                   

Overview



An environmental lawyer may represent a wide range of clients, including individuals, huge businesses, and government bodies. They are concerned with environmental rights and legislation, and they may work on initiatives including renewable energy, sustainability, and climate change.

Environmental law is a vast area of law that covers a wide range of environmental concerns such as water and air quality, hazardous waste, species protection, agriculture, wetlands, biodiversity, waste management, green initiatives, sustainability plans, and alternative energy sources.



Roles and responsibilities of Environmental Lawyers:



An environmental lawyer represents clients in legal matters such as clean technology, water law, climate change law, and the administration of public lands.



They work for environmental laws, sustainability, renewable energy, causes for climate change, and many more such resources.



Environmental lawyers defend clients or environmentalists in environmental problems, lobby for changes in environmental policy and legislation, and publish scholarly articles on the subject.



An Environmental Lawyer's job is to use environmental case law and legislation to advocate diverse environmental concerns. Environmental laws govern and define human actions that have an impact on geological and biological systems.



They have the responsibility to be an advisor as well as a legal advocate for the betterment of nature.



Contract and business agreement drafting, such as leases, licenses, purchase agreements, and partnership agreements



Benefits of choosing Environmental Lawyer as a career:



High Salary -These lawyers are paid very well by the governments and corporations who hire them  for the company.



Future scope - With the growing population, the threat to the environment is increasing hence there is a need for Lawyers who can fight for its preservation and could bring reforms in  Environmental Laws as per requirement.



People interaction- You have to interact with the people to know their cases, and communicate with judges and other lawyers Hence get a chance to interact with people.





Environmental Lawyers have several advantages over private practice, including useful experience and personal fulfillment, as well as a better work-life balance.



Being a lawyer is such a respected profession. People respect you and follow you as per you counsel them because of your knowledge and experience.



A basic procedure to become a Public Interest Lawyer:





STEP:1 After class 10th choose any stream be it Physics, Chemistry, Maths, or biology also it could be commerce or Arts Background.



STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12.



STEP:3 Complete Bachelor's Degree in any field such as Bachelor in Arts or Bachelor in Science or any other graduation Level Degree (Specialisation in the environment could be helpful)

                                                                     OR 

  You can also do a dual degree which covers both undergraduate and postgraduate levels which offers

            Bachelor's Degree + LLB (Legum Baccalaureus )



           Below are some courses: -



Bachelor of Arts and Bachelor of Laws (BA. LLB) 

Bachelor of Business Administration and Bachelor of Laws (BBA. LLB)

Bachelor of Commerce and Bachelor of Laws (B.Com LLB) 

Bachelor of Social Work and Bachelor of Laws (BSW LLB) 

Bachelor of Science and Bachelor of Laws (B.Sc. LLB)

Bachelor of Science in Law (BSL)

                                       OR 

           You can pursue LLB after Graduation Level Course.

         

          STEP:4 You can pursue an LLM (Master of Law) in Environmental Law this is a post-graduation.



STEP:5 On completing law courses, an individual can go for internships for the experience.

                                                                                                

Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment:



            ELIGIBILITY 



             Age Limit-



The candidate must be of a minimum age limit of 17 years to give the entrance exam.

      

             Education Qualification-

 

A LLB degree is a must for an individual to be an Environmental Lawyer. Also, you can choose to do an integrated course such as BA+LLB or  BBA LLB to become a lawyer whereas choosing a graduation-level degree in the environment will be beneficial.



             Entrance Exam-

A candidate needs to give CLAT Exam after 10+2 or could give DU LLB exam after graduation to get into a reputed college such as a Top National Law Universities for pursuing law. 



SKILLS REQUIRED

Skills in analysis and research- Lawyers need to be highly valued for their ability to do a lot of research on topics related to the cases they work on so they are required to be great at analysis.



 Paying close attention to detail-Lawyers, regard

A LLB degree is a must for an individual to be an Environmental Lawyer. Also, you can choose to do an integrated course such as BA+LLB or  BBA LLB to become a lawyer whereas choosing a graduation-level degree in the environment will be beneficial.



             Entrance Exam-

A candidate needs to give CLAT Exam after 10+2 or could give DU LLB exam after graduation to get into a reputed college such as a Top National Law Universities for pursuing law. 



SKILLS REQUIRED

Skills in analysis and research- Lawyers need to be highly valued for their ability to do a lot of research on topics related to the cases they work on so they are required to be great at analysis.



 Paying close attention to detail-Lawyers, regardless of their line of practice, must pay close attention to detail. Lawyers deal with a wide range of clients in a variety of law firms, and they rely on their attention to detail to discover discrepancies in their client's claims.



Time Management -Effective time management is also essential in this profession. Lawyers often bill on an hourly basis, which involves remaining on track for client meetings, mediation, hearings, and other activities during working hours.



Interpersonal abilities-Lawyers connect and collaborate with a wide range of clients and professionals. As a result, having excellent interpersonal skills, such as empathy and the capacity to connect with people, is advantageous.



Presentation Skills - As lawyers need to present in front of the judge what they have and to give their points on behalf of their Clients.







WORKING ENVIRONMENT

           

Environmental lawyers work in the corporate sector, and the government and usually Environmental lawyers work mostly in offices, although they also routinely represent clients in court and lecture at colleges.

Lawyers may spend a significant amount of time examining environmental issues or monitoring compliance with environmental legislation. Many environmental lawyers work long days and weekends. 

The work may be difficult, and lawyers must be able to operate under pressure and meet deadlines.

title: Epidemiologists
summary: Career Profile : Epidemiologists
description:
Career Profile : Epidemiologists 





          



                 Overview 



Who is an Epidemiologist?



Epidemiologists are trained in Biostatistics and Medicine and study the various properties of diseases that affect the people living in the society. 





Epidemiologists being scientists  discover the roots and causes of a disease and make preventive measures . 





 



Epidemiologists study clinical pathology and the disease causing organisms in a pandemic or an epidemic.



What kind of career profile is it ? 





The job of an epidemiologist consists of scientific research of diseases and the primary focus of this job is controlling the outbreak of a disease caused and  implementing new preventive measures. 

       

This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with Physicians, scientists and medical practitioners. 



An Epidemiologist has to work for hours continuously.  During an emergency or an epidemic crisis they need to analyze data and work with specialist statistical computer software along with collecting data for samples and tests.









Roles and Responsibilities of an Epidemiologist? 



The roles and responsibilities of an epidemiologist are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





The epidemiologists collect samples for various tests and research on the outbreak of diseases. 













The epidemiologists design statistical analysis plans and make reports. 











They help in providing intricate details and analysis of the emerging disease. 













They help in controlling the outbreak of a communicable disease in a global context.





 



They sometimes visit sites and conduct case studies and questionnaires. 











They create awareness by promoting the current discovery and medical advancements in technology. 











They also monitor and assist during an epidemic or a pandemic and advise on health policies. 









Benefits of Choosing Epidemiologist as a Career Profile : 





High employment rates and job opportunities 









There is a high employment rate as epidemiologists can work in different types of places such as Government Health Departments, National and Global Medical Research Centers, Private Hospitals, Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Industries. 

 



Variations in Job Timings 







There are  permanent and contractual jobs along with full-time and part-time jobs for epidemiologists that help in analysing communicable diseases. 

 Usually, in India a physicist needs to  work for 40-50 hours a week. 





Opportunity to take part in Health policies : 









Epidemiologists  analyze and investigate the roots of a disease and also take part in health policies and decision-making related to health.  If you want to take part in people's  welfare and want to make new developments in the field of medicine, you can choose this career option. 





Working Outdoors and interact with people 











 Epidemiologists sometimes need to visit the sites of the particular disease caused  and collect various samples for tests. They invent the roots of an epidemic, and interact with various people, do case studies and questionnaires. If you have good interactive skills and want to analyze various diseases, you can choose to become an epidemiologist. 



New challenges and working overtime 











Epidemiologists, also known as public health workers, need to work on complex and difficult projects in the field of diseases and medicine.  They even need to work for longer hours and also work overtime during an emergency. If  you are fond of new challenges and puzzles and love to solve them with patience and effort. You can choose to become an epidemiologist. 







How To Become an Epidemiologist? 





To become an epidemiologist, you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard: 



STEP -1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to take science  for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2 : Students need to study in a recognized institution and also obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12  in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Students need to  appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different colleges  in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in Biostatistics and Epidemiology or Rehabilitation Science. In some cases, students are chosen by the colleges  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree and get a B.Sc. degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters in Public Health or Occupational therapy. 





STEP - 6 : Students can also join an internship and get exposed to a real working environment. 







Eligibility And Skills Require
