Career Profile : Epidemiologists 





          



                 Overview 



Who is an Epidemiologist?



Epidemiologists are trained in Biostatistics and Medicine and study the various properties of diseases that affect the people living in the society. 





Epidemiologists being scientists  discover the roots and causes of a disease and make preventive measures . 





 



Epidemiologists study clinical pathology and the disease causing organisms in a pandemic or an epidemic.



What kind of career profile is it ? 





The job of an epidemiologist consists of scientific research of diseases and the primary focus of this job is controlling the outbreak of a disease caused and  implementing new preventive measures. 

       

This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with Physicians, scientists and medical practitioners. 



An Epidemiologist has to work for hours continuously.  During an emergency or an epidemic crisis they need to analyze data and work with specialist statistical computer software along with collecting data for samples and tests.









Roles and Responsibilities of an Epidemiologist? 



The roles and responsibilities of an epidemiologist are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





The epidemiologists collect samples for various tests and research on the outbreak of diseases. 













The epidemiologists design statistical analysis plans and make reports. 











They help in providing intricate details and analysis of the emerging disease. 













They help in controlling the outbreak of a communicable disease in a global context.





 



They sometimes visit sites and conduct case studies and questionnaires. 











They create awareness by promoting the current discovery and medical advancements in technology. 











They also monitor and assist during an epidemic or a pandemic and advise on health policies. 









Benefits of Choosing Epidemiologist as a Career Profile : 





High employment rates and job opportunities 









There is a high employment rate as epidemiologists can work in different types of places such as Government Health Departments, National and Global Medical Research Centers, Private Hospitals, Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Industries. 

 



Variations in Job Timings 







There are  permanent and contractual jobs along with full-time and part-time jobs for epidemiologists that help in analysing communicable diseases. 

 Usually, in India a physicist needs to  work for 40-50 hours a week. 





Opportunity to take part in Health policies : 









Epidemiologists  analyze and investigate the roots of a disease and also take part in health policies and decision-making related to health.  If you want to take part in people's  welfare and want to make new developments in the field of medicine, you can choose this career option. 





Working Outdoors and interact with people 











 Epidemiologists sometimes need to visit the sites of the particular disease caused  and collect various samples for tests. They invent the roots of an epidemic, and interact with various people, do case studies and questionnaires. If you have good interactive skills and want to analyze various diseases, you can choose to become an epidemiologist. 



New challenges and working overtime 











Epidemiologists, also known as public health workers, need to work on complex and difficult projects in the field of diseases and medicine.  They even need to work for longer hours and also work overtime during an emergency. If  you are fond of new challenges and puzzles and love to solve them with patience and effort. You can choose to become an epidemiologist. 







How To Become an Epidemiologist? 





To become an epidemiologist, you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard: 



STEP -1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to take science  for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2 : Students need to study in a recognized institution and also obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12  in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Students need to  appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different colleges  in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in Biostatistics and Epidemiology or Rehabilitation Science. In some cases, students are chosen by the colleges  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree and get a B.Sc. degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters in Public Health or Occupational therapy. 





STEP - 6 : Students can also join an internship and get exposed to a real working environment. 







Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become a Epidemiologist : 





   Age Limit :  





  The minimum age to apply as an epidemiologist is 18 years. 



As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications: 





A student must secure  50-60 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Biostatistics and Epidemiology, Rehabilitation Science or Paramedical. 



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course preferably in Epidemiology. 





Entrance Exams: 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify in AMU CAT (Aligarh Muslim University Common Admission Test), MGM Institute of Health Sciences All India PG Entrance Test, National Institute of Epidemiology Admission in order to be eligible as an epidemiologist. 





Working Environment: 





Epidemiologists  work in various kinds of places like Private Hospitals, Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Industries, Government Health Departments National and Global Medical Research Centers, NGOs. They also work with different teams of scientists and physicists. 





Epidemiologists usually need to work for 40 hours to research and investigate the outbreak of diseases and it's transmission in a global context. 





They also  research on topics like aging, cancer, reproduction, cardiovascular disease, diabetes, infectious disease, obesity and osteoporosis. They also need to make strategies and take prevention measures. 





Epidemiologists being scientists and disease detectives work part time as well as full time and also work for longer hours during a medical emergency or an epidemic. 





Skills Requirements: 





Good Communication skills 









Good interactive skills 









Excellent listening skills 











Computer skills











Time management skills and ability to work for longer hours 









Excellent decision maker 











Excellent knowledge in  medicine 







Practical knowledge and analytical thinking











Entrance Exam To Become a Epidemiologist 



An individual needs to qualify the entrance exams and sit for orientation conducted by universities in order to get admission.  The list of all the entrance examinations that will help students to become an Epidemiologist. 











Exams 

Courses Offered 

AIMS CET (Amrita Institute of Medical Sciences Common Entrance Test

BSc. Medical Laboratory Technology (MLT), BSc. Medical Radiologic Technology (MRT), BSc. Anaesthesia Technology (AT), BSc. Cardiac Perfusion Technology (CPT)

BSc. Cardio Vascular Technology (CVT)

MBA in Agri-Business, MBA in Financial Management/ MTTM.

MSW, MBA in Islamic Banking/ MBA in Islamic Banking and Finance, MBA in Hospital Administration

National Institute of Epidemiology - NIE 

Master of Public Health (M.PH), JEE Advanced  (Joint Entrance Examination – Advanced), BITSAT (Birla Institute of Technology and Science), IPU CET ( Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test.) 



SCTIMST-EE: Sree Chitra Tribunal Institute for Medical Sciences and Technology Entrance Examination

MPhil, M. Tech, Ph. D.



JIPMER-EE: Jawaharlal Institute of Postgraduate Medical Education & Research Entrance Examination

MBBS, B.Sc., M.Sc., P.hD. 









Top Courses To Become an Epidemiologist 



There are various courses and specializations available for students who want to become a physicist. The list given below has top courses to become an Epidemiologist.

This course is either merit based or an entrance exam is conducted by the University at a national level or state level. 









                                                  

Courses After Completing Bachelor's : 



You can pursue a masters in Epidemiology or in Public Health and Occupational therapy after completing Bachelors. You can also go for a PhD. in Epidemiology or join an entry level epidemiologist job. Here are the courses available after Bachelors and Master's programs. 



MSc. in Epidemiology : Masters of Sciences in Epidemiology 

Duration: 2 years 

Entrance exam : GATE, CUCET 



PhD in Epidemiology and Biostatistics - Doctorate program 

Duration: 3 years 

Entrance exam: UGC NET 







Top Institutions To Pursue Epidemiologist As A Career Profile:





There are several colleges  and universities where students can fulfill their dreams and pursue courses in epidemiology.  Explore the list given below for some best epidemiology colleges in India as well as abroad.









Top Epidemiology courses in India : 

West Bengal University of Animals and Fishery Sciences, Kolkata 

National Centre for Diseases Control, New Delhi 

National Institute of Epidemiology, Chennai 

All India Institute of Hygiene and Public Health, Kolkata 

Institute of Liver and Biliary Sciences, Delhi 









Top Epidemiology courses in abroad : 

University of Pennsylvania 

Yale University 

The University of Sydney 

University of Ottawa, Canada

George Washington University, USA 





Types of Specializations available for an Epidemiologist 





Pharmaceutical Epidemiologist : 











Pharmaceutical Epidemiologists investigate the outbreak of diseases and try to control further transmission of the disease to other parts of the society. They work for pharmaceutical firms and provide reports of several medicines and drugs after analyzing them in the human body. They work with tissue samples and tests different medicines in a laboratory. 





Veterinary Epidemiologist : 









Veterinary epidemiologists are specialized in curing animal related diseases.  



They investigate and discover several diseases which affect the animals and control them from spreading to other animals and humans. In cases of emergency, they provide medical help and  also prescribe medicines. 





Medical Epidemiologist : 











Medical epidemiologists  study clinical pathology and discover medications  for acute illness. 



They are specialized in clinical medicine and sometimes do public health campaigns to prevent disease.







Molecular Epidemiologist : 











These epidemiologists are specialized in molecular biology and study proteins and genes in order to discover the root causes of diseases and prevent  the disease causing organisms to grow further. 



Molecular Epidemiologists use molecules and genes in order to uncover the root causes of a disease. 



Molecular biologists typically work in pharmaceuticals, biotech and government firms. 







Pay Scale For Epidemiologist: 



The pay scale of an epidemiologist depends on several factors  like work experience and job levels. The information given below depicts the pay scale of epidemiology according to experience. 



Pay scale of a Epidemiologist according to the work experience:

Promotions And Upgrading Of An Epidemiologist: 

 

Epidemiologists and public health workers  have various scopes in different fields and follow a hierarchy.  There are different levels of physicists which are as follows: 





LEVEL-1: Interns









Interns signify that they are recent graduates  of medicine and  epidemiology. Internships  elevate the understanding of an individual and create a real life working environment for the individuals. 







LEVEL - 2 : Assistant Epidemiologist : 











An assistant epidemiologist  gradually opens the gateway of the real world of jobs and competitiveness. He or she deals with investigation, research, reports and also assists the senior scientists of an organization.





LEVEL - 3 : Junior Epidemiologist or Health Worker: 











A junior epidemiologist or a health worker works not only for survival and fulfillment of necessities but also for experience and job promotion. They create research and other scientific principles and are able to work independently without any one's supervision. 





LEVEL - 4 : Senior Epidemiologist 











Senior Epidemiologists are experienced people and  they have worked for the longest time in various aspects. They are able to identify and control diseases and make new theories and reports. 









Top Recruiting Domains For Epidemiologist:







Epidemiologists and public health workers are demanded equally for their  deep knowledge of medicine.  Choosing the work of an epidemiologist as a career option will open many job opportunities. Here are some of the domains mentioned where an epidemiologist can work. 









Top recruiting Companies for Epidemiologist:

Dr. Kanungo's Diabetes Centre.

Demand And Future Scope: 



According to reports it has been popular and known that there are better scopes for Epidemiologists. Being a medicine student  they can not only provide knowledge of medicine and health in a laboratory but also work in government firms and engage in health policies. 



After Covid 19 and its  medical emergencies, epidemiologists are in great demand as they are able to control a disease from transmitting. 







Top Inspiring Epidemiologists : 



Dr. Raman Gangakhedkar

title: Food Engineer
summary: An overview of Food Engineer as a career:
description:
Food Engineer

 

An overview of Food Engineer as a career:

Who is a Food Engineer?

A food engineer also known as a food technologist is responsible for taking care of the quality check, quality assurance and processing of food. Why deal with the development of healthy, safe, affordable and convenient food items. 

What is the role of a Food Engineer?

The role and responsibilities of a food engineer is to fulfil the following needs and demands of the association-



Modify existing products and processes and develop new ones



Developing and improving old ways of food producing concepts by researching 



Coordinating with the other staff members while working on a group project such as product launch or improvisation



Ensuring the quality check and safety of the products produced by the company



Managing the customers who come up with product related issues or any other general complaint. 



   Benefits of choosing this profile as a career

Security of job 



 Food production and manufacturing is one of the primary requirements across the world. Therefore, there is always a constant requirement of employees in different fields of food production.

Welfare of the society

 

By using the knowledge and experience of food science, the employees can produce good quality food products for the consumers keeping in mind their betterment.

Salary and Growth



Although right after graduation one can find jobs for the post of food technologist but  postgraduation and phd can provide with a wider range of scope and availability of jobs.  



  A basic procedure to approach this job:



Step 1 - The aspirant needs to complete class 10th and class 12th with Science stream with Physics,     Chemistry and Mathematics/ Biology as core subjects.

Step 2 - Apply for the admission process into different colleges and universities. 



Step 3 - Proceed by appearing for the entrance examination meant for Food  Engineering such as IIT JEE, AIEEE, GATE, state entrance exams etc.



Step 4 - After the clarification of required documents, the candidate will get admission 

              into the institution of the preference. 



Step 5 - Complete the 3-year long graduation course in the field of food sciences or 4 year long graduation course in the field of food technology.



Step 6 - Once the candidate has a required graduation degree and 4 years of experience as a trainee or an intern, one can apply for jobs for the different designations of food engineer. 



  Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :





Age limit- 



There is no specific age limit for becoming a food engineer. But there is definitely a certain age limit for writing the entrance examinations of Engineering .



Education qualification-



The candidate must have cleared 10+2 from a recognised board of education in Science stream with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics/ Biology as core subjects.

The candidate should have either a Diploma in Food Sciences or Technology or a graduation degree in Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) Food Engineering or Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) in Food Technology.

Post graduation in MTech (Food Engineering) is also acceptable.



Skills Required-



 To become a Food Engineer/ Food Technologist it is very much important for the candidate to be able to have the following set if skills which will help in working efficiently and effectively -



Ability to adapt and enhance 



 One should be able to perform up to mark as per requirements of the situation and time.



Ability to solve and reason the problem



Handling problems in a very efficient manner and coming up with instant and feasible solutions is one of the skills required for this job profile of a Food Engineer.



Awareness and Alertness



The candidate needs to be aware of all the latest developments and advancements in the field of science and food technology



Excellent understanding of surroundings and its requirements



Working according to the requirements of the consumers and according to the demands of the company is what one needs to imbibe in order to stand out.



Working Environment -

A food technologist is sometimes supposed to work in laboratories dealing with sample testing and analysing

At times, he or she may be asked to do some field work which involves making sure that the production and manufacturing of the products is running smoothly

There can also be a constant demand for efforts in terms of creativity, data analysis, project management and strategic planning.  

A food engineer is required to work for minimum 35 hours in a week

One can perform the duty partly from home and partly working at the office. 

 Entrance exam to follow the route:



Given below is the lost of few entrance exams available for getting admissions into universities which offer engineering in Food Technology-

Central Food Technological Research Institute Entrance Exam 

GATE Food Technology Entrance Exam

Indian Institute of Cro

              into the institution of the preference. 



Step 5 - Complete the 3-year long graduation course in the field of food sciences or 4 year long graduation course in the field of food technology.



Step 6 - Once the candidate has a required graduation degree and 4 years of experience as a trainee or an intern, one can apply for jobs for the different designations of food engineer. 



  Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :





Age limit- 



There is no specific age limit for becoming a food engineer. But there is definitely a certain age limit for writing the entrance examinations of Engineering .



Education qualification-



The candidate must have cleared 10+2 from a recognised board of education in Science stream with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics/ Biology as core subjects.

The candidate should have either a Diploma in Food Sciences or Technology or a graduation degree in Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) Food Engineering or Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) in Food Technology.

Post graduation in MTech (Food Engineering) is also acceptable.



Skills Required-



 To become a Food Engineer/ Food Technologist it is very much important for the candidate to be able to have the following set if skills which will help in working efficiently and effectively -



Ability to adapt and enhance 



 One should be able to perform up to mark as per requirements of the situation and time.



Ability to solve and reason the problem



Handling problems in a very efficient manner and coming up with instant and feasible solutions is one of the skills required for this job profile of a Food Engineer.



Awareness and Alertness



The candidate needs to be aware of all the latest developments and advancements in the field of science and food technology



Excellent understanding of surroundings and its requirements



Working according to the requirements of the consumers and according to the demands of the company is what one needs to imbibe in order to stand out.



Working Environment -

A food technologist is sometimes supposed to work in laboratories dealing with sample testing and analysing

At times, he or she may be asked to do some field work which involves making sure that the production and manufacturing of the products is running smoothly

There can also be a constant demand for efforts in terms of creativity, data analysis, project management and strategic planning.  

A food engineer is required to work for minimum 35 hours in a week

One can perform the duty partly from home and partly working at the office.

title: GREEN TECHNOLOGIST
summary: CAREER PROFILE: GREEN TECHNOLOGIST
description:
CAREER PROFILE: GREEN TECHNOLOGIST 



Overview:

Who is a Green Technologist?

 

Green technologist is a type of technology that is considered environmentally friendly.

They aim to reduce the emissions of carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gasses. 

They encompass a wide area of scientific research, material science, atmospheric science and hydrology. 





What kind of Job is it, Field or desk?

The job of green technologists is that they describe the use of technology and science to reduce human impacts on the natural environment. 

It is generally a desk job, laboratory and field job. 





Roles and responsibilities of a Green Technologist:

The roles and responsibilities of a Green Technologist are mentioned below:

Identify scientific tests

Green Technologists have to collect different samples and then do scientific tests of it and analyze the result and make a report of it. The test can be of anything related to the environment like soil testing, type of the plants etc.









Prepare charts and reports that summarize test results

After testing they have to prepare the reports and charts about the analysis and summarize the results in an understandable way. 









Maintain equipment that is used to monitor pollution levels.

They have to maintain different equipment that they use on a regular basis for testing and recording. For example,  taking care of the equipment used to monitor pollution levels.









Discuss test results and analyze with their clients.

Then they have to discuss the whole procedure of the test and the final conclusions and results with the clients and discuss the solutions. 









Collect samples of air, soil, water and other materials for laboratory analysis. 

The most important thing to do is take and collect samples of different parts of the environment like air, soil, water etc.







Benefits of Choosing Green Technologist As A Career Profile:

Better understanding of the world:





Studying subjects such as physics, chemistry and biology can be helpful in learning. They assigned projects and assignments. 



Scientific progress- a crucial part of our future:



Many changes and inventions occurred for the better development of the country. Mankind is making a huge impact on the environment and resources.  





Large number of opportunities:



A physics degree can lead to a career in various sectors while a degree in biology can lead to a career such as biomedicine, agriculture. 



Decent salary



Green Technologists have a decent salary and can have a good standard of living . They have salaries equivalent to the different career profiles and do not have a low salary.



Environment caring  



The job does a good job because they are working for the betterment of the environment. Therefore it also gives job satisfaction to the employee.



 

                                                      

How To Become a Green Technologist?



To become a green technologist right after 10th follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete 10th and choose science stream.

Step-2: Complete 12th in science stream with physics, chemistry and biology as their main subjects.

Step-3: Appear for the Green Technologist Entrance Exam 

Step-4: Qualify the entrance exam for this career 

Step5: qualify bachelor degree such as B.SC or B.Tech in green technologist.

Step6: after obtaining a graduation degree. 

Step-7: obtain licensure in this field after completing a diploma, bachelor or master’s degree 

Step-8: Earn additional certifications and continue practicing.

 

Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

The age limit for becoming a Green Technologist is maximum 22. 

Educational qualifications:

A student must have passed 12th in science stream with at least 70% aggregate marks from  any recognized board.

For admission, candidates can apply with a 10th DMC certificate, having at least 50% aggregate marks.

They can do a bachelor's degree with 60% marks from a recognized college. 





Skill requirements:

To become a green technologist, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Analytical skills:



Environment science technicians must be able to carry out wide range laboratory and field results. 



Critical thinking skill:



They reach their conclusions through sound listening and judgment. 



Interpersonal Skills 



They need to be able to work well with others as a part of a team, as they often work together in a laboratory. 



Listening Skills:



They must follow the instructions given to them by environmental scientists and specialists. 



Speaking  skills:





They must be able to discuss their results of the work with their clients and colleagues.  



Work Environment:

Green Technologist have to mainly work in laboratories for experimentation and for taking samples they have to go to different places like near to a river or a dam, different land for testing its soil , in cities for checking population level etc.

title: Gastroenterologists
summary: Career profile: Gastroenterologist
description:
Career profile: Gastroenterologist



               



Overview 



Who is a Gastroenterologist ? 





Gastroenterologists are doctors who diagnose and treat digestive system issues and sometimes deal with tests and minor surgeries. 



They concentrate problems like the small intestine, gallbladder, colon and pancreas. 



              





They cure gastrointestinal and hematological diseases. 



What kind of career profile is it ? 



The job of a gastroenterologist focuses on acute digestive problems and stomach related problems.

 

This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with physicians, nurses and medical practitioners.



A gastroenterologist has to work for hours continuously and monitor the nursing staff. 





Roles and Responsibilities of a Gastroenterologist? 



The roles and responsibilities of a gastroenterologist are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows :





Gastroenterologists focus on a patient's health and monitor their medical history. 



                           



They provide records and reports to the government officials and advise diet plans to patients. 



               



They perform specialized tests including X-Ray, MRI, and ultrasound scanning.





                 



They manage endoscopic procedures and provide medications to the patients. 



                 



They perform minor and major surgeries in the digestive tracts.



                        



                   



Benefits of Choosing Gastroenterologist as a Career Profile : 





High Employment rates



             

             



There is a high employment rate as gastroenterologists  can work in different types of places such as Government Health Departments, National and Global Medical Research Centers and Private Hospitals.



Opportunity to take part in health policies



                



Gastroenterologists analyze and investigate the roots of a disease and also take part in health policies and government policies.  If you want to take part in people's  welfare and want to make new developments in the field of medicine, you can choose this career option. 



Traveling outdoors



              



Gastroenterologists need to travel to several government hospitals and rural areas and cure digestive problems. They need to provide medications and create awareness camps. 





Working overtime



             



Gastroenterologists work on complex gastric problems and sometimes perform endoscopy. They sometimes need to work overtime and cure various stomach problems. 







How To Become a Gastroenterologist? 





To become a gastroenterologist, you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard: 



STEP -1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to take science  for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2 : Students need to study in a recognized institution and also obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12  in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Students need to  appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different colleges  in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree. In some cases, students are chosen by the colleges  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree and get a M.B.B.S. degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters in Gastroenterology. 



STEP - 6 : Students can work as an assistant  and get exposed to a real working environment. 





Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become a Gastroenterologist : 

  

Age Limit :  





  The minimum age to apply as a gastroenterologist is 18 years. 



As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 



Educational Qualifications: 



A student must secure  50-60 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree. 



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course. 



Entrance Exams: 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify in

 National Eligibility cum Entrance Test for Post Graduation, 

All India Institute Of Medical Science in order to be eligible as a gastroenterologist. 





Working Environment: 



Gastroenterologists work in various kinds of places like Private Hospitals, Global Medical Research Centers, NGOs.



 They also work with different teams of doctors and nurses. 



Gastroenterologists usually need to work for 40 hours and focus on gastric and pancreas problems. 



Skills Requirements : 



Good Communication Skills

    

            



Communication is the act of transferring information and aids the speakers to become an extrovert person. Good communication caters in seeking a good position in a company

STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters in Gastroenterology. 



STEP - 6 : Students can work as an assistant  and get exposed to a real working environment. 





Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become a Gastroenterologist : 

  

Age Limit :  





  The minimum age to apply as a gastroenterologist is 18 years. 



As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 



Educational Qualifications: 



A student must secure  50-60 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree. 



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course. 



Entrance Exams: 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify in

 National Eligibility cum Entrance Test for Post Graduation, 

All India Institute Of Medical Science in order to be eligible as a gastroenterologist. 





Working Environment: 



Gastroenterologists work in various kinds of places like Private Hospitals, Global Medical Research Centers, NGOs.



 They also work with different teams of doctors and nurses. 



Gastroenterologists usually need to work for 40 hours and focus on gastric and pancreas problems. 



Skills Requirements : 



Good Communication Skills

    

            



Communication is the act of transferring information and aids the speakers to become an extrovert person. Good communication caters in seeking a good position in a company and gastroenterologists need to have good communication skills and focus on specialized tests. 





Good listening skills



           



  Listening and speaking are directly proportional to each other and is a perfect way of living life. It helps to learn the intricate details of patients' problems and medications. 





Time management



             



             



Time management  involves proper planning and goal setting. It elevates people's position and provides a positive energy to work for longer hours. 





Practical knowledge



               



Practical skills and analytical thinking improves, creates the abilities to solve problems and  opens the real competitive environment of the world which helps the person to learn more things.

Types of Specializations available for a Gastroenterologist: 



Medical Gastroenterologists



                

               



Medical Gastroenterologists focus on the digestive tract and are medical professionals. 





They perform minor and major surgeries including gallbladder and pancreas problems. 



Nutrition Specialist





                



Nutrition Specialists work in public and private health centres and focus on patient's diet plans. 



They provide advice on eating nutritious food. 



Hepatologists 



              



Hepatologists focus on liver, pancreas and gallbladder problems. 



They work on long term projects and manage surgeries. 





Colonoscopist 



           

                



Colonoscopists are specialized in colonoscopy and concentrate on the entire colon. 





They treat and cure problems like irritated tissues, polyps or cancer. 





Hepatologists



                    



Hepatologists are specialized in hepatology and treat liver problems. 



They treat and cure pathologies of the biliary duct.

Promotions And Upgrading Of A Gastroenterologists : 

 

Gastroenterologists have various scopes in different fields and follow a hierarchy.  There are different levels of gastroenterologists which are as follows: 





LEVEL -1 : Primary Health Worker 





             



Primary health takes care of the patients and concentrates on the welfare of the patients in every ward of the hospital. 





LEVEL -2 : Medical Officer 



             



Medical officers monitor patients and take care of the records and reports of the patients. They can work part - time and full time according to their wishes and company's contract. 





LEVEl - 3 : Researcher 



               



Researchers create new techniques and theories with the help of which the doctors are able to cure the patients. 



LEVEL - 4 : Surgeons





              



Surgeons manage operations and surgeries of tumors, pancreatitis and gallbladder stones. They work full time and also work independently.

title: General Surgeon
summary: Name of the Career profile: General Surgeon
description:
Name of the Career profile: General Surgeon 



                        



Overview



General surgery is a surgical specialty in which doctors conduct treatments for a wide range of common diseases. They are also in charge of patient care before, during, and after operations. General surgeons can diagnose and treat a wide range of disorders, particularly those affecting the abdominal organs.



Roles and responsibilities of a General Surgeon







Manage the surgical team by guiding them throughout the operations or surgeries and performing procedures on their own also for the treatment of the patient.





Creating the whole roadmap of the operation to be performed whether it is Preoperative, intraoperative, or postoperative.



Telling the patients about the medication and sharing surgical outcomes with patients and medical teams as well.





Making sure that all medical laws, norms, regulations, and standards are followed also the policies and procedures of the healthcare facility.









Benefits of choosing this profile as a career 



High-paying jobs-The income is one of the most appealing aspects of becoming a Surgeon. Although juniors may not immediately notice a financial boost, general practitioners with experience can earn three-figure incomes.





Job stability-Aside from a high graduate employment rate, doctors seldom struggle to keep their employment. Unlike other professions where employees may be concerned about being relevant, surgeons do not encounter this difficulty. 





Continuous education-In the medical area, new ways, and discoveries are constantly being discovered. This entails ongoing learning and the ability to expand on your current expertise as a surgeon . 







Gain respect-When you work as a surgeon, you are in a position of power, and people will respect your judgments and suggestions.  This leads to a sense of fulfilment and confidence in your talents.





A basic procedure to become a General Surgeon:

       

       The basic procedure to be a general surgeon 



       STEP:1 After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology



       STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English as a     

        compulsory subjects so that you can become eligible for MBBS.



       STEP:3 Complete your MBBS.



       STEP:4 Get a Post Graduation degree that is a Master's of Surgery.



       After completing your MS degree, you will be able to effectively practice as a surgeon in India.

       



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment



             ELIGIBILITY 



             Age Limit-



The candidate must be of minimum age limit is 17 years to give the medical entrance exam and maximum age of 25 years.          



             Education Qualification-



Candidates must possess an MBBS degree.

Followed by Master of Surgery.



             SKILLS REQUIRED



Knows how to effectively manage a surgical team which means they should have better management skills.





Caring nature so that person could provide exceptional patient care and have strong surgical skills.





Demonstrates strong analytical and problem-solving skills to deal with unknown challenges during the surgeries.





Works calmly and diligently in fast-paced and high-stress medical situations which means one should have the patience to deal with patients



.



.

Entrance exams:



Scorecards from specific national level common entrances which are accepted by many institutions, such as





National Entrance cum Eligibility Test( NEET)

Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education and Research ( PGIMER)

All India Institute of Medical Sciences.  (AIIMS)

Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Medical Sciences entrance exam

Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University Entrance Exam

Manipal Entrance Test

Christian Medical College Entrance Exam

Promotions and Upgradations:





Some surgeons improve their careers because they specialise on a certain part of the body, such as the heart or brain. Hence this could be upgradation for them.

Some may prefer to teach once they have excelled in their careers since academic settings are often slower-paced and less stressful than medical settings.

Many surgeons find it enjoyable to teach junior surgeons the approaches and ideas that have worked for them.



Speciality under General surgery:



There are several subspecialties under General Surgery such as:



Breast Surgery-Breast surgery can be done on either a male or female patient (s). Breast operations come in a variety of forms. Some are done for medical conditions like cancer, while others are done for aesthetic purposes.



colorectal-surgery for colorectal cancer terms during a colonoscopy, which is a process in which a special device (the colonoscope) is introduced into the rectum to inspect the rectum and colon, polyps, which are tiny growths on the inner lining of the colon, are removed.



 endocrine surgery-Surgery of the endocrine glands, such as the thyroid, parathyroid, adrenal, endocrine pancreatic, and several neuroendocrine glands, is the subject of the surgical subspecialty known as "endocrine surgery.



 upper and lower gastrointestinal (GI)-Diseases of the digestive system's organs are treated by gastrointestinal surgery. This comprises the stomach, small intestine, big intestine, rectum, and oesophagus . The pancreas, liver, and gallbladder are also included.



 transplant (kidney, liver, pancreas)-In a transplant procedure, a donor's healthy organ is removed and given to a recipient whose own organ has failed or been irreparably damaged. Usually, this operation saves lives.



 vascular surgery- vascular surgeons concentrate particularly on the circulatory system. Patients who have conditions, especially affecting the arteries and veins are frequently referred to vascular surgeons.



Top recruiters for General Surgeon:



Tata Memorial Hospitals





Apollo hospital



Medanta



 Lilawati Hospitals



   Fortis hospitals









 

Demands and future scope:





The report also stated that the chances of the ratio improving over the next 15 years are slim. Even by 2030, when the population is expected to reach 1.476 billion. 



 It is clearly obvious that India requires physicians, particularly General Surgeons, who are in high demand.

General surgeons could also further in future work in

Clinic Practice:- For many doctors, opening a clinic has been a popular choice. It has done so because it provides the ability to charge rates according to convenience.



Government Position:- There is a high need for physicians in the public sector. Additionally, they receive a high and typical income in addition to additional perks.



Private Sector:- India has developed as a medical tourism destination for both domestic and foreign patients. Many international hospital systems are in dire need of competent and skilled workers. 

Super-Specialization:- Those with a master's degree who are interested in pursuing a super-specialty have the option of continuing their study. Super-specialty hospitals favor doctors with advanced degrees in their fields.





Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile



Dr. Praveen Garg





Dr. Praveen Kumar Garg is a top surgical oncologist with over 21 years of expertise in treating patients with all types of malignancies and tumours. He is a highly trained surgeon who specialises in oncological procedures for the treatment of Lung Cancer, Gastrointestinal Cancer, Soft Tissue Sarcomas, and Gynaecological Cancers.



Dr. Sharat Verma





Dr. Sharat Varma is a skilled surgeon with expertise in Paediatric Gastroenterology, Hepatology, and Liver Transplantation. He has 14 years of experience and has worked in renowned medical institutions in India, Europe, and the United States. His main interest is transplant Hepatology.



Dr. Manish Joshi





Dr. Manish Joshi is a well-known and skilled surgeon who specialises in Surgical Gastroenterology, GI Oncology, and Advanced Laparoscopic treatments. He is an experienced Gastroenterologist with over 19 years of surgical expertise.



Dr. Neelam Mohan



Dr. Neelam Mohan is one of India's finest paediatric gastroenterologists and hepatologists. She has conducted several procedures with outstanding results throughout her 23 years of expertise. In 2016, the President of India bestowed the B. C. Roy National Award for her.



Dr. Deepak Govil



Dr. Deepak Govil is a highly skilled GI and laparoscopic surgeon with several awards to his name. Dr. Govil has done all sorts of major Gastric-surgeries, from general to advanced, in his distinguished medical career, having received education and training from leading medical institutes.

title: Geological Engineer
summary: An overview of career profile-
description:
Geological Engineer



   An overview of career profile-

Geological Engineers belong to the field of technical science wherein the aspirants are supposed to apply their knowledge of engineering in solving geological problems such as landslides, environmental pollution, earthquakes, mining etc.

 To become a geological engineer, one should have graduated in the Engineering Geology which is one of the various branches of engineering. 

Roles and Responsibilities: 

With job comes along many roles and responsibilities and therefore, following are few of the many roles and activities a Geological Engineer needs to fulfill- 

Analyzing the collected geographical data and producing results



 Assist in preparation of construction plans, specifications and cost estimates including material quantity calculations.





Manage the operational activities of field works



Assigning duties to junior staff 



Report geotechnical recommendations integral to successful design, construction and maintenance of roads, airports and other engineered structures



Advantages of being a geologist-

Increase in vacancies 



Demand for geologists is increasing nowadays considering the ongoing situation of environment and earth.

Good amount of Salary 



Geologists are paid heavily depending upon their designation, qualification and experience

Work around the world 



 Getting employed across the world is also one of the many benefits of being a geological engineer. As per the demand of manpower, a geological engineer can be called upon anywhere. 

Beneficial impacts on the surroundings and the society 



Working for the betterment of the environment is equal to bringing goodness to the society

   A basic procedure to approach this job:



Step 1 - The aspirant needs to complete class 10th and class 12th in Science stream with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics as the core subjects. 



Step 2 - Apply for the admission process into different colleges and universities.

 

Step 3 - Proceed by appearing for the entrance examination meant for Geological Engineering.



Step 4 - After the clarification of required documents and passing the examination, the candidate will get admission into the institution as per the preference. 



Step 5 - Complete the 4-year long engineering course in geology.



Step 6 - Appear for the entrance exam meant for Geological Engineering, i.e. JEE Mains, JEE Advance, VITEEE, BITSAT etc. The course of geological engineering is accredited by the Accreditation Board of Engineering and Technology (ABET).



Step 7 - Once the candidate has a required graduation degree, the candidate can now practice by applying for various internship programs.

   Eligibilities & Skill required & the working environment:

 



Age limit- 



There is no specific age limit for becoming an engineer. But there is definitely a certain age limit for writing the entrance examinations of Engineering.

Education qualification-





The candidate must have cleared 10+2 from a recognized board of education with Science stream.

The candidate must have scored an aggregate of 50% in all the subjects.

The candidate should have a graduation degree in Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) in  Geological Engineering. 

Post-graduation in MTech in Geological Engineering is also beneficial in becoming a Geologist.

For higher studies, one can get admission into Doctorate of Geological Engineering.



Skills Required-





 To become a geological engineer, it is very much important for the candidate to be able to have the following set if skills which will help in working efficiently and effectively –



1. Good communication and written skills

	

2. Ability to come forth with advance and creative ideas



3. Awareness and sincerity towards the work allotted



4. Should be able to work both indoors and outdoors



5. Accepting challenges and deadlines







 Working Environment- 







Physical hardships - Even though it seems that a geologist needs to make use of theoretical knowledge, at the same time the engineer needs to work both indoors and outdoors.  

Working Hours- sometimes geologists are also required to work more than supposed working hours depending upon the need of the hour. Usually, they are expected to work for 40 hour/per week. 

Healthy relationships at the working place- the employee needs to maintain good working relations with his or her colleagues. Sometimes, because of the kind of working environment, geological engineers might not be able to maintain a good level of communication with other people. 



  Entrance exam to follow the route:



Serial No.

Name of the Exam

Examination Conducting Body

1.

JEE (Joint Entrance Exam)Mains

National Testing Agency (NTA)

2.

VITEEE (Vellore Institute of Technology Engineering Entrance Examination)

VIT University

3.

BITSAT (Birla Institute of Technology and Sciences Admission Test)

Birla Institute of Technology and Science

4.

JEE (Joint Entrance Exam) Advanced

One

Promotions & upgrading:



Most geologists do field exploration and lab testing in their first two years. Usually paired with a “mentor” or “senior geologist,” entry-level employees visit sites and learn how to take samples, label and store them 

Many in the field are promoted to “senior geologist” positions which involve the supervision of newer geologists and have more supervisory and oversight responsibilities, moving away from those as tester or field agent

The majority (over 60 percent) of ten-year geological survivors are employed by large companies, universities, or the federal government. The remaining 40 percent are private consultants or work for small firms with fewer than six employees.

   Demands & Future Scope



Geological engineering is one of the top-paying careers these days. Its scope is much higher abroad. The geologist job market is expected to grow by 14.1% between 2016 and 2026 in the US.

. One can seek jobs in the following fields:

Residential constructions

Commercial projects

Governmental installations

Mine, forestry, and coastal developments

Public works department

Research and Development

The average salary of a geologist is around 7 LPA.

Countries like the US, UK, and Australia also have greater opportunities.

 

Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:

1. James Hutton

A Scottish geologist, Hutton is also known as the father of modern geology and has a major hand in bringing forth geology as a modern science.



2. Charles Lyell

Born on 14 November 1797, Charles Lyell is the author of the famous “ Principles of Geology” which was published in the year 1830-1833. He has a major contribution in establishing the causes behind the natural occurrence of earth’s phenomenon. 



3. Mary Horner Lyell

Wife of Mr. Charles Lyell, Mary Horner Lyell was a very famous conchologist and geologist who gave her contribution in the field of geology by assisting her husband in many of the major projects.







4. Alfred Wegener

Mr. Wegener was a German geologist who was known for his experiments and findings in the field of meteorology. He was also a geophysicist, meteorologist, climatologist and a polar researcher.



5. Inge Lehmann

Awarded with the William Bowie Medal in the year 1971, Inge Lehmann was a Danish seismologist and geophysicist who established the fact that earth has a solid inner core which is covered with molten outer core.

title: Governor
summary: Name of the career profile-Governor
description:
Name of the career profile-Governor









An overview of career a profile:

A Governor is the nominal head of the state with him not holding any executive powers generally. 

A governor acts as the union government’s representative of the state and is rarely native to the state that he’s appointed

Most of the Executive and legislative powers are vested in the governor. 

A Governor is also the primary head in many Union territories, where there isn't a chief minister. 



Roles and responsibilities of a Governor 



To appoint the chief minister of the state as well as the cabinet of ministers accompanying the CM. 





















To oversee the smooth functioning of the state on the behalf of the union/Central government as an officer loyal to the center. 





He also has the responsibility to select the State budget and all the bills can only be passed with his approval.























Maintaining control over a state in case of an emergency where the governor becomes the real head of the state as an agent of the president. 

Passing any legislative bill is also the job of the governor which can only be passed with his or her approval





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Ability to change the state and country, since they hold all the legislative powers, hence can implement schemes for betterment of people. 







A Governor is a very esteemed post and people revere the person with much respect in India, because of their high standing.















Various benefits, including allowances for various amenities such as electricity, phone bills, convenience and accommodation. 





The power to elect and choose the leaders of the state and if they don't satisfy him/her, has the power to dissolve them. 

A massive allotted house along with a lavish lifestyle and salary without much traveling. 





 A basic procedure to approach this job: 

There is not a proven or tested basic method in order to become a governor but one of the best method to become one goes in the following way:



Step 1: First complete your 10th class with good grades with at least 65% marks. 

Step 2: After that complete 12th class with any stream of your choice with minimum 60% marks. 

Step 3: Take any bachelor’s or professional degree you want to take and make a good career in it so that you'll have an excellent academic record. 

Step 4: Give any civil service exam, in order to become a high ranking member of the civil force such as an IPS or IAS officer. 

Step 5: After this you'll have to continue working and making a good name for yourself. When you turn 35, you'll be officially eligible to be appointed a governor. 

Step 6: In order to increase your chances to be noticed by the President of India, you'll have to align yourself with powerful members of the ruling political party of the state. 

Step 7: Once the president asserts that you'll be a good pick, she may appoint you as the governor of the state she deems fit for you.



Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



Age limit 

Anyone to be appointed a governor can't be younger than 35. So the minimum age to be eligible for a chance to become governor is 35. 



Educational qualifications 

There is not any particular educational qualification needed in order to become a governor, but the more one is educated, the more appealing he or she will be for designation. 



Skill Requirements

The skills required of a governor are: 





Commitment

A governor has to be dedicated and committed to the welfare of his or her state so that they can make decisions that’ll benefit it. They'll have to think of their state before personal gain. 







Public speaking skills

A Governor should have very good public speaking skills since they'll have to present their views in front of all the cabinet and other ministers as well as impose his decisions on people under his or her command. 



Sense of justice

A governor should be a  very noble and virtue-driven person, because he has the responsibility to uphold law in the state he’s in charge of and for that he needs to have a strong sense of justice. 







Critical thinking 

  A governor should be able to think deeply about all the situations surrounding their state and     should be able to make swift decisions regarding the future of these situations.





Trustworthy

A governor has the charisma and the drive to deal with any kind of  person and be able to make those people trust him or her. He should be able to gain the confidence of the people and the cabinet of ministers.





















Creative

A governor should have skills to not only quickly and calmly solve any emergencies but also to find time saving and creative ways of dealing with these problems.



Work Environment 



The work environment of a governor generally involves meetings and discussion with both the ministers and the representatives of the people.

He also has the responsibility to select the State budget and all the bills can only be passed with his approval.























Maintaining control over a state in case of an emergency where the governor becomes the real head of the state as an agent of the president. 

Passing any legislative bill is also the job of the governor which can only be passed with his or her approval





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Ability to change the state and country, since they hold all the legislative powers, hence can implement schemes for betterment of people. 







A Governor is a very esteemed post and people revere the person with much respect in India, because of their high standing.















Various benefits, including allowances for various amenities such as electricity, phone bills, convenience and accommodation. 





The power to elect and choose the leaders of the state and if they don't satisfy him/her, has the power to dissolve them. 

A massive allotted house along with a lavish lifestyle and salary without much traveling.

There is not a proven or tested basic method in order to become a governor but one of the best method to become one goes in the following way:



Step 1: First complete your 10th class with good grades with at least 65% marks. 

Step 2: After that complete 12th class with any stream of your choice with minimum 60% marks. 

Step 3: Take any bachelor’s or professional degree you want to take and make a good career in it so that you'll have an excellent academic record.

Step 6: In order to increase your chances to be noticed by the President of India, you'll have to align yourself with powerful members of the ruling political party of the state. 

Step 7: Once the president asserts that you'll be a good pick, she may appoint you as the governor of the state she deems fit for you.



Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



Age limit 

Anyone to be appointed a governor can't be younger than 35. So the minimum age to be eligible for a chance to become governor is 35. 



Educational qualifications 

There is not any particular educational qualification needed in order to become a governor, but the more one is educated, the more appealing he or she will be for designation. 



Skill Requirements

The skills required of a governor are: 





Commitment

A governor has to be dedicated and committed to the welfare of his or her state so that they can make decisions that’ll benefit it. They'll have to think of their state before personal gain. 







Public speaking skills

A Governor should have very good public speaking skills since they'll have to present their views in front of all the cabinet and other ministers as well as impose his decisions on people under his or her command. 



Sense of justice

A governor should be a  very noble and virtue-driven person, because he has the responsibility to uphold law in the state he’s in charge of and for that he needs to have a strong sense of justice. 







Critical thinking 

  A governor should be able to think deeply about all the situations surrounding their state and     should be able to make swift decisions regarding the future of these situations.





Trustworthy

A governor has the charisma and the drive to deal with any kind of  person and be able to make those people trust him or her. He should be able to gain the confidence of the people and the cabinet of ministers.





















Creative

A governor should have skills to not only quickly and calmly solve any emergencies but also to find time saving and creative ways of dealing with these problems.



Work Environment 



The work environment of a governor generally involves meetings and discussion with both the ministers and the representatives of the people.

It means a governor constantly works in close proximity within the state and doesn't have to travel far from the state. 

A governor also has to regularly meet with the center to discuss various things. 

A Governor has a very busy routine split between surveys,meetings and other similar activities which rarely give him the time to relax.

This rigid working lifestyle cannot be made flexible since a governor has to always be prepared for work.



Entrance exam to follow the route:



There are entrance exams for governors, as each state's governor is directly picked by the President of India, thus you can't give an exam to become one. 



Course description with their expenses: 



Courses after class 12th 

People of any branch and any degree can be chosen for the post of a governor, so you should focus on a subject you like and remember to score good in it. 



Courses after graduation

Just like with graduation, it doesn't really matter which part of the graduate degree you pursue since the only eligibility of the governor is to be minimum 35 years of age. 



Top institutions to pursue the course:

There are not any top institutions in particular, since there is not any limitation on branch or stream to be chosen in order to be appointed. 



Type of specialization available under this job profiles :

While there are no specializations per se under this job profile, in India governors are of 2 types:



State Governor

A state governor is the nominal head of the state, who is a representative of the central government and holds the legislative and judicial powers of the state. Even though he doesn't have executive powers, all decisions must be at first. 





Lieutenant Governor

In the union territories of India, there is no chief minister and all the executive, legislative and judicial powers to govern the state lie with the lieutenant governor who directly reports everything to the President of India. 



Although not a political post, there's a third line of governors present in India, who's the governor of the Reserve Bank of India or RBI. He's the top person of India's entire banking system. 





Pay-Scales in this career profile:



Salary according to Job Designation: 

According to their designation, the 2 governors are paid the following salaries



Designation

Salary 

State Governor

350,000 Rs/month + Allowances

Lieutenant Governor

225,000 Rs/month +Allowances 







Payscale according to experience:



While there are no raises in the governor's salary which is fixed, the allowances given can change depending on Schedule and economy of the country/state. 



Promotions & upgradings:



A governor also has the chance to run for the Chief minister, President and Prime minister elections by joining any political party who it's eligible for contention. 

The Governor of a State shall be appointed by the President by warrant under his hand and seal. Article 156. Term of office of Governor : (1) The Governor shall hold office during the pleasure of the President.

A person becoming the governor, holds the office for 5 years after which they can be Re-Appointed or not.



Top recruiting agencies for this profile



The only bodies which employ or appoint a governor are the state and union territories. Hence they are the only recruiters.







Demands & Future Scope 



A governor is a designation for which one would have to work a lot and wait for a long-time in order for it to be fulfilled and thus there is no demand or future scope for this profile since it entirely depends on the president. 





Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Some famous people who also followed this profile are:



Shri Phagu Chauhan

He is the 29th and current governor of the state of Bihar. He is also a member of the 17th legislative assembly of the state of Uttar Pradesh from where he also contested for the elections. 





P. S. Sreedharan Pillai

He is the current  and 19th governor of the state of goa and in addition to that he had also previously been the governor of the state of mizoram. He is also an Attorney.





Bandaru Dattatreya 

He is the current governor of Haryana and the 21st one. He is originally from Telangana and has also previously held the office of the governor in Himachal Pradesh.







Anandaben Patel 

She is the current governor of Uttar Pradesh and has previously served as first female chief minister of the state of gujarat. She was also an education cabinet minister from 2002-07.







La. Ganesan

He is the current governor of West Bengal and also the provisional governor of Manipur.

title: Hospital Adminstrator
summary: Name of the career profile: Hospital Administrator
description:
Name of the career profile: Hospital Administrator







An overview of career a profile:

A hospital administrator is one on whom lies the entire responsibility of managing the hospital ,such as managing the various employees,taking care of the needs of the patients,organising and maintaining the various services offered by the hospital.

They also plan all the budgets and plan the hiring of new employees in addition to renewal of old contracts.

They also take care of all the  financial and technological matters of the hospital.



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Ensuring quality of the various services offered by the hospital in addition to watching that all the medicine and drugs are of top notch quality without compromise.







Recruiting new doctors, nurses, cleaners and other hospital employees in order to maintain the roster of the hospital and to make sure that there is no shortage of staff in cases of emergencies.







Creating new policies so that there will be no loophole in the hospital protocol and to ensure that it follows all the guidelines as mentioned by the medical association of india.







Development of the infrastructure of the hospital and to help in the progress of the hospital by the means of keeping the latest medical machines and implements. 







They also handle the public relations on behalf of the hospital to make sure that a fair amount of public exposure is given to the hospital to make sure patients will prefer to get admitted due to a good public image.

















Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Great demand and ample career opportunities



Healthcare administration  has been on a steady rise in recent years due to rapid increase in the number of medical facilities. Due to this there is a greater need for those in the administration role to manage these new facilities.



Lucrative salary packages and frequent pay hikes. 





Hospital administration boasts of one of the highest fresher salaries in the medical and management fields combined. This is due to the fact that despite high demand there is a shortage of workforce.



It is a very rewarding work



As someone who directly contributes to the welfare and safety of people, it's a job profile that results in a lot of self satisfaction. Unlike doctors however, medical administrators have the power to affect a  whole hospital which make them a powerful means of change





Lots of opportunity for progress 



A medical administrator is a post which allows one to really branch out into a variety of different roles. This is due to the fact that medical administrator in itself is a role in between two different fields, thus the practitioner can engage in a variety of future roles .



An overall fun and interesting job





Management is a role in which you'll have to meet with a lot of different people on a daily basis, due to which, you'll always be in interesting discussions and thus will never consider your job boring. 



Unaffected by recessions and economic struggles 





The role is unaffected by the myriad ups and downs of the global economy. Even in the harshest conditions, hospitals will continue to be operated, resulting in continued opportunities. 



A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a Hospital Administrator, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects in either Science or Commerce branch. 

Step-2: Prepare for the Entrance Exams or college necessary for getting into your college of choice. 

Step-3: Earn a 3 year Bachelor's degree in any relevant degree in hospital management.

Step-4: Sit in College Campus for job opportunities or apply Off Campus.

Step-5: Alternatively you can go for a Master's degree and improve your knowledge and skills and then go for employment. 

Eligibility and skills requirements:

Age Limit



There is no age limit for being a Hospital Administrator though the minimum age limit is 21.

Educational qualifications 

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied commerce or science. 

They ought to have a 3-year bachelor's degree in hospital management or administration in hospitality.

Sometimes an additional master’s degree or MBA is also required. 



Skill requirements



 To become a successful hospital administrator, the following skills are necessary:



Communication



A hospital administrator should have excellent communication skills showing them to converse in a variety of situations since they'll have to talk with patients, medical staff, board members as well as non medical staff, for which they need to be fluent..

Teamwork



A good administrator knows his strengths and weaknesses. That's why he should be able to make any Target possible with the help of a whole team. Delegation of work to the appropriate specialists and settling all the management work is what a Administrator must know

Planning ability



In an ever changing industry such as the healthcare fie

Management is a role in which you'll have to meet with a lot of different people on a daily basis, due to which, you'll always be in interesting discussions and thus will never consider your job boring. 



Unaffected by recessions and economic struggles 





The role is unaffected by the myriad ups and downs of the global economy. Even in the harshest conditions, hospitals will continue to be operated, resulting in continued opportunities. 



A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a Hospital Administrator, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects in either Science or Commerce branch. 

Step-2: Prepare for the Entrance Exams or college necessary for getting into your college of choice. 

Step-3: Earn a 3 year Bachelor's degree in any relevant degree in hospital management.

Step-4: Sit in College Campus for job opportunities or apply Off Campus.

Step-5: Alternatively you can go for a Master's degree and improve your knowledge and skills and then go for employment. 

Eligibility and skills requirements:

Age Limit



There is no age limit for being a Hospital Administrator though the minimum age limit is 21.

Educational qualifications 

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied commerce or science. 

They ought to have a 3-year bachelor's degree in hospital management or administration in hospitality.

Sometimes an additional master’s degree or MBA is also required. 



Skill requirements



 To become a successful hospital administrator, the following skills are necessary:



Communication



A hospital administrator should have excellent communication skills showing them to converse in a variety of situations since they'll have to talk with patients, medical staff, board members as well as non medical staff, for which they need to be fluent..

Teamwork



A good administrator knows his strengths and weaknesses. That's why he should be able to make any Target possible with the help of a whole team. Delegation of work to the appropriate specialists and settling all the management work is what a Administrator must know

Planning ability



In an ever changing industry such as the healthcare field, one who is at the administrator post, should be aware of all the new and ongoing wellness policies and be quick to act upon them and make contingency plans well in advance to prevent mishaps 

Problem solving



A crisis can happen anytime in a hospital and to prevent that from turning into a big problem is the job of the administrator. Be it a critical patient or any legal trouble to even the renovation of the hospital, solving all of these issues quickly is a necessary skill. 1

Patient care



Even though mostly an administrative role, a hospital administrator should be innately familiar on how to take care of sick people or patients undergoing treatment. They need to have a considerate and kind heart and be ready to serve. 

Data analysis



A hospital is full of data, ranging from patient records, to the details covering the various supplies, inventory of medicines and drugs and the health-care services. These along with the records of the employees and the finances are all analysed by the administrator so that the hospital functions smoothly. .

Work environment













Generally hospital administrators only have to go to the hospital they're employed at without much travelling related to work.

In case the hospital is part of a group, he or she may have to attend meetings and conferences in other cities but that's rare. 

They'll have to work with people from medical, finance and administration backgrounds along with patients. 

The work hours are generally long without much break time and some even on holidays since hospitals don't have a free day.



Entrance exam to follow the route:

There are a number of exams conducted on All-India, state level as well as individual come level for candidates who are interested in hospital administration. Some popular ones are:





Name of Examination

CAT(Common Admission Test)

MAT(Management Aptitude Test)

XAT(Xavier Aptitude Test)

CMAT( Common Management Admission Test)

SNAP (Symbiosis National Aptitude Test)

NMAT (National Management Aptitude Test)



Course description with their expenses: 

Undergraduate Hospital administration courses

The courses mentioned below can be pursued right after completing 12: 

Course name

Duration

Fees

1.5-4 Lacs

The courses mentioned below can be pursued after completing graduation:

Top institutions to pursue the course:



Beat colleges to pursue a hospital management degree are: 



Institution Name

DYPUSM Mumbai - School of Management Dr DY Patil University

IGNOU Delhi - Indira Gandhi National Open University

Jamia Hamdard, New Delhi

IMS DAVV Indore - Institute of Management Studies Devi Ahilya University

Tamil Nadu Open University, Chennai

Delhi Institute of Pharmaceutical Sciences and Research, New Delhi

GITAM Institute of Management, Visakhapatnam

AMU Aligarh - Aligarh Muslim University



Type of specialisation available under this job profiles:

There are many specialisations available under hospital administration. Some of them are

Finance Manager



They are responsible for maintaining all the financial records at a hospital. From paying wages to the employees to the restocking of inventory and the expenditure on maintenance is all up to them. They also manage payments given by patients and any grants received by the hospital. 

Manpower systems analyst





They are responsible for handling all the employees in a hospital. From the renewal of offer letters to the hiring of new doctors and support staff as well maintained cordial relations with the management and board of directors, it's all their job. 

Hospital Management



Hospital management is one of the most important roles all around the hospital. The manager is responsible for keeping the premises in a good condition, keeping sure that any renovation work is quickly completed and the overall look of the hospital is positive. 

Patient financial specialist



They work with the families or attendees of the patient and help them in understanding the hospital policies and about the payment options and signing of agreements along with helping them with public and private insurance. They help patients to find medical attention in a way they can afford. 

Program Manager



Program manager is in charge of supervising and organising projects as a project manager to make sure they are finished on schedule and within budget. They also see whether these programs follow the medical guidelines. 



Business Manager



They are responsible for seeing through the hospital’s public image, taking care of all the advertising and whether the image of the hospital is positive and favourable. He makes sure that the patients will find the hospital their best option.

Promotion and upgradation of this career profile:



The promotions and upgradation in this job profile go as follow:



Intern

Generally the candidates enter as intern or junior managers. Under this role they mostly perform Uber senior managers. After gaining relevance experience they get salary hikes and promotion.



Hospital management

Under this position they look after the welfare of the hospital directly. They also act as intermediates between the higher authority figures and the doctors and non medical workers. After a few years they get promoted to the post of a manager.



Program/Finance/Business Manager

Depending on their work niche and the kind of work they handled during their time as a primary manager, they get promoted in either business, finance or medical programs. In this they do specialised work only limited to their designation.



Specialist

After this comes the specialist also known as section head. This means the top position in the respective section and control all activities related to the niche.
