Demands & Future Scope 







As discussed above, people versed in hospital administration are in high demand in both India and abroad. 

This is because it is estimated that by 2025 the number of hospitals in India will see a rise especially in semi urban areas. 

This demands a large number of both medical professionals as well as management specialists. so this is currently one of the fastest career profiles.



Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Sandra L. Fenwick







Sandra is the CEO of the Boston Children’s hospital and one of the leading figures in the welfare of children who has done much charity work. She is the holder of numerous honorary degrees due to her involvement in healthcare and recipient of many awards.



Katherine Kuzmeskas 

 

She is the founder of SimplyVital health which uses blockchain technology to produce a steady pipeline of medical data for both research and welfare purposes. She wants to move the healthcare system from fee-based to value care oriented industry.

Raj Panjabi



Raj is a medical administrator who, using his influence in the medical field, has been part of LastMile Health as its CEO and has been crucial in saving lives and providing healthcare in the most remote parts of the world.

Peter Pronovost



Pronovost’s work includes the creation and use of checklists for doctors that lessen the likelihood of medical errors which improved conditions of hospitals. States all throughout the nation have since adopted the method..

Bernard J. Tyson



Bernard J. Tyson is CEO of Kaiser Foundation Health Plan, Inc.& Hospitals, Kaiser Permanente, one of America's top healthcare organisations and a not-for-profit health plan, focuses on ensuring that its members have access to high-quality, affordable healthcare and enhancing the health of their communities..

description:
Human Resource Manager

                      

➢Overview

Human Resource manager as the name suggests, manages human resource of any organization. By human resource we mean the employees or the personnel.

They oversee various management related to employees like recruitments, Interviewing, planning, selecting, consulting with higher authorities  etc.

Therefore, they play a role of a link between the management of the organization and its employees.





Roles and Responsibilities 



Human Resource Planning



Their most important role is to plan about the human resource, that is, finding out the current and future needs of  human resources of the organization. They also have to plan the process and criteria of hiring any employee line skills required.

               



Job Analysis and Design



In this they have to analyze the essential qualifications, duties, tasks, projects, skills and knowledge for a certain job profile. And in job design they have to integrate the requirements of the employee with the aim of the organization.

               



Hiring Candidates

Their main job is to hire candidates according to the requirements of the country. The process is done by the human resource manager like interviewing the candidates, examining the resumes etc.

             

Training and Development



The other duties of HUman resource manager  include training and development of the hired candidates and of the employees. They need to provide training sessions when the candidates are hired to give them knowledge about the working of the organization.

 

                



Design Workplace Policies



Human resource managers have to design and develop workplace policies for the employees. Basically they make the agreements which are signed by employees about the policies, rules and regulation they have to be obliged to.

Earn A High Salary

The job of a human resource manager is financially rewarding because it comes in the top fields of management. So the salary of the HR manager is very high. 

   



Gain Job Stability and security

Human Resource management has a stable job growth and they have a job security because every firm needs human resource and to manage them they have a need of Human resource manager.

                





Get A Chance To Influence

The Human resource manager is the one who recruits the employees. So by choosing the right and perfect employees according to the requirements they can influence the growth of the organization. 

                  



Develop The People Around You

Roles and Responsibilities 



Human Resource Planning



Their most important role is to plan about the human resource, that is, finding out the current and future needs of  human resources of the organization. They also have to plan the process and criteria of hiring any employee line skills required.

In this they have to analyze the essential qualifications, duties, tasks, projects, skills and knowledge for a certain job profile. And in job design they have to integrate the requirements of the employee with the aim of the organization.

The other duties of HUman resource manager  include training and development of the hired candidates and of the employees. They need to provide training sessions when the candidates are hired to give them knowledge about the working of the organization.

 

                



Design Workplace Policies



Human resource managers have to design and develop workplace policies for the employees. Basically they make the agreements which are signed by employees about the policies, rules and regulation they have to be obliged to. 

             







➢ Benefits of choosing Human Resource Management as a career  



Earn A High Salary

The job of a human resource manager is financially rewarding because it comes in the top fields of management. So the salary of the HR manager is very high. 

   



Gain Job Stability and security

Human Resource management has a stable job growth and they have a job security because every firm needs human resource and to manage them they have a need of Human resource manager.

                





Get A Chance To Influence

The Human resource manager is the one who recruits the employees. So by choosing the right and perfect employees according to the requirements they can influence the growth of the organization. 

                  



Develop The People Around You



Human resource managers train the staff and the freshers according to the organization's requirements. Therefore, they help to develop the skills and knowledge of the people around them. And this helps the organization to grow more.



            



➢How to become an Human Resource Manager

Step 1 - Complete your 10+2 or equivalent in any stream from a recognized school with minimum 50% of the aggregate marks.



Step 2- Earn your bachelor's degree or equivalent in Human resource management from a government recognized college or university.



Step 3 - Earn a master's degree  in Human resource management from a government recognized college or university.







Step 4 - Apply to jobs

Once you have the required experience to qualify for an HR management position, start your application progress. Often, an HR manager position requires some years of experience





Eligibility and Skill requirements



Age Limit 

The minimum age for admission is 17 years old and there is no maximum age imit but some colleges have a age limit of 22 years for admission in bachelors degree.

              



Education Qualification



To be eligible to get admission to MA in Human Resource Management course, candidates need to have these requirements:



A candidate applying for the Human resource manager  admission must have passed 10+2 with any stream from a recognized school.

A candidate must have studied English at an Intermediate level till 10+2.

Candidates are required to have passed the qualifying exam with at least 50% aggregate.







Skills requirement to become human resource manager





 Communication skills



Human resource managers are required to have good communication skills because they have to communicate with other department employees and also during hiring they have to interview the candidates and have to also work with higher authorities and clients.

               



Problem-solving Attitude



 They should have the skills of problem solving because they have to listen to the problems of the employees and provide solutions to them. They have to make policies and rules so they need to have knowledge of the problem that occurred before.

            



Organizational skills

Human resource managers should have organizational skills because they have to keep the record of all the employees of the organization. So if they are not organized well it can lead to problems in future.



   



Negotiation skills



Human resource manager should have strong negotiation skills because it is required to negotiate with the employees at the time of hiring and also afterwards. When the employees ask for high salary they need to negotiate so that the organization does not have to incur high costs.

               



Strategic thinking



They should think of strategies and techniques through which they can increase the productivity and knowledge of the employees so that it can be beneficial to the organization.

                



 Decision-Making Ability

Human resource managers should have good decision making skills because they have the authority and responsibility to decide and hire the employees according to all the requirements. If decisions are not correct it can hinder the growth of the organization.

  



Working Environment 



The working environment of the human resource manager is in the office of the company and with the clients and employees.

They do not get field work anytime and they have flexible work hours.

They work for 9 hours a day(9 to 5) and have to work 5 to 6 days a week according to the organization.

In this specialization they have to take care of the training and development of the newly hired employees and also the employees which were already there. They have to examine that the employees are aware about how to work with the technologies or computers. 











Compensation and Benefits

In this specialization they have to take care of the payments of the employees and the benefits they will get. They also of have to see the performance of the employees and give incentives if the performance is good



Recruiting

Their work is to recruit and hire new employees when needed. They need to do the whole process of hiring employees which include shortlisting resumes, taking tests, interviewing and everything related to it.





Financial Management

In this specialization human resource manager work out all the finances related to the employees. This includes the total cost incurred on human resource, their insurance policies premiums, their public provident funds etc.

title: IAS
summary: The administrative branch of the All India Services of the Indian Government is the Indian Administrative Service (IAS).
description:
CAREER PROFILE : IAS







Overview



           Who is an IAS?



The administrative branch of the All India Services of the Indian Government is the Indian Administrative Service (IAS).







The IAS is an integral part of the permanent bureaucracy of the country and the executive branch of the Government of India, just like in other parliamentary democracies.



What kind of Job is it, Field or desk?

A large portion of an IAS officer's job description is office administration (desk work and file monitoring tasks) and touring, in which the officers visit designated areas of the district or the State and observe activities taking place there.

Roles and responsibilities of an IAS Officer:

IAS officers are in charge of monitoring how government programs and policies are put into action. If any policy is implemented than IAS has to do follow up of it that it is being implemented correctly or not. And take care that no wrong things are done with the help of  those policies.

                                        

Responding to significant accidents, riots, and natural disasters within one's area of responsibility is the duty of an IAS officer. He or she is in charge of organising relief efforts.

                              

An IAS officer also has to manage and distribute funds that are allotted for different schemes according to the needs of the area under his supervision.He need to make sure that the funds are distributed in the right way and in the right thing.

                          

                            

Subdivision-level duties: An IAS officer's duties as a sub divisional officer mostly revolve around maintaining law and order, development, and administrative management.



At the district level, an IAS fulfils the same duties as an SDM, including supervising the SDMs, as a district officer, collector, or deputy commissioner.







Benefits of choosing IAS as a career profile



 The Influence and Respect an IAS Officer receives -



Humans enjoy holding positions that the entire country looks up to. An IAS officer profession offers unrivalled authority, power, and respect. For this reason, the position is regarded as the most prestigious job in the history of the nation.



                      



IAS Officers have  Job Security because it is a government job. The best  thing about government jobs is job security and other benefits like insurance and other incentives.So this is the best benefit of being an IAS officer.





Excellent Payscale and Huge Facilities

 An IAS officer has a handsome salary and also get different facilities like house, car and other basic needs. They also get other monetary incentives with the salary.









Huge Power Comes with Huge Responsibility for Making Policies: The feeling of authority  and decision power is felt by the IAS officer as he or she has huge power in different areas of decision making.







Being and IAS officer is a status symbol in India and also the satisfaction of serving to the country by making necessary changes in it and using the power in the right way is different.

                          



How To Become an IAS?



Step 1 - The candidates must earn their graduation degree from an accredited university after completing their 12th grade education. The candidates have a wide range of degree options to pick from.



Step 2 - A bachelor's degree in a topic that aligns with the IAS syllabus or any other degree whose syllabus primarily aligns with the IAS syllabus will be beneficial for preparation.



Step 3 - The IAS admission exam is also open to individuals who obtained their undergraduate degrees through correspondence or distance learning.



Step 4 - Any student from any background is eligible to enrol in a degree program linked to the IAS Officer Carrier.



Step 5 - Candidates must have passed their 10+2 exam, or any exam that is an equivalent, from CBSE or any board that is recognized.

Political science, geography, economics, sociology, psychology, public administration, and philosophy, among other subjects, must have been studied by the students.



Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

The age limit of an IAS Officer is 21 to 32 years. There is an age relaxation and maximum number of attempts as per categories :

UPPER AGE LIMIT

MAXIMUM NUMBER OF ATTEMPTS

The UPSC does not judge applicants unfairly based on their degree test results. As a result, there is no required minimum qualifying percentage for IAS eligibility. The following list includes the UPSC's requirements for educational eligibility for the UPSC exam:

The applicant must be a graduate of a university with official recognition.

A large portion of an IAS officer's job description is office administration (desk work and file monitoring tasks) and touring, in which the officers visit designated areas of the district or the State and observe activities taking place there.

Roles and responsibilities of an IAS Officer:

IAS officers are in charge of monitoring how government programs and policies are put into action. If any policy is implemented than IAS has to do follow up of it that it is being implemented correctly or not. And take care that no wrong things are done with the help of  those policies.

                                        

Responding to significant accidents, riots, and natural disasters within one's area of responsibility is the duty of an IAS officer. He or she is in charge of organising relief efforts.

                              

An IAS officer also has to manage and distribute funds that are allotted for different schemes according to the needs of the area under his supervision.He need to make sure that the funds are distributed in the right way and in the right thing.

                          

                            

Subdivision-level duties: An IAS officer's duties as a sub divisional officer mostly revolve around maintaining law and order, development, and administrative management.



At the district level, an IAS fulfils the same duties as an SDM, including supervising the SDMs, as a district officer, collector, or deputy commissioner.







Benefits of choosing IAS as a career profile



 The Influence and Respect an IAS Officer receives -



Humans enjoy holding positions that the entire country looks up to. An IAS officer profession offers unrivalled authority, power, and respect. For this reason, the position is regarded as the most prestigious job in the history of the nation.



                      



IAS Officers have  Job Security because it is a government job. The best  thing about government jobs is job security and other benefits like insurance and other incentives.So this is the best benefit of being an IAS officer.





Excellent Payscale and Huge Facilities

 An IAS officer has a handsome salary and also get different facilities like house, car and other basic needs. They also get other monetary incentives with the salary.









Huge Power Comes with Huge Responsibility for Making Policies: The feeling of authority  and decision power is felt by the IAS officer as he or she has huge power in different areas of decision making.







Being and IAS officer is a status symbol in India and also the satisfaction of serving to the country by making necessary changes in it and using the power in the right way is different.

                          



How To Become an IAS?



Step 1 - The candidates must earn their graduation degree from an accredited university after completing their 12th grade education. The candidates have a wide range of degree options to pick from.



Step 2 - A bachelor's degree in a topic that aligns with the IAS syllabus or any other degree whose syllabus primarily aligns with the IAS syllabus will be beneficial for preparation.



Step 3 - The IAS admission exam is also open to individuals who obtained their undergraduate degrees through correspondence or distance learning.



Step 4 - Any student from any background is eligible to enrol in a degree program linked to the IAS Officer Carrier.



Step 5 - Candidates must have passed their 10+2 exam, or any exam that is an equivalent, from CBSE or any board that is recognized.

Political science, geography, economics, sociology, psychology, public administration, and philosophy, among other subjects, must have been studied by the students.



Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

The age limit of an IAS Officer is 21 to 32 years. There is an age relaxation and maximum number of attempts as per categories :

Educational qualifications:

The UPSC does not judge applicants unfairly based on their degree test results. As a result, there is no required minimum qualifying percentage for IAS eligibility. The following list includes the UPSC's requirements for educational eligibility for the UPSC exam:

The applicant must be a graduate of a university with official recognition.

Candidates who are in their final year or who are awaiting results are also eligible to take the UPSC preliminary examination.

Skill requirements:



Patriotic: IAS officers must have the feeling of patriotism in them because they have to serve the nation and work for the betterment and development of the nation. Therefore this skill or feeling is the first important thing required to be an IAS officer.



Legal and Rational action: At the time of decision making in any situation in which they have to decide what is right and what is wrong , they should have knowledge of the laws and take decisions rationally and try to do justice.





Responsibility and Liability: IAS officers should have the skill of taking responsibilities and completing it. The power they get with the responsibility, they are liable to not misuse the powers and use it in the right way.



Hard Work and Commitment: The work of the IAS officer needs a lot of commitment and hard work. They have a tight schedule and other unforeseen situations are also there. So they need to be very dedicated and committed towards their professional life.



Decisive and Resilient in approach: IAS must have good decision making skills that are too quick. Because they have to decide and manage many things like where to implement what and how much funds to be allotted where etc.





Principle of Justice: The IAS officer is the ray of hope for many people so they need to give justice to the things they have to do and they need to be unbiased and make decisions that are for the betterment of the people.



Transparency and Integrity: IAS officers should be transparent and should take everyone together and think of everyone’s betterment and growth.





Working Environment

The working environment of an IAS officer is in the office allotted to them and for meetings they have to work in conference halls.

They may have to go in the regions they are allotted for survey and supervision of the environment and taking care of its development





                        











Entrance exam to follow the route:







You must pass the annual Civil Services Examination held by the UPSC in order to become an IAS officer. The exam is held to select candidates for roughly 25 civil service positions, including those in the IAS, IPS, IFS, Central government, and other affiliated services. Each year, around January or February, UPSC publishes a notification requesting applications. The preliminary examination, or first phase, is often held at the end of May or beginning of June.



The three phases of the Civil Services Examination are as follows:



Preliminary Assessment

Main Civil Services Examination

Character Assessment on interview

IAS officers are hired by the Indian government, which also provides jobs for them. An IAS officer has the option of working for the Central Government or another government anywhere in India or abroad. Additionally, an IAS officer may be hired by autonomous organisations, subordinate organisations, PSUs, UN agencies, or international organisations like the Asian Development Bank and the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development.



Demands & Future Scope 



In the past few years, the number of applicants taking the Indian Civil Services Exam (CSE) has more than doubled. The Indian Civil Services have always been regarded as the best job option for graduates of the arts! To enter the coveted Indian Civil Services, graduates and young professionals from the fields of science, commerce, business management, etc., have recently begun to apply for the CSE.



IAS, IPS, IFS, IRS (20+ jobs/services) officers can become CSE graduates. The Civil Services, which encompass a wide range of positions, have more authority and power than any other service in India. 



The unique chance to pursue one's personal goals while also promoting social welfare is provided by civil service.



One of the most prestigious exams in our nation is the Civil Services Exam, which is administered by the Union Public Service Commission. Every year, thousands of candidates show up for the exam. Numerous opportunities have pounded on the doors of young people all around the nation since the 1990s, when the economy of the nation began to open up. Young and elderly alike have had their aspirations realised by the IT and ITES companies, which continue to employ thousands of people annually.







Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Armstrong, Pame -





 Armstrong Pame, the first IAS officer from the Zeme Tribe of the Naga People, is frequently referred to as the "Miracle Man" by the locals. Pame became the Sub-Divisional Magistrate of Tousen in 2012 after taking the UPSC exam in 2008 and passing it. 



Aruna Sundararajan - 







Her area of expertise as an IAS officer in the Kerala cadre was e-governance and women's empowerment. She had a big impact on the growth of e-governance in the state of Kerala while she was the IT secretary of Kerala. 

Anshul Mishra - 





Anshul Mishra made some substantial systemic reforms while serving as the collector of Madurai. After assuming the position in 2012, he quickly started a Facebook page to engage with the public and respond to their complaints. 



Dr. Samit Sharma





Many of us may recall seeing him on Aamir Khan's well-known show Satyameva Jayate, when he was recognized for his assistance with a project involving generic medications. His goal had always been to increase access to and affordability of generic medications for all Indians.



Sabharwal Smitha







Smitha was appointed Municipal Commissioner of Warangal after completing her UPSC exams with flying colours and finishing in fourth place nationally. One of her most prominent projects was the public-private partnership-managed "Fund Your City" campaign, which was launched with the goal of expanding parks, walkways, and traffic junctions.

title: IPS ( Indian Police Officer)
summary: IPS ( Indian Police Officer)
description:
IPS ( Indian Police Officer)



 

An overview of IPS as a career-

What is IPS?

IPS (Indian Police Service) is a government run body of skilled and trained officers in the field of nation serving and it also provides senior level leadership to police forces- both in the States and Centre.

What is the role of an IPS officer?

 Being an IPS officer is not an easy task as the job comes along with many roles and responsibilities- 

Border responsibilities and duties - The IPS officers are deployed at different border locations across nations for safety purposes. 



Maintaining public peace and order - One of the major role of an IPS officer to maintain law and order in the country or region where he or she is appointed. 



VIP security - Some of the IPS officers also provide security and safety to important political people and even to the president of the nation. 



Railway policing- Since railways is the most commonly used transporting medium, many officials are appointed at the railway stations for security purposes. 



Anti-smuggling and drug trafficking - Eliminating or at least reducing the smuggling of drugs has been the priority if the government for very long  



Tackling Economic Offences - It is the responsibility of the officers in the IPS department to handle all crimes related to economic functioning of the country such as corruption, corporate fraud, public fraud, tax evasion, goods smuggling, stock manipulation, currencies forgery, credit card fraud, environmental crime, intellectual property infringement and the more recent phenomenon of cyber crime.



   Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Serving the nation comes with a lot of perks-

Rent free accommodation at minimum rates- IPS officers are given free accommodations or with minimum rates possible. The government has also provided with the facility of accommodations called Government Headquarters.



Sufficient pay scale- IPS officers are awarded with a good amount of salary. 



Job stability- After becoming an IPS officer, career becomes more stable. 



Official vehicles facility- Some of the IPS officers get the facility of travelling in vehicles provided by the government. 



Free electricity/ Phone call facility 



A basic procedure to approach this job:



Step 1- Complete class 12th with a stream of choice.

Step 2- Apply for the UPSC entrance examination

Step 3- Clarify the three stage examination ( UPSC Preliminary Examination, UPSC Mains Exam and UPSC Personality Evaluation Test)

Step 4- Train for a certain time period as required.

Step 5- Promotions will be applicable for as per the performance.

 Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



 Nationality-

The candidate should possess the citizenship of India.

Subject of Nepal or BhutaN.

Person of Indian origin.

A Tibetan refugee who started residing in India permanently before 01.01.1962

Age Limit-

The candidate should belong to the age group of 21 years to 32 years as of the current year.

There are certain age limit relaxations for the candidates belonging to the reserved categories, such as OBC/SC/ST/EWS/PwD.

 

 Education Qualification-

It is important for a candidate to have at least a graduation degree from a recognised board of education. 

Those candidates who are in their final year of qualifying examination can also apply for the entrance examination meant to clear for becoming an IPS officer. 

The UPSC is the examination conducting authority which organises the IPS exam every year in three stages-

Civil Services Exam (Prelims)

Civil Services Exam (Main)

UPSC Personality Evaluation Round 



Skills Required-



Hard working, independent and strong headed



Brilliant decision making power



Co operative attitude towards roles and responsibilities



Excellent and thorough awareness of the law and order 



Working Environment for an IPS Officer-



An IPS officer is always on duty and thus is surrounded by problems and complex situations 24/7.

The working environment of an IPS Officer is quite risky and full of unstable circumstances thus only those candidates should apply for the post who are very determined and serious about the profession.

   Entrance exam to follow the route-



Civil Service exam also known as IAS or UPSC exam is conducted annually by the Union Public Service Commission which is a government body designed to handle the posting and employment of eligible candidates as per the requirement. This exam offers an opportunity to the candidates to prepare themselves for these nation-serving jobs.

Step 3 - An interview round will be conducted for those shortlisted candidates who have su

title: Inorganic Chemist
summary: Name Of the Career Profile: Inorganic Chemist
description:
Name Of the Career Profile: Inorganic Chemist

                      



     ➢An overview of career profile:



Inorganic chemists are scientists who investigate the characteristics of inorganic chemical substances such as metals and minerals. 

Inorganic chemists work in industries ranging from mining to microchips. Their work is built on understanding: inorganic element behaviour and analogies, and. How to modify, separate, and use these materials.



Roles and Responsibilities







Methods for recovering metals from waste streams are being developed with the involvement of inorganic chemists.





Working as an analytical chemist in mined ore analysis is also the work of an Inorganic Chemist.







Conducting research on the use of inorganic compounds for soil treatment and for other use as well.





Ceramics and superconductors, for example, are changed, separated, and utilised in products by organic chemists.





Obtaining samples, researching and investigating compounds, substances, and mixes with the use of specialist equipment, and creating extremely intricate evaluations of your findings







     ➢Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Choosing an Inorganic Chemist for the future opens up many career opportunities be it research, job, or social work.





Inorganic chemistry’s involvement in daily life  is rapidly evolving by the arrival of many methods in the modern world.





Advancements in chemical usage and the artificial world have made it an intellectually challenging and rewarding career choice to be an inorganic chemist.



Being in the medical field is a sense of respect is it for the individual or for people around him/her.



Choosing a profile related to medicine or medical has always been very promising be it salary, respect and many other benefits.



     ➢A basic procedure to approach this:  

         



STEP 1: After class 10th choose subjects Physics, chemistry, and Biology.

STEP 2: After class 12th with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology, you must study for a Bachelor of                                                         

Chemistry or related fields as a minimum for entry-level chemists.

STEP 3: Pursue a master's in Science in Inorganic chemistry  for research jobs.                                 

STEP 4: Pursue a Phd. in a related field.



     ➢Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment:

           ELIGIBILITY



Age Limit-  A candidate should complete a minimum of 17 years of age to get admission in the   course.



 Educational Qualification- A candidate should hold at least a bachelor's degree in a related field. If you further want to go for research then a master's and doctorate(Ph.D.) is a must to achieve a higher post in this field.



           SKILLS REQUIREMENT 



Individuals who choose a job as a chemist must manage time between experiments and assure the proper completion of a project before its deadline. He or she must keep meticulous records of processes and time. 



Issue Solving Ability: Chemists must be able to recognise a problem and propose the best feasible solution to address it. Before implementing a remedy, he or she assesses its efficacy.



Communication abilities as scientists must be able to effectively communicate. He or she should be able to properly record the technique or method. He or she should be able to comprehend what another team member is saying or attempting to express.



Analytical skills are also one of the most important skills an individual should have to be a chemist as they need to analyse the symptoms, results, and experiments and then conclude the result.





Organising Skills are a must, chemists must analyse a large amount of data in order to forecast techniques, methods, and results. He or she must have excellent organisational abilities. I





WORKING ENVIRONMENT



An Inorganic chemist may operate indoors in an office, research laboratory, industry, or educational institution. Geochemistry and mining, for example, need personnel to operate both outside and underground.

They may generally work 40 hours per week.Also sometimes extra work is required to finish the pending work or projects.



    ➢Entrance exam to follow the route:



Central Universities Entrance Test.

Jawaharlal Nehru University Entrance Exam

Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test.

BHU Undergraduate Entrance Test.

Banaras Hindu University Postgraduate Entrance Test (BHU PET).

Delhi University Entrance Exam

Joint Admission Test for Masters (JAM)

Courses after 12th

 Option after class 12th to follow the path

     ➢Promotions & upgradings:

         

Ph.D. chemists often head research teams and have authority over project direction and content, although even Ph.D. holders have opportunities to progress as they gain experience.

 As chemists gain experience directing research projects, they may be able to take on larger, more involved, and more expensive initiatives.



     ➢Top recruiting agencies for this profile:

     ➢Demands & Future Scope 

           

Because inorganic chemistry careers are diverse, job descriptions and educational qualifications cover a wide range.Inorganic chemistry is a very useful discipline that is used in a variety of applications. 

Inorganic chemistry careers are available in a variety of sectors, including geochemistry, materials science, textiles, nanochemistry, and personal care.



     ➢Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Alfred Werner





Alfred Werner was a Swiss chemist who studied at the ETH Zurich and taught at the University of Zurich. In 1913, he was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for suggesting the octahedral structure of transition metal complexes. Werner laid the groundwork for contemporary coordination chemistry.



William Lipscomb





William Nunn Lipscomb Jr. was an American inorganic and organic scientist who won the Nobel Prize in nuclear magnetic resonance, theoretical chemistry, boron chemistry, and biochemistry.



Harry B. Gray





Gray earned his bachelor's degree in chemistry from Western Kentucky University in 1957. He began his career in inorganic chemistry at Northwestern University, where he got his Ph.D. under the supervision of Fred Basolo and Ralph Pearson in 1960.



Lesley Yellowlees





Lesley Jane Yellowlees, CBE FRSE FRSC is a British inorganic chemist who studies spectroscopy, electron transfer processes, and electron paramagnetic resonance spectroscopy.



Geoffrey Wilkinson







Sir Geoffrey Wilkinson FRS was an English scientist who pioneered inorganic chemistry and homogeneous transition metal catalysis. He was a Nobel laureate.

title: Instrument Engineer
summary: Career Profile : Instrument Engineer
description:
Career Profile : Instrument Engineer 



        





Overview 



Who is a Instrument Engineer



Instrument Engineers investigate and analyze the existing machinery  and discover the parts of inefficiency, developing these parts in a new form. 





Instrument Engineers are creative and with their scientific skills they create certain instruments and sometimes work with engineers to develop efficient machinery. 





        



They are engineers who plan, install and maintain control systems and other equipment within the manufacturing units.





What kind of career profile is it





The job of an Instrument Engineer is encompassed by scientific skills and the primary focus of this job is identifying the faults and flaws of an existing machinery. 





This job profile has a number of challenges as the instrument engineers need to work in teams with other engineers. 





They need to work for long hours and provide documentation and reports of the working machinery. 





Roles and Responsibilities of a Instrument Engineer 





The roles and responsibilities depend on the type of tests and analysis the instrument engineers focus on. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows 





The instrument engineers designs prototypes of the machinery and redevelops designs of the machinery to match the budget and specifications. 



         





They provide training on topics like machinery and new equipment and provide technical manuals. 





       







They are also responsible for making models for efficiency tests and designing new control systems. 





            







They sometimes manage some operations and write test procedures. 





          





They provide reports and test detailed analysis to the industries and improve the quality of the old machines. 





          







Benefits of Choosing Instrument Engineer as a Career Profile 







High Employment rates and job opportunities 



     







There is a high employment rate as instrument engineers work in different places and for the improvement and redevelopment of machineries the companies demand the instrument engineers. 





Work outdoors



         



In most cases, the instrument engineers have to work outdoors and also need to visit sites for investigating the machinery and equipment. 





Work in teams

Who is a Instrument Engineer



Instrument Engineers investigate and analyze the existing machinery  and discover the parts of inefficiency, developing these parts in a new form. 





Instrument Engineers are creative and with their scientific skills they create certain instruments and sometimes work with engineers to develop efficient machinery. 





        



They are engineers who plan, install and maintain control systems and other equipment within the manufacturing units.





What kind of career profile is it





The job of an Instrument Engineer is encompassed by scientific skills and the primary focus of this job is identifying the faults and flaws of an existing machinery. 





This job profile has a number of challenges as the instrument engineers need to work in teams with other engineers. 





They need to work for long hours and provide documentation and reports of the working machinery. 





Roles and Responsibilities of a Instrument Engineer 





The roles and responsibilities depend on the type of tests and analysis the instrument engineers focus on. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows 





The instrument engineers designs prototypes of the machinery and redevelops designs of the machinery to match the budget and specifications. 



         





They provide training on topics like machinery and new equipment and provide technical manuals. 





       







They are also responsible for making models for efficiency tests and designing new control systems. 





            







They sometimes manage some operations and write test procedures. 





          





They provide reports and test detailed analysis to the industries and improve the quality of the old machines. 





          







Benefits of Choosing Instrument Engineer as a Career Profile 







High Employment rates and job opportunities

Good communication skills is the only way of crossing the ladder of success. It attracts people and the convincing attitude helps to improve the rate of people in a company.

Creativity entwines tradition and helps to cultivate new things and restore the quality of products. Instrument Engineers need to have creative skills and make new equipment.

title: Intellectual Property Lawyer
summary: Name of the career profile: Intellectual Property Lawyer
description:
Name of the career profile: Intellectual Property Lawyer





An overview of career a profile:

Intellectual property lawyers are trained professionals who specialise in three domains of intellectual property.

They deal in a variety of different industries from medical and scientific discovery patents to concepts and marketing schemes by different companies. 

You can say an intellectual property lawyer protects an idea from any unwarranted uses.  



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Protection of copyrights

Intellectual property lawyers make sure that all the copyrights of a firm or company are protected. This is because a company has spent a lot of money to make their copyrights such as logos. These need to be protected to in turn protect a firm from exploitation





Drafting documents 

This is a practice common to all law branches. In this IP lawyers take care of all the documentation of a company including but not limited to the registrations of trademarks and patents. They also draft documents of lawsuits and their replies to the concerned parties. 





Overseeing patents 

A patent basically means registering an original idea and making sure only the individual or corporation who invented it can use it for a select period of time. An IP lawyers sees the smooth functioning of this process and make sure there are no legal issues





Legal Research 

Another aspect of law common to all branches, this basically entails in-depth research on the laws concerning intellectual properties to be up to date with all the intricacies of law and any changes in the law and to prepare necessary counter measures. 





Protection Copyrights and trademarks



Copyright and trademarks are specialised media forms in various forms whose sole rights to be used are held by the concerned company. A lawyer protects theses from piracy and fraud to protect the intellectual rights of the company. 







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Stability in career

Intellectual property law is one of the most stable branches of law. This is due to their nature as permanent employees and intimate association with the intellectual rights sector. In today's age intellectual rights are highly coveted and thus these lawyers are highly valued. .





Job opportunities

As one of the fastest growing career profiles in the field of law, it entails a myriad of job opportunities for candidates. This is due to the growing awareness of intellectual rights of people and corporations. 







Ability to protect ideas and creativity

IP lawyers make it possible for individuals as well as corporations to protect their original ideas and creations and help in the overall growth due to the unlimited potential of each new idea. They also help to protect clients from exploitation and cheating.





A very good package 

Those in this career profile are subject to very good salary packages since copyrights and trademarks are worth millions of dollars and those protecting them are rewarded handsomely. 





Accelerate business growth

By contributing to the efficient creation of trademarks and patents for a business or corporation, you are helping them in cementing their position and product in the market. This leads to a better growth rate for the concerned company. . 





A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become an IP lawyer follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 in any stream like arts, science or commerce.

Step-2: Complete an integrated course in Law such as BA.LLB or B.Com LLB. 

Step 3: Alternatively you can complete a 2 year diploma in intellectual rights law and then complete your bachelor's in law

Step-4: Appear for the Bar exam so that you can start your practice as an advocate. 

Step-5: Now you can start private practice or apply at law firms.

Eligibility and skills requirements:

 Age Limit

The age limit set by the Bar council of India is 45 years. 

You should pursue a degree in order to get licensed.



Educational qualifications 

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Science, Mathematics, Biology,  physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished their undergraduate degree in intellectual rights law

Finished their internship and should have received their licence.

Examination 

They should have passed through an eligibility test such as CLAT or their preferred college’s assessment exams.



 Skill requirements

To become a successful IP  Lawyer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.





Intellectual curiosity



A good sense of intellectual curiosity is needed to truly understand your work as an IP lawyer. This includes learning the process of creation of various trademarks. They should also innately understand the creative process that goes behind intellectual 

Negotiation skills



You'll have to be very good at negotiations since you'll be dealing with a variety of cases that are based on money such as legal settlement

Step-5: Now you can start private practice or apply at law firms.

Eligibility and skills requirements:

 Age Limit

You should pursue a degree in order to get licensed.



Educational qualifications

They ought to have finished their undergraduate degree in intellectual rights law

Finished their internship and should have received their licence.

Examination 

They should have passed through an eligibility test such as CLAT or their preferred college’s assessment exams.



 Skill requirements

To become a successful IP  Lawyer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.





Intellectual curiosity



A good sense of intellectual curiosity is needed to truly understand your work as an IP lawyer. This includes learning the process of creation of various trademarks. They should also innately understand the creative process that goes behind intellectual 

Negotiation skills



You'll have to be very good at negotiations since you'll be dealing with a variety of cases that are based on money such as legal settlements. This is because no company wants legal issues and if possible always solve any lawsuits out of court with monetary or other means. 

Writing skills 



As someone who will have to deal with trademarks, patents, and copyrights, ip lawyers have to be very good at writing and drafting so as to help their clients in registering and making a mark with their idea or products. 

Great communication skills



Another skill necessary for any branch of law to be precise, an IP lawyer has to be good at being assertive and letting the other party know about their decisions and their views. 

Creativity



Since intellectual rights are many times present in the virtual space and sometimes it's hard to know what's a violation and what's not, an IP lawyer has to be creative in coming up with solutions in a non hostile way without compromising their clients interests. 

Through legal knowledge



A IP lawyer needs to be totally aware of the various laws and should be completely accustomed to legal procedures. They should also be up to date with any law changes 

Work environment



Most IP lawyers have a very flexible work environment which generally only demands them to go to office when they have a case or a client requires their services.

While a lawyer can be subject to much travel depending on getting clues for their cases, generally these cases are far and few between and they don't have to travel much.

Anytime there is a meeting or litigation process regarding new patents, copyrights or trademarks,then an IP lawyer is required.

There are different entrance exams available to pursue intellectual property rights law as a career option. Below given are some of the entrance exams:

Some of the most specialisations available under intellectual rights law are:

Legal Advisor



The role of a legal advisor is to assist managers and directors through the process of making company laws while also helping them navigate through the legal front of their corporation such as lawsuits, etc. 

Trademark Attorney





As the name suggests these lawyers are responsible for the whole process of trademark registration as well drafting documents overseeing how the trademarks are continually being renewed and protected.

Patent Lawyer



A patent lawyer is responsible for the filing and protection of ideas that are registered as patents under the patent. These lawyers make sure their client’s ideas don't get stolen and exploited by other individuals or corporations. They also take care of all litigation matters. 



Copyright Lawyers



copyrights generally exist for media sources such as films, characters , digital media or/and books. These copyrights are protected by a copyright lawyer who makes sure there is no fraudulent or commercial use of the material by anyone except the owners. 



Trade secrets Specialist 



Trade secrets are specific processes and ingredients or perhaps a specific location which is important to the manufacturing of a product. These secrets are strictly guarded and Trade secret lawyers protect and uphold these.

title: Interior designer
summary: An overview of career a profile:
description:
Interior Designer





An overview of career a profile:



Interior designers help companies/ residents in planning the structure, decoration, layout, colour schemes, furnishing of their places. 

The main purpose of interior design is to make a property look attractive and representable, with useful items. 

After pursuing an interior design course, one can become interior designers or decorators in companies/houses.



Roles and Responsibilities



In the budgeting stage, determine the project needs and cost of completion.So that it become easier for clients and for you as well to understand the budget and time required.



Establish a deadline for finishing an interior design job.Plans contain sources for items and supplies.



Make "mood boards" to illustrate your design philosophy. So that the clients have better understanding of your creativity and imagination.



Make use of computer applications when designing to make the imagination in visuals.



After the design is finished, check to see if the customer is satisfied with the work been done.







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career

Amending experience

One of the best reasons for doing an interior designer course is: an individual gets to work with people at various levels of styles and development. This provides a great exposure to them, which is beneficial for their upcoming work life.







Various upcoming projects

A regular interior designer course has a huge variety of challenging projects for students to be engaged in a wider range of skills including: 

Patience

Relationship with clientele

Technological knowledge

Commitment to projects

Variety of creativity

Admirable opportunities

There is a huge demand for designers, who can convert small areas into a useful place, an attractive area with decorative items, aesthetic areas for personal or professional use. That's why choosing an interior designer course opens a gate of grateful opportunities.



Full creativity freedom

Students are free to work according to their own skills without any certain pattern or rules. Interior designing is a course for artisans or creative enthusiasts. Aspirants choose this course to develop their careers as creative designers. Aspirants are expected to think out of the box most of the time and give a completely different suggestion for respective projects, which not everybody could think of and make the best out of that place. Every industry provides a significant space that allows aspirants to explore their talents.





Innovation and Imagination

In today's world, industries don't allow aspirants to explore their creativity to their best professional capacity. However, pursuing an interior design program allows aspirants to test the limits of innovation and Imagination, giving them space to explore their new designs and creativity.



Advantages of interior design program: 

Increasing demand

This industry is growing at an unstoppable pace worldwide, which means that those with the required skills and creativity gets work in various industries.





Way of working

Work lifestyle of an interior designer is completely dynamic and spontaneous. Many people envy interior designers as they are provided with the opportunity to work from home, which benefits them to work according to their preferable timings, dress how the feel like.





Being your own boss

As a qualified interior designer, aspirants gets to enjoy unique benefits and rewards by exploring their skills according to the client's goals. 





Creative liberty

Aspirant is free to work based on his skills, without any certain rules. That's how they can turn a waste corner in a house into a useful place which looks aesthetic and attractive by adding decorative items.





Job satisfaction

Aspirants bring their own creative vision to life and work, knowing their potential while working in a company or an industry. They give their best to be satisfied with their work.

STEP 3:Complete your undergraduate degree such as Bachelor of design or diploma or certification course in design field.

STEP 4: Complete your Master’s in Design or Masters in Arts or related field.

full_content:
Interior Designer





An overview of career a profile:



Interior designers help companies/ residents in planning the structure, decoration, layout, colour schemes, furnishing of their places. 

The main purpose of interior design is to make a property look attractive and representable, with useful items. 

After pursuing an interior design course, one can become interior designers or decorators in companies/houses.



Roles and Responsibilities



In the budgeting stage, determine the project needs and cost of completion.So that it become easier for clients and for you as well to understand the budget and time required.



Establish a deadline for finishing an interior design job.Plans contain sources for items and supplies.



Make "mood boards" to illustrate your design philosophy. So that the clients have better understanding of your creativity and imagination.



Make use of computer applications when designing to make the imagination in visuals.



After the design is finished, check to see if the customer is satisfied with the work been done.







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career

.

The candidate must have completed class 12.

The candidate must have achieved a prescribed minimum marks in class 12.

The candidate must have taken a prescribed subject combination in 10+2.



              Eligibility for admission in PG courses:

An aspirant must have completed UG level of education in the respective design

The candidate must have achieved a relevant minimum mark in UG.

              Eligibility for admission in Diploma/Certificate courses:

Aspirant must have completed 10+2 level of education

50% marks at H.S.C examination 

Some colleges offer diploma courses/certificate courses in Interior Design after class 10.



SKILLS REQUIRED



Outstanding communication -You'll be required as an interior designer to clearly convey your concept to your clients and to listen intently.





Creativity-A student must be extremely creative and be able to come up with original designs and styles that fit the client's preferences and personality.





Solver of issues-Additionally, this encourages the use of novel and varied graphic technology; Solution-Focusing Ability: An interior designer must frequently address issues that



Observation of details-With regard to space measurements, drawing, and design execution, attention to detail might be helpful. You may manage instals by using this talent.



Writing and illustrating-Drafting expertise is used by interior designers to produce images of buildings. taking precise measurements to create an accurate drawing

title: Jewellery Designer
summary: ➢ An overview of career a profile:
description:
Jewelry Designer

➢ An overview of career a profile:

A jewelry designer plans and creates the patterns for rings, necklaces, bracelets and other accessories. They often generate a few prototypes of a product and test the models for durability, comfort and style. Jewelry designers work in a variety of environments, including:Jewelry stores, especially those focusing on custom designs Design studios and workshops ,Manufacturing facilities ,Jewelry repair shops







Roles and Responsibilities 

Drafting designs on paper or with computer software



Selecting and assessing the quality of gemstones, such as diamonds, rubies and emeralds

Evaluating wax models and determining how to improve them



Casting new pieces in metal, using wax models as a guide

Crafting custom designs



Polishing and smoothing metal to ensure the jewelry is comfortable for the wearer

Cleaning jewelry before distribution by using chemicals and polishing wheels

Reshaping metal, inserting gems and ensuring the attachment is secure



➢ Benefits of choosing this profile as a career:

A Rewarding Career







Become an Entrepreneur









Become a Gemmologist







Become a Jewellery Designer & Consultant





➢ How to become a Jwelry  Designer 

Step 1 -  Candidates who want to pursue a UG degree course in Jewellery Design should have completed their Class 12/ HSC/ 10+2 (or equivalent exam) from a recognised board with passing marks.



Step -  2  Aspirants from any stream, Arts/ Commerce/ Science are eligible to pursue a course in Jewellery Design.



Step -  3. To secure admission in PG level Jewellery Design course, candidates need to have completed their graduation from a recognised college/ institute.



Most colleges prefer design graduates to apply for courses offered at the PG level, however, aspirants from other streams can also apply for admission if they possess the required skill-set and aptitude.

➢ Eligibility And Skills Requirement To Become A Jewelry Designer 

Age limit:

The minimum age limit to apply for this job profile is 17 years.



Educational qualifications:

 The qualification and eligibility criteria to become a jewelry designer in India are explained below.



Candidates must have cleared their Class 10 and 12 exams or equivalent exams from a recognised university.

Candidates should enrol in a UG jewelry design course.

This is optional. Those candidates who want to do masters in jewelry design should have cleared their UG exam with a good score.



Entrance exams:

Top Entrance Exams

UCEED, NATA, SEED, NID DAT, CEED, AIEED



Skills requirement:

Creativity



Communication Skills



Good Eye-sight



Innovative



Visual Imagination

Technical Knowledge



➢ Entrance Examination 

Admission into most of the institutes offering BDes Jewellery Design course is based on scores in entrance examination. Some of the popular entrance exams conducted in India is listed below:

NID.  (National Institute of Design)

CEED (Common Entrance Exam for Design)

AIEED ( All India Entrance Examination for Design)

NIFT (National Institute of Fashion Technology)

MIT DAT (Design Aptitude Test)



➢ Courses 

Courses after 12th

BDes in Jwellery Designing ( 4 yrs) 

B Voc in Jwellery Designing (3yrs) 

BSc in Jwellery Designing (3 yrs) 

BA in Jwellery Designing (3yrs) 

Bsc in Jwellery Designing and Technology (3 to 4 yrs )

Courses after graduation 

M sc in Jwellery Designing (2 yrs) 

M Des in Jwellery Designing (2 yrs) 

MA in Jwellery Designing (2 yrs) 

MSc in Jwellery Designing and Technology (2 yrs)











➢ Top institutions to pursue the course:

The list of top colleges to become a jewelry designer in India is as follows:



Jaipur’s ARCH College of Design & Business





Biyani Group of Colleges (BGC), Jaipur





Ellen’s School of Art and Design (ESAD), Jaipur







Jain University (JU Bangalore), Bengaluru







NSAM Academy of Fashion & Interior Design (NSAM Academy), Navi Mumbai.





Parul University, Gujarat





Bengaluru’s Vogue Institute of Art and Design (VIAD)







➢ Type of specialisation available under this job profiles :

1. jewelry Designer: Candidates who choose this job profile will create jewels and accessories out of various precious metals, crystals, beads, and gemstones







2. jewelry Consultant: Those people who are working under this profile are responsible for assisting their clients or customers regarding their jewelry selection, explanation of jewelry metals, and customer requirements within their budgets.







3. Gemologist: Those people who have chosen this profile are gemstone and gem experts. Gemologists examine several elements of gems and stones in order to assess their quality, authenticity, and worth. Gemologists are also responsible for identifying the composition and purity of gemstones.







➢ Pay-Scales as per the experience:

The annual salary of a jewelry designer in India ranges between Rs 4 lakhs and Rs 6 lakhs per annum. However, this salary varies

➢ Demand and Scope 

The gems and jewellery sector plays a significant role in the Indian economy, contributing around 7% to country’s GDP and 15% to India’s total merchandise export.



 It employs over 4.64 million people, which is expected to reach 8.23 million by 2022. 



One of the fastest growing sectors, it is extremely export oriented and labour intensive. Based on its potential for growth and value addition, the Government declared gems and jewellery sector as a focus area for export promotion. 







The Government has undertaken various measures recently to promote investment and upgrade technology and skills to promote ‘Brand India’ in the international market.





➢ Top inspiring personalities 

1. FARAH KHAN ALI (FARAH KHAN FINE JEWELLERY)

Farah Khan Ali is a gemologist and renowned jewellery designer who had her professional training, at the gemological institute of America, Santa Monica, California, in 1992 93 where she graduated top of her class. With 16 years of focus and experience in this field, she still feels like a creatively inclined soul who happened to come into jewellery designing by chance, only to discover that she was born for it.





2. SUHANI PITTIE

Suhani Pittie is an Indian jewellery designer based in Hyderabad, India. She launched her own jewellery label, Suhani Pittie label in 2004. The World Gold Council has enlisted Pittie as one of the top 10 most inventive and ingenious jewellery designers in the world.







3. ASHA KAMAL MODI (ART KARAT)

Asha Kamal Modi launched ART KARAT in 1988. with a vision to

create silver jewellery that is so elegant and spectacular that it

eradicates the irrational obsession of women with gold

She is one of India's most recognized jewellery designers and has

designed jewellery for more than 10 Bollywood blockbusters

including Devdas, Monsoon Wedding. Kamasutra, Zubeida, Ram

leela and Padmavati.







4. VARUNA D JANI

Varuna D Jani launched her signature label ‘Varuna D Jani’ in 2006 and a flagship store in 2008. Her brand has always looked ahead, and dared to be different; her expertise in the field has managed to break norms yet keep tradition intact.



She has showcased her collections at India International Jewellery Week and Shenaai. Her designs have been gracedby actress like Sonal Chauhan and Bipasha Basu.







5. MIRA GULHATI

Mirari is a fine jewelry company based in New Delhi that has established a reputation for creating individually crafted treasures and dazzling limited edition 'must-haves' for today's discerning buyers – clients seeking exclusive designs using only the rarest, unblemished stones sourced from around the world.

title: Marine Biologist
summary: Career profile: Marine Biologist
description:
Career profile: Marine Biologist



Overview: 

Who is a Marine Biologist?

A marine biologist is a professional who studies the life, behavior, physiological processes, and diseases of marine organisms in their natural habitats.



What kind of career profile is it?

The job of a marine biologist is mostly a field job. They need to visit the oceans, collect samples for research and then communicate the findings to other departments involved.

This job profile has various challenges where they need to collect samples, manage instrumentation, remain updated about new discoveries in the field, analyze collected data and provide assistance as well.

The work hours depend on the type of research the marine biologist has been allocated to conduct.

Roles and Responsibilities of a Marine Biologist: 

The roles and responsibilities of a marine biologist depend on the specialization or the type of organization he works with. But, a general overview of the Roles and Responsibilities of a Marine Biologist is given below. 

Collect samples from the ocean and perform research and analysis on them.





Manage agencies and assure that the environment regulations are being followed strictly.







Get information about various activities of marine breeds and plants, diseases that affect them, their behavior and effect on environment, effect of human activities on different marine breeds etc.







They must be good in formulating their proposals so that they receive funds for research and fieldwork.







Monitor the natural habitats of various species, identify the endangered ones, formulate solutions that are sustainable to protect marine life.





Present the research findings to stakeholders, policymakers and make the public aware of human activities that can affect marine life along with ways to conserve it.









Benefits Of Choosing Marine Biologist as A Career Profile:

Lower Competition:



Since this is a new field, there is not much competition in the field and one has much higher chances of a successful career if he/she has good theoretical and practical knowledge in the field.



High paying salary:





Marine biologist jobs pay higher than most other jobs in government as well as the federal sector.



Job security:





There are various private and government sectors where you can start your career as a marine biologist. For example: in research projects funded by the national government, in laboratories, in companies who are involved in areas like fisheries etc.



Opportunity to contribute towards sustainability: 



With your research and findings as a marine biologist, you will be responsible for conveying the inferences to the stakeholders and decision-makers so that they can develop sustainable solutions for the world to thrive, which is the need of the hour.







Numerous career opportunities:



From being into core marine biology professions like zoologist, fisheries biologist, etc. to academia: there are numerous career opportunities in this domain.







How To Become a Marine Biologist?



To become a marine biologist, you must follow the career path given below :

STEP-1: Complete 10th standard and choose science stream with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Mathematics as mandatory subjects in 11th standard.

STEP-2: Pass 12th standard with at least 55% marks in each of the above-mentioned subjects.

STEP-3: Apply for a B.Sc. (Bachelor of Science) Course in Marine Biology, Biology, Ecology, Biotechnology etc.

STEP-4: Complete the BSc course.

STEP-5: After completing BSc, opt for MSc in Marine Biology.

STEP-6: Choose a specialization in MSc if available.

STEP-7: After completing Masters, apply for internships in various societies and government research centers in this domain. You can also pursue an M. Phil or P.H.D after MSc.

STEP-8: After having experience in internships, you can apply for a job in the domain. 

Eligibility And Skills Requirement to Become a Marine Biologist:

Age limit:

The minimum age limit to apply for this job profile is 17 years.



Educational qualifications:

A candidate must have scored at least 55% aggregate in 12th standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology, Mathematics as mandatory subjects.

The minimum educational qualification to become a marine biologist is a BSc degree.

You can pursue higher studies (MSc, P.H.D) after BSc.

Entrance exams:

Entrance exams depend on the university you are going to take admission in. Some universities may provide direct admission as well after 10+2.

Working Environment:

Working environment for a marine biologist is mostly field work. They mostly spend their time on ships, boats, and oceans researching and collecting samples using standard lab equipment.

Marine biologists also need to attend seminars and meetings to convey their findings to stakeholders as well as make organizations aware of government rules, regulations and sustainable proposals.

Skills requirement:

Good communication skills:

A marine biologist is a professional who studies the life, behavior, physiological processes, and diseases of marine organisms in their natural habitats.

The work hours depend on the type of research the marine biologist has been allocated to conduct.

Roles and Responsibilities of a Marine Biologist: 

The roles and responsibilities of a marine biologist depend on the specialization or the type of organization he works with. But, a general overview of the Roles and Responsibilities of a Marine Biologist is given below. 

Collect samples from the ocean and perform research and analysis on them.





Manage agencies and assure that the environment regulations are being followed strictly.







Get information about various activities of marine breeds and plants, diseases that affect them, their behavior and effect on environment, effect of human activities on different marine breeds etc.







They must be good in formulating their proposals so that they receive funds for research and fieldwork.







Monitor the natural habitats of various species, identify the endangered ones, formulate solutions that are sustainable to protect marine life.





Present the research findings to stakeholders, policymakers and make the public aware of human activities that can affect marine life along with ways to conserve it.

Lower Competition:



Since this is a new field, there is not much competition in the field and one has much higher chances of a successful career if he/she has good theoretical and practical knowledge in the field.



High paying salary:





Marine biologist jobs pay higher than most other jobs in government as well as the federal sector.



Job security:





There are various private and government sectors where you can start your career as a marine biologist. For example: in research projects funded by the national government, in laboratories, in companies who are involved in areas like fisheries etc.



Opportunity to contribute towards sustainability: 



With your research and findings as a marine biologist, you will be responsible for conveying the inferences to the stakeholders and decision-makers so that they can develop sustainable solutions for the world to thrive, which is the need of the hour.







Numerous career opportunities:



From being into core marine biology professions like zoologist, fisheries biologist, etc. to academia: there are numerous career opportunities in this domain.







How To Become a Marine Biologist?



To become a marine biologist, you must follow the career path given below :

STEP-1: Complete 10th standard and choose science stream with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Mathematics as mandatory subjects in 11th standard.

STEP-2: Pass 12th standard with at least 55% marks in each of the above-mentioned subjects.

STEP-3: Apply for a B.Sc. (Bachelor of Science) Course in Marine Biology, Biology, Ecology, Biotechnology etc.

STEP-4: Complete the BSc course.

STEP-5: After completing BSc, opt for MSc in Marine Biology.

STEP-6: Choose a specialization in MSc if available.

STEP-7: After completing Masters, apply for internships in various societies and government research centers in this domain. You can also pursue an M. Phil or P.H.D after MSc.

STEP-8: After having experience in internships, you can apply for a job in the domain. 

Eligibility And Skills Requirement to Become a Marine Biologist:

Age limit:

The minimum age limit to apply for this job profile is 17 years.



Educational qualifications:

A candidate must have scored at least 55% aggregate in 12th standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology, Mathematics as mandatory subjects.

The minimum educational qualification to become a marine biologist is a BSc degree.

You can pursue higher studies (MSc, P.H.D) after BSc.

Entrance exams:

Entrance exams depend on the university you are going to take admission in. Some universities may provide direct admission as well after 10+2.

Working Environment:

Working environment for a marine biologist is mostly field work. They mostly spend their time on ships, boats, and oceans researching and collecting samples using standard lab equipment.

Marine biologists also need to attend seminars and meetings to convey their findings to stakeholders as well as make organizations aware of government rules, regulations and sustainable proposals.

Skills requirement:

Good communication skills:



In order to convey your findings to your mentors or to agencies, you must have good communication skills.









Ability to work for long hours



Marine Biologists may need to work for long hours to submit research findings. Also, conducting tests in laboratories may take a lot of time.



Effective decision making:



Based on a marine biologist’s proposals and findings, the organization concerned will take necessary steps for implementation. Hence, you need to be an effective and responsible decision maker to be in this job role.



In depth knowledge of marine biology and the respective field:



As this job involves much research work, aspirants must have in-depth knowledge of the subject and also other domains, for example: zoology, fishery etc. They must also keep themselves updated about new findings in the field.



Good data analysis skills:



After collecting sample data, marine biologists need to conduct tests and analyze it to find results. Hence, they need good data analysis skills. A good knowledge of statistics is preferable.



Excellent practical skills:



Using lab equipment effectively requires good practical skills. Also collecting the right samples, figuring out areas for research need practical skills which you can refine with experience.



Ability to keep oneself always updated about new discoveries in the field:





You need to remain updated with new discoveries in the topic that you are going to research on. This helps to have good prior knowledge before you dive deep into your research.



Remain flexible: 



There might be situations where you have to work at night to observe or collect samples for e.g.: in the case of bioluminescent creatures.



Physical Fitness: 



Marine biologists may need to dive in the ocean for sample collection and observation. This requires good physical fitness.





Good Computer Operation skills:





Being technically sound is required in today’s digital world. In this job role too, you need to know how to use computer devices efficiently to perform analysis, generate reports etc.





Proficiency in verbal and written communication:



Proficiency in Verbal communication is important to convey your proposals to officials. Proficiency in written communication is important when publishing research papers, generating reports etc.



Time management skills:





You must have   good time management skills to schedule time for research work, thesis/paper publishing and attending corporate meetings as well.





           Entrance Exam to Become a Marine Biologist:



There is no common entrance exam to pursue UG Marine Biology in India. But universities may conduct their own entrance exams for students wishing to pursue a BSc Degree.



Few entrance exams for MSc Marine Biology aspirants are:



AIIMS PG (All India Institute of Medical Sciences Post Graduate Exam)

CSIR UGC NET (Council of Scientific and Industrial Research University Grants Commission National Eligibility Test)

JRF (Junior Research Fellowship),

AURCET (Andhra Pradesh Research Common Entrance Test)

Fish and Wildlife Biologist:





They study the biology, behavior, and habitats of animals as well as fish and work for non-profit organizations, research laboratories, or government agencies.







Fisheries Biologist: 



 Fisheries biologists study the natural habitats of various kinds of fishes, ideal conditions for their growth, their impact and need in the environment etc. Fishery biology focuses on the behavior of fish in their natural surroundings.





Biological technician: 

 

Biological technicians perform scientific tests, experiments, and analysis under the supervision of experienced mentors and interpret the findings.
