Promotions And Upgrading of a Marine Biologist:

The career of a marine biologist lacks promotional structure. But an overview is given here:

LEVEL-1: Interns

Interns are the recent graduates of BSc or any other equivalent course. 

They practice marine biology under an experienced mentor from any organization.

LEVEL-2: Junior Research Fellow

They have some experience or are MSc graduates who pursue research in the field.

They are responsible for conducting research work, performing tests and learning to analyze results under a senior marine biologist.



LEVEL-3: Senior Marine Biologist

They have a good level of experience and are responsible for providing assistance, managing expeditions and training junior researchers.

Demand And Future Scope: 



Marine biologists are in high demand internationally since coastal environments are increasingly becoming a favorable choice for human settlement.

 As the pressure of population on land is increasing, fishery is becoming an important research domain at present. Hence, marine biologists who can work in this domain are in great demand.

Also, expeditions to snowy continents, discovery of underwater masses at present are fueling the research work regarding new forms of life and their impact on human environment. Hence, marine biologists are required to lead these operations.

Top Inspiring Marine Biologists:

Nancy Knowlton: Author, Researcher, Marine Biologist, P.H.D





She completed her education at Harvard University and the University of California. She is known for her work in ecology, research in coral reefs etc. She was the Sant Chair for Marine Science at the Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History.



Henry Bryant Bigelow: American oceanographer and marine biologist



He is known for being the founding director of Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution, describing multiple species, carrying out multiple expeditions.  The American Academy of Arts and Sciences offered him a Fellowship. He published several books and research papers.



Syed Zahoor Qasim: DSc., P.H.D

          

A member of the Planning Commission of India and the Director of Central Marine Fisheries Research Institute, Kochi, Kerala, Sir Syed Zahoor Qasim led India’s Exploration mission to Antarctica. He was awarded the Padma Shri and the Padma Bhusan by the government of India.



Anne Rudloe: Co-founder of Gulf Specimen Laboratory



She was the first woman to complete the "Scientists in the Sea" program conducted in the United States Naval base in Panama City. The program was associated with underwater research using diving techniques. She was a professor of biological science. She also founded the Panacea Institute of Marine Science in Panacea, Florida.

Eugenie Clark: Ichthyologist, Doctorate of Zoology



Known as ‘The Shark Lady’, she is famous for promoting marine conservation and also being a pioneer in the field of research using scuba diving. She had authored two popular books and was also a subject for many books.

title: Mathematician
summary: Career profile : Pure Mathematicians
description:
Career profile : Pure Mathematicians



             





Overview 





Who is a pure mathematician ? 



A pure mathematician is a professional who specializes in mathematics and creates statistical data for improvement. 





A pure mathematician makes an intricate analysis with the help of mathematical formulas and models. 





               





They also interpret data and  collect data from statistical and experimental sources. 







What kind of career profile is it ? 



The job of a pure mathematician consists of approving and disapproving theories and the primary focus of this job is creating experiments and research projects. 



This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with other mathematicians and engineers. 



They create computational methods and computer codes and provide reports and theories to companies. 







Roles and Responsibilities of a Pure Mathematician? 



The roles and responsibilities of a pure mathematician are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates on. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 





Pure mathematicians study the intricate details and models and solve practical problems in various aspects including business, engineering and science.

  



             





They study inferences acquired from other observations or experiments.



               



They create mathematical theories, concepts and in algebra and geometry.



              





They participate in decision making and create report conclusions. 



              





They create new mathematical techniques and detect the relationships between mathematical principles





               





Benefits of Choosing Pure Mathematician as a Career Profile : 





High employment rates and job opportunities 



             



There is a high employment rate as pure mathematicians can work in different types of places such as engineering firms, finance, banking and accountancy firms, government agencies and insurance companies. 





Variations in Job Timings 





            



There are  permanent and contractual jobs along with full-time and part-time jobs for pure mathematicians that help in creating computer models. Usually, in India a pure mathematician needs to  work for 40-50 hours a week. 





Opportunity to create new theories





           



Pure mathematicians are specialized in mathematics and are provided with various opportunities of creating new theories and reports which develops an unique interest among the mathematicians. 



New Challenges

  

          



Pure mathematicians  need to work on complex and computation methods.  They even need to work for longer hours and you are fond of new challenges and puzzles and love to solve them with patience and hard work.  You can choose to become a pure mathematician. 





How To Become a Pure Mathematician? 





To become a pure mathematician,  you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard: 



STEP -1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to take science  for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2 : Students need to study in a recognized institution and also obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12  in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Students need to  appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different colleges  in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in Pure Mathematics. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree and get a B.Sc. degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters in Pure Mathematics. 









Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become a Pure Mathematician 





   Age Limit :  





  The minimum age to apply as a pure mathematician is 18 years. 



As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications: 





A student must secure  50-60 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Pure Mathematics..



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course in Mathematics. 





Entrance Exams: 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify the following exams in order to be eligible as a pure mathematician

National Entrance Screening Test (NEST)

CMI Entrance Test.

Delhi University Entrance Test (DUET)

Working Environment: 





Pure mathematicians work in various kinds of places like government and private firms, engineering firms, factories and banks. 

They also work with different teams of scientists and other mathematicians. 

They usually need to work for 35 hours to research and manage mathematical theories. 

They also  research on topics like Algebra, Analysis, Combinatorics and Probability, and Geometry.

Excellent interactive skills

Computer skills are a key to success with the growth of technology in recent years . Good knowledge in the field of computers and the pure mathematicians need to have good computer skills so that they can create computer models.

title: Mechanical Engineer
summary: An overview of career a profile:
description:
Mechanical Engineer



 

   An overview of career a profile:

What do we understand by the term ‘Mechanical Engineer’?



The job of a mechanical  engineer is to design, create, manufacture and evaluate the functioning of parts of motor parts of motorbikes, cars, tractors etc. There are different types of mechanical engineers depending on the specialisation in one particular field.



What is the role of an ‘Mechanical Engineer’?

The role of an engineer is to fulfil the following needs and demands of the association-

 Create 





The role of a mechanical engineer is to bring forth new designs and suggestions such as new structures, models or functioning methods of various mechanical parts of any machine.



Operate



Operating involves keeping in check the correct operation of all the machines and instruments which are put into use.





Solve Problems related to mechanical engineering





Develop and implement the functioning of mechanical equipments





Test the proper operation of mechanisms related to vehicles





Management





Without proper management, there can be no efficiency and effective functioning, which is why it is also the job of senior executive engineers to keep a watch on the activities of the staff as per the demand of the organisation. 

  Benefits of choosing this profile as a career

Job Stability

 

A mechanical engineer gets a definite place for work which results in job stability depending upon the quality of his or her work. 

Higher income-

 

Be it a Chemical Engineer or Automobile engineer, there is an ample amount of income generation which comes along with different perks and benefits. 

Transferable job opportunities



Based on the quality of the work, a mechanical  engineer has the liberty to work as per the convenience and there are opportunities available such as transferring from one place to another as per needs. 

Work around the world



Getting employed across the world is also one of the many benefits of being a mechanical  engineer. As per the demand of manpower, a mechanical engineer can be called upon anywhere. 



 

  A basic procedure to approach this job:



 Step 1 - The aspirant needs to complete class 10th and class 12th with Science stream with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics as core subjects.

Step 2 - Apply for the admission process into different colleges and universities. 



Step 3 - Proceed by appearing for the entrance examination meant for Mechanical Engineering such as IIT JEE, AIEEE, BITSAT, state entrance exams etc.



Step 4 - After the clarification of required documents, the candidate will get admission 

             into the institution of the preference. 



Step 5 - Complete the 4-year long engineering course in the concerned field.



Step 6 - Once the candidate has a required graduation degree and 4 years of experience as a trainee or an intern, one can apply for jobs for the different designations of mechanical engineer. 

  Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



Age limit- 



There is no specific age limit for becoming an engineer. But there is definitely a certain age limit for writing the entrance examinations of Engineering .



Education qualification-



The candidate must have cleared 10+2 from a recognised board of education in Science stream with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics as core subjects.

The candidate should have either a Diploma in Engineering or a graduation degree in Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) Mechanical Engineering or Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) in Mechanical Engineering

Post graduation in MTech (Mechanical Engineering) is also acceptable.



Skills Required-



 To become a Mechanical Engineer it is very much important for the candidate to be able to have the following set if skills which will help in working efficiently and effectively -



The candidate needs to be aware of all the latest developments and advancements in the field of science and technology 



He or she should have a good hold on communication and should be able to convey his or her ideas in a comprehensive manner.



Problem solving attitude is a win-win scenario for any organisation to look for in their employees.



Brilliant management and leadership skills



Efficient Working Methods help in being productive and completing the tasks assigned on time. 





Working Environment- 

The working environment for a mechanical engineer is full of technology driven activities and projects. The work of an engineer is quite flexible as it depends upon the need and demand of the situation.

The surrounding atmosphere is full of practicality and physical hardwok,

There can also be a constant demand for efforts in terms of creativity, data analysis, project management and strategic planning.  

Entrance exam to follow the route:



The following is the list of entrance exams to get admissions into colleges for Graduation-

AIEEE- All India Engineering Entrance Examination.

AICET- All India C

title: Mechatronics Engineers
summary: Career Profile - Mechatronics Engineer
description:
Career Profile - Mechatronics Engineer  

    

      



Overview 









Who is a Mechatronics Engineer ? 





A Mechatronics Engineer blends mechanical, electronic, and computer engineering which caters in designing new products. 





Mechatronic Engineers are a subdivision of  engineers  who explore and improve the mechanical systems which exist.



          





They design product specifications, 

programming logic for manufacturing equipment and electro mechanical systems. 







What kind of career profile is it? 





The job of a Mechatronics Engineer encompasses designing and developing machineries and other prototypes. 



This job profile has a number of challenges as they need to focus on technical projects, create mechanical models and implement design solutions. 



A Mechatronics Engineer has to work for long hours and also provide engineering reports and document designs. 



Roles and Responsibilities of a Mechatronics Engineer 



The roles and responsibilities of a Mechatronics Engineer depends on the types of research and designs on which he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows: 



        



The mechatronics engineers create software designs and also help in developing automated control systems  in the environment. 





           





They research communication technologies and provide training on automated control. 



          





They help in analyzing manufactured products and amalgamate mechatronics elements in the equipment that existed.



          



They need to identify the building product prototypes and enhance the safety measures. 

          



           

            



Benefits of Choosing Mechatronics Engineer as a Career Option 



Versatile Job Option 



        



 Mechanical engineering jobs are dynamic and versatile as they not only analyze and develop new products and equipment but also identify safety measures and product specifications. 





High Employment rates and job opportunities 



           



There is a high employment rate as mechatronics engineers are needed in various industries for testing their products and inventing new product models. 



Opportunity to invent new equipment

       

         



Mechatronics engineers design and develop new products with their creative and scientific skills. If you are fond of inventing and designing new products, you can choose to become a Mechatronics Engineer. 







Highly creative 



        



Mechatronics engineers need to possess the quality of creation and creative abilities. They need creative abilities along with their scientific knowledge and create new products. 





How to become a Mechatronics Engineer 



If you want to become a Mechatronics Engineer, you need to follow the provided steps after class 10 : 



STEP -1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain  at least 50-60  percent marks. The candidates need to choose a science stream with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 





STEP - 3 : Appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different institutions in order to get admission for a  Bachelor's degree in Mechatronics or mechanical engineering.  In some cases, students are chosen  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree and get a B.Tech  degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can enter a job and gain experience or apply for post graduation or an internship. 



STEP - 6 : You can apply for a professional engineer licence and become a professional mechatronics engineer. 







Eligibility And Skills To  Become a Mechatronics Engineer : 





Age Limit: 



The minimum age of becoming a Mechatronics Engineer is 18 years. 



The individual needs to be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications: 





A student must secure  50 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Mechanical Engineering or Electronic Engineering. 



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course preferably. 



Entrance Exams





A candidate needs to appear and qualify JEE Main, WBJEE, BITSAT  in order to be eligible as a mechanical engineer. 





Working Environment 



Mechanical Engineers work in various kinds of places and also work with different teams. 

They  usually need to work for longer hours and work in places like Aerospace,  Automotive, Biomedical, Construction and Building . 





Mechanical Engineers  work part time as well as full time and also work for longer hours during a crisis. 







Skills Requirements



Creative abilities



       



Communication skills



       



Listening skills 



         



Time Management skills 

     

        









Entrance Exams to become a Mechatronics Engineer



A student needs to clear entrance exams conducted by universities and institutions in order to get admission in top colleges. Here is a list of entrance examination

title: Medical Physicist
summary: Name of the Career profile: Medical Physicist
description:
Name of the Career profile: Medical Physicist



                             

Overview

A medical physicist is a health professional who has received specialised education and training in the principles and procedures of using physics in medicine and is capable of performing effectively in one or even more subfields of medical physics.

Medical physicists are active in research at various levels, including fundamental, theoretical investigations into novel physical ideas that might be applied for diagnosis and therapy. Equipment development and testing clinical trials of novel imaging and therapy strategies



Roles and responsibilities of a Medical  Physicist:

A medical physicist's primary responsibility is to contribute to the diagnosis and treatment of individual patients as part of a multidisciplinary team.



Physicists provide scientific and technical advice on the design of radiation facilities as well as the safe handling, storage, and disposal of radioactive materials.



Basic scientists in universities, equipment manufacturers, and a variety of medical practitioners, including radiographers, radiologists, and radiation oncologists, are frequently involved in collaborations.



Another important aspect of the role is teaching, whether on official courses in partnership with institutions or through workplace-based programs.



Benefits of choosing Medical Physicist as a career:



It requires less debt than medical school and pays more in the beginning.



Less stress, a great social life, and you'll be well-known in a short time.



There will be far less competition than in medical school, so you should be able to achieve career satisfaction very quickly.



You'll enjoy the technical physics aspect and will soon be able to teach.

             



A basic procedure to become a  Medical Physicist:

          STEP:1 After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology/Maths.



         STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Maths.



         STEP:3 Complete your undergraduate degree in physics or any other related field.



         STEP:4 Complete a master's degree in physics, medical physics or related fields 



         STEP:5 Can also earn a Doctorate for higher posts.



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment:



            ELIGIBILITY 



             Age Limit-



The candidate must be of a minimum age limit of 17 years to give the entrance exam.

      

             Education Qualification-

 

A master's degree or Ph.D. in physics, medical physics, or a similar subject is normally required for positions as a medical physicist.



SKILLS REQUIRED

Self-discipline - Medical physicists must maintain motivation since they spend a significant amount of time examining enormous datasets in order to identify patterns that may generate insights. 



Communication -Medical physicists must be able to interact successfully with physicians and other team members.



Analytical abilities-They must be exact and accurate in their analysis, or their study would be invalidated.



Problem-solving abilities- Medical physicists address complicated scientific issues by scientific observation and analysis, as well as creative thinking.





WORKING ENVIRONMENT

            

Medical Physicists provide clinical service and consulting teaching at hospitals, healthcare institutions, medical clinics, and private practice. They also work in the radiation field's research and development divisions on occasion.

full_content:
Name of the Career profile: Medical Physicist



                             

Overview

A medical physicist is a health professional who has received specialised education and training in the principles and procedures of using physics in medicine and is capable of performing effectively in one or even more subfields of medical physics.

Medical physicists are active in research at various levels, including fundamental, theoretical investigations into novel physical ideas that might be applied for diagnosis and therapy. Equipment development and testing clinical trials of novel imaging and therapy strategies



Roles and responsibilities of a Medical  Physicist:

A medical physicist's primary responsibility is to contribute to the diagnosis and treatment of individual patients as part of a multidisciplinary team.



Physicists provide scientific and technical advice on the design of radiation facilities as well as the safe handling, storage, and disposal of radioactive materials.



Basic scientists in universities, equipment manufacturers, and a variety of medical practitioners, including radiographers, radiologists, and radiation oncologists, are frequently involved in collaborations.



Another important aspect of the role is teaching, whether on official courses in partnership with institutions or through workplace-based programs.



Benefits of choosing Medical Physicist as a career:



It requires less debt than medical school and pays more in the beginning.



Less stress, a great social life, and you'll be well-known in a short time.



There will be far less competition than in medical school, so you should be able to achieve career satisfaction very quickly.



You'll enjoy the technical physics aspect and will soon be able to teach.

             



A basic procedure to become a  Medical Physicist:

          STEP:1 After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology/Maths.



         STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Maths.

 Professor Keith Boddy, his interest in radiation safety served as the foundation of his profession for most of his life. He developed his team into one of the top medical physics departments in the nation over his 20 years as the department head in Newcastle, UK.

title: Medical Technician
summary: Name of the Career Profile : MEDICAL TECHNICIAN
description:
Name of the Career Profile : MEDICAL TECHNICIAN

                                                                            



    Overview

 Medical technicians are the people who play a very important role in the healthcare industry by providing support and help for physicians and hospitals.

They do basic tasks on an everyday basis related to medical analysis which are conducted in laboratories.

They work under the guidance of the lab technologist or the laboratory manager.

                               

                                  

                  Roles and Responsibilities:

To collect and prepare the blood, urine, and tissues samples for testing and analysis.



                            







To identify the abnormalities after analyzing the samples collected and examining them.



                               



To maintain the laboratory for example, maintaining the laboratory inventory levels and restocking it if necessary.



                        



Prepare reports of the results of the samples taken and summarize the result they got from analysis.

                                                             

                 



           

Benefits of choosing this career profile

It is one of the most important parts of the healthcare system because without the medical technicians the operations in the hospitals will be the toughest.



                          



Medical technicians play a link between the doctors and other things or patients.

        

                         



It is very reputed job because without them the communication in the hospitals become very hard



                     



It gives a good amount of income and they also get job security once joined in a hospital or laboratory.



                           





A basic procedure to approach this job



                          Complete class 10







                         Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 50% in science stream with            

                         Biology, chemistry and physics from any recognized school.

                       



                      Earn an Associate’s degree of medical technician from an accredited college .





                         Obtain certification to work in clinical lab settings.



                                    

                                       Gain work experience as a medical technician and do bachelor’s degree which                

                                       will make you a medical technologist .



Eligibility and skills required:



Age limit:

There is no age limit to pursue this course



              Educational Qualifications

The candidate must have passed 12th class in science with a biology stream from any recognized school.

Candidates must have an associate degree and a certificate.

Candidates can also do bachelor’s in medical technology.





              Skills Requirement

                  



Organizational skills                                            Analytical skills









                                                                                                                               Knowledge of bio- Technical skills                                    chemistry techniques           



                                                Operating lab

                                                                              equipment





Analytical skills

Medical technicians require analytical skills for analyzing the samples taken of blood, urine, etc. and provide reports of the test. It is their primary responsibility to take samples and analyze them so this skill is very important.



Knowledge of Biochemistry techniques

They should have knowledge of biochemistry techniques and theories because to examine the cell abnormalities and for taking samples this skill is needed and is very important.



Operating Lab equipment

Technicians should know how to operate the lab equipment and other resources. Because for examining the samples and assisting in hospitals they should know how to use the equipment.



Organizational skills

Organizational skills are very important for medical technicians because there are so many samples at a time and many other things to do. Therefore, being organized about the work is very necessary.





           Work environment

Medical technicians usually work in laboratories for taking samples of different things and examining them.



                            



Medical technicians also assist in hospitals so they have to work in hospitals and help the doctors.



                           





Entrance exam to become Medical Technicians

Some colleges take entrance exams for admission while some offer admission on the basis of merit. So for admission to the entrance exam, one has to make sure that he or she passes with the minimum cutoff. Different colleges have different entrance exams.

Types of specialization available for medical technician



Clinical Pathology

In clinical pathology, Medical technicians conduct lab tests and examine the tests done on body fluids and tissues. The tests are done to find out if there are any infectious or non- infectious disease markers or not. The different types of test they need to conduct are of blood, urine, stool, sputum, spinal fluid etc. ,



                              





Surgical Technologists

They assist the doctors during the surgeries by helping them with the preparations , patient care and keeping record of the patients about the heartbeats and everything. Also they prepare the operating room or transfer the patient from the normal room to OT. Basically they work under the supervision of surgeons.



                           

Pharmacy Technicians

Pharmacy technicians make sure that the medication required to the customers is ready by the time. Their work includes labeling the medicines according to prescription , inventory management etc.. They work under the supervision of a Pharmacist.

 

                   



MRI technicians

MRI technicians are responsible for doing MRI tests and preparing reports of it. They are specialized radiology technicians.

Promotions and upgradations 



In the career profile of medical technicians after the promotion the technician becomes a research  associate or a manager on the basis of the degree and the experience.The promotions in this field are limited.

One can upgrade their designations by upskilling them and can switch there brance=hes of the technicians.



Top recruiters agencies

1.Apollo Hospitals

  

2.HCG hospitals

  



3.Dr. Lal PathLabs Ltd.

    

4.Metropolis Pathology



   



5.Thyrocare



   



6.Zydus Hospital 



   



Demand and future scope



Number of laboratories and hospitals are rising day by day so the demand for medical technicians will rise and also the staff needed after the pandemic is very high so the demand for this career profile will always increase.

The scope of this career profile will increase because now some medical technicians are also going in the field of teaching. The awareness and knowledge about this kind of vocational course will increase day by day.

It has a high growth because as day by day the technology upgrades the need for technicians increases.





Top inspiring medical technicians

1.  Karl Landsteiner

   

Karl Landsteiner was a physician, biologist and immunologist. He contributed by differentiating different blood groups and identifying the Rhesus Factor. He also discovered the polio virus.

 

2.  Joseph Lister

   

 

 

Joseph Lister was a British medical technician or a pathologist. He is the pioneer of antiseptic surgery and preventive  medicine. He got many medals for this work.

 

3.  Paul Ehrlich

                          

Paul Ehrlich was a German physician and scientist and a medical technician who got Nobel Prize because of his work. He worked in different fields like immunology, antimicrobial etc.

 

4.  Cesar Milstein

   

 

Cesar Milstein was an Argentine medical technician, after that a biochemist. He did a lot of research on antibodies and he is acknowledged for developing the Hybridoma technology . He also got the Nobel Prize for this.

 

5.   Baruj Benacerraf

   

 

 

Baruj Benacerraf was an American immunologist and a medical technician who discovered the MHC genes. This made him win the Nobel Prize in Medical in 1980 and many other awards in the rest of the life.

title: Medicinal Chemist
summary: Name Of the Career Profile: Medicinal Chemist
description:
Name Of the Career Profile: Medicinal Chemist



                   



     ➢An overview of career profile:



A medicinal chemist learns and creates chemical substances for use as pharmaceuticals. These chemists play an important role in the pharmaceutical sector by using chemical research techniques to identify natural healing substances or generate artificial ones.

The use of chemical research methodologies in the manufacture of medications is known as medicinal chemistry.



Roles and Responsibilities



Medicinal chemists play an important role in drug development by selecting and synthesising molecules that establish structure-activity correlations and demonstrate effectiveness and safety in preclinical animal testing.







Combinatorial chemistry, high-throughput screening, and molecularly defined targets that enable structure-based drug creation have transformed the contemporary chemist's function.





In vitro pharmacokinetic property screens, the emphasis on generating drug-like compounds, and in vitro toxicity screens are all key new breakthroughs that help medicinal chemists today.







More in vivo testing early in the drug discovery process, allowing medicinal chemists to promote their drug candidates during development, and passing on the tacit knowledge of experienced medicinal chemists to their younger colleagues are all suggestions for enhancing the drug discovery process.





To develop and test new pharmaceutical goods, they work in collaboration with other scientists and engineers.





     ➢Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Choosing a Medicinal Chemist for the future opens up many career opportunities be it research, job, or social work.







Medicinal chemistry’s involvement in daily life  is rapidly evolving by the arrival of many methods in the modern world and also because of the growing population.





Advancements in chemical usage and the artificial world have made it an intellectually challenging and rewarding career choice to be an inorganic chemist.







Choosing a profile related to medicine or medical has always been very promising be it salary, respect and many other benefits.



Being in the medical field is a sense of respect for the individual or for people around him/her.



➢A basic procedure to approach this:  

       

      STEP:1 After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology



       STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology.



 STEP:3 Complete your Bachelor’s Degree in Science (Pharmaceutical or organic chemistry or  related field)



       STEP:4 Pursue Masters in Science major organic chemistry 



       STEP:5 (Optional)PhD in organic chemistry



➢Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



           ELIGIBILITY



 Age Limit-  A candidate should complete a minimum of 17 years of age to get admission in the course.



           Educational Qualification- A candidate should hold at least a bachelor's degree in a related field.



           Skills Requirement 



Individuals who choose a job as a chemist must manage time between experiments and assure the proper completion of a project before its deadline. He or she must keep meticulous records of processes and time. 







Issue Solving Ability: Chemists must be able to recognise a problem and propose the best feasible solution to address it. Before implementing a remedy, he or she assesses its efficacy.







Communication abilities as scientists must be able to effectively communicate. He or she should be able to properly record the technique or method. He or she should be able to comprehend what another team member is saying or attempting to express.







Organizing Skills are a must, chemists must analyze a large amount of data in order to forecast techniques, methods, and results. He or she must have excellent organizational abilities. I





Analytical skills are also one of the most important skills an individual should have to be a chemist as they need to anaylse the symptoms, results, and experiments and then conclude the result.





WORKING ENVIRONMENT





A Medicinal chemist may operate indoors in an office, research laboratory, industry, educational institution or hospitals. 

Also Medicinal Chemists operate in pharmaceutical manufacturing firms, testing laboratories, offices, chemical manufacturing enterprises, and academic institutions including colleges and universities.



 ➢Entrance exam to follow the route:



Central Universities Entrance Test.

Jawaharlal Nehru University Entrance Exam

Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test.

BHU Undergraduate Entrance Test.

Banaras Hindu University Postgraduate Entrance Test (BHU PET).

Delhi University Entrance Exam

Joint Admission Test for Masters (JAM)

     ➢Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Alexander Fleming





Alexander Flemming had a reputation for brilliance in medical and chemical research prior to discovering penicillin. After witnessing numerous troops die of sepsis as a result of infected wounds during World War I, he aggressively explored viable antiseptic medications.



Jesse Boot 



Although Jesse's father, John Boot, founded Boots Pharmacy, it was Jesse who transformed the firm into the global phenomenon that it is today.Goods were purchased in quantity at a far lower cost than the brand's competitors, allowing them to be offered at a much lower cost.



John Pemberton





Coca-Cola was created by qualified pharmacist John Pemberton to help him overcome his morphine addiction. Initially promoted as a 'Pemberton's French Wine Cola,' the drink contains alcohol, kola nut, and damiana.



Hubert Humphrey





With a degree in Pharmacy, the sky's the limit, as American Vice President Hubert Humphrey demonstrated. Although he never became president, Humphrey was not only a licensed pharmacist, but also a political force to be reckoned with.



Friedrich Serturber





 Friedrich Serturner, working as a pharmacist's assistant, was the first chemist to isolate morphium  (morphine) from opium. Opium was a commonly available and addictive substance in the 18th century (and, as you may have heard, it wasn't just popular for its medicinal virtues).

title: Microbiologist Career File
summary: An overview of career a profile:
description:
Microbiologist 



 

   An overview of career a profile:

What do we understand by the term ‘Microbiologist’?

A microbiologist is a scientist who studies living microorganisms and infectious particles, such as bacteria and viruses, which are only visible under a microscope. 

What is the role of a ‘Microbiologist’?



The role of a microbiologist is to fulfil the following needs and demands-

Research -





There is a lot of research involved on a daily basis in order to find out the relevance and reasons behind certain problems which cannot be resolved through basic experiments.



Diagnostics





Everything in the field of medical science is linked to each other and thus it becomes more important to find out the causes behind one particular disease outbreak in order to eliminate or break the chain of transfer.

Analyzation 



This role involve analysing the collected data samples and producing results 

   Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Working for the social cause

Microbiologists work for the betterment of society in terms of healthcare and environment.





Higher pay

There is a good amount of income generated by performing the roles and responsibilities of a microbiologist.



Better Working Conditions



The laboratories are well lighted and clean. Some microbiologists also get the instruments and equipment for experiments and research. 

Access to numerous archives and research data -



For studying and discovering, there is a lot of research involved in the background for which the microbiologist gets the chance to go through all the old papers and research. 

   A basic procedure to approach this job:



Step 1 - The aspirant needs to complete class 10th and class 12th with Science stream.



Step 2 - Apply for the admission process into different colleges and universities.

 

Step 3 - Proceed by appearing for the entrance examination meant for Biomedical Engineering.



Step 4 - Appear for the entrance exam meant for Microbiology, i.e.CUET, AMUEEE, BHU UET, IISER IAT Complete the 3-year long biomedical engineering course.



Step 5 -  After the clarification of required documents and passing the examination, the candidate will get admission into the institution as per the preference. 



Step 6 - Complete the 3-year long graduation course in microbiology.



Step 7 - Once the candidate has a required graduation degree, the candidate can now practise by applying for various internship programs. 

   Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :

 

Age limit- 

In order to be eligible for becoming a microbiologist, it is important for the candidate to be of the age group - 21 years to 37 years .

Education qualification-

The candidate must have cleared 10+2 from a recognised board of education with Science stream.

The candidate must have scored an aggregate of 50%  in all the subjects.

The candidate should have a graduation degree in Bachelor of Science (BSc) in Microbiology

Post graduation in MSc (Microbiology) is also beneficial in becoming a Microbiologist. 

Skills Required-



To become a Microbiologist  it is very much important for the candidate to be able to have the following set if skills which will help in working efficiently and effectively -

Ability to solve and reason the problem



Excellent awareness of surroundings and its requirements



Perceive and visualise



Ability to work in a group 



Working environment for a Microbiologist-





Microbiologists typically work in laboratories, offices, and industrial settings where they conduct experiments and analyse the results. Microbiologists who work with dangerous organisms must follow strict safety procedures to avoid contamination. Some microbiologists may conduct onsite visits or collect samples from the environment or worksites, and, as a result, may travel occasionally and spend some time outside.

   Entrance exam to follow the route:



The following is the list of entrance exams to get admissions into colleges for Graduation-

Lovely Professional University Microbiology Entrance Exam

M.Sc (Biotechnology)& IIT JAM Entrance Exams

Mahatma Gandhi University Microbiology Entrance Exam

Manipal Academy of Higher Education Microbiology Entrance Exam

Osmania university Microbiology Entrance Exam

University of Calcutta Microbiology Entrance Exam

University of Mumbai Microb

title: NURSE
summary: CAREER PROFILE: NURSE
description:
CAREER PROFILE: NURSE



Overview:

Who is a Nurse?

 

Nurse is a healthcare profession that deals with the various aspects of patient care.

Nurse works with the doctor and other personnel of the healthcare industry.

Nurse is the one who serves the people who need medical assistance by giving them assistance.





What kind of Job is it, Field or desk?

The job of a nurse is to serve the people who need medical assistance. 

Nurses work in close contact with the doctors and other personnel of the healthcare industry.



Roles and responsibilities of a Nurse:



The roles and responsibilities of a nurse are mentioned below:

The main role of the nurse is to treat the medical emergencies that are coming in the hospitals. They need to take care of the patients and play a crucial role when the doctors are still not arrived. They have to make necessary arrangements for the patients.







To resolve and report the patients needs and problems like asking the patients about the problem they are facing and providing the medications after consulting the doctor. 







To monitor and record a patient's condition and document provided care services. This includes recording the blood pressure or sugar level  and other things and consulting with the doctor. Also, provide the prescription given by the doctor to the patients.









To work within and cooperate with a multidisciplinary team because nurse play as a link between the doctors, patients and other staff. So they need to be in touch with others and communicate with each other.







To focus on a patient's needs and act on them and make them feel calm and happy. Also, they play an important role in the recovery of the patients. They need to calm down the environment and make the patient feel happy.







Benefits of Choosing Nurse As A Career Profile:

Noble Profession:

Nurses care for their patients selflessly and often have to work for long hours to provide care to their patients. They provide care to their patients because they feel the need to do so , because of that this profession is called a noble profession.

     



Job Security : 

Nurses play a necessary and important part in the healthcare system. Nurses are so ingrained in society, there is always a need for them, mostly other than other professions. 

   





Excellent Wages and Benefits:

A Nursing career provides excellent wages and benefits. Registered nurses earn a fair salary for their work performed. They are usually paid on their working hourly basis. 

 

                        

 

Opportunities for advancement: 

A Nursing career offers many opportunities for advancement. They are able to advance into a management position with only their nursing degree. Many nurses are promoted as team leaders first and leading their shift hour and having some management responsibilities.      



Flexible work Hours:

Nurses have flexible work hours and do not have to work overtime. They need to work according to the time of the duty allotted. They have to work in shifts so they have a fixed schedule for everyday.         

                                                                                               





How To Become a Nurse?



To become a nurse right after 10th follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete 10th and choose science stream as if you are interested in a medical profession.

Step-2: Complete 12th in science stream with physics, chemistry and biology as their main subjects.

Step-3: Appear for the Nurse Entrance Exam such as AIIMS nursing entrance exam, Indian Army Nursing Exam.

Step-4: Qualify the entrance exam for this career and then attend an accredited nursing program whether it’s CAN, LPN, BSN or something else.

Step-5: obtain licensure in nursing after completing a diploma, bachelor or master’s degree registered under Indian Nursing Council.

Step-6: Earn additional certifications and continue practicing.

 

Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

The age limit for becoming a Nurse depends on the route you choose after 12th. 

Educational qualifications:

A student must have passed 12th in science stream with at least 50% aggregate marks  from  any recognized board.

For admission, candidates can apply with a 10th DMC certificate, having at least 50% aggregate marks. 

Entrance exam for the nursing course is compulsory for admission in this career course. 







Work Environment:

Ambulatory healthcare services includes industries such as physicians’ offices, home healthcare, and outpatient care centers. 

Nurses who work in home health travel to patients’ homes; public health nurses may travel to community centers, schools, and other sites.Nurses have to mostly work in hospitals and operation theaters according to their shifts.

Nurses have to also work in different healthcare camps and donation camps organized by healthcare centers in different villages and cities.

Overview:

Who is a Nurse?

 

Nurse is a healthcare profession that deals with the various aspects of patient care.

Nurse works with the doctor and other personnel of the healthcare industry.

What kind of Job is it, Field or desk?

The job of a nurse is to serve the people who need medical assistance. 

Nurses work in close contact with the doctors and other personnel of the healthcare industry.



Roles and responsibilities of a Nurse:



The roles and responsibilities of a nurse are mentioned below:

The main role of the nurse is to treat the medical emergencies that are coming in the hospitals. They need to take care of the patients and play a crucial role when the doctors are still not arrived. They have to make necessary arrangements for the patients.







To resolve and report the patients needs and problems like asking the patients about the problem they are facing and providing the medications after consulting the doctor. 







To monitor and record a patient's condition and document provided care services. This includes recording the blood pressure or sugar level  and other things and consulting with the doctor. Also, provide the prescription given by the doctor to the patients.









To work within and cooperate with a multidisciplinary team because nurse play as a link between the doctors, patients and other staff. So they need to be in touch with others and communicate with each other.







To focus on a patient's needs and act on them and make them feel calm and happy. Also, they play an important role in the recovery of the patients. They need to calm down the environment and make the patient feel happy.







Benefits of Choosing Nurse As A Career Profile:

Noble Profession:

Nurses care for their patients selflessly and often have to work for long hours to provide care to their patients. They provide care to their patients because they feel the need to do so , because of that this profession is called a noble profession.

     



Job Security : 

Nurses play a necessary and important part in the healthcare system. Nurses are so ingrained in society, there is always a need for them, mostly other than other professions. 

   





Excellent Wages and Benefits:

A Nursing career provides excellent wages and benefits. Registered nurses earn a fair salary for their work performed. They are usually paid on their working hourly basis. 

 

                        

 

Opportunities for advancement: 

A Nursing career offers many opportunities for advancement. They are able to advance into a management position with only their nursing degree. Many nurses are promoted as team leaders first and leading their shift hour and having some management responsibilities.      



Flexible work Hours:

Nurses have flexible work hours and do not have to work overtime. They need to work according to the time of the duty allotted. They have to work in shifts so they have a fixed schedule for everyday.         

                                                                                               





How To Become a Nurse?



To become a nurse right after 10th follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete 10th and choose science stream as if you are interested in a medical profession.

Step-2: Complete 12th in science stream with physics, chemistry and biology as their main subjects.

Step-3: Appear for the Nurse Entrance Exam such as AIIMS nursing entrance exam, Indian Army Nursing Exam.

Step-4: Qualify the entrance exam for this career and then attend an accredited nursing program whether it’s CAN, LPN, BSN or something else.

Step-5: obtain licensure in nursing after completing a diploma, bachelor or master’s degree registered under Indian Nursing Council.

Step-6: Earn additional certifications and continue practicing.

 

Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

The age limit for becoming a Nurse depends on the route you choose after 12th. 

Educational qualifications:

A student must have passed 12th in science stream with at least 50% aggregate marks  from  any recognized board.

For admission, candidates can apply with a 10th DMC certificate, having at least 50% aggregate marks. 

Entrance exam for the nursing course is compulsory for admission in this career course. 







Work Environment:

Ambulatory healthcare services includes industries such as physicians’ offices, home healthcare, and outpatient care centers. 

Nurses who work in home health travel to patients’ homes; public health nurses may travel to community centers, schools, and other sites.Nurses have to mostly work in hospitals and operation theaters according to their shifts.

Nurses have to also work in different healthcare camps and donation camps organized by healthcare centers in different villages and cities.



Skill requirements:

To become a Nurse, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Excellent Communication Skills are required because they have to talk with patients and ask them about their problems and also guide and provide them proper medication. Also they have to talk with doctors and tell them about the problems of the patients and then communicate everything.



Ability to adapt:  This means that the nurses should be able to understand the patients problems and their behavior and then make them feel safe and calm so that they can recover quickly. Therefore treating them like a family and adapting them is required.







Emotionally Support Other Nurses : This skill is required to work together and help and guide each other. Everyone should have the skill of guiding the other staff members if they are not able to understand anything. This helps to reduce the load and work together.



Analytical skills: They have to take the record of different activities in the patient’s body and analyze it and provide the report of the patient's health to the doctor. therefore the skill of analytics is required.





Organizing skills: This is a very important skill because they have to take care of so many patients at a time so organizing their records and medicines is very much required. Otherwise, it may lead to big problems.





Entrance Exams To Become A Nurse :

There are different entrance exams available to pursue Nurse as a career option. A student must clear the entrance exam in order to be eligible for the admissions. Below given are some of the entrance exams for nurse degree:

Lady Hardinge Medical College

This course is a type of diploma in nursing after 12th. In this course one focuses on caring for patients who are suffering from mental disorders or distress. This diploma course can be pursued from many top colleges. 



Diploma in Nursing: 







In this diploma course students are eligible for various entry- level options in administrative and office positions.

There is a lot more shortage of nurses because of the virus spread in the country and nurses are more in demand but there is a shortage in that. So in future there are more nurses needed. The number of hospitals, healthcare centers in the country are rising and hence the need and demand for nurses will increase.

It has a lot of scope in future because with time the technology is increasing so the human resource is needed to work with the technology in hospitals and laboratories. And also the gender biased ness in the nurse career profile is decreasing



Top Inspiring Lawyers

Here is the list of some of the most celebrated and highly honored Nurses from India.

1. Florence Nightingale:

 

Much of the sanitation procedures used now were founded by Florence Nightingale whose primary aim was to increase the survival rate of patients in hospitals, while she worked to heal soldiers from Crimean War.

2. Dorothea Dix 



She went on to do further investigations in various other states like New Hampshire, Louisiana, North Carolina. 

3. Clara Barton



She got the permission to create the American Red Cross and now it has become synonymous with disaster relief in many parts of the world too. 

4. Mary Eliza Mahoney



She started to work in private care and then founded the National Association of Colored Graduate Nurses in 1908.

5. Virginia Lynch



She made it a point to learn more about how such items could be used to track the rapists. Thanks to her, the world has forensic nursing today which helps to land more criminals in jail.

title: Name of the ASTRONOMER
summary: Name of the career profile: ASTRONOMER
description:
Name of the career profile: ASTRONOMER

➢    Overview 

An astronomer is a scientist who focuses on the study of celestial objects, such as planets, galaxies, black holes, and stars.

Astronomers spend their time doing data analysis, publishing research articles, or developing computer tools that make it easier to find the data they have gathered.

They provide us with information with the information about space about the space and celestial bodies.

Roles & responsibilities of a Astronomer

                        

Their role is to test hypotheses and learn about the properties of matter and energy.

                       

They design and carry out scientific experiments and research for the betterment of society.

                         

Additionally, they use mathematical computations to examine facts from physics and astronomy, such as the existence of planets in distant solar systems or new material qualities. 

                         

They gather information about stars, planets, and other celestial bodies using tools like optical or radio telescopes.

                        

Pay extreme attention to objects that might crash into the earth, such as meteors.



Work with other researchers, like meteorologists, to anticipate climate designs and other events



➢    Benefits of choosing this profile as a career

                    

Chance to work in an organisation like NASA/ISRO which is considered such a huge respect for an individual and their family.                       

               

Enjoy the freedom of conducting their own research, arriving at their own conclusions and making their own decisions.                        

        

It is a fascinating subject with many research opportunities and to gain knowledge and earn experience.

           

 Being an astronomer can be both fiscally profitable and intellectually stimulating . Hence a sense of relax and better working environment.

            

Many more benefits such as being on Television ,better salary pay, chance to contribute in advancement of weather forecast , no normal office days etc.



A basic procedure to approach this job:

           

Step-1: Complete class 10.

Step-2: Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 50% in science stream with physics, chemistry and mathematics as compulsory subjects from any recognised board in order to get a seat in a college.

Step-3: After completing 10+2 candidates can enrol for a bachelor's degree programme which will  take 3 years.

Step-4: Candidates can also complete a PostgraduatePost Graduate course in related specialisation without any gap in the academic year.

Step-5: After completing a Master's Completing Master’s degree, candidates can start working in the industry at an entry-level.

Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

              Age limit:

 The  candidate must be minimum of  17 years old to pursue this field as their career.

             Educational qualifications:

A student must have passed class 12 in science stream with at least 50% aggregate from any government-recognized board or private institution.

Candidates must complete 3 years of undergraduate course with honours in Physics with Mathematics from any recognized institute.

Candidates can complete a PostgraduatePost Graduate course in Physics or Astronomy which will take 2 years, and pass the required entrance exam in order to qualify as an astronomer.

For PhD, candidates must clear the Joint Entrance Screening Test (JEST).



              Skill requirements

             To become an astronomer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities:

              

Problem solving skills to deal with all the unknown challenges and have a positive approach towards solving the problem and should not give up and face the challenges instead.

           



People and technology skills as they have to deal with the team and also many technical instruments presents in the research center or in their working environment.

           



Knowledge of  scientific rules and strategies required for problem solving hence a person should have good knowledge in their educational field and awareness of scientific updations.

             



Critical thinking should have analysis and evaluation skills to solve the problems coming to get the conclusion.

           



Research skills as they need to perform scientific experiments on daily basis. Studies and scientific experiments needs to be accurate and free of errors.

           

       Work environment

            

Astronomers may work in workplaces; investigate research facilities, and observatories. Most physicists and astronomers work full time, and a few work more than 40 hours per week.



Entrance Exams To Become a Astronomer:

Candidates can appear for several exams conducted by various Universities across India to pursue a career as an astronomer:

Aligarh Muslim University Entrance Exam

Andhra University Entrance Exam

Aryabhatta Re

Types Of Specializations Available For an Astronomer:

Research Scientist:



A research scientist designs, undertakes, analyses information from laboratory-based experiments and investigations.

Astronomer:

            

Prepares and assesses scientific theories and research proposals. They explore the ever-expanding space from a position of standstill due to the issue of light reaching us.

Space Scientist:

          

 Prepares presentations and observation papers recorded during the research process to track the work and progress done by the team.

Astrophysicist:

          

Watches space, utilises telescopes and analyses outer space and observes the atmosphere and sees the changes .

Observational astronomers:

            

Observational Astronomers develop and test scientific theories, analyse data and make research proposals.

Promotion and Upgradation:

         

You will be guided by your supervisor, who will also oversee your research, as you pursue a PhD while holding a research position. Additional training could be made available to you by the university or by outside groups like Vitae, which promotes the professional growth of researchers.

Most astronomers hold at least one postdoctoral employment after receiving their PhDs. Additionally, national (UKRI) and foreign funding organisations provide individual fellowships that let you advance independently in your research career.

Through conferences, events, and networking with colleagues, you'll need to stay current on advances in your industry. Professional organisations like The Royal Society and the Royal Astronomical Society provide opportunities.



Top Recruiting Agencies

             Top organisations which hire astronomers are:

National Aeronautics and Space Administration



European Space Agency

             

Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency

              

Indian Space Research Organisation



Space X

              

Leolabs



Orbital Insight



Demands And Future Scope:



Overall work of physicists and astronomers is anticipated to develop 8 percent from 2020 to 2030, around as quick as the normal for all occupations.

 Approximately 1,500 openings for physicists and astronomers are anticipated each year, on normal, over the decade. Numerous of those openings are anticipated to result from the need to replace specialists who exchange to distinctive occupations or exit the labour constrain, such as to resign



Top Inspiring Astronomers:



Stephen Hawking (1942 - 2018) 

           

Hawking's first finding, made at Cambridge University during his time researching, is that from the time the Universe started (with the Big Bang), it would also end. His discoveries were published in many books, such as the top-selling 'A Brief History of Time'

Henrietta Swan Leavitt (1868 - 1921)

              

Henrietta’s discovery has provided astronomers with the ability to measure the distance between galaxies, especially those far off, which came to be known as the ‘standard candle’ – an astronomical object with luminosity, on the cosmic distance ladder (methods used to determine the distance between celestial objects).

Aryabhata

            

Indian mathematician and astronomer Aryabhata pioneered the concept of “zero” and used it in his “place value system.” He had also worked on the approximation of pi and laid down the basic concepts of trigonometry, such as sine and cosine.

Suresh

          

Byrana Nagappa Suresh is an Indian aerospace scientist. He served as the Director of Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre (VSSC), ThiruvananthapuramHe is known for his contribution to development of Indian launch vehicles and Space Capsule Recovery Experiments (SRE).

A S Kiran Kumar

           

Aluru Seelin Kiran Kumar is an Indian space scientist and the chairman of the Indian Space Research Organization having assumed office on 14 January 2015. He is credited with the development of key scientific instruments aboard the Chandrayaan-1 and Mangalyaan space crafts.

title: Nature Conservation officers
summary: Name of the Career profile: Nature Conservation officers
description:
Name of the Career profile: Nature Conservation officers



                        



     ➢ Overview 

A nature conservation officer is a law enforcer who safeguards wild animals and natural ecosystems in areas where hunting and fishing are legal. Nature conservation officials are variously referred to as game wardens, wildlife or natural resource managers, and peace officers.

Nature conservation officers may receive training in a range of vehicles, such as all-terrain vehicles or boats, to help them explore their job location



Roles and responsibilities of a Nature conservation officer

Investigating possible dangers reported by visitors so that no one is harmed and to maintain the security..

In the given region they provide hunter safety classes and various precautions to ensure the law.



Reporting on observations and environmental protection measures



Vehicle searches and seizures of items not authorized at a facility. They have to make sure that anything which harms nature is being seized.







.

     ➢ Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



It provides a fantastic outside working environment.If you enjoy spending time in nature this job is for you.



Compensation and Opportunities: these are paid leave, insurance coverage, retirement plan, and more. As an opportunity, you get to serve mother nature .



Job satisfaction-You receive the satisfaction of safeguarding rare/endangered/threatened species and sequestering thousands of tonnes of carbon.



You can specialise in related topics and undertake research on them. The government supports such participation and even provides paid time off.





     ➢ A basic procedure to approach this job

      

STEP:1  Complete class 10th.Choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology after your class 12th.

STEP:2 Complete your class 12th with subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology with an aggregate of 50% marks.

STEP 3: A bachelor's degree is required  Some of the specializations are Environmental science, Biology, Ecology, Forestry, and Botany. 

STEP 4: A master's or doctoral degree will be more beneficial to enhance your skills. Those can be M.Sc. (Master of Science) in Botany, M.Sc. (Master of Science) in Wildlife Science, M.Sc. (Master of Science) in Biology, M.Sc. (Master of Science) in Environmental Science, M.Sc. (Master of Science) Forestry and L.L.M. (Master of Legislative Law) International Environmental Law





     ➢ Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment



      ELIGIBILITY 



       Age Limit-   A candidate should be above 18 years to be a Nature conservation officer



      Education Qualification-A candidate should hold a  bachelor's degree in a related field  .



        SKILLS REQUIRED

Problem Solving Skills: A wildlife enforcement officer is in charge of resolving problems by analyzing potential solutions. 

             



Physical fitness and activity are required for a wildlife enforcement officer. As they are likely to encounter illegal hunters and poachers, they must be prepared with skills and equipment.



 Liking for nature: A wildlife enforcement officer must spend most of his or her time patrolling in the woods. Being a nature lover helps him or her do better at work. 



Dare to deal with unknown challenges or dangers in the forest area.





            WORKING ENVIRONMENT

           

A nature conservation officer, regardless of who they work for, spends a lot of time outside in      locations like state parks, nature preserves, and outdoor trails or fishing areas.

      ➢ Type of specialization available under this job profiles 



Policy and Society-The Policy and Society speciality focuses on understanding how humans interact with nature in general. This specialism addresses topics such as nature policy, human-environment interactions, and stakeholder processes related to nature.

     ➢ Promotions & upgradings

Being in an natural conservation officer will give an opportunity to get promoted and get known by the world and also a chance to receive many awards for contributing to society .

Famous Nature Conservatives:



Sumaira Abdulali



Muhammad Sumaira She was born in Mumbai, India, in May 1961. She is an environmentalist who focuses on noise pollution and sand mining.



Sunita Narain



Political activist Sunita Narain is a proponent of the green idea of sustainable development. She supports the environment.



Vandana Shiva



Vandana Shiva is an academic and environmental activist. She founded Navdanya, a movement for farmers' rights and biodiversity preservation.



Anadish Pal



Anadish Pal is a poet, inventor, and ardent environmentalist who has been fighting to keep Delhi's trees alive.



Medha Patkar





One of India's most well-known environmentalists is Medha Patkar. She spearheaded the revolutionary Narmada Bachao Andolan, which was crucial in changing India's environmental policy.
