title: Nuclear Engineer
summary: Name of the career profile- Nuclear Engineer
description:
Name of the career profile- Nuclear Engineer



An overview of career a profile:



Nuclear Engineers are responsible for creating and maintaining the world’s most powerful as well as most dangerous power source, i.e nuclear energy. 

Nuclear engineers have to work on extremely toxic yet beneficial materials and generally develop a variety of batteries, instruments, control panels and similar equipment. 

A Nuclear Engineer is one of the more recent branches of engineering and while the job itself is dangerous, it comes with huge benefits and the opportunity to work with very accomplished people.

Submarines to power plants, military to public use, a nuclear engineer can be employed in any of these.



Roles and responsibilities of a Nuclear Engineer

Shutting down of facilities once work is finished

Nuclear engineers are responsible for the shutting down of various sections of the powerplant and also see the disposal of used nuclear fuel or waste and see where the uranium Ross are stored due to high radioactivity. 





Fixing and maintaining equipment and instruments.



 They are also responsible for the repair of the many delicate and highly important pieces of equipment used by workers and other engineers. Since just a slight exposure to uranium or plutonium can cause genetic diseases, engineers always keep things in perfect condition. 





Theoretical Research 



Harnessing nuclear energy is a very dangerous and difficult process and to continue to find newer more safer solutions to reduce pollution and threat to workers involves doing complex calculations and Research and is one of the key roles of a Nuclear Engineer. 



Checking the transmission and maintenance of the machinery.



Nuclear engineers also see whether all the outputs are working and the produced power is making it’s way to the recipients. They are also responsible for the continued functioning of the hydraulics, reactor cores, coolant and monitoring system. 



Preparing reports and project plans 

The engineers also make regular reports on detailed project plans on future projects resulting in expansion or decommissioning of facilities. These reports are very important..

Communicating with other departments

Engineers also interact with professionals of other fields , in order to keep everything in check and documented, because one small mistake may cause a potential catastrophe. This includes discussions and meetings with construction professionals, power grid managers, and government officials.





Weaponry development

While not as benign as the others, it's general knowledge that military powers around the world have tried or are trying to make nuclear weapons, India being one of the few successful ones. These engineers take active part in Development of these in tie-ups with the military. 







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



As a career profile which very few people take, the job of a Nuclear Engineer is very sought after with guarantee of being hired. 



The salary package is very high compared to all other branches of engineering with average salaries of nuclear engineers ranging between 50,000-55,000$ a year. 



It is held in a very high esteem since very few are able to actually become one, so people see you with respect and admiration. 



Those with a curious mind and a zeal for learning will find this an especially rewarding prospect since nuclear engineers have to devote a lot of their time to research. 



Being able to help their nation is another benefit of being an engineer, since any and all progress or discoveries you make will directly benefit the nation and it’s power needs. 





Those who don't like to travel much, will also find this appealing, since once you get employed at a facility, you'll only have to work there till you leave. 







A basic procedure to approach this job:





Step 1: Complete 10th class with minimum 65% marks

Step 2: Complete 12th class in science stream with good marks in Chemistry, Physics and Mathematics. 

Step 3: Once you complete high school, give the right entrance exams and enter any recognized institute with a bachelor’s degree in Nuclear engineering, Solar and Alternative Energy Engineering, mechanical engineering or chemical engineering

Step 4: After completing your bachelor’s you have to give the GATE(Graduate Aptitude Test for Engineers) and OCES(Orientation Course for Engineering Graduates and. Science Postgraduates) exam. If you qualify that you'll be taken to the DAE (Dept. Of Atomic Energy) training school. 

Step 5: After 1 year of training you'll be placed at either DAE or AERB.

Step 6: Alternatively you can complete your masters in Nuclear science related courses and then directly apply for an entry level job. 



Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



Age Limit

The candidates interested have a maximum age of 26 years for general category, 29 got OBC and 31 for SC/ST. 



Educational qualifications 

One
full_content:
Name of the career profile- Nuclear Engineer



An overview of career a profile:



Nuclear Engineers are responsible for creating and maintaining the world’s most powerful as well as most dangerous power source, i.e nuclear energy. 

Nuclear engineers have to work on extremely toxic yet beneficial materials and generally develop a variety of batteries, instruments, control panels and similar equipment. 

A Nuclear Engineer is one of the more recent branches of engineering and while the job itself is dangerous, it comes with huge benefits and the opportunity to work with very accomplished people.

Submarines to power plants, military to public use, a nuclear engineer can be employed in any of these.



Roles and responsibilities of a Nuclear Engineer

Shutting down of facilities once work is finished

Nuclear engineers are responsible for the shutting down of various sections of the powerplant and also see the disposal of used nuclear fuel or waste and see where the uranium Ross are stored due to high radioactivity. 





Fixing and maintaining equipment and instruments.



 They are also responsible for the repair of the many delicate and highly important pieces of equipment used by workers and other engineers. Since just a slight exposure to uranium or plutonium can cause genetic diseases, engineers always keep things in perfect condition. 





Theoretical Research 



Harnessing nuclear energy is a very dangerous and difficult process and to continue to find newer more safer solutions to reduce pollution and threat to workers involves doing complex calculations and Research and is one of the key roles of a Nuclear Engineer. 



Checking the transmission and maintenance of the machinery.



Nuclear engineers also see whether all the outputs are working and the produced power is making it’s way to the recipients. They are also responsible for the continued functioning of the hydraulics, reactor cores, coolant and monitoring system. 



Preparing reports and project plans 

The engineers also make regular reports on detailed project plans on future projects resulting in expansion or decommissioning of facilities. These reports are very important..

Communicating with other departments

Engineers also interact with professionals of other fields , in order to keep everything in check and documented, because one small mistake may cause a potential catastrophe. This includes discussions and meetings with construction professionals, power grid managers, and government officials.





Weaponry development

While not as benign as the others, it's general knowledge that military powers around the world have tried or are trying to make nuclear weapons, India being one of the few successful ones. These engineers take active part in Development of these in tie-ups with the military. 







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



As a career profile which very few people take, the job of a Nuclear Engineer is very sought after with guarantee of being hired. 



The salary package is very high compared to all other branches of engineering with average salaries of nuclear engineers ranging between 50,000-55,000$ a year. 



It is held in a very high esteem since very few are able to actually become one, so people see you with respect and admiration. 



Those with a curious mind and a zeal for learning will find this an especially rewarding prospect since nuclear engineers have to devote a lot of their time to research. 



Being able to help their nation is another benefit of being an engineer, since any and all progress or discoveries you make will directly benefit the nation and it’s power needs. 





Those who don't like to travel much, will also find this appealing, since once you get employed at a facility, you'll only have to work there till you leave. 







A basic procedure to approach this job:





Step 1: Complete 10th class with minimum 65% marks

Step 2: Complete 12th class in science stream with good marks in Chemistry, Physics and Mathematics. 

Step 3: Once you complete high school, give the right entrance exams and enter any recognized institute with a bachelor’s degree in Nuclear engineering, Solar and Alternative Energy Engineering, mechanical engineering or chemical engineering

Step 4: After completing your bachelor’s you have to give the GATE(Graduate Aptitude Test for Engineers) and OCES(Orientation Course for Engineering Graduates and. Science Postgraduates) exam. If you qualify that you'll be taken to the DAE (Dept. Of Atomic Energy) training school. 

Step 5: After 1 year of training you'll be placed at either DAE or AERB.

Step 6: Alternatively you can complete your masters in Nuclear science related courses and then directly apply for an entry level job. 



Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



Age Limit

The candidates interested have a maximum age of 26 years for general category, 29 got OBC and 31 for SC/ST. 



Educational qualifications 

One must first complete 10th class and 12th class in science stream. 

One must have a bachelor's degree in an engineering course focusing on nuclear science. 

One must have at least 55% GATE(Graduate Attitude Test in Engineering) score. 

Candidates should also preferably have a master's degree in Nuclear science. 

Candidates should have given the OCES exam and should have DAE certification. 



Skill Requirements





Skills required of any Nuclear Engineer are:













Analytical Skills

It is very necessary for an engineer to have analytical skills in order to safely evaluate various situations and should have the presence of mind to take necessary countermeasures.



Communication Skills

An engineer should have great communication skills too, so that he can easily interact with all the different sections that are involved in the smooth functioning of a reactor. This includes meetings, reports and various other interactions. 



Computer skills

An engineer should have thorough knowledge of computational abilities such as coding, computer systems and resolving network errors because all of these are essential to maintain a functioning reactor. 



Conceptual Skills

A Nuclear engineer should have complete knowledge about every principle of nuclear science so as to be able to find unique and resourceful solutions to any and all discrepancies that might happen in the case of any problem at the reactor. 



Delegation Skills

They should have good leadership skills so as to delegate minor tasks to their subordinates, since as an engineer you would have many different tasks to work on and so you should have good control over your team, so that they do the job for you. 



Mathematical skills

Nuclear science is a complex subject and even after getting the job, Nuclear engineers have to continually work on improving their understanding on the workings of how reactions happen, in order to maintain and in many cases improve the existing facilities. 



Decision-making skills 

You must be quick thinking in order to take immediate actions in the event anything happens. Since a reactor is a very dangerous place, one must be very collected and mature and should be able to have good skills. 



Work Environment 



Nuclear engineers generally work from offices but depending on their designation, they can be required to work from the industrial zones their workplace is based in. 

They have to wear heavy protective equipment such as gloves, full body suits and sometimes helmets and masks too. 

Generally these facilities are present in urban or semi urban locations so employees don't have to travel very far. 

You also have to work in close proximity with others, that's why you'll have to get used to heavy crowds and large workplaces. 



Entrance exam to follow the route:



There are several exams conducted on All-India and state level for candidates to build a career in Nuclear engineering. Some of the most important ones are:



Names of exams

JEE Mains (Joint Entrance Exams-Main)

JEE advance(Joint Entrance Exams-Advance)

SRMJEEE(Sri Ramaswamy Memorial Joint Engineering Exams)

BITSAT(Birla Institute of Technology and Science Admission Test)

Type of specialization available under this job profiles :



There are a variety of specializations for nuclear engineers to make a career in. Some of the most popular ones are:

Project Engineer

A project engineer as the name suggests is one who plans various projects and future prospects of the facilities, takes care of the technical activities and makes sure all things are functioning properly.

















Atomic Process Engineers



These engineers perform various tasks related to nuclear science and work on the introduction and implementation of atomic processes that are safer and much better in fields of energy generation. This also includes working on new methodology. 



Radiation Engineer



These are the people who are responsible for monitoring the whole facility and noticing any and all discrepancies in the radiation of the reactor. These are also the ones to make sure that the radiation levels remain below harmful levels and no risk to life is present















. 

Systems Engineer



These are the people who maintain the main system and various instruments and equipment of the power plant. They see the safe disposal of nuclear waste and where the residue of nuclear fuel is stored. 



Materials Engineer 



They are responsible for managing all the materials involved in the various processes. They interact with the different contractors and are involved with fuel, metal and waste.



Physical Scientist 

A physical scientist focuses mainly on Physical properties and energy. They conduct research and studies in the field





Engineer Associate 

An Associate Scientific Engineer interacts with all  team members to ensure they are working efficiently. They use different methods to make it inclusive. Its function is to improve productivity.





Pay-Scale in this career profile:



Just like any other job profile, the salary of a Nuclear Engineer also fluctuates a lot depending on the organization, the job designation and years of experience.

Promotions & upgradings:



Generally just like any other career profile, with experience comes greater packages and increased responsibilities.

Mostly higher studies like a master’s degree or a doctorate results in being eligible for these posts. Generally just even a single previous job experience can result in a massive upgrade. 

Every 2-3 years nuclear engineers experience a salary hike of 12-20% with other benefits.

In order to increase your salary packages, and your position within your field, becoming a licensed nuclear Engineer by DAE while also applying for various certifications is a must



Top recruiting agencies for this profile



The Top ten recruiters for Nuclear engineers in India are:

 Demands & Future Scope 

Due to the clean nature of nuclear energy becoming more and more apparent day by day, both government and private sector is trying to build nuclear reactors, an increased number of engineers are needed to fulfill the vacancies

Because of these reasons there is an increased demand and very lucrative salaries awaiting nuclear engineers since this sector is gradually becoming very important.

Due to this there is much future scope in this sector which makes a great career profile for aspiring students.

     

  Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Some famous people who also followed this profile are:



Dr Homi Jahagir Bhabha



India’s foremost scientist and engineer in the field of nuclear physics and development of Indian Atomic program, he was also the director of Tata institute of fundamental research and was close to making India a Nuclear Power till his untimely death in 1966. 



Raja Ramanna



Raja Ramanna was an instrumental person in the Indian Atomic Research program and was the man who saved the Indian atomic program after Bhabha’s death. He joined the atomic program in 1964 and was affiliated with it for 40+ years, due to which he was awarded a Padma Vibhusan.

Homi Nusserwanji Sethna



Homi Nusserwanji Sethna was an Indian nuclear scientist and a chemical engineer, gaining international fame as the Chairman of the Atomic Energy Commission during the time when the first nuclear test, codename Smiling Buddha in Pokhran Test Range in 1974 was conducted.



Anil Kakkodar



Anil Kakodkar is another prominent Indian Nuclear engineer who was instrumental in developing India’s nuclear program and was the president of the atomic commission of India for many years.



P. K. Iyengar



Padmanabhan Krishnagopala Iyengar, was an Indian nuclear physicist who is widely known for his central role in the development of the nuclear program of India

title: Nuclear Physicist
summary: Name of the Career profile: Nuclear Physicist
description:
Name of the Career profile: Nuclear Physicist



                                

Overview 

Nuclear physicists investigate many approaches for applying contemporary science to make nuclear operations easier, safer, and more effective. They frequently play critical roles in sensitive operations.

Nuclear physics is a highly specialised branch of physics that studies the behaviour of matter at the atomic level, namely neutrons and protons, and their consequent fusion and fission, which involve massive amounts of energy.



Roles and responsibilities of a Nuclear Physicist:

A Nuclear Physicist conducts experiments and applies current science in novel ways.



Develops better, cleaner, safer, or more efficient methods of producing nuclear energy. 



You might also work in a laboratory, researching the characteristics of radioactive materials.



 A nuclear Physicist evaluates new findings which are being discovered.





Benefits of choosing a Nuclear Physicist as a career:

Being a nuclear physicist opens up many career opportunities, as there is a need for many people in the research field. The growing population and modernization of the world will lead to more and better opportunity.



Going into a research field is a very respected job by society. Hence you and your job is highly valued both by the government as well as the people. People respect the people who are related to the medical field hence this job has huge respect among people.



High-paying jobs being in a research field, the government itself pays a researcher. Hence get high payment as they achieve this position after many achievements and good work in their field.

In the growing phase of the population, there is a lot of need for the physicist hence their demand and pay will eventually grow.

             

Take use of real-life experience. An individual acquires valuable hands-on experience that helps them build their professional abilities.







A basic procedure to become a  Nuclear Physicist:



       STEP:1 After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology/Maths.



       STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Maths.

       

 STEP:3. Earn a Bachelor's degree in an area related to nuclear physics, with typical options in the  sector including mathematics, physics, and engineering



       STEP:4 Work in a real laboratory and conduct nuclear experiments for different research projects.



STEP:5 Obtain a master's or doctorate in physics or nuclear engineering, as these fields are having  identical tasks and training to those of a nuclear physicist.



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment



             Age Limit-



The candidate must be of a minimum age limit of 17 years to give the entrance exam.

      

             Education Qualification-

 

Candidates must possess a Bachelor's degree and Master’s degree in a related field.



SKILLS REQUIRED



Mathematical and scientific abilities are important for becoming a nuclear physicist because you will very probably apply these academic techniques as part of your regular professional obligations. 





Nuclear physicists must pay close attention to detail in order to be safe during their varied work processes and experiments. Nuclear physicists frequently work with radioactive elements that may cause long-term harm if exposed.





Analytical skills: Nuclear physicists employ analytics when performing experiments, especially at the end when it's time to draw scientific conclusions based on the results. 





Communication: Nuclear physicists frequently work with radioactive materials that can have harmful effects on human and environmental health, therefore it is critical that they communicate efficiently at all times to remove supervision and decrease risk in the workplace.





WORKING ENVIRONMENT







They often work as researchers at colleges, universities, government agencies, or energy companies. 

Nuclear physicists play a vital role in the extraction of nuclear energy and the complex scientific processes that are involved in it, using their years of training, research, and education as resources



Entrance exams:



Central Universities Entrance Test.

Presidency University Bachelor's Degree Entrance Test (PUBDET).

BHU Undergraduate Entrance Test.

Banaras Hindu University Postgraduate Entrance Test (BHU PET).

Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering.

Type of specialisation available:



 Ph.D. Nuclear Physics or Doctor of Philosophy in Nuclear Physics. Nuclear physics is the study of the building blocks and interactions of atomic nuclei. The study of the nature of nuclear force, nuclear astrophysics, and basic symmetries in nature is the subject of research. 

Nuclear physics offers a wide range of practical applications, including nuclear power, archaeological dating, biochemical labelling, smoke detectors, nuclear medicine, and so on.



Pay-Scales as per the Experience:

Promotions and Upgradations:



Nuclear physicists are frequently employed as researchers at schools, universities, government agencies, and energy firms. Nuclear physicists use their years of study, research, and education to play an important part in the extraction of nuclear energy and the complicated scientific procedures that go with it.

If they perform well in their field then could get fame and create history .



Top recruiters for Nuclear physicists:



Defense Research and Development Organization (DRDO)





Bhabha Atomic Research Centre(BARC)





Indian Space Research Organization (ISRO)



Nuclear Power Corporation of India Ltd.(NPCIL)



Department of Atomic Energy (DAE)





Demands and Future Scope:



India aspires to become self-sufficient in terms of energy, defence, and medical technology breakthroughs. As a result, a large labour force of skilled scientists who can handle technology and plan nuclear waste management prudently is required.

As the days are passing technologies and research fields are growing hence also people are less attracted towards research fields so if you are having an interest in research and higher studies then your career is going to be very bright because there is a lot of need for Nuclear Physicists.



Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Homi J. Bhabha



Homi Jehangir Bhabha, FRS was an Indian nuclear physicist who founded the Tata Institute of Fundamental Research and taught physics there.



Ravi Grover



Ravi B. Grover is a mechanical engineer and nuclear physicist from India. He is the Homi Bhabha National Institute's founding vice-chancellor, a member of the Atomic Energy Commission, and head of the Board of Research in Nuclear Sciences.



Raja Ramanna



Raja Ramanna  was an Indian scientist best recognized for his early involvement in India's nuclear program. Ramanna worked under Homi Jehangir Bhabha.



Padmanabhan Krishnagopala Iyengar



 Padmanabhan Krishnagopala Iyengar is popularly known as P. K. Iyengar. He was an Indian nuclear scientist who played a key role in the creation of India's nuclear program. Iyengar was previously the director of BARC and the former head of India's Atomic Energy Commission.

Patricia Lewis



Patricia Lewis (born 1957) is a nuclear physicist and weapons control specialist from the United Kingdom and Ireland who is now the Research Director for International Security at Chatham House. She is also a member of the Global Commission on Internet Governance.

title: Ocean Engineer
summary: Name of the Career Profile : OCEAN ENGINEER
description:
Name of the Career Profile : OCEAN ENGINEER



                                   



Overview

Ocean engineers are usually involved in the study of man – made marine structures like boats or ships and also study the ocean.

They work on studying the ocean environment so that industries can build their technical support and also for transportation.

In short, they provide solutions to the problems related to the ocean and its environment with the help of engineering and technology.





Roles and Responsibilities

To design underwater transportation mediums for industries.

                 

To do research about the impact and effects of the ocean waves, currents on man-made systems.

            

To do research about the extraction of energy like oil from ocean. For example, extraction of petroleum from oceans.



          



To innovate technological solutions for preservation and restoration of seas and oceans.



          





To use engineering skills for solving the offshore and onshore problems faced by industries.

          



Prepare and design layout for different ocean systems and also provide design and analysis for installation and repair of this systems.



           



Benefits of choosing this career profile



This career has a lot of job opportunities because this is a very wide field and has a lot of scope. This is because it has to work all together.



  



The study of this field is acceptable all over the world. So if one pursues ocean engineering in India he or she can get a job anywhere in the world on the basis of performance.

    



There are both onshore and offshore jobs available in this career. On shore work includes working on ships and boats for analyzing and repairing work designs. Offshore work includes all the controlling part.



        



It is a highly paid and reputed job in India. And there is high growth in this career because of its wide scope.



      



A basic procedure to approach this job



                          Complete class 10







                         Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 50% in science stream with            

                         mathematics, chemistry and physics from any recognized school.

                       Complete graduation in B.Tech. in Marine Engineering from a recognized college.





                        Candidates can also do master’s program in marine engineering that is, M.Tech. in                        



                         Marine Engineering.





                                    After this they can apply for jobs according to its field.



Eligibility and skills required:



Age limit:

There is no age limit to pursue this course



              Educational Qualifications

The candidate must have passed 12th class in science with mathematics stream from any recognized school.

Candidates must have completed undergraduate degree in B.Tech. in marine engineering.

Candidates can also do post-graduate in M.tech. in marine engineering.





              Skills Requirement

                  

























 Creativity                                                                     Mathematical skills











                                                                                                                                                              Problem Solving              Computer Skills      



                                                

                                                            Communication Skills 









  Creativity (Designing skills)

This skill is required because they need to design systems and technology for making the transportation and other operations easy both onshore and offshore. So innovation is a must for ocean engineers.



Mathematical skills:

This job requires an advanced level of mathematical skills and knowledge of applying them. This ability is required for calculating the impact of waves and currents and also help in off-shore work. 



Communication skills:

Communication with other people in the same field is very necessary because  all have to share relevant information to each other. Also, the designers and engineers have to communicate their ideas and designs to clients so this skill is required.



Problem Solving:

Ocean engineers have to solve all the problems related to onshore and offshore therefore, they should know how to tackle these problems and derive solutions in an effective manner. This is very important because on an everyday basis there are a lot of problems coming.



Computer skills:

Ocean engineers must have knowledge of how to do the computer work of their field because for research and analysis knowledge of computers is very important. Also computer skills are required for designing.

           Work environment

Ocean engineers have to mainly work on-shore that is, on the ships and boats or underwater for repair and initiations.



They also work off shore

In short, they provide solutions to the problems related to the ocean and its environment with the help of engineering and technology.

To design underwater transportation mediums for industries.

                 

To do research about the impact and effects of the ocean waves, currents on man-made systems.

            

To do research about the extraction of energy like oil from ocean. For example, extraction of petroleum from oceans.



          



To innovate technological solutions for preservation and restoration of seas and oceans.



          





To use engineering skills for solving the offshore and onshore problems faced by industries.

          



Prepare and design layout for different ocean systems and also provide design and analysis for installation and repair of this systems.



           



Benefits of choosing this career profile



This career has a lot of job opportunities because this is a very wide field and has a lot of scope. This is because it has to work all together.



  



The study of this field is acceptable all over the world. So if one pursues ocean engineering in India he or she can get a job anywhere in the world on the basis of performance.

    



There are both onshore and offshore jobs available in this career. On shore work includes working on ships and boats for analyzing and repairing work designs. Offshore work includes all the controlling part.



        



It is a highly paid and reputed job in India. And there is high growth in this career because of its wide scope.



      



A basic procedure to approach this job



                          Complete class 10







                         Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 50% in science stream with            

                         mathematics, chemistry and physics from any recognized school.

                       Complete graduation in B.Tech. in Marine Engineering from a recognized college.





                        Candidates can also do master’s program in marine engineering that is, M.Tech. in                        



                         Marine Engineering.





                                    After this they can apply for jobs according to its field.



Eligibility and skills required:



Age limit:

There is no age limit to pursue this course



              Educational Qualifications

The candidate must have passed 12th class in science with mathematics stream from any recognized school.

Candidates must have completed undergraduate degree in B.Tech. in marine engineering.

Candidates can also do post-graduate in M.tech. in marine engineering.





              Skills Requirement

                  

























 Creativity                                                                     Mathematical skills











                                                                                                                                                              Problem Solving              Computer Skills      



                                                

                                                            Communication Skills 









  Creativity (Designing skills)

This skill is required because they need to design systems and technology for making the transportation and other operations easy both onshore and offshore. So innovation is a must for ocean engineers.



Mathematical skills:

This job requires an advanced level of mathematical skills and knowledge of applying them. This ability is required for calculating the impact of waves and currents and also help in off-shore work. 



Communication skills:

Communication with other people in the same field is very necessary because  all have to share relevant information to each other. Also, the designers and engineers have to communicate their ideas and designs to clients so this skill is required.



Problem Solving:

Ocean engineers have to solve all the problems related to onshore and offshore therefore, they should know how to tackle these problems and derive solutions in an effective manner. This is very important because on an everyday basis there are a lot of problems coming.



Computer skills:

Ocean engineers must have knowledge of how to do the computer work of their field because for research and analysis knowledge of computers is very important. Also computer skills are required for designing.

           Work environment

Ocean engineers have to mainly work on-shore that is, on the ships and boats or underwater for repair and initiations.



They also work off shore while designing and analyzing there new technologies or meeting clients etc.









Entrance exam to become ocean engineers



Some colleges take entrance exams for admission while some offer admission on the basis of merit. So for admission to the entrance exam, one has to make sure that he or she passes with the minimum cutoff. Different colleges have different entrance exams.

Types of specialization available for ocean engineer



Junior Ocean Engineer

Junior Ocean engineers specifically do work related to providing solutions for designing and  building solutions to monitor and control the coastal environment. They use the technology, engineering and mathematics for the functioning of this structures.



                   



Naval Architect

Naval Architects are involved in specializing in warship and submarine construction. They design, construct and repair naval vessels. They make all the transportation possible in water.

                       









Marine Engineer

Marine engineers are involved in operations, maintenance and repair of the marine systems made that are ships. They usually work onshore and have to travel where the ships travel.

                   



Petroleum engineer

Petroleum engineer are responsible to research and design equipments for extraction of

Therefore, there are many options available for a graduate to choose according to its interest.

The future needs more ocean engineers because the shipping companies are increasing day by day and the demand for them is increasing.



















Top inspiring Ocean Engineers



1. Yefim Nikonov:





He made the first military submarine.The submarine was barrel shaped and it was made by him in 1721.This is huge inspiration for marine engineers because this can help them to innovate new designs.



   2.John will Griffits

             



  He was an American marine engineer, specifically a naval architect who developed the first clipper ship, the Rainbow which was designed to trade with China in the year 1845. This was a great invention at that time.





3.Robert Livingston Stevens

  

Robert Livingstone Stevens was an American Engineer and ship designer who invented the widely used inverted -T railroad rail and spike. He also tested the steamboat which was invented by his father.



4.David Watson Taylor

   

David Watson Taylor was the American marine Architect and ship designer who designed the first ship model and tested its establishment and also formulated the basic principles of ship design for future.



5.John Ericsson

   

 He was a Swedish – American ocean engineer and a naval architect who invented the first armored turret warship and developed the screw  propeller which is used in many marine systems like ships, submarines and other technologies.

description:
Name of the career profile: Osteopaths

                       

Overview



An osteopath is a  physician who combines both conventional therapies and osteopathic manipulative therapy to treat pain and stress in the musculoskeletal system.



The majority of persons who visit an osteopath want treatment for disorders affecting the muscles, bones, and joints, such as lower back discomfort. neck ache that isn't complex.



Osteopaths utilize manual manipulation, stretching, and massage to improve joint mobility, reduce muscular tension, promote blood and nerve supply, and aid your body's natural healing mechanisms.



Roles and responsibilities of an osteopath







Case histories, physical examinations, observation of patients' body structure and motion, and medical tests are used to diagnose patients' symptoms.





Treating biomechanical issues which include backache, general joint problems, sports injuries, arthritis, stress, and repetitive strain injuries, etc.





Educating patients about therapeutic treatments, such as home exercises and lifestyle adjustments, in order to improve patients' health and well-being.





Referring patients to experts and communicating with other health providers as needed.





focuses on the whole body, including the spine, neurological system, and soft tissues (including muscles, ligaments, and tendons).



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career 



Osteopathic medicine is one of the fastest-growing healthcare professions in the country, with a surge in both the number of doctors practicing in the United States and the number of medical students.







Osteopathic medicine offers all of the advantages of contemporary medicine, such as prescription medicines, surgery, and the use of technology to diagnose disease and assess injuries.



the normal pay package for Osteopaths working in a hospital starts with a minimum of Rs. 16 lakhs per annum if a beginner 



There are less number of osteopaths found in India hence can say they have huge demand and many patients.



Choosing an osteopathy for the future opens up many career opportunities be it interaction with people, job, or social work.





A basic procedure to become an osteopath







STEP 1: After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology.



STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English as compulsory subjects so that you can become eligible for MBBS.

                                                               OR

The basic criteria for Osteopathy courses in India are that the candidate must have completed MBBS or Bachelor of Naturopathy and Yogic science or any bachelor's degree in the medical field.



STEP:3 Complete a master’s level qualification in Osteopathy.



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment



             Age Limit-



The candidate must be of minimum age limit is 17 years to give the medical entrance exam

And a maximum age of 25 years.



            Education Qualification-

The educational qualification for this profile should be Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery(MBBS), or Bachelor of Naturopathy and Yogic science, or any bachelor's degree in the medical field.



SKILLS REQUIRED



Independent and highly motivated to always be ready to help their patients. As being an osteopath brings you should always be ready to help.





Interested in health science and human anatomy to learn better in their field and succeed in their career as interest in their job could lead you to excel in your field.





Good hand-eye coordination so that they can make their patients perform exercise better.And gurantee recovery very soon and in a better way.





The ability to communicate effectively with others and comprehend their issues or illnesses to help them in their recovery process.





Interested in people and their health because they require a lot of human interaction to understand their problems, help them guide their treatment and result in a recovery.





WORKING ENVIRONMENT







An osteopath is often employed in private practice or clinic. These clinics may be part of a bigger practice that includes allied practitioners like chiropractors and acupuncturists.



 Individual customers can be treated by self-employed osteopaths in private residences and nursing homes.

Top institutions to get trained for Osteopathy

Type of specialization available:

Promotion and Upgrades:

title: Pediatrician
summary: Name of the career profile: Paediatrician
description:
Name of the career profile: Paediatrician







An overview of career a profile:



A paediatrician is a medical practitioner who specialises in the healthcare and treatment of babies and young children upto 6-7 years old. 

Those who have a special connection to children or just want to work for the welfare of children generally choose this career since it helps them to treat and save little children. 

A paediatrician also is the one who parents generally visit to get vaccinations against diseases. 



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Paediatricians conduct various physical and psychological tests in order to correctly measure the cognitive and emotional growth of young children to make sure they're in good health. 







They are also responsible for maintaining the vaccination routine of children from the time they're born, upto the age of 10 to make sure they develop immunity against most infectious diseases. 







They are also responsible for curating detailed meal plans for kids to make sure they are getting all the nutrients. This is because kids are fussy eaters and a paediatrician rightly knows what food they need. 







In case of any injury to a young child, a paediatrician is one who performs all the treatment like disinfecting, dressing and bandaging so that the wounds quickly heal. 







Paediatricians are also responsible for conducting psychotherapy and counselling in order to lessen the trauma of injury, accident or similar issues. Since children have fragile bodies, a regular doctor can't be consulted. 

.



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Actively contributing to the betterment of children by curing Young children of various diseases and making their future brighter in addition to immunisation of youth. 



There is a huge variety of specialisations available in this job profile, due to which you can learn various things once you decide to go for higher studies since there's much to learn.







They command huge respect in the society because of their contributions to both the society and their involvement in saving other lives. 









Improving the quality of life of the nation by reducing child mortality rates, thus helping in making sure that the youth population of the nation remains healthy and progressive





Due to the variety of patients a Paediatrician sees in their practice, they learn a lot of things much faster than general practitioners. Due to this their progress is much better & they learn more. 



Understanding child psychology in order to correctly assess what a child is going through, is there any underlying trauma or substance abuse. Through this they can solve many domestic violence issues and parental neglect as well.







A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a paediatrician, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 with subjects like Science, Mathematics, Biology,  physics, and chemistry.

Step-2: Give the relevant entrance exam to be eligible for taking admission in a medical College. This can be NEET by NTA(National Testing Agency).



Step-3: Earn a 5.5 Years bachelor's degree in M.B.B.S(Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery) which is 4.5 years of studies and 1 year of Internship

Step-4:After some time as a general Practitioner, complete your Master's (M.D) in paediatrics  in order to be eligible to be called a Paediatrician.

Step-5: Get your Practising licence in order to start your own clinic or get employed at a hospital or similar medical facility

Eligibility and skills requirements:



     Age Limit

There is no age limit for being a Paediatrician, though the minimum age limit is 17. 

You should pursue M.B.B.S and have a practitioner's licence. 

     Educational qualifications

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Science, Mathematics, Biology,  physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished 5.5 years of M.B.B.S including 4.5 years of Studies and 1 Year of internship. 

They should have the licence to practice as a doctor. 

A master's degree or M.D in Paediatrics is sometimes required too.



     Skill requirements



To become a successful Paediatrician, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Great at communication



Good communication skills are very necessary for a Paediatrician, since their patients are children, they'll have to be good at talking and engaging young children to better understand their maladies. 

Attention to detail



Since kids are not very good at expressing themselves, therefore it is upto the Paediatrician to pay attention to every small detail so as to correctly diagnose what is wrong with the child.

Good with children



Since a paediatrician will be working with children, it's imperative that they know about how children behave and can engage children in conversations and appear harmless and playful to children.

Emotional Resilience



Working with children requires an emotional ma

Step-4:After some time as a general Practitioner, complete your Master's (M.D) in paediatrics  in order to be eligible to be called a Paediatrician.

Step-5: Get your Practising licence in order to start your own clinic or get employed at a hospital or similar medical facility

Eligibility and skills requirements:



     Age Limit

There is no age limit for being a Paediatrician, though the minimum age limit is 17. 

You should pursue M.B.B.S and have a practitioner's licence. 

     Educational qualifications

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Science, Mathematics, Biology,  physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished 5.5 years of M.B.B.S including 4.5 years of Studies and 1 Year of internship. 

They should have the licence to practice as a doctor. 

A master's degree or M.D in Paediatrics is sometimes required too.

To become a successful Paediatrician, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Great at communication



Good communication skills are very necessary for a Paediatrician, since their patients are children, they'll have to be good at talking and engaging young children to better understand their maladies. 

Attention to detail



Since kids are not very good at expressing themselves, therefore it is upto the Paediatrician to pay attention to every small detail so as to correctly diagnose what is wrong with the child.

Good with children



Since a paediatrician will be working with children, it's imperative that they know about how children behave and can engage children in conversations and appear harmless and playful to children.

Emotional Resilience



Working with children requires an emotional maturity which is necessary because many times there will be distressing situations which may endanger the treatment of the patient. In such scenarios a Paediatrician should keep their cool.

Understanding of different Cultures



There will be numerous instances where a paediatrician has to see children of other countries and many times of indigenous cultures. In such situations a paediatrician should not be prejudiced and aim to understand the different cultures.

Work environment



Paediatricians are employed at various medical facilities ranging from private clinics to hospitals.Depending on where they are employed they might have to travel a lot.

Depending on where they work their work hours can be a few hrs of the appointment timings to anytime whenever the hospital requires them. 

Paediatricians are also many times employed at schools or corporate firms where their role is to make sure the products are safe for children. The work hours here are a lot less..



Entrance exam to follow the route:

Type of specialisation available under this job profiles:

Some of the specialisations under paediatrics are:

Hospitalist



These Paediatrics work permanently at the hospital and take care of the younger patients admitted in the hospital and they are generally required in the emergency room with many serving as consultants before surgeries. 

Child Abuse Pediatrician



These are Paediatrics who work in the sensitive zone of child abuse. These doctors have nerves of steel and generally work in close proximity with lawyers and social welfare workers. they are specialised at telling which injuries are normal and whcih are caused by child abuse.

 Haematology/ Oncology



These are cancer specialists who deal with child patients who have cancer or might have it. They are often associated with the same patients for a long time since the treatments for these diseases are very long. they need to have emotional intelligence to deal with these.

Neonatalists



These are the doctors who care for high risk pregnancies. During the period of pregnancy they care for the mother to make sure there is a safe birth. Once the operation is finished, they care for the newborn baby and make sure they grow up healthy.

Emergency Paediatric Medicine





A wide variety of ailments, from trauma to internal medical issues, must be diagnosed and treated by emergency medical professionals. Although some work for large general hospitals with a continuous flow of young patients, the majority are employed by children's hospitals.

Promotion and upgradation of this career profile:

The promotion process in the job profile goes like this:



Intern

First the candidate after graduation enters as a house surgeon or intern. In this stage he or she practices under another doctor and only assists in work without any practice of their own.



Junior Doctor

Once the candidate finishes their internship, they get their licence and start practising as a junior Doctor.



Doctor

Once they gain experience they eventually get more important cases and have other interns and junior colleagues working under them.

Specialist

Once the M.D in paediatrics is completed, the doctor is officially a specialist who works specially on Paediatrician cases.



Senior Doctor

After a few years of experience as a specialist, you may finally be designated as a senior doctor. This means you're the top doctor in your respective department. This means a very high salary.

Demands & Future Scope 







With the increasing number of medical facilities all over India , there is a rising demand for Paediatricians. 

India has seen a rise in rural hospital facilities and there is a need for well trained paediatricians because of which it is estimated that by 2024 India will see a rapid rise in this profile.

Those interested in going abroad also have a great chance as paediatricians is one of the most sought after career profiles in countries like France and Germany.



Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Benjamin Spock

One of the leading paediatrics of the 20th century, he revolutionised how child care is looked upon and is famous for using psychoanalysis as a medium to understand children’s needs and help them to express their thoughts. 







T. Berry Brazelton

 

Brazelton is an american paediatrician who is known for his excessive research on cognitive behaviours and has been the president of the Society for Research in Child Development. In addition to this he is also known for his work on neonatal behaviour.

Leila Denmark



One of the foremost researchers and paediatricians of the 20th century. She is credited as the co-discoverer of the whooping cough vaccine.she was also the recipient of the fischer prize in1935 for her achievements in the medical field. in addition to that she was also the first person who objected to cigarettes and drugs during pregnancy

Hattie Alexander



Hattie Alexander was a microbiologist and paediatrician who is known for her research in the field of antibiotic resistance .She is also known for her discoveries in the finding of remedies against influenza. For her many contributions she was the holder of many honourable posts.

Frederick Chapman Robbins



He is a nobel prize winner who was awarded the accomplishment for his role in the discovery of cell culture and the synthesis of poliovirus which was instrumental in the development of vaccines.

title: Physicists
summary: Career profile : Physicist
description:
Career profile : Physicist 



        



Overview



Who is a Physicist ? 



A Physicist is trained in physics and studies the various properties of matter and energy. 



A physicist is a scientist who explores and discovers the fundamental properties that govern the structures of matter, time and energy. 



        





Physicists create equipment such as particle accelerators and electron microscopes and studies about the principles of matter and energy that can be used both in theoretical and practical areas. 









What kind of career profile is it ? 





The job of a physicist is encompassed by scientific reasoning and the primary focus of this job is designing new equipment and implementing new scientific theories created from research.

       

This job profile has a number of challenges as they also need to work in teams with engineers and scientists and also sometimes study applied physics. 



A physicist has to work for hours continuously.  During emergency situations they even need to invent medical equipment and other machinery. 





Roles and Responsibilities of a Physicist? 



The roles and responsibilities of a physicist depend on the types of research on which he concentrates. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows 





The physicists help in teaching the people about the working of Earth, nature and the Universe.



             

They study the force of gravity and atom formation and make reports based on these observations.



           



They also focus on the fundamentals of molecules and the evolution of the universe. 







The physicists interconnect mathematical studies and abstract thinking, explaining the physical interaction of the world. 





They often solve practical problems and develop new technologies with the help of their knowledge and applied physics. 



            



Benefits of Choosing Physicist as a Career Profile:



High employment rates and job opportunities 



              



There is a high employment rate as physicists can work in different types of places such as in research labs, museums,  oil fields, the government, various industries, businesses, schools, hospitals and power plants. 

 



Variations in Job Timings 





     



There are full-time and part-time jobs  for physicists and this makes it easier to concentrate and develop scientific equipment.

 Usually, in India a physicist needs to  work for 40-45 hours a week. 





Opportunity to invent new equipments 



                 

 

Physicists, being a scientist, design new machinery and equipment for the benefit of the people. If you want to make any development for your society and also want to invent new products,  you can choose this career option. 





Working outdoors and in urban societies 



         



In most cases, physicists need to work outdoors and collect various samples.  

Physicists have to live near urban areas due to the sudden growth of multinational companies and laboratories in the city areas.  If you are fond of traveling and living in urban areas, you can choose to become a physicist. 





Working Overtime 

    



Physicists work independently, in laboratories and in many multinational companies. They sometimes need to investigate projects and  work overtime. This facility aids in  learning new things and will also provide incentives for working overtime. 



Highly paid Job







There are various types of specializations available for a physicist and is one of the highly paid jobs.  In India, the average salary of a physicist is INR 45,000. 



Highly Creative



       



If you love to create new things, you can choose to become a physicist. A physicist's  job is highly creative as he or she invents new products amalgamating physics and applied physics. 





How to become a Physicist ? 



If you want to become a physicist, you need to follow the steps provided below after Class 10. 



STEP-1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to choose science stream for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2: Students need to obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12 board examinations in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different institutions in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in physics and applied physics. In some cases, students are chosen  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree and get a B.Sc. degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for  post graduation or an internship. 



STEP - 6 : You  can register yourself in the Indian National Science Academy and work as a physicist. 





Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become a Physicist : 





   Age Limit : 



The minimum age to apply as a physicist is 18 y

STEP - 3 : Appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different institutions in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in physics and applied physics. In some cases, students are chosen  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree and get a B.Sc. degree.

Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become a Physicist : 





   Age Limit : 



The minimum age to apply as a physicist is 18 years. 



As per the rules, there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications : 





A student must secure  50 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Physics, Mathematics or Applied Physics.  



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course preferably in Physics. 





Entrance Exams : 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify Joint Entrance Screening Test (JEST), UGC- NET or GATE in order to be eligible as a scientist. 



Working Environment : 



Physicists work in various kinds of places and also work with different teams. 

Physicists usually need to work for longer hours and work in places like laboratories, hospitals, research centers, colleges and universities. 



They research and investigate in different fields like biophysics, astrophysics and nanotechnology. 



Physicists being a scientist work part time as well as full time and also work for longer hours during a crisis or a medical emergency. 







Skills Requirements : 





Excellent Creative skills 



     





Good Communication skills 













Good listening skills 





Mathematical skills 













Computer skills 









Time management skills 









Good Practical skills 



















Entrance Exam To Become a Physicist 



A student needs to clear entrance exams conducted by universities and institutions in order to get admission in top colleges. Here is a list of all the entrance examinations that will help students to become a physicist. 





JEE Main (Joint Entrance Examination – Main ) 

       

   Courses offered  B.Tech Civil Engineering, B.Tech Computer Science and Technology, B.Tech Information Technology.

B.Tech Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering, B.Tech Aerospace Engineering.



JEE Advanced  (Joint Entrance Examination – Advanced) 



Courses offered : Bachelor of Architecture, B.Tech, B.S., Dual Degree Bachelor of Technology and Master of Technology, Bachelor of Design. 





   BITSAT (Birla Institute of Technology and Science) 



Courses offered: B.E. Civil Engineering, 

B.E. Electrical and Electronics,  B.E. Electronics and Communication Engineering, 

B.E. Manufacturing Engineering.





IPU CET ( Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test.) 



Courses offered:  B.Sc , M.Sc , BHMCT and M. Tech and B. Tech (Biotech). 







Top Courses To Become a Physicist



There are various courses and specializations available for students who want to become a physicist. The list given below has top courses to become a doctor.



B.Sc. in Physics -  Bachelor of Sciences in Physics 

            Duration : 3 years 

            Entrance exam: held by the University or 10+2 marks

             Duration : 3 years 

             Entrance exam : JEE Main, JEE         Advanced

Duration : 4 years 

Entrance exam : JEE Main, JEE Advanced.

Duration: 4 years 

Entrance exam: JEE Main 

  



Courses After Completing Bachelor's : 



You can pursue a masters in physics or applied physics after completing Bachelors. You can also go for an internship or a PhD. in physics after your Masters.  Here are the courses available after Bachelors and Master's programs. 



MSc. in Physics: Master's of Sciences in Physics

Duration: 2 years 

Entrance exam : Entrance exams are conducted by the universities. 



PhD in Physics - Doctorate program 

Duration: 3 years 

Entrance exam: UGC NET 





Top Institutions To Pursue Physicist As A Career Profile:



There are several institutes and universities to pursue courses in physics. Explore the list given below for some best physicist colleges in India as well as abroad.





Top Physics and Engineering colleges in India:





Indian Institute of Technology, Madras



                   



Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore 



              







Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay 







Indian Institute of Science Education and Research, Pune



            





Indian Institute of Technology, Bhubaneswar         



           



Indian School of Mines, Dhanbad 



            





University of Delhi 



 







Top Physics and Engineering Colleges in abroad:



Stanford University, USA 



           



 University of Toronto, Canada 





             





  The University of British Columbia.



                  



 Northeastern University, USA



              

    





Types of Specializations available for a Physicist: 





 Material Scientist :





  





 Material scientists research and examine substances and also create l improved products. They also  test the quality of the goods being manufactured. 



They also enhance the quality of ceramics, plastics/polymers and superconducting materials. 





           Photonics Engineer 



       



Photonic engineers develop unique products like  electronics, solar-powered cells and prototypes. 









           Nuclear Physicist 









Nuclear physicists study subatomic particles, radiation and atoms. They also develop theories and principles and methods for applying physical laws. 





         Mechanical Engineer 





      



Mechanical Engineers  work on electricity-generating equipment such as gas generators and turbines, robots, elevators and escalators. 



Mechanical engineers work for the development of society with the help of modern technology. 







Pay Scale For Physicists :

title: Physiotherapist
summary: Career Profile : Physiotherapist
description:
Career Profile : Physiotherapist 

    



       



Overview 



Who is a Physiotherapist? 



A physiotherapist is specialized in healthcare and aids people during illness and injury through exercise and therapy. 

A physiotherapist is a part of healthcare and is often known as a health professional who helps to restore people's health and remove injury and ailments.  



      

They work and help patients and facilitate in removing the disability and creating a functional ability with the help of movements, exercises, therapies and strict diet plans. 







What kind of career profile is it ? 





The job of a physiotherapist is encompassed by various treatments and therapies and the focus of this job is to prevent injury pain, examine patient's reflexes and nerves. 



       

This job profile has a number of challenges as they also need to assist several patients and provide theories, exercises and movements on the basis of their injury . 



They need to sometimes work  in teams with doctors and health workers and discuss complex projects. 





Roles and Responsibilities of a Physiotherapist?



The roles and responsibilities of a physiotherapist depend on the detailed analysis and interpretation about a patient's injuries and disabilities which he concentrates on. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows: 



Physiotherapists being a part of healthcare services helps in restoring the  body movements with the help of exercises and awareness. 









They reduce pain not only physically but mentally and enlighten the traumatic world of the patients. 











They help in reducing back pain, neurological pain, sports injuries, arthritis and sudden injuries. 











They also reduce and remove acute illness like asthma, chronic heart diseases and heart attacks. 

 







Benefits of Choosing Physiotherapist as a Career Profile:





High employment rates and job opportunities 











There is a high employment rate as physiotherapists and can work in different types of places such as hospitals, healthcare centers and also become a professor. 





New Emerging Field 









Physiotherapy is an emerging field and being a part of healthcare is given much importance. It has exposure to various fields and during the pandemic it gave relief to many people suffering from back pain and neurological problems. 



Opportunity to work independently







 



Physiotherapists have great opportunities as they can work in a hospital and also work independently and attend patients on a weekly or monthly basis. 



Workings outdoors 









Physiotherapists need to work outdoors as they need to visit sites and patient's houses for checkups. 





How to become a Physiotherapist? 



If you want to step into the path of physiotherapy you need to explore these steps provided below after Class 10. 



STEP-1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to choose science stream for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2: Students need to obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12 in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different institutions in order to get admission for a diploma course or a Bachelor's degree in Physiotherapy. In some cases, students are chosen  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree and get a B.Sc. degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for a master's degree in physiotherapy. They can do specializations in orthopaedic, clinical electrophysiology, geriatric and paediatric physiotherapy. 











Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become a Physiotherapist : 



   Age Limit : 



The minimum age to apply as a physiotherapist is 18 years. 



As per the rules, there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 



Educational Qualifications : 



A student must secure  50 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Physiotherapy. 



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course preferably in Physiotherapy. 





Entrance Exams : 



A candidate needs to appear and qualify CET(Common Entrance Test ), AIMEE (All India Medical Entrance Examination),Lovely Professional University National Entrance and Scholarship Test in order to be eligible as a physiotherapist. 



Working Environment : 



Physiotherapists work in various kinds of places and Kerala Engineering Architecture Medical Also work with different healthcare teams. 



They need to work for longer hours and work in places like private hospitals, health care centers and laboratories. 



They also work as a Sports Physio Rehabilitator or an Exercise Physiologist.  



They can work on a permanent as well as on contractual ba

title: Polymer Engineer
summary: Name of the career profile-Polymer Engineer
description:
Name of the career profile-Polymer Engineer



An overview of career a profile:



As the name suggests, a polymer Engineer oversees the production and creation of polymers in order to create eco-friendly varieties of plastic. 

A polymer Engineer has a deep understanding of both how polymers work and the science behind producing them from scratch. 

Polymer engineers use the principles of plant design, process design, thermodynamics, and transport phenomena to develop such products.

So in short a polymer engineer is an expert at designing, analyzing and modifying the polymer materials



Roles and responsibilities of a polymer Engineer



Designing

Polymer engineers are responsible for designing various new types of polymer which are eco-friendly and easier to process. This involves lengthy research. 

Managing the Laboratory



Since a polymer engineer is the foremost expert when it comes to how polymers work and are manufactured , therefore in a developmental or production lab they are often the in-charge.







Researching on new materials

It is also the job of a polymer engineer to research the after effects and details of the new polymers they are designing or have already designed. They have continually observed if the polymers are safe or not. 



Analysis of polymers 

Polymer engineers are responsible for the various products and compounds their employer makes or uses since even a slight fault in anything can cause future problems.









Helping in processing

Engineers also have the responsibility to decide which polymer is best for the manufacturing of a particular plastic product, due to which, they also decide the best method to process it.





Coordination between senior developers and workers

Engineers are also the sort of connecting bridge between various senior employees who are in managerial roles and the workers who are responsible for the work.



Troubleshooting 

An engineer is also responsible for finding solutions for any and all problems that occur in the processing and ensures that there would be no further problems. 



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



No need for travel, since all the work will be done from one place. Because of this, those who for whatever reasons can't afford to leave their place, can easily work. 



No deadlines and no rush for work, since mostly polymer engineers are involved in research and monitoring which doesn't require them to rush, and calmly do their work.

Lucrative salaries with great benefits is another major reason to pursue polymer engineering, since the polymer industry is termed as a big priority sector and thus great incentives. 



For those who want to commit themselves to research, polymer engineering is one of the best topics to have a career in sincea major part of this discipline consists of researching various polymers and their relations with other materials. 



Discovering new things is also one major benefit of this profession since polymer engineers are expected to discover various new substances for both production and as raw material. 





Those who like technology and want to work with advanced technologies, can also find the benefit in this career profile since polymer engineering involves working with complex technologies everyday



     A basic procedure to approach this job

Step 1: Complete 10th class with minimum 65% marks

Step 2: Take science stream in 12th class and maintain minimum 60% marks overall and in Physics, chemistry and mathematics. 

Step 3:After this you have to do a bachelor's degree in Polymer engineering. Alternatively you can go for chemical, textile or materials engineering. 

Step 4: Once you complete your bachelor’s you can either go for employment or complete high education in masters.

Step 5: Alternatively you can also go for an internship in order to learn even more about your interests.

Step 6: After this you may continue working as a polymer engineer for any firm..



Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



Age limit 

Just like any other engineering discipline, it has a maximum age limit of 27-31



Educational qualifications 



One must first complete 10th class and 12th class in science stream. 

They must give an entrance exam and graduate from any recognized College with a degree in an engineering course related to study, manufacture and production of  polymers.

It's preferable to have an internship experience too.



Skill Requirements





Essential skills needed by someone who wants to be a polymer Engineer are: 



Mathematical Skills

Engineers are required to have excellent mathematical skills in order to perform the many calculations one has to do while working on polymers in order to get the exact amounts needed for a process to work.



Analytical Skills 

Engineers are also expected to have great analyzing skills in order to quickly find any solution that may arrive during the many stages of polymer production.

As the name suggests, a polymer Engineer oversees the production and creation of polymers in order to create eco-friendly varieties of plastic. 

A polymer Engineer has a deep understanding of both how polymers work and the science behind producing them from scratch. 

Polymer engineers use the principles of plant design, process design, thermodynamics, and transport phenomena to develop such products.

So in short a polymer engineer is an expert at designing, analyzing and modifying the polymer materials



Roles and responsibilities of a polymer Engineer



Designing

Polymer engineers are responsible for designing various new types of polymer which are eco-friendly and easier to process. This involves lengthy research.

Since a polymer engineer is the foremost expert when it comes to how polymers work and are manufactured , therefore in a developmental or production lab they are often the in-charge.

Discovering new things is also one major benefit of this profession since polymer engineers are expected to discover various new substances for both production and as raw material. 





Those who like technology and want to work with advanced technologies, can also find the benefit in this career profile since polymer engineering involves working with complex technologies everyday

Step 1: Complete 10th class with minimum 65% marks

Step 2: Take science stream in 12th class and maintain minimum 60% marks overall and in Physics, chemistry and mathematics. 

Step 3:After this you have to do a bachelor's degree in Polymer engineering. Alternatively you can go for chemical, textile or materials engineering.
