Step 5: Alternatively you can also go for an internship in order to learn even more about your interests.

Step 6: After this you may continue working as a polymer engineer for any firm..



Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



Age limit 

Just like any other engineering discipline, it has a maximum age limit of 27-31



Educational qualifications 



One must first complete 10th class and 12th class in science stream. 

They must give an entrance exam and graduate from any recognized College with a degree in an engineering course related to study, manufacture and production of  polymers.

It's preferable to have an internship experience too.



Skill Requirements





Essential skills needed by someone who wants to be a polymer Engineer are: 



Mathematical Skills

Engineers are required to have excellent mathematical skills in order to perform the many calculations one has to do while working on polymers in order to get the exact amounts needed for a process to work.



Analytical Skills 

Engineers are also expected to have great analyzing skills in order to quickly find any solution that may arrive during the many stages of polymer production.





Knowledge of chemistry

A thorough knowledge of chemistry is very necessary in order to correctly understand what kind of processes are used in the manufacturing of polymers and making the process non-lethal.



Creativity and zeal for knowledge 

A polymer engineer should have a creative and inquisitive mind in order to try to find better solutions for creation processes and creating new polymers, developing new molds, etc.



Communication skills 

As mentioned above, a polymer must have excellent conversational skills and should be able to convey their thoughts to both their seniors as well as juniors what they think and their opinion on a process is.



Good auto-CAD skills

Since it’s a very hard process to design new molds and understand the molecular patterns of polymers, engineers have to be good at auto-CAD in order to easily design things.





Work Environment 

Polymer engineers generally work in laboratories with continuous air conditioning, so comfort wise the work environment is great.

While there is no work pressure the working hours are generally long with average 40-45 hrs of work time each week.

The work many times involves solo work so for those who are shy around new people this is a very great career prospect.

Generally you will be surrounded with machines and technology all around and ample chances for personal research so its great for inquisitive people.





Entrance exam to follow the route:



To get into a college for a course of polymer engineering, there are many exams conducted on All-India as well as state level. Some of the most popular ones are:

Type of specialization available under this job profiles :



The various types of specialisations under polymer engineering are: 



Mold Designers

Mold Designers are polymer engineers who use Auto-CAD software in order to design new kinds of plastic Molds for new polymers and products to be made from these polymers. They routinely visit the production center in order to check if their molds are working correctly. 



Production Technologist

A production technologist ensures that all the machinery is functioning properly during the production process and there is no problem in the final product. In the other they are responsible for the quality control.







Production planners

As the name suggests, a production planner makes the various preparations and is responsible for making sure that there are no time delays and that there is accuracy in the production. These specialists are sometimes promoted to managerial roles. 









Quality Engineers

As the name suggests these engineers keep watch over the entire production process and make sure that the polymers and their subsequent products match the company's quality level and if there is any problem, how to resolve it. 



Polymer specialists

These are responsible for researching and finding out the flaws of benefits of existing polymers, how safe to use are they and what changes can be made for increased efficiency and better performance. 

Material Engineer

A materials engineer takes care of all the material that is ordered by the factory and makes sure that there is no bad material and all materials are up to the mark for the processing.

 



Energy Performance Engineer

They make sure that the energy required for the process ia always available and there is no problem with the energy supply.

title: Project Architect
summary: Name of the Career profile: Project Architect
description:
Name of the Career profile: Project Architect



                          

Overview

A project architect is a person in charge of managing the architectural aspects of the creation of a design, as well as the preparation of construction documentation and specifications.

 Project architects collaborate closely with customers to understand project needs, such as structural aspects and total budget.



Roles and responsibilities of a Project Architect:

Budgets, material documentation, and specification lists are being prepared for customer approval.



Regular site visits to verify construction complies with state laws.Assuring that the project is completed on schedule and within budget.



Developing progress reports, timetables, and amended budgets.



After the project is finished, a final examination is performed.To make sure no error or defect is there in the project.





Benefits of choosing Project Architect as a career:



High Salary -This field is something which requires creativity and which is rare and if you have it  then  you are paid well by the companies  



Future scope - In the growing modern world people want lavish life and beauty around them so the demand of architectures and eventually Project Architect are high in demand.



People interaction- You have to interact with the people to know their demands and desires , visit sites and  enquiry things.Hence get a chance to interact with people.



Gain multitasking skill - As you have to oversee , manage , visit sites etc hence you enhance your multitasking  skills.



A basic procedure to become a Project Architect:



            STEP:1 After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Maths.



            STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Maths. 

                                                                     

            Take a 3 years diploma course in Architecture after class 10th.



            STEP:3 Complete Bachelor's Degree in Architecture or design or related field.

                                                                     OR 

           You can also do dual degree which covers both undergraduate and postgraduate levels which offers

            Bachelors in Architecture + Masters in Architecture degrees .



STEP:4  (Optional) You can also Pursue your Master in Architecture or Master in Architecture (specialisation)

                                                                                                           

         

Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment:



            ELIGIBILITY 



             Age Limit-



The candidate must be of a minimum age limit of 17 years to give the entrance exam.

      

             Education Qualification-

 

A Bachelor's degree in Architecture or a related field is required to be a project architect or a management degree will be like a cherry on top of your career growth.



SKILLS REQUIRED

Outstanding communication and reporting abilities as they are required to communicate with the clients . Noting the clients requirement the progress or faults anything could be there hence require reporting abilities as well. 





Capability to convert ideas into effective construction plans, which are needed in this peak. As this field is very creative and imaginative.







Superior project management abilities because they have to manage huge projects be it the completion of it, the improvement required, and timely inquiry of it.







Estimation of things be it understanding of building costs or deadlines they have to estimate and to make sure things are done before the deadline.





WORKING ENVIRONMENT

           

Project architects operate in offices, but they also visit building sites to check on progress and to make sure that the work  is completed as per the requirements. 

They may also be required to travel in order to meet with clients, show their designs, or attend conferences. 

They can also work on multiple projects at the same time which could  be difficult.

Estimation of things be it understanding of building costs or deadlines they have to estimate and to make sure things are done before the deadline.

They may also be required to travel in order to meet with clients, show their designs, or attend conferences. 

They can also work on multiple projects at the same time which could  be difficult.

title: QUALITY ASSURANCE ENGINEER
summary: QUALITY ASSURANCE ENGINEER
description:
QUALITY ASSURANCE ENGINEER



 

➢    Overview 

They test as well as audit both hardware and software products in order to ensure that they meet quality standards.

They monitor every phase of the product or software development process, like design, development, testing, debugging as well as delivery before it is released to the people.

Roles & responsibilities of a Quality Assurance Engineer

 

To look after projects in order to achieve better customer satisfaction.



Create, Plan and manage the overall Quality Planning strategy of a product or a software.



Suggest solutions to the problems as identified in the product.



Collect data



Prepare and then present metrics and reports to Senior Management



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career





A quality assurance engineer’s  job growth rate is high as they are in huge demand. The employment rate of quality assurance engineer thus as pointed out as pointed out in a recent report is expected to to grow by 22% between the year 2020 and 2030.



They earn a decent amount of money. The average base salary of a quality assurance engineer in India is Rs 5lakhs per year.

            

They have a career of opportunities like for example they can become a enterprise architect, or a project manager or a software test lead.





The job is not stressful and overburdening.



The training required to become a quality assurance engineer does not take much time.



A basic procedure to apply for this job

 

To become a quality assurance engineer, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10.

Step-2:  Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 50% in science stream [should have computer as subject] from any recognized board to get a seat in a college.

Step-3:  Complete Graduation in computer science courses or a related field.

Step-4: Complete Masters degree in computer science courses or a related field

Step 5-Candidates can opt for different certifications in order to get professional knowledge of software testing.

Step 6- Apply for jobs in different companies.



Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

●       The candidate should be 21 years of age and not more than 30 years of age as on 1st January 2023.

Educational qualifications:

●    A student must have passed class 12 in science stream with at least 50% [should have science as a subject] aggregate from any recognized board.



Qualify entrance exams like JEE Mains, JEE Advanced, WBJEE etc in order to get a seat in college.



Candidates must complete undergraduate courses in computer science or related courses [B.Sc. in Computer Science] which will take 3 years or a B.Tech. in Computer Engineering which will take 4 years.

Complete Masters in computer science courses [MSc.in Computer Science or M.Tech. in Computer Engineering] which will take 2 years.

Candidates can opt for different certifications like for example ISTQB (International Software Testing Qualifications Board), CAST (Certified Associate in Software Testing), CETPA etc in order to get professional knowledge of software testing

Apply for jobs in different companies.



Skill requirements:

To become a quality assurance engineer, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Good communication skills in order to communicate with clients, managers in order to understand the requirements of a product and update them about the progress.





Good time management skills in order to complete a project on time.



Good problem solving skills in order to handle difficult situations in the workplace. 





Good analytical skills in order to critically identify issues or problems that may affect the performance of a product.





Good team working skills in order to work efficiently with different clients, managers, developers etc.





Good technical skills in order to make changes as required.







Work environment:





A Quality Assurance Engineer works in offices, industries or companies designing, developing, testing, and fixing bugs of a program or a product  before it is released to the people.



Entrance Exams in order to pursue career as a quality assurance engineer

Below given are some of the top entrance exams to pursue career as a quality assurance engineer:

Joint Entrance Examination [JEE] Mains

Joint Entrance Examination [JEE] Advanced

West Bengal Joint Entrance Examination [WBJEE]

Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering (GATE)



Courses To Become a Quality Assurance Engineer

●        Undergraduate courses to become a Quality Assurance Engineer

The courses mentioned below can be pursued right after completing 12: 

Course

Duration

Average Course Fees

B.Tech. in Computer Engineering

 4 years

INR 2 to 10 lakhs [LPA]

B.Sc. in Computer Science

3 years

INR  60,000-3 lakhs [LPA]



Postgraduate courses to become a Quality Assurance Engineer



The courses mentioned below can be pursued after completing graduation:



Course

Duration

Course Fees

M.Tech. in Computer Sc

Top Institutions To Pursue Career as a Quality Assurance Engineer

There are numerous good colleges and institutes providing admissions to pursue career as a quality assurance engineer:

Types Of Specialisations Available For a Quality Assurance Engineer

Some of the most popular quality assurance engineer specialisations are listed below. 

Software Tester

 

They conduct manual as well as automated tests in order to ensure that the product or the software created by developers is fit for purpose and does not have any issues or bugs before it is released to the people.

Software Test Lead



Their main responsibility is to lead, guide and monitor the design, analysis, implementation and execution of the product or software, test cases, test procedures and other test suites.

Quality Assurance Analyst



They test the components and overall functionality of a software, product or a website in order to identify and then resolve the required issues or defects. 

Software Test Engineer



They plan, design and conduct test procedures in order to ensure that the program or the product works at its best or performs to specifications before it is released to the people.

Pay Scale For a Quality Assurance Engineer according to designation

The statistics given below represent the average pay scale of a quality assurance engineer  in India:

Promotions And Upgradations Of a Quality Assurance Engineer



Quality Assurance Engineers have various scopes in different fields and follow a hierarchy.  There are different levels of quality assurance engineers which are as follows: 





LEVEL-1: Interns









Interns signify that they are recent graduates of computer engineering courses in order to pursue career as quality assurance engineer. Internships  elevate the understanding of an individual and create a real life working environment for the individuals. 







LEVEL - 2 : Junior Quality Assurance Engineer/Analyst







They work both for experience and job promotion. They assist the senior quality assurance engineers in order to enure that the product or the software meets the expectations of the people and also reports issues or bugs found in a software and fixes them.





LEVEL - 3 : Senior Quality Assurance Engineer





They give managerial tasks to the junior quality assurance analysts or engineers along with other tasks that is designing, developing, testing, debugging as well as delivery of a product or a software before it is released to the people.

Top Recruiting Agencies

According to recent reports, demand for quality assurance officer is quite high and the employment rate is expected to grow by 22% between the year 2020 and 2030

As play an important role in the society, they are recruited by a large number of companies like Amazon, Google, SAP, Tata Consultancy Services,Cognizant Technology Solutions and many other companies.

Writer of different articles, opinions, and insights on Twitter about software testing, he is a famous testing expert and a software quality assurance engineer.

title: RADIO JOCKEY
summary: CAREER PROFILE: Radio Jockey
description:
CAREER PROFILE: Radio Jockey



Overview:

Who is a Radio Jockey?

 

Radio Jockey popularly known as a RJ, the host of a radio show or podcast who provide entertainment and information through various radio programmes. 

They present the content before the audience.They provide content like information, news, or entertainment like music, games and quizzes etc.

Mainly they are the presenter of a radio programme. 





What kind of Job is it, Field or desk?

They are the ones presenting the radio programme.

Radio Jockey, RJ works in a studio.



Roles and responsibilities of a Radio Jockey:

The roles and responsibilities of a radio jockey are mentioned below:

Target Audience

 

In this career, individuals work hand in hand with the creative programme of producer and writer, By keeping in the mind that audience target is more important.They need to write scripts and content according to the target audience.



Planning: 



Planning is the main and the most important role for them. They work according to the plan for a living. They need to plan their shows in advance so that there is no problem at the timeof the shows.



Proofreading:  



They need to proofread their content for the show to be recorded on air because sometimes, if this step is skipped then it can lead to problems and controversies.



Organizing music



They need to strategically organize the music according to the broadcast of the content.They also need to take care that the radio company you are working in has the rights to that music.



Promotion 



To promote any brand with which the company has partnered, RJ needs to develop different fun contests and games which attract the listeners and that can be beneficial to both the brand and the radio company.







Benefits of Choosing Radio Jockey As A Career Profile:

socialization:



Through this as a career one can socialize better and can meet various types of people. A large mass of the audience knows about you because of your work and you can connect with them.Also they meet new people for interviews.





Salary:



Making the audience entertained and happy. This is also the reason the demand for RJ's is high and their salaries are also high. It pays a decent amount of salary and it can give a good living and lifestyle.



Helps in trending  the world: 



All the practical challenges have their benefits. RJ provides their innovative ideas and implementation of that idea and makes them a new trend. 

Popularity:



This job gives popularity to the RJs because people listen everyday and they could connect with them so they become popular between the people. Also, they are known from their shows so if the show gets popular the RJs get popular.

Flexible working hours:



the working hours of the RJ is flexible because they need to work at the time of their shows or the time scheduled for them and additionally they have to make scripts and prepare for the next show so this proc=vides flexible working hours.



How To Become a Radio Jockey?



To become a nurse right after 10th follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete 10th and choose any stream.

Step-2: Complete 12th with the stream you chose.

Step-3: Appear for the entrance exam if you want to take admission in Radio Jockey as a career.  

Step-4: Qualify the entrance exam for this career 

Step-5: obtain bachelor degree such as BA in Journalism and Mass Communication, BA in Mass Communication, etc.

Step6: obtain a postgraduate degree such as post graduation diploma in radio programme. 

Step-7: choose a suitable minor and complete the internship.

Step-8: apply for apprenticeship.

Step-9: work in a related job.

Step-10: learn experience and apply for the main job as a RJ. 

 

Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

The age limit for becoming a Radio Jockey is at least 21 years. 

Educational qualifications:

A student must have passed 12th with minimum 50% marks from any recognized board.

And passed 10th class with minimum 50% marks.

Have passed Bachelor degree with aggregate  marks of 60%. 





Skill requirements:

To become a Radio Jockey, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Creativity:



This is most necessary to become a fashion designer. Art provides the material to convert that in clothing.



Time Management :



They must know how to manage the time as when they are being aired, how to manage the things according to the time they have to deal with all of that. 



Decision Making skills:



Individuals should have the capability to make a decision and contribute to the foundation of popular content based shows.  



Interpersonal Management:



Candidates work with the majority of individuals in various positions both on radio shows as well as with guests on the show.



Communication skills:

title: Residential Architect
summary: Name of the career profile-Residential Architect
description:
Name of the career profile-Residential Architect



An overview of career a profile:



Residential architects as the name suggests are specialists in the designing of housing and residential buildings ranging from villas and bungalows to apartments and similar homes. 

They generally interact with clients to decide on spatial and functional elements of a home. 

A residential architect can be employed by both an architectural firm or they might be self employed. 

Residential architects are the connecting links between clients and the construction workers. 









Roles and responsibilities of a residential architect 



Project Discussion 

Since the construction of a residential house is a long and major process, the architect, after being appointed, holds various discussions with both the clients, and the engineers and construction workers. This is done so that what the client envisions can be easily understood by the workers. Major topics of discussion are, availability, budget, safety and security and time needed. 



Documentation and Drawing

Architects before the start of the actual construction, have to make all the necessary legal documentation in addition to creating all the blueprints of the house to be constructed. This makes sure that there are no problems once the construction starts.



Cost Estimation

 Architects calculate the various costs that might arise in the various stages of the construction. An architect shouldn’t underestimate the hidden costs of building a residential area such as installations and landscaping while also making sure of any increase or decrease of labor and material charges. 





Managing Contracts

Architects are also responsible for selecting the right contractors and suppliers that will fall within the budget of the client. Once all tenders and contracts are taken care of, they make an analysis report letting the client know about the estimated cost of their project. 



Customers retention 

Any successful architect should be able to retain customers so that all their clients will come to them for future projects. This is an important role since it signifies , client’s trust on your work and the excellence of your craft. 





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Architecture is based on creativity, thus an architect has the ability to turn their creativity into reality on their projects. This makes architecture the perfect career for a creative person. 





Architects get to see the fruits of their labour, as once the building finishes construction, they'll be able to see whether the new place matches their vision or not, making the whole experience satisfying.



















Architects are held in high esteem, cause they are an integral part of modern society and help in fulfilling the dreams of millions of people, by making the dream house they want. Plus they're subject to very lucrative salary packages, hence their high place in social hierarchy. 





Architects can do what they love for the rest of their lives, since their entire career profile revolves around designing and creating new buildings which forever, fulfills their creative instinct. 













Architects are good at managing stress and hence can deal with life situations with a very calm and collected approach, this being due to their highly creative career profile while makes patience a key attribute. 





Architects are surrounded with like minded people, thus making their job, one full of fun, progress and creativity. This results in high productivity and overall great quality of life. 



Architects are great at Expressing their feelings, thus making them capable of always presenting their thoughts without any fear of ridicule or discomfort. 







     A basic procedure to approach this job:





Step 1: Complete 10th class with minimum 65% marks

Step 2: Take science stream in 12th class and maintain minimum 60% marks

Step 3:After this you have to do a bachelor's degree in architecture or B.Arch(5 Years). 

Step 4: Alternatively you can go for other architecture related bachelor's like Bachelor’s in    Engineering and Architectural Design, Bachelor of Engineering in Landscape Architecture, BSc/BA in Architecture Technology & Construction Management, BA (Hons) in Interior Architecture & Design

Step 5: Once you complete your bachelor’s you can either go for employment or complete high education in masters (M. Arch).

Step 6: After this you have to get licensed as an architect. In India, the Council of Architecture, licenses architecture graduates. 

Step 7: After this you may continue working as a private architect or for any firm..



Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



Age limit 

Architecture doesn't have an age limit, and one may try as late as one wants.



Educational qualifications 



One must first complete 10th class and 12th class in science stream. 

They must give an entrance exam and graduate from any recognised C

Name of the career profile-Residential Architect



An overview of career a profile:



Residential architects as the name suggests are specialists in the designing of housing and residential buildings ranging from villas and bungalows to apartments and similar homes. 

They generally interact with clients to decide on spatial and functional elements of a home. 

A residential architect can be employed by both an architectural firm or they might be self employed. 

Residential architects are the connecting links between clients and the construction workers. 









Roles and responsibilities of a residential architect 



Project Discussion 

Since the construction of a residential house is a long and major process, the architect, after being appointed, holds various discussions with both the clients, and the engineers and construction workers. This is done so that what the client envisions can be easily understood by the workers. Major topics of discussion are, availability, budget, safety and security and time needed. 



Documentation and Drawing

Architects before the start of the actual construction, have to make all the necessary legal documentation in addition to creating all the blueprints of the house to be constructed. This makes sure that there are no problems once the construction starts.



Cost Estimation

 Architects calculate the various costs that might arise in the various stages of the construction. An architect shouldn’t underestimate the hidden costs of building a residential area such as installations and landscaping while also making sure of any increase or decrease of labor and material charges. 





Managing Contracts

Architects are also responsible for selecting the right contractors and suppliers that will fall within the budget of the client. Once all tenders and contracts are taken care of, they make an analysis report letting the client know about the estimated cost of their project. 



Customers retention 

Any successful architect should be able to retain customers so that all their clients will come to them for future projects. This is an important role since it signifies , client’s trust on your work and the excellence of your craft. 





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Architecture is based on creativity, thus an architect has the ability to turn their creativity into reality on their projects. This makes architecture the perfect career for a creative person. 





Architects get to see the fruits of their labour, as once the building finishes construction, they'll be able to see whether the new place matches their vision or not, making the whole experience satisfying.



















Architects are held in high esteem, cause they are an integral part of modern society and help in fulfilling the dreams of millions of people, by making the dream house they want. Plus they're subject to very lucrative salary packages, hence their high place in social hierarchy. 





Architects can do what they love for the rest of their lives, since their entire career profile revolves around designing and creating new buildings which forever, fulfills their creative instinct. 













Architects are good at managing stress and hence can deal with life situations with a very calm and collected approach, this being due to their highly creative career profile while makes patience a key attribute. 





Architects are surrounded with like minded people, thus making their job, one full of fun, progress and creativity. This results in high productivity and overall great quality of life. 



Architects are great at Expressing their feelings, thus making them capable of always presenting their thoughts without any fear of ridicule or discomfort. 







     A basic procedure to approach this job:





Step 1: Complete 10th class with minimum 65% marks

Step 2: Take science stream in 12th class and maintain minimum 60% marks

Step 3:After this you have to do a bachelor's degree in architecture or B.Arch(5 Years). 

Step 4: Alternatively you can go for other architecture related bachelor's like Bachelor’s in    Engineering and Architectural Design, Bachelor of Engineering in Landscape Architecture, BSc/BA in Architecture Technology & Construction Management, BA (Hons) in Interior Architecture & Design

Step 5: Once you complete your bachelor’s you can either go for employment or complete high education in masters (M. Arch).

Step 6: After this you have to get licensed as an architect. In India, the Council of Architecture, licenses architecture graduates. 

Step 7: After this you may continue working as a private architect or for any firm..



Eligibilities & skill requirements &  their working environment :



Age limit 

Architecture doesn't have an age limit, and one may try as late as one wants.



Educational qualifications 



One must first complete 10th class and 12th class in science stream. 

They must give an entrance exam and graduate from any recognised College with an architecture related bachelor's degree. 

They must then get certified as an architect by the Council of Architects, India. 



Skill Requirements





Skills required of any architect are:



Excellent Design Services 



An architect should have outstanding drawing and creative skills. These skills will make sure that their work will be top notch and will appeal to both their clients and to their peers. Great creative abilities is a must for an architect. 



Communication Power

An architect should also be great at conveying his or her thoughts and should be able to let their teammates know their opinions. They should also be great at communication in order to maintain a clientele. 



Listening skills

Since most of the residential projects are made with the clients needs and wants in mind, an architect needs to be a great listener in order to grasp the minute details of a client's request. They should also be able to do the same with construction engineers and contractors. 



Sketching skills 

Most of the time, an architect will have to create detailed sketches to know the client as well as the workers and contractors, the specifics of the various areas and rooms of a house. Nowadays expertise in designing software is required. 



Technical abilities

An architect should also be technically accomplished since every work of theirs can result in injuries to people if there's any faults. That’s why they should have excellent technical skills in order to make and maintain great projects. 



Problem solving skills

Many times architects will come across situations, where they might have to make various recalculations regarding the placement of rooms, corridors, whether the kind of building adheres to legal limits, etc. In such an event, an architect should be able to quickly solve the problems. 



Collaboration skills

An architect has to be a team player and should have good collaborative skills so that, he or she can easily work with other people because in construction the teamwork of a lot of people is necessary and it's upto the architect to maintain that teamwork. 



Visualization Skills

An architect should be able to visualize what their clients want from them while also making sure that their creative control is not jeopardized. To ensure this they need to be extremely creative and quick thinking. 



Passionate 

An Architect should be full of drive and passion in order to fully manifest their client’s wishes. 



Compassionate 

An architect should be able to understand their clients and not be aggressive or arrogant. They should be able to connect with their projects. 



Work Environment 

Architects generally work in a work space surrounded by other people of the same interest and thus are mostly satisfied with their professional setting.

Most architects have to work on-site, since their technical and conceptual knowledge is always in need but self employed architects may work from their own offices. 

An architect also meets with a lot of people, so their work environment is varying and let's them see a variety of sceneries. 

Working hours totally depend on you, it can be long or short or Flexible depending on demands.





     Entrance exam to follow the route:



To get into a college for a course of Architecture, there are many exams conducted on All-India as well as state level. Some of the most popular ones are:



Names of exams

JEE Main (Joint Entrance Examination Main) for Architecture

Type of specialization available under this job profiles :



The various types of specialisations under Architecture are



 Green Energy Architect 

It is one where the architect tries to make the residential complex solely run on green energy, I.e energy that doesn't cause any pollution or use fossil fuels. This type of architect likes to present to themselves a challenge. 





Urban Planner



This is the type of architect who tries to integrate their projects thoroughly with the neighborhood. These are concerned with how a neighborhood looks and thus believe in following a theme for their designed buildings. 



Landscape Architect 

This type of architect specializes in the parks, scenery, and surroundings of a residential house. While they still are into residential designing, their expertise lies in integrating the scenery according to the house or vice versa. 



Interior Architecture



These types of architects are concerned with how the furniture is placed according to the housing and if the interior of the house follows a particular theme or not. In other words, the inside of the house is what their expertise is. 





Luxury Architect 

These are the kind of architects who specialize in luxury grade residential houses for wealthy people. These architects include celebrities in their clientele and earn much more than regular architects . These are the superstars of the construction field.



Restoration Architect

It is the type of architects who renovate abandoned or damaged vintage houses or otherwise inhospitable houses as ones in where people can live. These architects generally have deep knowledge in history and know what kinda if restoration work has to be done. 



Conservation Architect

One who works towards the conservation of residential areas or residences, subject to conservation and preserving and of historical importance. These types of architects are generally found working with the government and the heirs of former historical people. 







Pay-Scales in this career profile:



Just like any other job profile, the salaries of architects are dependent on their job designation and their experience in the respective fields:

Promotions & upgradings:



Generally just like any other career profile, with experience comes greater packages and increased responsibilities.

Mostly higher studies like a master’s degree or a doctorate results in being eligible for these posts. Generally just even a single previous job experience can result in a massive upgrade. 

Every 2-3 years architects experience a salary hike of 12-20% with other benefits.

In order to increase your salary packages, and your position within your field, becoming a licensed architect while also applying for various certifications is a must. 



     Top recruiting agencies for this profile



The Top ten recruiters for Architects in India are:

Demands & Future Scope 



Architecture is a field which will only see a rise in the future, due to the booming prices of real estate all around India and other parts of the world. 

Architects are needed by both public and private, with a good amount of hiring power also resting in the government sector. Due to this, as long as you've sufficient skills, there will be ample opportunities for you. 

Those who want to settle abroad, also have it easy since as a certification driven career profile, architecture will be able to land you a job in your favorite country, as long a you've the right certifications. 

Architects also have zero chance of unemployment since being a creativity driven industry means most love their job and have a thorough understanding of their career and its mentioned tasks. 



Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Some famous people who also followed this profile are:



BV Doshi

He is among the most famous and most accomplished Indian architects of all-timeHe is the recipient of many architectural prizes such as the Pritzker Architecture Prize and Ordre des Arts et des Lettres of France. 

Laurie Baker 

British born Indian architect, Laurie is also known as the Gandhi of Architecture. He was best known for blending regional practices of architecture along with low cost materials in order to create unique construction which seamlessly mixed with the surroundings.

Sheila Sri Prakash

She is one of the preeminent Indian architects, and also a pioneer for women in this field. She has over 1200 projects to her name and was the first Indian lady to start her own architectural firm. She single handedly changed the field for women. 



Brinda Somaya 

Another female architect, she had pursued vernacular architecture when started her own architectural firm, from nothing but her own backyard. These leanings eventually earned her a Wienerberger Golden Architect Lifetime Award, the only woman to do so.



Charles Correa

Perhaps the single most influential and accomplished Indian architect of current time, his architectural designs have gathered much praise from all around the world. He is known for introducing unique Western designs in Indian architecture.

title: Seismologist
summary: Name of the Career Profile : SEISMOLOGIST
description:
Name of the Career Profile : SEISMOLOGIST

                    

                                                                    



Overview

Seismologist is a career profile in which a scientist studies earthquakes and seismic waves that move through the earth.

They examine and calculate the earthquakes and find out how dangerous they are. Then they try to minimize their effect and try to improve the construction standards

Therefore they study the internal structure and composition of the earth and try to determine the factors related to the cause of the earthquake.



                           



          Roles and Responsibilities

Research about the earthquakes happened to determine the causes and effects of it.



                         

To prepare the reports of the study of the earthquakes along with the locations and magnitude of it.



                       

To make a report of the prevention measures to be taken for preventing the earthquakes or decreasing its impacts on the earth.



                    

To collect the data of seismic waves and than analyze them to identify the hazards in different geographical area.

                                                    

To identify the pattern of earthquake and to measure ground motion during the earthquake.



                                              



Benefits of choosing this career profile

The pay scale of a seismologist is decent and it is a financially rewarding career to pursue. The salary they get, employees can have a good lifestyle with it.



                     





They play a very crucial role in dealing with natural calamities and for developing preventive measures for it.So, they are trying to save many lives by preventing the causes of earthquakes.



                    



They get an opportunity to work with the government organizations and research institutes.This automatically makes them reach a good status because they are having a government job.



                 



Job security is another benefit they enjoy because most government jobs have a very high job security and  reputation. So this is another great benefit.

                 

They have flexible work hours and have to work for 5 days a week so this helps them to enjoy their social life and reduce workload.

                 

              

A basic procedure to approach this job



                       Complete class 10







                      Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 50% in science stream with            

                      mathematics, chemistry and physics from any recognized school.

                       

                                              

                      Complete graduation in Geophysics, Geography or Earth science(B.Sc.) from a 



                      recognized college                                   



                                  Candidates can also do master’s program(B.Sc.) in the same field of undergraduate 

                                  from a recognized college and then PhD in Geography



                                  After this they can apply for jobs according to their field.





Eligibility and skills required:



Age limit:

There is no age limit to pursue this course, because there is no higher age limit in giving its entrance exam JEE.Therefore there is no upper age limit for pursuing seismology.



              Educational Qualifications

The candidate must have passed 12th class in science with mathematics stream from any recognized school.

Candidates must have completed undergraduate degree in B.Sc. in Geophysics, Geography, or related fields.

Candidates can also do post-graduate in M.Sc. in the field of the bachelor’s degree.

Candidates can also do Ph.D. in Geography .





           Skills Requirement

                  

                                                     Technical skills

Analysis









                                                                                                                               Critical Thinking

Communication skills                                               

                                                 Organizational skills











Technical skills:

There are a lot of technical parts in the seismologist job so having knowledge of computer technology is very important. For example, Seismologist may have to prepare reports with the help of some special software.



Communication skills:

Communication with other people in the same field is very necessary because all have to share relevant information to each other. Also, seismologists have to work with fellow scientists and researchers for making reports and analyzing them.



Critical thinking:

Seismologists have to determine the factors or causes of the earthquake and seismic activities in different geographic areas so the skill of critical thinking is required to analyze the data and give a conclusion.

title: Sports Psychologist
summary: Name of the career profile: Sports Psychologist
description:
Name of the career profile: Sports Psychologist





An overview of career a profile:



Sports psychologists are certified professionals who know and understand how psychological factors affect athletes performance, their potential, growth, failures, success etc. 



Sports psychologists aren't just involved with professional athletes but they also work with amateurs and just normal people who want to start exercising and get in shape and to help them remove psychological barriers. 



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Improving the performance of an athlete and helping them make progress by the help of psychological training and mental exercises. 







They monitor their athletes regularly to find any psychological barricades that are affecting the athletes and their growth.





Help in the visualisation of goals, dreams and next steps in career by formulating what exactly is the career path of the athlete and what should be the next logical step







They also have a role in controlling anxiety or stress of an athlete with the help of counselling or therapy or just plain discussions to make athletes feel more assured of their success.







Teaching relaxation techniques so that the athletes can focus on getting their mental and physical strength back after a match or a game so as to preserve their body better and be ready for the next challenge. 







In the case of a team sport, they help in building solidarity and unity in a team with the help of teamwork training so that in a real match the different individuals come together. 





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



High base salary and lucrative packages

Sports psychology is one of the most premier fields which allows you to work in a very professionalised field with a high average salary and constant hikes to increase your wage over the years. 





Meeting lots of new people and working with them

Another big reason to choose this career profile is that as a sports psychologist, you'll be able to meet with a lot of different people in various different disciplines and get to work with them. This means lots of variety in work. 





Growing demand

As a major sporting nation with an incredibly good team in cricket and also much focus on Olympic sports, India has predicted a growth rate of 12% in the coming years in the field of sports management. Therefore it is a very in demand job profile right now and high degree of employment opportunities. 





Very flexible work hours without pressure

Sports psychologists generally aren't permanent employees and therefore are not bound by any time constraints and can work at their own leisure. Mostly they are only required once or twice a week. 





 Lots of chances to travel abroad

Those who are interested in travelling abroad or even travelling on a large scale in India itself will find this career prospect great since they can venture to a variety of different places due to work, especially if they're part of the National side. 







You can contribute to the progress of sports

A sports psychologist is intimately associated with professional sports persons and thus in the process of helping them overcome their flaws and leading them to success, they're also inadvertently helping the nation to success.









A basic procedure to approach this job:

To become a sports Psychologist, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 in any stream like arts, science or commerce.

Step-2: Earn a bachelor's degree in sports psychology

Step-3: Earn a Master’s Degree in sports Psychology.

Step-4: Complete your doctorate in sports Psychology (Ph.D. or Psy.D)

Step-5: Complete your internship or in house practice for a few years

Step-6: Complete training, get licensed and start your practice. 

Eligibility and skills requirements:

     Age Limit

There is no age limit for being a sports Psychologist.

You should pursue a degree and should have completed your internship in order to get licensed.

     Educational qualifications

In grades 10 and 12, candidates should have studied Science, Mathematics, Biology,  physics, and chemistry.

They ought to have finished their undergraduate degree in sports Psychology.

They ought to have finished their Master’s Degree in sports Psychology.

Depending on the field and specialisation a candidate should have done a doctorate. 

Finished their internship and should have received a licence.



     Skill requirements



To become a successful sports Psychologist, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Excellent communication skills



As someone who has to work intimately with athletes, a sports psychologist should've very good communication skills in order to correctly assess what's right and what's wrong with an athlete by discussions by carefully asking questions about the person's psyche..

Interest in sports



It goes without saying but someone who is interested in this career profile

     Skill requirements



To become a successful sports Psychologist, a candidate must possess the following skills and qualities.

Excellent communication skills



As someone who has to work intimately with athletes, a sports psychologist should've very good communication skills in order to correctly assess what's right and what's wrong with an athlete by discussions by carefully asking questions about the person's psyche..

Interest in sports



It goes without saying but someone who is interested in this career profile should have an inherent interest in sports and should be passionate about them because only then can they rightfully and successfully assist athletes to fulfil their goals 

Good decision making ability



A good psychologist should be able to make fast and controlled decisions because many times the team may find themselves in high pressure situations where one decision may lead to success or failure. To avoid failure, a psychologist has to make cold factual decisions or trust their instinct. 

Sensitivity and Understanding



Psychologists need to be empathetic and understanding of the problems faced by athletes. They'll have to thoroughly understand what's plaguing a player and is there any underlying trauma that's the reason behind the bad performance and what can be done to better it. 

Patience and calmness



When dealing with sports persons who are in a slump, a psychologist needs to be very patient since it might take a long time to understand what is the root cause of a problem and even longer to cure the trauma. A psychologist can't be impatient. 

Problem solving ability



When working with patients, once figuring out what's the root cause of a slump or why can't one stick to an exercise routine, the psychologist should be able figure out a fool proof solution to solve the said problem and get better results. 

Work environment



Many sports psychologists are office based or hospital based but they might sometimes be fully involved with a particular team or individual. This is generally the case with professional athletes. The psychologist gives his expert guidance on an exclusive basis to a well known national or international team or a professional singles player. 

Sports psychologists have very flexible work hours with them generally working on their own terms. For those who don't like travelling it might be exhausting, but those who like travelling it's one of the best career profiles.

There are no further specialisations available under the job profile as such. Though generally there are 2 considerable types of sports psychologists. Self employed and those associated with a particular team or player. 



Freelance Sports Psychologist 

As the name suggests these psychologists operate from their own office/hospital and aren't affiliated with any particular team or individual. They provide their expertise and services to anyone who books an appointment. They generally are dependent on whether the base of their operations is sports savvy or not. 







Team Affiliated Sports Psychologist

As the name suggests, these psychologists are contracted employees and are generally associated with a particular team. Coleman Griffith

One of the premier sports writers who has authored more than 35 Books, and has been part of numerous training programs. He has been the psychology consultant of National Collegiate Athletic Association(NCAA) as well as being an important part of many College level champion teams.

title: Tax Lawyer
summary: Name of the career profile: Tax Lawyer
description:
Name of the career profile: Tax Lawyer





An overview of career a profile:



Tax lawyers are legal practitioners involved in cases related to the tax levied by the state and central government on its citizens. 

Tax lawyers also deal with taxation law to help individuals and corporations to safely navigate through the taxes they owe the government. 

Tax lawyers is a specialised branch of law which only deals with taxes. 



Roles & responsibilities of this career profile:

Legal research 

Many tax lawyers do legal research on a regular basis to make sure that they're up to date with all the current laws regarding taxation. In case laws change they inform their clients beforehand. 







Drafting documents 

Tax lawyers also help their clients in preparing the appropriate paperwork for a variety of different activities such as employment letters, wills, property papers, bonds, etc. 







Handling tax disputes

Tax lawyers also act as mediators between their clients and the IRS in determining whether the taxed amount is right or to see if there is any scope of negotiation and to see through that negotiation. 





Representing Clients in Court

In case of any legal dispute, they are also responsible for defending their clients in the civil law to see if they're really guilty or not.







Advising clients on legal matters

Tax lawyers also conduct consulting and help in guiding their clients in their tax scheme and what actions they can take to reduce their taxes or not to pay them. 







Helping during the dissolution of companies or marriages

When a company decides to break away or a divorce happens, tax lawyers help in determining the settlement amount and what portion of it is applicable to be taxed. 





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Stability in career

As long as there will be taxes there, there will be a great demand for tax lawyers. One primary reason for this is despite the fluctuation of the economy and recession, the government will still be levying taxes and thus the services of legal advisors will be required. 





Many different job opportunities

There are a variety of different job roles available under taxation law. Because of this one can compare different fields and decide which specialisation suits them best. 







Great work life balance

Tax lawyers have much shorter work hours than most other lawyers . Since taxation cases happen at a more lenient frequency, taxation lawyers have shorter working hours with flexible working hours. Only exception will be February(tax season). 





A very good package 

Taxation lawyers are subjective to get lucrative salary packages, because they are high in demand while also providing services to corporate firms and rich businessmen which results in high income and great exposure. 





You can make a difference

If you are someone who wants to make a difference by reducing corruption and helping the economy of the nation and also helping different businesses stay afloat by ensuring their taxes aren't too high. 0

To become a Tax lawyer follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and 12 in any stream like arts, science or commerce.

Step-3: Earn a bachelor’s degree in law or L.LB

Step-4: Alternatively you can complete an integrated course like BA.LLB for 5 years

Step-5: Get your practising licence.

Step-6: Now you can start private practice or apply at law firms.

The age limit set by the Bar council of India is 45 years. 

You should pursue a degree in order to get licensed.

They ought to have finished their undergraduate degree in Taxation Law.

Finished their internship and should have received licensure.

Tax Collector

The role of a tax associate varies from company to company and also depends on the policies of the firm. Their role consists of preparing tax returns for business and properties while also assisting in paying bills and reducing costs. 

Chief Financial Officer



A chief financial officer is responsible for all the financial actions of a corporation and is responsible for tracking the flow of money. He is also responsible for cost cuttings and monitoring increased or decreased taxes. 

Financial advisor



A financial advisor generally works with individuals rather than corporations. They generally help people navigate through their taxes and also help individuals reach their financial goals while also informing them about the tax rates on various items. 

Tax analyst



A tax analyst is generally someone who works in a corporate environment and his responsibilities include analysing a firm's tax obligations and to make sure that they pay the amount required in time and avoid any problems.

The career progression in this job profile goes as follows:

After completion of training period, they get promoted to tax Associate. Here they work as junior lawyers and perform basic tax related tasks.

After this they get promoted to an analyst position where their job mostly revolves around the tracking of flow of money in the economy.

Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile:



Nani Palkhivala







He was an Indian lawyer, jurist and economic liberalist who played an important role in numerous supreme court hearings ranging from freedom of speech, to the rights of citizens and the power of parliament. He was also monumental in the role of government in the matters of education. 



Harish Salve

 

He was a former solicitor general of India and has also held the post of the Queen's counsel for the courts of England and Wales. He was appointed for this post in 2020. He is also famous for representing Kulbhushan Jadhav in the international court of justice. 

Soli Sorabjee



He is a supreme court lawyer and has been part of many monumental hearings in the history of Indian law. He was appointed as the Attorney General of India from 1998-2004, and is the only person who held the office for two terms.

Ram Jethmalani

He had previously held the positions of Chairman of the Indian Bar Council and Union Law Minister for India. He was chosen to lead the Supreme Court Bar Association in 2010. Additionally, he received the International Jurist Award and the Human Rights Award from World Peace Through Law in 1977..

title: Textile Engineer
summary: Name of the Career Profile : TEXTILE ENGINEER
description:
Name of the Career Profile : TEXTILE ENGINEER



                                        



Overview



Textile engineers do all the work related to the textiles like yarns and cloths and all things related to it.

They study the whole process of production of cloth and apply that knowledge in the real production process.

For example, textile engineers also develop different types of fabric with experimenting and research like sweat proof fabric, or artificial synthetic fabrics.

                                

Roles and Responsibilities:



To do research about new materials and to know the characteristics of the existing materials and improve their quality.

    



To innovate new types of fibers like synthetic fibers and test them with various products to know its impact. Over the years different synthetic fibers are developed like rayon, artificial silk etc. so these types of innovations are needed.



    



To select different raw materials for the production process of various clothes. For example, selecting the best quality of cotton at a reasonable price or other materials required like dyeing colors etc.



     



To design machines that can be used in production of textiles like machines for making yarn or making a cloth. For example, weaving machines which can make yarn or cloth more quickly.

      



To make the operations of technology more efficient to increase productivity.



   







Benefits of choosing this career profile



This job has a huge scope because it is an evergreen industry and this industries are all over the world.

                

One can also open its own business because textile engineers have all the knowledge related to the production process of textiles. Startups in this field are appreciated so much because of its huge scope.

      



There is a huge growth chance in this industry as it has different works and job profiles to do in this field. All the departments of this industry can have a textile engineer hired because all the specializations are available.

     

Another advantage of being a textile engineer is that they get job security and job satisfaction. The Job of textile engineer has job security because their is less competition in this field of engineering.

      

They have fixed 9 to 5 job because they have to work only during the production of the textiles. They have to do overtime only if they have to export or have  a very big order.





.

STEP 1 : Complete class 10

STEP 5: After this they can apply for jobs according to their field.

Creativity (Designing skills)

Textile engineers need creative skills for innovating new types of fabrics keeping in mind all the factors and thinking something new and developing something new that can make lives easy. For example, Heat resistant fabric for fire fighters or for general use.



Technical Knowledge

The candidate should have all the technical knowledge about the product process and all the technicalities involved in this industry. This is necessary because the engineers have to teach the technicians about everything.



Focus on Detailing:

This skill is very important in the textile industry because the detailing is what consumers pay for. For example , even if one or two threads are out from the fabric, consumers don’t buy them. Therefore, accuracy and finishing skills are the crucial ones.



Knowledge of understanding different fabrics and its techniques

The candidate must have the knowledge of the type of fabric, its properties, its limitations and everything. One should be able to find out with a  touch that the fabric quality is good or not. And he or she should also have knowledge of the whole technique of making it.



Patience 

The whole process of production of textile is very lengthy and slow and this needs a lot of patience in the operation management. Therefore, the operation engineer needs patience while working in the textile industry.



           Work environment

The work environment of textile engineers is mainly in factories and mills and related to machines. Also they supervise the technicians so they mostly work between machines and labor in the factory or mill.





Sometimes one has to also work in laboratories for research if he or she is in that branch of the textile industry for developing and testing fabrics.

Types of specialization available for Textile engineer

They work on textiles required for medical purposes like masks, coats etc. .They also analyze and test the impact of the fabrics in different circumstances . 





Production Engineer

                      

                     



Production engineers need to take care of the overall production of the textile from the first step to the last step. They have to usually work indoors in the mills for supervising and also checking that the production is cost effective and efficient.

          

Operation Engineer

                                                      



Operation engineer takes care of the supply chain and all the operations taking place like production planning , sales etc. They also take care of the logistics like transportation of the raw materials and finished products.





Quality Control Supervisor

              

                  



They take care of the quality of all the things involved in production like raw materials, the final product, the packaging and everything. Quality assurance means accuracy in all ways like measurement, detailing and overall quality.



Process Improvement Engineer



                   



They have to take care of the root cause of the problems coming and solve them and  prepare reports and analyze them. They also develop ideas for increasing productivity and efficiency.

title: Theoretical Physicist
summary: Career profile: Theoretical physicist
description:
Career profile: Theoretical physicist





Overview: 

Who is a Theoretical physicist?

 Theoretical physicist is a scientist who tries to understand and research on the complexities of how the universe works and the relations or interactions between matter and energy using mathematics, calculations, chemistry, biology, and other series of theories. 

What kind of career profile is it?

The job of a theoretical physicist does not involve much lab work. They commonly work in offices, universities, research centers etc.

Theoretical physicists must have a deep passion for their field as their jobs mostly include calculations and studying to discover the unknown.

Theoretical physicists are sometimes required to travel for attending seminars.

Roles and Responsibilities of a Theoretical physicist: 

Common duties of theoretical physicists may be to:

Research:



They need to research physical phenomena related to the topic they are working on.

Develop theories:



Based on their calculations and observations, they must develop related theories.

Create methods:



They must know how to create methods or demonstrate experiments where their discovered physical laws work.

Refine existing theories:



They are also expected to check the validity of existing theories, find special cases etc. using their calculations.

Discussions:



They are required to attend meetings to discuss their findings, ideas with colleagues and other scientists.



Teaching:





They must teach their findings to other trainees and students.



Maintain Computer Software:



They must maintain the equipment and the computer software they are using to conduct research.



Assistance:





They must provide assistance to government assets like the military with their knowledge and know-how of operations where their theories can be valuable.

Benefits Of Choosing Theoretical physicist as A Career Profile:

Lower Competition:



Since this is a new field, there is not much competition in the field and one has much higher chances of a successful career if he/she has good theoretical and practical knowledge in the field.

High paying salary:





Theoretical physicist jobs pay higher than most other jobs in government as well as private sector.

Chance for contributing in science and communities:



The individuals working in this field provide valuable knowledge to several types of industries, including: technology, medicine, space, education, public safety etc. They help the community by finding the interactions of the world(matter) with energy.



Opportunity to make ground-breaking discoveries:



As a theoretical physicist, you can discover valuable theories that can help in the advancement of science.

Too high Funds not required:



Funds are rarely needed as this career mostly involves calculations, studies, and research. Hence, here you can devote most of your time to exploring the topic you are researching without worrying much for funds.





How To Become a Theoretical physicist?



To become a theoretical physicist, you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard.

STEP-1: Complete 10th standard and choose science stream with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Mathematics as mandatory subjects in 11th standard.

STEP-2: Pass 12th standard with at least 60% marks in each of the above-mentioned subjects.

STEP-3: Apply for a B.Sc. (Bachelor of Science) Course or a BTech in Theoretical Physics(preferred), Physics

STEP-4: Complete the BSc/ BTech course.

STEP-5: Prepare for exams like GATE, IIT JAM etc.

STEP-6: After clearing the exam, opt for MSc in Physics with specialization in Theoretical Physics.

STEP-7: To reach the level of a Researcher, you must complete a P.H.D and then start an internship. 

Eligibility And Skills Requirement to Become a Theoretical physicist:

Age limit:

There is no maximum age limit to apply for this job profile 

Educational qualifications:



A candidate must have scored at least 60% aggregate in 12th standard with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics as mandatory subjects.

The minimum educational qualification to become a theoretical physicist is a MSc degree.



Entrance exams:

Entrance exams depend on the university you are going to take admission in. Some universities may provide direct admission as well after 10+2.

To become a theoretical physicist, you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard.

STEP-1: Complete 10th standard and choose science stream with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and Mathematics as mandatory subjects in 11th standard.

Working Environment:

Working environment for a theoretical physicist is mostly in research settings. They work in laboratories, universities, research centers etc.

They can also work in offices, government agencies etc. Usually, they need to work 40 hours a week on average. They may also need to travel to attend seminars.



Skills requirement:

Advanced mathematical, research, analysis, and computer skills:



As a theoretical physicist, most of your work will involve research and validating theories which requires complex mathematics solving, data analyzing and good computer operating skills.

Extensive knowledge of science and engineering:



This domain requires an in-depth knowledge of the subject to pursue research. Also, to provide assistance in operations, you must have a good knowledge of engineering.

Strong critical thinking skills:



In many cases, you may need to solve critical problems in a short period of time and hence, strong critical thinking is required.

Complex problem-solving abilities:



As you carry on with your research work, you may find that many theories in that domain could not find solutions to certain problems. For eg: No theory has given a clear explanation of the mystery of the Bermuda Triangle. Hence, to validate or update these theories , you must have complex-problem solving abilities.

Time management skills:



Time Management skill is vital for any domain . As a theoretical physicist, you must schedule time for research, analysis, study and calculations efficiently.

Strong oral and written communication skills:



As a theoretical physicist, you need to explain your findings by writing research papers or conducting lectures in universities, seminars etc. Hence, communication skills are needed.

Innovation, creativity, and attention to detail:



You need to think out of the box to solve problems that may at times, have yet not been discovered or researched upon. Hence you must be innovative, creative and have an eye for detail.

Ability to work with little to no supervision:



Experienced theoretical physicists usually don’t work under any supervisor and hence you must have the ability to complete accurate work with little supervision.

Theoretical Physicist can specialize in various areas like:

Astronomy/Theoretical Astronomer:





Theoretical Physicists working in the astronomy branch work with study, calculations and observations of matters related to space like black holes, nebulas, etc.



Nuclear Physics: 





Theoretical Nuclear Physicists develop models for describing the process inside the nucleus of an atom.



Material Theoretical Physicist:





They study materials using condensed matter physics, continuum physics and chemistry.

.

Optical Physicist:



They study the properties of light and its behavior as detected using instruments.

Average Pay Scale [LPA]

Intern/Entry Level

10

Pay Scale as per Specialization:

Specialization

Average Salary [LPA]

Theoretical Astronomer

8.5

Theoretical Nuclear Physicist

11

5.2

Theoretical Optical Physicist

12.5





Promotions And Upgrading of a Theoretical physicist:

The career of a theoretical physicist lacks promotional structure. But an overview is given here:

LEVEL-1: Interns/ Trainee

Interns are recent graduates of the BSc or BTech course.

They practice research under an experienced senior in their work areas.

LEVEL-2: Junior Researcher:

They have some experience or are MSc graduates who work in offices, government organizations etc. 

They are responsible for conducting tests, managing the equipment they use and also for working closely with pathologists in the lab.

LEVEL-3: Senior Researcher in Theoretical Physics:

They have a good level of experience and are responsible for providing assistance, managing expeditions and training junior researchers.



Top Recruiting Domains for Theoretical physicist:

Here are the domains where theoretical physicists can work:

Government Agencies

Research Organizations

Laboratories

Universities

Offices

Top recruiting Agencies for Theoretical physicists:



NASA ( National Aeronautics and Space Administration)



PPPL (Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory)



NIST ( National Institute of Standards and Technology)





ISRO ( Indian Space Research Organization)



DRDO ( Defence Research and Development Organization)



IIST ( Indian Institute of Space Science and Technology)





VIT-AP ( Vellore Institute of Technology- Andhra Pradesh)



IISC ( Indian Institute of Science)





Demand And Future Scope: 



With the advancement of science and technology, the job market for theoretical physicists is expected to grow by 11% by 2029.

With increasing development, scientists and physicists are working collaboratively to solve man’s curiosity to know about the mysteries of the universe. As a theoretical physicist, you will be able to contribute to the expedition and may surprise yourself by discovering something that the world never knew!

Top Inspiring Theoretical physicists:

Albert Einstein: P.H.D





Considered as the greatest and most influential physicist, Einstein is widely known for developing the theory of relativity and his contributions in developing the theory of quantum mechanics.



Sir Isaac Newton: M.A



An English mathematician, physicist, astronomer, theologian, and author, Newton is considered as the most influential theoretical physicist of all time and is famous for discovering the principle of gravity.



Stephen Hawking: P.H.D



One of the most inspiring and influential theoretical physicist of all time, Stephen Hawking was the director of research at the Centre of Theoretical Cosmology in Cambridge University. He is best known for his works in relativity, black holes theory, thermodynamics etc. One of his most popular books is ‘A Brief History of Time.’



Richard Feynman: P.H.D



Richard Phillips Feynman, an American theoretical physicist, is known as the Father of Theoretical Physics. He is known for his work in formulation of quantum mechanics, the theory of quantum electrodynamics, the physics of the superfluidity of supercooled liquid helium, as well as his work in particle physics where he proposed the Parton model. 



Schrödinger: P.H.D



Erwin Schrodinger, was an Austrian physicist who developed a number of fundamental results in quantum theory. The Schrödinger equation helps to calculate the wave function of a system and the changes of it over time which can be used to explain the mysteries of the universe.
