Career profile: Unani consultant

 





Overview:

Who is an Unani consultant?



Unani Consultant is a professional physician who gained the knowledge of medicine from the homeopathic school.



Unani consultants diagnose patients and give them Unani medications which are only approved by Homoeopathy and recognised by Indian medicine association.





What kind of career profile is Unani consultant?



Unani is the alternate medicine system in which patients are treated by enhancing the natural healing energy of the human body.















Unani Consultant’s job requires a lot of hard work but is extremely rewarding. Unani Medicine offers safe and cost efficient treatment to the people who can’t afford pricey treatments.

Unani physicians work in terms of creating certain blends, certain types of medicinal aspects, which can later be used by the patients to cure their diseases.

.

Roles and responsibilities of an Unani consultant:



Prescribe affective medications





As a doctor/consultant they need to prescribe or suggest the accurate medicines to the patients for them to get better as soon as possible, the medications include pastes, tablets etc made by the Unani consultants themselves. 



Treating conditions with herbal and natural methods.

Unani consultants work their ways by treating the patients with herbal or natural remedies which they themselves make, like herbal pastes or medicines which contain many natural substances. 



Perform minor surgeries





Conducting minor surgeries as a doctor is what Unani consultant’s one of the responsibilities, they have to get through the surgeries to ensure the patients health.



communicate to the government



As a part of their job is to spread awareness that natural remedies can work exactly magic like the chemical ones. And nature is any day better, they have to set-targets for some treatments like childhood immunisation. 



Request laboratory tests



Unani consultants also have to go through body tests to know what the patients exactly are suffering from, it helps them to know how to cure the patients more easily and swiftly.







Benefits for choosing Unani consultant as a career profile:



Efficient salary



Unani consultants make money in mostly the same ratio as any other medical practitioner. The great demand of people for Unani treatments keeps their salary prospect consistent and happy

. 

Self employment 



Unani consultants usually are welcomed in hospitals which offer a rewarding future to them, they choose to practice in private clinics just for themselves. It gives them the freedom of work they want for themselves and they can work according to their own orders and heart.



High demand



Due to the increasing want for money from private hospitals, people who have an unstable financial status suffer the most, people can’t afford going to private hospitals because of the tremendous amount of money they demand, so with time people are sticking to their roots by choosing Unani, which makes it stay in demand. 



Rewarding career



As they’re helping the poor and needy, that definitely is a rewarding thing to do. Helping and treating people who possibly cannot afford treatments but still look for the possibilities to get better, Unani consultants make it easier for them. 



Respected profession





With time people are understanding the value of natural substances and natural remedies, they’re understanding how it’s better than the chemicals we choose to use on an everyday basis, it makes Unani consultants the eye opener and they are approached by even government health schemes, that indeed makes it a respected career.



How to become an Unani consultant?



To become an Unani consultant, you must follow the career path given below :



 STEP 1:The candidate has to pass class 10th with Urdu, Arabic or any Persian language as a subject. 

STEP 2: The candidate has to pass 10+12 degrees with Physics, chemistry, bio and English and their main subjects with a minimum of 50%. 

STEP 3: The candidate has to qualify for the NEET-National Eligibility cum Entrance test, exam in order to get into a college of their choice.

STEP 4: The candidate has to get a bachelor’s degree in Bachelor’s Of Unani Medicine And Surgery (BUMS).

STEP 5: Then the candidate has to get a Doctor Of Medicine (MD) In Unani, get a license and start practising for the general public.



Eligibility and skills requirement to become a Unani consultant:



Age limit:



The minimum age to become an Unani consultant is 17 years.

There is no maximum age as such.



Educational qualification:



A candidate must have finished class 10th with Urdu, Arabic or any Persian language. 

They also need to finish their 12th with physics, chemistry, biology and English as their mandatory subjects with 50%. 

Candidates to get a bachelor’s degree and then a Doctor Of Medicine (MD) in Unani.

Unani physicians work in terms of creating certain blends, certain types of medicinal aspects, which can later be used by the patients to cure their diseases.

.

Prescribe affective medications

communicate to the government

Request laboratory tests

Due to the increasing want for money from private hospitals, people who have an unstable financial status suffer the most, people can’t afford going to private hospitals because of the tremendous amount of money they demand, so with time people are sticking to their roots by choosing Unani, which makes it stay in demand. 



Rewarding career



As they’re helping the poor and needy, that definitely is a rewarding thing to do. Helping and treating people who possibly cannot afford treatments but still look for the possibilities to get better, Unani consultants make it easier for them. 



Respected profession





With time people are understanding the value of natural substances and natural remedies, they’re understanding how it’s better than the chemicals we choose to use on an everyday basis, it makes Unani consultants the eye opener and they are approached by even government health schemes, that indeed makes it a respected career.



How to become an Unani consultant?



To become an Unani consultant, you must follow the career path given below :



 STEP 1:The candidate has to pass class 10th with Urdu, Arabic or any Persian language as a subject. 

STEP 2: The candidate has to pass 10+12 degrees with Physics, chemistry, bio and English and their main subjects with a minimum of 50%. 

STEP 3: The candidate has to qualify for the NEET-National Eligibility cum Entrance test, exam in order to get into a college of their choice.

STEP 4: The candidate has to get a bachelor’s degree in Bachelor’s Of Unani Medicine And Surgery (BUMS).

STEP 5: Then the candidate has to get a Doctor Of Medicine (MD) In Unani, get a license and start practising for the general public.



Eligibility and skills requirement to become a Unani consultant:



Age limit:



The minimum age to become an Unani consultant is 17 years.

There is no maximum age as such.



Educational qualification:



A candidate must have finished class 10th with Urdu, Arabic or any Persian language. 

They also need to finish their 12th with physics, chemistry, biology and English as their mandatory subjects with 50%. 

Candidates to get a bachelor’s degree and then a Doctor Of Medicine (MD) in Unani.

After the following steps the consultants can get a license and start working for the firm.













Entrance exams:





The entrance exam any candidate needs to qualify is NEET - National Eligibility cum Entrance, on a national level.



Skills requirements:



Research skills



As we know Unani Medicine is still in the process of researching things to treat the general public even better, they need high research skills to keep finding new things about medicine regularly. 



Diagnostic Skills



These consultants need diagnostic skills to know what a person is going through very quickly and be able to locate the issue to give the patients swift and good treatment. 

















Communication skills



As consultants they need to talk and understand people and their problems, for this they need excellent communication skills. 



Understanding of Unani Manufacture







As Unani makes their own medicines, the consultants need to know what is being made and manufactured, if it’s safe and healthy to use or what ingredients their medicines or pastes consist of. 



Time management 





A Unani consultant’s work life can be very tiring and challenging and even busy at times, they need to adjust their timings so it doesn’t become hectic and frustrating for them in order to have a healthy work and personal life.













Working environment:



An Unani practitioner may work as a physician at a government or private hospital or Unani. Professionals in this area can also obtain employment opportunities with businesses that deal with Unani preparations.

These professionals can also practice self employment as in starting their own clinics and having their own time schedules and flexible hours.

Career profile: Mathematical Physicists 



                 



Overview 





Who is a Mathematical Physicist? 





Mathematical physicists are specialized in  mathematics and create development in mathematical methods. 



They create, perform and manage complex calculations  and also participate in writing journals. 



                       



They provide theories and observations  in mathematical terms . 





What kind of career profile is it ? 



The job of a mathematical physicist  focuses on analysis and modeling, software development and theoretical physics research. 





This job has a number of challenges as they need to sometimes work in teams with scientists and university lecturers. 





A mathematical physicist has to work for hours continuously. 



 

Being an educator, they need to analyze data and even work on mathematical theories. 





Roles and Responsibilities of a Mathematical Physicist? 



The roles and responsibilities of a mathematical physicist are of various kinds and depend on what he concentrates on. An overview of the roles and responsibilities are as follows : 



Mathematical physicists study and analyze data with the help of mathematical equations. 



                     



They create mathematical modeling and research on mathematical theories. 



                                       



They solve practical problems with the help of applied mathematical theories. 



                         











Benefits of Choosing Mathematical Physicist as a Career Profile : 





High Employment rates and job opportunities 

 

                          





There is a high employment rate as scientific research and development services firms, laboratories and universities. 





Variations in Job Timings



                      





There are  permanent and contractual jobs along with full-time and part-time jobs for mathematical physicists that help in analyzing the structure of complex mathematical concepts.  Usually, in India a mathematical physicist needs to  work for 40-50 hours a week. 





Opportunity to develop mathematical concepts

  

                  



Mathematical physicists analyze and investigate the theory of mathematical equations and its structure. If you want to take part in further development in mathematics, you can choose this career option. 



Highly creative



                    



Mathematical physicists  need to possess the quality of creation and creative abilities. They need creative abilities along with their scientific knowledge to create mathematical developments. 



Working Overtime



              



A mathematical physicist can work in various areas  and sometimes they research new techniques independently. They need to discover new procedures and make reports. Their work schedules are sometimes flexible and sometimes work overtime and learn new techniques.









How To Become a Mathematical Physicist? 





To become a mathematical physicist you must follow the career path given below right after the 10th standard: 



STEP -1 : Students need to complete Class 10 and obtain at least 50 percent marks. The candidates need to take science  for their Class 11 and Class 12 with subjects like Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



STEP - 2 : Students need to study in a recognized institution and also obtain at least 50 percent marks in their Class 12  in each of the above mentioned subjects. 



STEP - 3 : Students need to  appear and qualify the entrance exams conducted by different colleges  in order to get admission for a Bachelor's degree in Mathematics or Physics. In some cases, students are chosen by the colleges  based on their Class 12 marks. 



STEP - 4 : Complete the Bachelor's degree. 



STEP - 5 : After completing a Bachelor's degree, students can work in an entry level job or apply for Masters in Physics. You need to complete a PhD. in Physics or Mathematics. 







Eligibility And Skills Requirements To Become a Mathematical Physicist 





   Age Limit :  





  The minimum age to apply as a mathematical physicist is 18 years. 



As per the rules,  there  is no age limit but the individual should be a citizen of India. 





Educational Qualifications: 





A student must secure  50-60 percent in 12th Standard with Physics, Chemistry and Biology. 



A student should have a Bachelor's degree in Mathematics or Physics. 



A candidate needs to complete a Postgraduate course.



You need to complete a PhD. program in Physics or Mathematics.  





Entrance Exams



A candidate needs to appear and qualify in

Jawaharlal Nehru University Entrance Examination

 Banaras Hindu University Postgraduate Entrance Test

Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering 

to become a mathematical physicist. 



Working Environment 



Mathematical physicists work in various kinds of places like  scientific research and development services firms. 



They also work with different teams of scientists. 



Mathematical phy

Skills Requirements: 



Computer skills 





                     



Computer skills are the basic need since the evolution of technologies.  Computers provide good performance and provide error free work. Computer skills are a key to success and provide several opportunities.

Time management saves  time and helps to create a proper planning and daily schedule.  A mathematical physicist has to work hard and work for longer hours. 



 Decision maker

Practical skills  improve performance and create the capability to solve problems. It opens the real competitive environment of the world which helps the person to learn more things.

Types of Specializations available for a Mathematical Physicist:



Astrophysicist: 





                 



Astrophysicists are specialized in astronomy and focus on the space and the structure of the galaxies. 

They study about the evolution of the universe and stars. 





Quantum Physicist:



                    





Quantum physicists focus on the radiation of the atomic scale and deal with the nature of matter and radiation. 

They study about the behavior of matter and their interactions. 





Particle Physicist: 





                





Particle Physicists focus on protons, electrons and neutrons. 

They study particles produced by radiative and high energy scattering processes.  





Solid State Physicist : 



                  



Solid State Physicists study the various properties of bulk matter through methods such as quantum mechanics, crystallography. 

They focus on the mechanical and thermal behavior of solids. 



Plasma Physicist:



                  



Plasma Physicist deals with the properties and applications of plasma. 

They focus on scientific theories and research and also provide reports of the plasma.

Mathematical physicists have various scopes in different fields and follow a hierarchy.  There are different levels  which are as follows:

title: Virologist
summary: Name of the Career profile: Virologist
description:
Name of the Career profile: Virologist



                         

Overview

Virology is a branch of science that deals with viruses. Animals, the human body, plants, microbes, fungus, the natural environment, and agricultural environments are all possible hosts for the virus.

A virologist is a specialist in virology, or the study of viruses.

Virologists are doctors or researchers who study viruses and other types of viral illnesses.



Roles and responsibilities of an Virologist



Coordinate  Research Teams- Coaching and educating the research team on correct equipment use and procedures is critical to the success of the research effort.



Research-Virus activity is often analysed in closely controlled facilities using scientific equipment such as electron microscopes and sophisticated computer software.



 Present Research-When research initiatives are done, virologists are in charge of conveying the findings to their employer, grantor, or supporting organisation.



Work as consultant-Many virologists work at major medical clinics or hospitals. They frequently work with other medical personnel who deal with particular patient problems.



Benefits of choosing this profile as a career 



Job security is always on top if you choose a medical field .



High demand in future because growing population make it growing demand of Virologist 



Their starting salary is very high and keeps increasing as time.



Virologists can also go in the Research field , most of the work in this field is laboratory based.





A basic procedure to become a Virologist





       STEP:1 After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology



       STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology



 STEP:3 Complete your  B.Sc. degree from a science background like Microbiology, Virology, Life   Science, Biochemistry, Biotechnology, Zoology, MBBS, Medical Lab Technology, and any degree in science background. 



       STEP:4  Go for M.Sc in Virology



       STEP:5.You can also opt for a Ph.D. course in Virology 



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment



            ELIGIBILITY 



             Age Limit-  A candidate should be at least above 18 years.



             Education Qualification- A candidate should hold a degree of MSc in Virology.



WORKING ENVIRONMENT



Virologists can be found in a range of contexts, including hospitals, pharmaceutical businesses, research institutes, and government bodies. They usually work regular hours, however, they may be needed to work late nights or on weekends to conduct tests or monitor patients.

Virologists operate in a number of contexts, including hospitals, pharmaceutical businesses, research facilities, and government organizations. They normally work regular hours, however they may be needed to work evenings or weekends to conduct research or monitor patients.

Demands and future scope:



The worldwide virology market was valued at $1693 million in 2017 and is predicted to increase at a compound annual growth rate of 5.4% between 2018 and 2023, with the COVID-19 .pandemic expected to drive growth even further.

Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile



Dr W Ian Lipkin



Walter Ian Lipkin is the John Snow Professor of Epidemiology at Columbia University's Mailman School of Public Health. An academic laboratory dedicated to the study of microbes in acute and chronic disorders.



Peter Pivot



The European Commission stated Tuesday that Peter Piot is credited with discovering the Ebola virus and doing groundbreaking HIV-AIDS research.



Wendy Barcley



Wendy Barcley is a professor at Imperial College London. In May 2007, she moved to Imperial College with her research group from the University of Reading, where she worked since 1995. Graduated from Cambridge University.



HS Savithri

title: Auctioneer
summary: Name of the Career profile: Auctioneer
description:
Name of the Career profile: Auctioneer



          



Overview



An auctioneer is someone who oversees an auction or public sale where people can bid on objects.

At public auctions, auctioneers sell a wide range of items. Auction sales can be arranged for people, corporations, or as a result of a court order.



Roles and responsibilities of an Auctioneer





Auctioneer specifies the terms and conditions under which the sale will take place. He or she creates advertising that includes information about each auction.





Describe the property to the audience which is being auctioned to so that the pubic have a clear view of what they are going to buy.







Before the auction, evaluate the property which is going to be auctioned



.

 

When there are no higher offers, the auction is closed, and also if the owner does not accept the bid, remove the property from the auction.







Benefits of choosing this profile as a career 



Auctioneers operate in an exciting and challenging industry.The duties of an auction professional go well beyond chant speed. Hence they enjoy their work.







Good salary -Unquestionably, an auctioneer can raise more money and so earn more money.If you dream of lavish life then Auctioneer is a good career choice.







Experienced Auctioneers get more commission rates and they can also start their own businesses as well which will give you a brighter career.





Many seasoned auctioneers eventually start their own businesses, improving their income and securing more lucrative deals.

             

 

A basic procedure to become a Auctioneer



 



There is no formal qualification required to be an auctioneer. You can also follow the given procedure



STEP:1  Choose any stream after 10th.Becoming an auctioneer requires a minimum of a high school diploma. While it's not a requirement, some auctioneers also pursue a college degree.



STEP:2 Complete class 12th 



STEP 3: To better understand pricing and sales methods, or to learn how to advertise auctions, earning an associate's or bachelor's degree in business or marketing or advertising may be beneficial. Agriculture and history majors can also work well.



STEP 4: Apply for the license that your city or state demands once you have completed your education.



Individuals interested in working as an auctioneer's assistant can apply directly to auction houses and self-employed auctioneers. 

Some positions are listed on Internet job boards and in publications that circulate in locations where there are a lot of auction sales.

  



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment

           

             Age Limit- 



Above 18 years to be eligible for givng the exams of graduate levels.



              Education Qualification-



 High school level or intermediate level works well in pursuing auctioneer as a career . However it is not mandatory to follow the path.



Graduation with a Bachelor of Fine Arts , Bachelor in History, or Bachelor's degree in Bussiness will also be helpful in future to be an auctioneer.

 

SKILLS REQUIRED



Auctioneers have a talent for organizing and promoting because they need to sell objects or real estate in a profitable manner .

             



 Effective public speaking skills to persuade the audience,they need the best speaking skills .You may discover more about auctioneers and their work by going to auctions.







Ability to entertain audiences for a longer period of time hence need humor and intellect.These workers must be able to get along with others and be empathic, especially if they work at estate sales.







Auctioneers must know the specifics of the products they are offering and keep an eye on the spectators to determine who will place the winning bid.



















WORKING ENVIRONMENT







Auctions occur in cities and small communities and in all sorts of weather. During a state fair, auctioneers may work within a vast hall or outside. 

The nature of goods being auctioned also influences conditions of working  



Entrance exams:



Some of the top BFA entrance exams conducted for admission in Bachelors in Fine Arts are BHU UET, UPSEE, AUCET, ITM NEST



Banaras Hindu University Undergraduate Entrance Test

Uttar Pradesh State Entrance Examination

Andhra University Common Entrance Test

ITM national entrance and scholarship test



The IPU CET, DSAT, and JNUEE admission examinations are the most well-liked for BA in History.





Indraprastha University Common Entrance Test

Dayananda Sagar University Test

Jawaharlal Nehru University Entrance Examination



If you want to enter a specific college it can ask you to give an entrance exam as per the college.



Few colleges take admission on the basis of merits also such as Miranda College,Hindu College and Lady Shri Ram College for Women.





Courses description with their expenses:

BA Fine Arts /BA History

Type of specialization available:

Pay-Scales as per the experience:

4,27,097 INR

Promotions and Upgradations:



            



Successful auctioneers obtain lucrative assignments and hefty commission rates. Many seasoned auctioneers eventually venture out on their own, increasing their profits and arranging more successful transactions.

The only upgrade they could do is practice and gain experience.

Demands and future scope:





The job prognosis for auctioneers will most likely be determined by the industry in which they operate. For example, if cattle prices fall and banks foreclose on farm owners who cannot make their payments, real estate auctioneers specialized in farm estate auctions would likely see a surge in business.



 In general, the Internet will continue to deprive auctioneers of business. Auction platforms like eBay enable users to bid on almost anything over the Internet without the use of an auctioneer. 



Jobs in auctioneering, on the other hand, will become available when skilled individuals retire or leave the industry.







Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile



Hervé Poulain







Hervé Poulain, an auctioneer, has spent his life mixing his passions for fast automobiles with art, says Christopher Silvester. Hervé Poulain is the world's quickest auctioneer - not with his hammer, but behind the car. He completed the 24-hour race at Le Mans eleven times.



TJ Freije

title: Chiropodist
summary: Career profile: Chiropodist
description:
Career profile: Chiropodist



                   



Overview

Chiropody is a health care specialty that evaluates and treats foot problems. When it comes to foot problems, physicians and other healthcare professionals frequently recommend their patients for consultation.

A chiropodist is a specialist in the examination, diagnosis, and treatment of foot and lower limb problems. The majority of people will experience foot problems at some point in their life.



Roles and responsibilities of a Chiropodist



Perform foot and ankle surgeries such as bone spur removal, fracture treatment, and other foot and ankle abnormalities correction.

             

Provide advice and instruction to patients on foot and ankle care as well as overall wellness measures.





Coordination of patient treatment with other physicians is required.To be a good chiropodist.



Identifying systemic medical diseases that may initially present in the foot and treating the symptoms of these ailments, such as inflammation or ulceration





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Take use of real life experience. Students from college acquire valuable hands-on experience that helps them build their professional abilities.





You may expect a successful career. People of all ages are growing more interested in integrating or complementary healthcare as a means of treating pain and improving overall wellness.







There is a very high demand if you open your own clinic to treat patients and help them get rid of the illness.







Continuous education-New methods and discoveries are continually being developed in the medical field. This requires constant learning and the capacity to broaden your present surgical competence.





A basic procedure to become a Chiropodist:



The basic procedure to be a general surgeon 



       STEP:1 After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology



       STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English as a     

       compulsory subjects so that you can become eligible for MBBS.



       STEP:3 Complete your MBBS.



       STEP:4 Get a 4-year Doctor of Podiatric Medicine (DPM) degree(abroad)



      STEP:5   After that 3-year residency training



      After the above mentioned steps get a national board certification state license to practice



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment



            ELIGIBILITY 



             Age Limit-



The candidate must be of minimum age limit is 17 years to give the medical entrance exam and maximum age of 25 years.          



             Education Qualification-



Candidates must possess an MBBS degree.

Followed by a 4-year Doctor of Podiatric Medicine (DPM) degree.

    

             SKILLS REQUIRED

Communication Skills: students ought to be able to communicate well with others. These doctors have frequent contact with patients and must be skilled at connecting with them.





Intellectual: Another key part of podiatry is rationality. DPMs must be capable of strong and imaginative thinking and reasoning.

 



Calm attitude: Podiatrists must be calm in their demeanour. Because they deal directly with patients, they must remain calm and reasonable while also soothing and comforting them.





Detail-Oriented: To give correct diagnosis and treatment, a podiatrist must be detail-oriented. Before deciding on a treatment strategy, they must consider a patient's medical history and other criteria.



Entrance exams:



Scorecards from specific national level common entrances which are accepted by many institutions, such as

National Entrance cum Eligibility Test( NEET)

Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education and Research ( PGIMER)

All India Institute of Medical Sciences.  (AIIMS)

Christian Medical College Entrance Exam

Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Medical Sciences entrance exam



              Above mentioned entrance exams are for getting admitted to MBBS

full_content:
Career profile: Chiropodist



                   



Overview

Chiropody is a health care specialty that evaluates and treats foot problems. When it comes to foot problems, physicians and other healthcare professionals frequently recommend their patients for consultation.

A chiropodist is a specialist in the examination, diagnosis, and treatment of foot and lower limb problems. The majority of people will experience foot problems at some point in their life.



Roles and responsibilities of a Chiropodist



Perform foot and ankle surgeries such as bone spur removal, fracture treatment, and other foot and ankle abnormalities correction.

             

Provide advice and instruction to patients on foot and ankle care as well as overall wellness measures.





Coordination of patient treatment with other physicians is required.To be a good chiropodist.



Identifying systemic medical diseases that may initially present in the foot and treating the symptoms of these ailments, such as inflammation or ulceration





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career



Take use of real life experience. Students from college acquire valuable hands-on experience that helps them build their professional abilities.





You may expect a successful career. People of all ages are growing more interested in integrating or complementary healthcare as a means of treating pain and improving overall wellness.







There is a very high demand if you open your own clinic to treat patients and help them get rid of the illness.







Continuous education-New methods and discoveries are continually being developed in the medical field. This requires constant learning and the capacity to broaden your present surgical competence.





A basic procedure to become a Chiropodist:



The basic procedure to be a general surgeon 



       STEP:1 After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology



       STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English as a     

       compulsory subjects so that you can become eligible for MBBS.



       STEP:3 Complete your MBBS.



       STEP:4 Get a 4-year Doctor of Podiatric Medicine (DPM) degree(abroad)



      STEP:5   After that 3-year residency training



      After the above mentioned steps get a national board certification state license to practice



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment



            ELIGIBILITY 



             Age Limit-



The candidate must be of minimum age limit is 17 years to give the medical entrance exam and maximum age of 25 years.          



             Education Qualification-



Candidates must possess an MBBS degree.

Followed by a 4-year Doctor of Podiatric Medicine (DPM) degree.

    

             SKILLS REQUIRED

Communication Skills: students ought to be able to communicate well with others. These doctors have frequent contact with patients and must be skilled at connecting with them.





Intellectual: Another key part of podiatry is rationality. DPMs must be capable of strong and imaginative thinking and reasoning.

 



Calm attitude: Podiatrists must be calm in their demeanour. Because they deal directly with patients, they must remain calm and reasonable while also soothing and comforting them.





Detail-Oriented: To give correct diagnosis and treatment, a podiatrist must be detail-oriented. Before deciding on a treatment strategy, they must consider a patient's medical history and other criteria.



Entrance exams:



Scorecards from specific national level common entrances which are accepted by many institutions, such as

National Entrance cum Eligibility Test( NEET)

Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education and Research ( PGIMER)

All India Institute of Medical Sciences.  (AIIMS)

Christian Medical College Entrance Exam

Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Medical Sciences entrance exam



              Above mentioned entrance exams are for getting admitted to MBBS

Demands and future scope:







Although chiropody medicine is not officially recognised as a speciality by the Medical Council of India, there is a rising need among the region's thirty thousand physicians to address a variety of foot diseases, including diabetic foot disease.



Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile



Dr. Purushottam Reddy



Dr. Purushottam Reddy Padala is a  specialist who has gained a reputation as one of the greatest orthopaedic surgeons specializing in foot and ankle disorders. Dr. Reddy is the Director of Happy Feet Foot Clinics in Hyderabad. He has worked as a top orthopaedic surgeon at some of the world's most famous facilities.



Dr. Govind Singh Bisht





Dr. Govind Singh Bisht received his MBBS from SNMC Agra in 1992 and worked in New Delhi at Safdarjunj Hospital and Ram Manohar Lohia Hospital. Dr. Govind received his Fellowship in Podiatry from Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center in Boston, USA, in 2001, and has been working as a Podiatrist since then (Foot care Specialist).



Dr. Girish Motwani





Dr. Girish Motwani is a well-known doctor in Nagpur who has gained the distinction of being one of the best orthopaedic surgeons in the region. Dr. Motwani is a Consultant Orthopedic Surgeon who specialises in FOOT ANKLE and Peadiac Surgery.



Dr. Parina Bajaj





Dr. Parina Bajaj is a Diabetic Foot Surgeon also Varicose Veins Surgeon with experience in Lasers (EVLT), Sclerotherapy, Microphlebectomy.Set up First Wound Care Department in India to treat Chronic and Non-Healing Wounds



Dr. Hiten Patrel 





Dr. Hiten Patrel is a vascular surgeon. Currently employed as a consultant Vascular and Endovascular Surgeon in Aadicura Superspeciality Hospital. Visiting Consultant in Vadodara's major multispecialty hospitals.

title: Cryptographer
summary: Name of the Career profile: Cryptographer
description:
Name of the Career profile: Cryptographer



                          



Overview



A cryptographer is someone who creates (or deciphers) the encryption code used to protect data. Computer-encrypted data employs extremely lengthy, complex encryption methods that are extremely difficult and time-taking to break.

If you appreciate puzzles, you could enjoy working as a cryptographer. Cryptography is the study of how to make information safe so that only the intended recipient may view it.

Cryptographers utilise their understanding of codes and computers to safeguard data and information.



Roles and responsibilities of an Auctioneer







Ensure that financial data is secure and only accessible to authorised account holders. So we can say that the security of our data is in their hands.





Keep up with current research and coding and application methodologies, which could be beneficial in their field .







Examine systems for vulnerabilities and assure their accuracy and dependability. Hence they need to make sure that the system is not in a vulnerable state.







Aid in solving and security issues for the government or businesses.Using mathematical and/or statistical algorithms, analyse data to fix any security problems.





Benefits of choosing this profile as a career 



With the tremendous increase in the need for cryptographers in the world, one may anticipate higher earning potential and faster career advancement.







If you want to work in India's top research and development sector and have strong technical and mathematical talents, start polishing your cryptography skills.







Remote Freelancers are in high demand-People are increasingly pursuing a full-time profession as a covid in recent years.







If you have a passion for cryptography talents, you will not only be employed by prestigious firms, but you will also become the company's most valuable asset.







A basic procedure to become a Cryptographer



          

 Cryptography takes such a specific skill set, education is a crucial element. While several jobs permit entry without a comprehensive high school diploma, cryptography is not one of them.



STEP:1  Complete class 10th.Choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Maths after your class 10th.

STEP:2 Complete your class 12th with subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Maths.

STEP 3: A bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, or Computer Engineering is required.  Non-technical degrees are also acceptable but you should have a significant amount of job experience.

STEP 4: A master's or doctoral degree will be more beneficial to enhance your skills.



Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment

           

             ELIGIBILITY 



              Age Limit-  



             Above 18 years



             Education Qualification-



Cryptographers must have a college-level understanding of computer science and mathematics.   Bachelor's degree in computer science or maths 



SKILLS REQUIRED



A desire to solve riddles and problems. To crack codes or to encrypt and decrypt data and could work well with security.



Ready to always Accept new challenges because they need to face many challenges related to security threats and to ensure data protection in their daily life.





Fundamental understanding of all major programming languages, including Java, Python, C, and C++. Which conveys should have programming logic or logical thinking so that one can easily learn to program.







Trustworthy because cryptographers deal with and manage confidential information so they have to be trustworthy so that no data is leaked or the company can rely on that person.







WORKING ENVIRONMENT







The number of coworkers a cryptographer may have varied depending on the sector and the level of confidentiality of their job. 

A cryptographer in a high-level government post, for example, may work alone more frequently to protect sensitive information.



Entrance exams:

JEE Main  -Joint Entrance Examination

 VITEEE -Vellore Institute of Technology Engineering Entrance Examination

SRMJEEE - SRM Joint Engineering Entrance Examination

BITSAT  -Birla Institute of Technology and Science Admission Test

GATE- Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering 





Courses description with their expenses:



 Courses after 12th-



All of these fields offer the technical, mathematical, and logical abilities required to create and decipher complicated electronic codes.

They need to have knowledge in computer science or maths or statistics or any related subjects.



Name of the course

Promotions and Upgradations:

Top recruiters for Cryptographer:

Demands and future scope:





By creating techniques to encrypt data, cryptographers contribute to network and computer security.

 These experts are employed in the cybersecurity industry, which according to Cybercrime Magazine is expected to have 3.5 million job vacancies between 2021 and 2025, demonstrating a steady need for their services.

Hence choosing a career as a cryptographer will be a great choice.



Top inspiring personalities who have also followed this profile



Bruce Schneier





American cryptographer, privacy expert, computer security expert, and author Bruce Schneier. As of November 2013, Schneier is a Fellow at the Berkman Klein Center for Internet & Society and a Lecturer in Public Policy at the Harvard Kennedy School.



Adi Shamir





Israeli cryptographer and computer scientist who shared the 2002 A.M. Turing Award with American computer scientists Leonard M. Adleman and Ronald L. Rivest for their ingenious contribution to making public-key cryptography practical in reality.



Paul Kocher





Paul Kocher has earned an international reputation for his cryptography research and inventive ideas. Paul has built various cryptographic applications and protocols that have been successfully used in real-world systems as an active contributor to major conferences and as a leader in security projects.



Martin Hellman





Martin E. Hellman is well known for co-inventing public key cryptography with Diffie and Merkle, which enables safe Internet transactions among other things. Every day, it is utilised to move literally trillions of dollars.



 Shamir Whitfield Diffie





Shamir Whitfield Diffie is a cryptographer from the United States. B.S. (Mathematics, Massachusetts Institute of Technology, 1965). Ph.D. honoris causa (Swiss Federal Institute of Technology, 1992). He grew up in a Jewish immigrant community in New York City's Queens borough.

title: ELECTRICIAN
summary: Name of the career profile: ELECTRICIAN
description:
Name of the career profile: ELECTRICIAN



 

➢    Overview 

Electricians inspect, test, repair, install, modify electrical components and systems as and when required.

They work both inside our houses and outside buildings, factories,  construction sites, etc in order to ensure lights, industrial equipment, and appliances operate safely and reliably.



Roles & responsibilities of an Electrician 



Planning the electrical systems for new buildings. For example planning the best position for electrical outlets, light fixtures, heating outlets, and ventilation systems.





According to the municipal codes, they install wiring, lighting, and control systems in new as well as other existing buildings.







They also install switches, circuit breaker panels, relays, hangers, brackets, and other electrical control and distribution equipment.







Inspecting, repairing, and replacing circuit breakers, transformers, and other electrical components for faults and performing maintenance procedures in order to keep wiring, lighting, and other control systems in good working order.







Training other electricians and directing them to perform specific tasks





➢    Benefits of choosing this profile as a career





An electrician's job growth rate is higher than average and they make decent money.



An electrician has a career of opportunities that is to say  they can become promoted to Electrical Engineer, Contract Manager, or Electrical Design Engineer within their company and field.



An electrician can become self-employed.



No degree is required and they can earn while they learn.



➢    A basic procedure to approach this job:

 



To become an electrician, follow the path given below.

Step-1: Complete class 10 and choose science stream.

Step-2:  Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 60% in Science stream to get a seat in a college.

Step-3:  Complete Graduation in electrician courses [B.Tech./ BE] which will take 4 years. 

Step-4: Complete Masters in electrician courses [M.Tech./ M.E.] which will take 2years.

Step 5: After completing Electrician courses, the job profiles which are open to a candidate are Electrician, Electrical Manager, Maintenance Manager, Professor, Electrical Engineer, etc.  

Eligibility and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

●       The minimum age limit is 21 years.

Educational qualifications:

Complete class 12 with a minimum score of 60% in the Science stream to get a seat in a college.

Admissions to college are done on a merit basis and entrance exams scores of JEE Main, JEE Advanced, BITSAT, VITEEE, WBJEE, etc.

Complete Graduation in electrician courses [B.Tech./ BE] which will take 4 years. Candidates can also take up either certificate electrician courses [delivered either online or offline] or diploma in electrician courses which will take 3 years.

Complete Masters in electrician courses [M.Tech./ M.E.] which will take 2 years or PG diploma Electrician courses which will take 2 years.

After completing Electrician courses, the job profiles which are open to a candidate are Electrician, Electrical Manager, Maintenance Manager, Professor, Electrical Engineer, etc.  







Skill requirements:







Good communication skills









Good time management skills





Strong mechanical knowledge and skills







Good team working skills







Good problem-solving skills







Work environment:

Electricians work both inside our houses and outside buildings, factories,  construction sites, etc in order to ensure lights, industrial equipment, and appliances operate safely and reliably.

         



Entrance Exams To Become an Electrician

There are different entrance exams available to pursue electrician courses. Below given are some of the entrance exams which the candidates have to qualify in order to get admitted to a college:



Joint Entrance Examination [JEE] Main

Joint Entrance Examination [JEE] Advanced

Vellore Institute of Technology Engineering Entrance Examination (VITEEE).

West Bengal Joint Entrance Examinations Board [WBJEE]

Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering (GATE) 

Tamil Nadu Common Entrance Test  [TANCET]

Punjab University Common Entrance Test (PU CET) 

Andhra Pradesh Postgraduate Engineering Common Entrance Test [AP PGCET]



Courses To Become an Electrician

●        Undergraduate electrician courses

The courses mentioned below can be pursued right after completing 12:

Types Of Jobs Available For an Electrician

Electrical Manager

They work in power, transport, heating, refrigeration, and manufacturing industries designing and maintaining electrical machines, control systems, and other electrical equipments.

title: Ophthalmologists
summary: Name of the career profile: Ophthalmologists
description:
Name of the career profile: Ophthalmologists





➢    Overview 

An ophthalmologist is a medical doctor (MD) who specialises in eye and vision care.

Ophthalmologists are trained to examine the eyes, diagnose and treat diseases, prescribe drugs, and perform eye surgery.

Their task includes writing prescriptions for glasses and contact lenses.

      Roles & responsibilities of an Ophthalmologist

       



Ophthalmologists diagnose and treat eye injuries, infections, diseases, and disorders. Treatments  include drugs taken orally (by mouth) or topically (intraocularly), surgery, cryotherapy (cryotherapy), and chemotherapy (chemotherapy).





An ophthalmologist will check your eyesight and, if necessary, determine your eyeglass/contact lens prescription during a full eye exam.



 Ophthalmologists will test how your pupils react to light, check your eye alignment, and ensure that  your eyes may move because your muscles are in good condition.

Your retina and optic nerve will be examined by ophthalmologists for any early signs of eye conditions including cataracts or glaucoma.



Being an ophthalmologist it is necessary for them to keep tract of improvements and keep their good record and analysis.







➢	Benefits of choosing this profile as a career

Ophthalmologists or eye surgeons have well-paying, and personally fulfilling jobs. These specialists are in charge of diagnosing and treating eye diseases, sometimes surgically.



Ophthalmologists have a one-of-a-kind opportunity to make a genuine difference in the lives of others. Sight is one of the most vital senses we have, and being able to assist someone in gaining that gift is a wonderful opportunity.



The human population is growing at an exponential rate in society, with the elder generations accounting for the great majority. As a result of increased demand for glasses, eye surgery, and comprehensive eye care, ophthalmologists will be in high demand during the next decade.



Many ophthalmologists start their own practises to work for themselves. It is an excellent method for gaining personal control over one's own achievement. It allows for a more flexible work schedule as well as a stranglehold on consistent patients.





New methods and discoveries are continually being developed in the medical field. This requires constant learning and the capacity to broaden your present knowledge in the medical field.



➢    A basic procedure to approach this job:

STEP 1: After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Biology as mandatory subjects.

STEP 2: After class 12th with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology, you must study for a Bachelor of                                                         Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery(MBBS).

STEP 3: To pursue MBBS you can appear for an entrance exam like National Eligibility Entrance Test (NEET)     

                                                    OR

MBBS, B.Sc Ophthalmic Technology, B. Optom, Diploma in Ophthalmic Assistant, Certificate Course in Ophthalmology.

STEP 4: After MBBS, there are several other options such as:

              - Master of Surgery in Ophthalmology 

                                                    OR

              - Doctor of Medicine in Ophthalmology 

                               

                             

                                

Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment



       ELIGIBILITY 

      

        Age Limit- 



The candidate must be of minimum age limit is 17 years to give the medical entrance exam and maximum age of 25 years. 

 

       Education Qualification-



 An MBBS degree(graduation) could be one of the basic requirement for the education in the field.

 MD or MS or DNB (post-graduation) is necessary for better experience and knowledge.



          SKILLS REQUIRED 

      

There are several skills required that a person should have to be a successful Ophthalmologists  as mentioned below:



Excellent coordination between eyes and hands-Ophthalmology is a completely unique specialty as their attention area are eyes





Patience- They may need to sit for long periods with patients who want special attention: Persons who are afraid of  medical treatment might need additional patience.



Organizational skills- As they need to keep records and while performing surgeries they also require special attention to this quality.



Problem-solving abilities-They need to evaluate patients’ symptoms and then need to approach for treatments. 



Calm attitude:- They must be calm in their demeanor. Because they deal directly with patients, they must remain calm and reasonable while interacting.







Working Environment 





An ophthalmologist's work environment might differ based on whether they work in a hospital, a group practice, or their own clinic. They often have regular office hours and spend most of their time sitting or standing to assess patients and give essential medical treatments.

title: Radiologist
summary: Name of the Career profile: Radiologist
description:
Name of the Career profile: Radiologist 

                     

                          

Overview

A radiologist is a doctor who specialises in treating patients via the use of medical imaging known as radiography.

A radiologist is a certified medical practitioner who uses medical imaging techniques such as X-rays, ultrasounds, CT scans, and MRIs to identify, diagnose, and treat health issues.



Roles and responsibilities of a Radiologist:



choose the appropriate type of imaging for a patient based on their medical condition which is required to be done.



examine the patient's medical history and then take the necessary actions regarding the condition of the patient.



execute medical imaging techniques with or without the assistance of an imaging specialist evaluate and interpret medical pictures obtained during their evaluation make treatment recommendations and advocate for additional testing based on imaging results



provide medical advice to doctors and other medical experts monitor imaging methods.



Benefits of choosing Radiologist as a career:



High Salary -This field is something which is rare and if you have it  then  you are paid well by the companies  



Future scope - In the growing world  and population  people are dealing with various diseases and hence doctors of any field are  high in demand. Hence there is potential market growth 



Easiest and also a very demanding speciality although an equal amount of efforts are require to become a radiologist.



Doctors are highly respected across the world hence no doubt  you have already chosen an honourable profession.



Being a radiologist does not need you to spend your whole day at a hospital or clinic. Most hospitals only employ their radiologist for 8 hours per day, six days per week.





A basic procedure to become a Radiologist :



            STEP:1 After class 10th choose subjects Physics, Chemistry, and Maths/Biology.



            STEP:2 At least a 50% score in class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Maths/Biology.

                                                                

STEP:3 Complete your Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Medicine (MBBS) degree.

To become a radiologist, enroll in a three-year radiology residency program. A residency program is like an internship where you receive hands-on experience in the hospital setting.



STEP:4 (OPTIONAL) Complete your Masters  course: 



Diplomate of national board (DNB), which is a three-year course 

                                                           OR

Doctor of Medicine (MD), which is a three-year course. 

                                                             OR

Diploma in medical radiodiagnosis (DMRD), which is a two-year course

                                                                

Eligibilities & skill requirements & their working environment:



            ELIGIBILITY 



             Age Limit-



The candidate must be of a minimum age limit of 17 years to give the entrance exam.

      

             Education Qualification-

 

A  Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Medicine (MBBS) degree is mandatory to become a Radiologist whereas a master's  will be beneficial for the career.



SKILLS REQUIRED



Technical abilities-To operate diverse radiological equipment such as CT scanners, X-ray machines, and MRI scanners, radiologists must have great technical abilities. 



Human anatomy knowledge-Because radiologists spend a large portion of their workday reviewing images of human bodies, they must have a thorough understanding of human anatomy. 



Communication abilities-A radiologist's profession necessitates extensive communication with patients, doctors, and colleagues. A radiologist must be able to communicate complicated information in an intelligible way



Stress-management-Radiologists  operate in a high-pressure, fast-paced workplace. Their work-life balance may worsen as a result of the quantity of patients, resulting in dissatisfaction and stress. 



Pay attention -These specialists must have a keen eye for detail in order to analyse a patient's file, take notes from the scan, and identify probable diseases from the imaging.





WORKING ENVIRONMENT



The majority of radiologists work in medical settings such as hospitals, nursing homes, and private clinics. Others may opt to work in private practices or in the private sector.

 They  work in diagnostic laboratories.

 Radiologists also work as academic researchers instead of practising.



Entrance exams:

Scorecards from specific national level common entrances which are accepted by many institutions, such as

National Entrance cum Eligibility Test( NEET)

Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education and Research ( PGIMER)

All India Institute of Medical Sciences.  (AIIMS)

Mahatma Gandhi Institute of Medical Sciences entrance exam

Guru Gobind Singh Indraprastha University Entrance Exam

Manipal Entrance Test

Christian Medical College Entrance Exam

Type of specialisation available:



There are roughly four types of specialisation in radiology which are : 

Diagnostic radiologists

Interventional radiologists (IR)

veterinary radiologists

dental radiologists



Diagnostic radiologists



Radiologists are medical specialists that specialise in employing medical imaging (radiology) methods like X-rays, magnetic resonance imaging, nuclear medicine, positron emission tomography, and ultrasound to diagnose and treat injuries and diseases.



Interventional radiologists (IR)



Interventional radiology is a medical speciality that uses medical imaging to guide minimally invasive treatments like computed tomography, magnetic resonance imaging, and ultrasound.

Hence people who do this radiology are Interventional Radiologists.



Veterinary Radiologists



Veterinary radiologists specialise in diagnostic imaging, supporting primary care veterinarians in monitoring and sustaining the care of animals. These professionals' diagnostic knowledge can be critical for appropriate patient therapy.



Dental Radiologists



Dental radiographs can uncover hidden painful diseases, estimate the severity of dental disorders, evaluate treatment alternatives, provide intraoperative guidance, and track the efficacy of previous therapies.

Medical programmes: It trains the student to work as a doctor with a radiology specialty. They may also specialise in a specific area of radiography, such as oncology. Specialties, such as paediatric radiology, are also related to patient demographics.

Radiology professionals often work in medical care institutions. Whereas many people work in hospitals, others work in doctors' offices, diagnostic centres, outpatient care facilities, research centres, and even educational institutions.

Radiologists are in high demand since the number of radiologists per patient is quite low. The ratio in India is 1:100,000, the ratio in the United States is 1:10,000, and the ratio in China is 1:14,772.

 It results in extremely high patient inflows, keeping radiologists extremely busy and agitated.

title: audio copy copy
summary: Career profile: Audiologist
description:
Career profile: Audiologist 



Overview:

Who is an Audiologist?

Audiologist is a doctor who specializes in hearing healthcare. It is also an expert in identifying, diagnosing and treating problems with the auditory and vestibular regions of the ear.



The most common problem they deal with is loss of hearing sense in their patients.

They identify and manage hearing issues and some other neural issues too.

What kind of career profile is an Audiologist?

Audiologists are trained to work with patients with all possible age groups who come across hearing imbalances.

They specialize within the use of advanced technology to diagnose and evaluate the hearing aids of the patient.

Their work is to find proper rehabilitation and help for the people in need. Audiologists usually work 8 hours in 5 days of a business week on an average, they may have to take over time to respond to emergencies as well.

Roles and responsibility of an audiologist:

Look at patients with hearing problems, balance difficulties or any other ear-related problems.

Examining the patient's condition by looking at all the possible issues with the ear and finding solutions on how to treat it, is one of the major responsibilities of an audiologist.

Discuss the test and results with the patients.



Whatever be the test results for the patient, audiologists have to have sympathy and compassion and discuss it briefly with their patients and be transparent to them.















Gather and prescribe the treatment that is the best suited for the patient's condition.

Audiologists should find the best cures, treatments and medicine to make the patient get better sooner and faster.



Advise or give tinnitus treatment, a specific disorder that activates ear ringing.



Tinnitus is a disease which activates alarms in the earlobes and can be most common among the hearing aid patients, audiologists should consult this treatment if needed to the patient.



Advise  patients and their family to communicate or listen to them, for example: lip reading.



The patient's hearing issues should not stop them from interacting and communicating with their family, so the family members should find ways to make them feel belonged by the actions shown and suggested by the audiologist.

Keep patients monitor and progress records.

The progress in patients' hearing should be represented, in that way even the patient will have a ray of hope of getting or hearing better and the audiologist itself can be satisfied.



Benefits for choosing audiology as a career profile:

Respected profession

An audiologist might help a lot of people in need within his work life, and they have to clear a lot of educational obstacles to get where they can work as a professional, so yes they share a respected profession among society.



Excellent pay



    Compared to other professions audiologists have an above- average pay,  since the field is growing they are most likely to be more in demand in the coming days which will reflect to their pay scale directly.

A growing occupation

   Audiologists can become the need of the hour as the profession is most likely to grow by 34% by 2022. The growth rate is above average showing how in demand audiologists are going to be.

Audiologists can be life-changers for some, making a difference in someone’s life by making them connect to the world and making it a more beautiful place for them to live in. That can be very rewarding to some of the audiologists.

To become an audiologist you must follow the career path given below:

STEP 2: Get a BSc. In audiology degree from any known college. 

STEP 3: Get a MSc. in audiology degree from any known college.

full_content:
Career profile: Audiologist 



Overview:

Who is an Audiologist?

Audiologist is a doctor who specializes in hearing healthcare. It is also an expert in identifying, diagnosing and treating problems with the auditory and vestibular regions of the ear.



The most common problem they deal with is loss of hearing sense in their patients.

They identify and manage hearing issues and some other neural issues too.

What kind of career profile is an Audiologist?

Audiologists are trained to work with patients with all possible age groups who come across hearing imbalances.

They specialize within the use of advanced technology to diagnose and evaluate the hearing aids of the patient.

Their work is to find proper rehabilitation and help for the people in need. Audiologists usually work 8 hours in 5 days of a business week on an average, they may have to take over time to respond to emergencies as well.

Roles and responsibility of an audiologist:

Look at patients with hearing problems, balance difficulties or any other ear-related problems.

Examining the patient's condition by looking at all the possible issues with the ear and finding solutions on how to treat it, is one of the major responsibilities of an audiologist.

Discuss the test and results with the patients.



Whatever be the test results for the patient, audiologists have to have sympathy and compassion and discuss it briefly with their patients and be transparent to them.















Gather and prescribe the treatment that is the best suited for the patient's condition.

Audiologists should find the best cures, treatments and medicine to make the patient get better sooner and faster.



Advise or give tinnitus treatment, a specific disorder that activates ear ringing.



Tinnitus is a disease which activates alarms in the earlobes and can be most common among the hearing aid patients, audiologists should consult this treatment if needed to the patient.



Advise  patients and their family to communicate or listen to them, for example: lip reading.



The patient's hearing issues should not stop them from interacting and communicating with their family, so the family members should find ways to make them feel belonged by the actions shown and suggested by the audiologist.

Keep patients monitor and progress records.

The progress in patients' hearing should be represented, in that way even the patient will have a ray of hope of getting or hearing better and the audiologist itself can be satisfied.



Benefits for choosing audiology as a career profile:

Respected profession

An audiologist might help a lot of people in need within his work life, and they have to clear a lot of educational obstacles to get where they can work as a professional, so yes they share a respected profession among society.



Excellent pay



    Compared to other professions audiologists have an above- average pay,  since the field is growing they are most likely to be more in demand in the coming days which will reflect to their pay scale directly.

A growing occupation

   Audiologists can become the need of the hour as the profession is most likely to grow by 34% by 2022. The growth rate is above average showing how in demand audiologists are going to be.

Helping the needful 



Audiologists can be life-changers for some, making a difference in someone’s life by making them connect to the world and making it a more beautiful place for them to live in. That can be very rewarding to some of the audiologists.



Variety of work



They can even choose to teach in respected colleges, government university or giving lectures as well as share their vast knowledge by authoring books or articles to change people's lives and impacting them throughout the whole journey of audiology

How to become an audiologist?





To become an audiologist you must follow the career path given below: 

STEP 1: A candidate must pass their 12th with at least 50% in physics, chemistry, biology as their mandatory subjects.

STEP 2: Get a BSc. In audiology degree from any known college. 

STEP 3: Get a MSc. in audiology degree from any known college.

STEP 4: Get a license and start practicing. 

STEP 5: for further career enhancement one can do PhD in audiology from any recognised university of choice.

Eligibility and skills requirement to become an audiologist:

Age limit:



The minimum age to become an audiologist is 21 years.

The maximum age to study audiology is 35 years.

Educational qualifications:



A candidate must pass their 12th with a 50%  certificate at least with physics, chemistry and biology as their mandatory subjects.

Bachelor of science in audiology is the course one has to study, it’s the first degree in audiology.

Candidates can even take a diploma course in hearing aid and earmold technology or certificate course on communication disorders as such.

After that masters in science in audiology or any other specialization of choice can be proceeded.



Skills requirement:

Sensitivity and understanding.











As an audiologist one might come with patients with hearing difficulty, which will take patience to understand the client's aid and try to communicate and understand as per their comfort.



Good communication



 Interacting with patients will need excellent communication skills and verbal expressions too sometimes, as the patients an audiologist gets is most likely to have a hearing aid.



Thinking and reasoning skills



 Understanding the problem of the client and coming up with logical and accurate solutions which will help them get better needs faster thinking and a lot of logical reasoning.

Compassion



 An audiologist's clients are not always in the best state of mind, they will need a lot of sympathy and compassion to make the patient feel safe and comfortable,they need to connect with the patients to make  them more sure of the medical process.



Good time management







Audiologists might have trouble managing their work time and personal life with so many working hours and even emergencies at times. They need to be able to fix both the schedules in order to avoid a hectic life.

Working environment :

Most audiologists are employed in health facilities such as medical offices, audiology clinics and hospitals. Some work in schools or school districts, traveling between facilities. Others work in healthcare and personal care shops.

They usually work full-time, occasionally on weekends and evenings, to accommodate their patients.

Most audiologists usually work 40 hours per week, with eight hours per day, five days per week.

Types of specializations available for an audiologist:

Speech-language pathologists



They are responsible professionals responsible for preventing problems in speech, language,  social communication and cognitive communication.

They even give speech therapy to children as well as adults

Corporate audiologists



These audiologists don’t treat patients but work for companies and organisations to sell hearing devices.

They enhance their audiology knowledge with marketing skills and help promote audiology devices to audiology professionals

Pediatric audiologists



Audiologists which are trained to treat patients under the age group of 21 years, from babies to teens.

They may operate private offices to provide their services, and some go to the hospital to provide newborn hearing screening.

Audiology professors



They provide training for undergraduate and graduate students pursuing studies in audiology and other clinical areas.

Depending on their role or school, they can oversee the clinical work of students and provide mentoring and guidance.

Specialization 

Audiologists must obtain a bachelor’s and a master’s in audiology degree, at this stage many audiologists decide to specialize in their interested fields like, corporate audiology,and  many more. Their career can be enhanced by these few steps in the longer run.

Education

Audiologists can also want to share their knowledge and make a career out of it. For example, being a professor or lecturer can also be a great way of expanding their learning for audiologists.

Income

Audiologists can also expand their income forte by opening their own medical clinics or changing sectors as forensic or pediatric audiology.

Demands and future scope:



As per the preparations, audiology demands are very high all over the world. The hearing aid ratio is mostly going to peak and audiologists are the need of the situation. With never ending technologies and elderly there is not going to be a shortage of scope for audiology doctors.

The rapid change in technologies since a few years has allowed patients to be treated in even faster ways and by the help of machines and high tech facilities.

Audiologists have also been able to cure patients from different locations, showing how it goes world wide and they not only have a scope in India but all over the globe.

Top inspiring  audiologists:

Julien benoit



Julien was the best audiologist in 2018, he made sure his patients enjoyed the joy of hearing and worked endlessly towards it.

Benjamin Schadow



Benjamin is said to be the most intently working audiologist, he with the help of auxiliary devices treats his patients in the best way possible.

Wilgis Schoffel Meijer



Wilglis herself has been wearing hearing aids for 44 years, with prestigious awards and a pleasant and friendly aura. She is one of the best audiologists around the globe.



Miriam Gonzalez



Miriam is known to be very compassionate about her staff and clients, she and her team understood a lot of situations and worked practically to the cure.

Bridgitte Harley



Harley cured a patient's single sided deafness since she was 4 and helped her experience hearing again.

Overview:



Who is a cosmetologist?



A cosmetologist is a person who provides beauty services to people. 





They are experts in skincare and makeup. 

They also provide services such as manicure, pedicure and hairstyling.



What kind of career profile is cosmetologist?





It includes services such as beauty therapy 

In a career as a cosmetologist, a person works up to 8 hours in 5 days of a business week on an average.

Using beauty products to enhance a person’s appearance is the main part of their job. 

Advising the clients about a hair colour which suits their hair texture and their complexion 



Roles and responsibilities of a cosmetologist: 



Grooming nails





Cutting the clients nails, painting and polishing nails is part of a cosmetologist or a nail artists job. They can even make various designs and patterns with the help of nail paints and make them look even more beautiful and attractive. 



Styling hair





Hair stylists can make hairstyles of the best types on their clients or even what the clients themselves want. They do the hairstyles depending on the occasions, dresses, makeup or outfits of the client to make it look more appealing. 

















Cutting hair





A very big part of their job is cutting hair, in different styles, different cuts, different patterns, sometimes even colouring them according to the clients wish or the colours they’ve selected. This makes a person’s hair even more beautiful and textured. 



Performing pedicures and manicures 



Performing mani and pedi makes the clients hands and nails look glamorous and smooth in person and increases the shine. Hands and legs are a big plus point in an individual’s personality portrait. It's better to keep them clean and beautiful. 



Doing services like facials or scalp treatments.

Many career opportunities



            You can start by working for a salon or a beauty parlour and later you can open your own and manage it for career enhancement.



You can have a very flexible work schedule



     Many of the spas and salons allow you to work on times and days which are convenient to you and have flexible hours as the work system is in appointments and suits the clients as well as the staff.

Skills requirements:



Being likeable 



           As you are providing a service, you need to be very kind and sweet to the clients. This is how your clients will keep coming back to you.



Time management 



           You will give appointments to your clients by yourself and that’s why you need to be versatile and punctual about work.



Knowledge about skin hair and nails 



           You should have good knowledge in both of these departments to enhance the look of your client in order to expand your knowledge and business



Good communication skills



         Talking to customers how they like to be greeted and treated should be one of the major skillset being a cosmetologist. The clients should feel comfortable with their body,skin, hair etc in front of you for them  to get the treatment of their own choice.



Understanding the effect of beauty products



       As a cosmetologist you should be able to know which product is bad or can harm a client's body, understanding the beauty effects will make it easier for you to advise clients for their best look.



Working environment :



Cosmetologists work primarily in hairdressing salons or hairdressing salons, but some work in spas, hotels or resorts.  Others run salons or open their own stores following several years of experience.

description:
Career profile: Cytotechnologist



Overview:

Who is a cytotechnologist?

Cytotechnologists are laboratory professionals who work on cells and cell abnormalities.

They use a microscope to examine human cell slides for any indication that a cell is abnormal or ill.



They help in detecting cancer and other abnormalities in the cell by studying cytotechnology.

What kind of a career profile is a cytotechnologist?

Cytotechnologists use their independent judgment to examine human tissue and fluid samples at a microscopic level to identify cell traits.

This also includes any abnormalities or organisms present in the cell body.

All the other health professionals count on cytotechnologists to help detect the illness or any disease, and they take all the efforts to give them the accurate results.

Roles and responsibilities of a  cytotechnologist

Examine cell samples
