full_content:
Career profile: Cytotechnologist



Overview:

Who is a cytotechnologist?

Cytotechnologists are laboratory professionals who work on cells and cell abnormalities.

They use a microscope to examine human cell slides for any indication that a cell is abnormal or ill.



They help in detecting cancer and other abnormalities in the cell by studying cytotechnology.

What kind of a career profile is a cytotechnologist?

Cytotechnologists use their independent judgment to examine human tissue and fluid samples at a microscopic level to identify cell traits.

This also includes any abnormalities or organisms present in the cell body.

All the other health professionals count on cytotechnologists to help detect the illness or any disease, and they take all the efforts to give them the accurate results.

Roles and responsibilities of a  cytotechnologist

Examine cell samples



Cytotechnologists have to examine and check the cell samples very distinctly as they are going to be passed on for further procedure of the cell which will probably decide the issue with the patients.

Submit slides to pathologist



After their research they have to pass on the results to the pathologist for further process of the given data/cell.

Maintain and repair microscopes



As a cytotechnologist they have to work day and night with microscopes but while leaving they have to put everything in place, even repair at times for the next person's use.

Examine specimens using microscope



They have to examine the cells closely, for that microscope is needed, these specimens when testified come off as cancerous cells or many kinds of disastrous cells in a human's body.

Provide clinical data to patients.



After all the procedures they have to hand over the reports or the data to the patients in order to the patient to show it to their respected physicians.

Benefits for choosing cytotechnology as a career:

Job security

Cytotechnology is every physician's need, all the graphs about a patient are given by a cytotechnologist, they even discover new variants of diseases and viruses so with this ratio the job security is at its highest.

A growing occupation



Rationally the occupation of cytotechnology has its growth rate to 22% from 2012 to 2022, and with growing numbers of hospitals and labs all over the world the need of cytotechnician is also rapidly increasing.

Solid salary range



An average starting salary of a cytotechnologist is 3.1LPA with working experience and their working potentials, they earn pretty well on an average salary line and can be considered a good career option.

Can work anywhere





Any part of the world has laps followed by hospitals, cytotechs can find work nearly anywhere in the world and the salary will always be pretentious.

Independent working



They can work independently in laps, perform their own research too, have a fun environment and they also have the opportunities to save lives by accurate data. They can work with microscopes and make the best use of it, they get high tech tools to enhance their skills.

How to become a cytotechnologist?

To become a cytotechnologist, you must follow the career path given below:

STEP 1 : Complete 10th standard and choose stream science with physics, biology and chemistry as mandatory subjects.

STEP 2 : Pass 12th standard with at least 60% in each of the mentioned subjects

STEP 3 : Appear and qualify the medical entrance test for admission to  BSC(Bachelor of Science) in biology or modular biology

STEP 4 : Take admission to your chosen course in Cytotechnologist by earning a degree or any such related course

STEP 5 : Gain hands-on experience in their fields under the supervision of a licensed Cytotechnologist.

There are no national level exams specifically for cytotechnology but NEET (National eligibility cum entrance test) can help in getting government colleges.

Eligibility and skills requirement to become a cytotechnologist

Age limit:



The minimum age to become a cytotechnologist by studying cytotechnology is 21 years.

To get into any college to study cytotechnology is 35 years. Whereas there is no age as such to practice cytotechnology.

Educational qualifications



A candidate must finish 12th with 50% minimum. With physics, chemistry, math and biology as their mandatory subjects. 

They must have a BSc in cytotechnology from a recognized university to start practicing legally.

Candidates can even take diploma courses and certificate courses after 12th in cytotechnology. 

While doing masters, candidates can choose according to their preferences what they want to specialize in and can take their career to their specialization.

Skills requirement

Analytical skill



Cytotechnologists have to go through a lot of graphs and data to get the accurate result, they need to have excellent analytical skills to handle their profession.

Good eye sight



Sitting in labs and working through a microscope all day everyday and being able to see every minor detail needs a very good eyesight and it’s a major  in the career 

Computer skills



Cytotechnology requires technological knowledge and computer skills are mandatory for checking graphs, many kinds of data, charts etc.



Accuracy



Accurate testing is necessary as it can either save a person's life or might as well end it. In this case accurate information is quite important.

Time management



Cytotechnologists can have more than 8 hours working in a day, it's important for them to maintain their work and social life in balance in order to not make their work a burden and for them to stay happy.

Working environment :

Cytotechnologists work at hospital laboratories, private laboratories and universities. A laboratory may be located within a hospital, an independent laboratory or a research institution.

They typically work eight hours per day, five days per week. The majority of their time is spent under the microscope.

supporting scientists and operating engineers engaged in the operation of nuclear reactors, supporting the treatment and disposal of nuclear materials.

Military technologist



Military technology is the use of technology for use in warfare. It includes those types of technology that are clearly military in nature and not for  civilian use.

it’s not for civilians because generally because they have no useful or legal civil applications, or because they are dangerous to use without proper military training.

Cytotechnologist

Cytotechnologists are laboratory professionals who evaluate patient cell samples and are trained to observe small changes to accurately detect precancerous, and infectious conditions.

They are laboratory professionals who study the cell and its abnormalities.

Dental laboratory technicians use the imprints or molds of a patient's teeth to create crowns, bridges, dentures and other dental braces.

Even tho there is not much visibility to the public about the field there is a very high demand for cytotechnologists in the upcoming and present market

Cytotechnologists are always going to be the need of the hour and a reputed profession to make a future out of it.





Top inspiring cytotechnologists:

Cyril Dean Darlington





Darlington is the most famous cytologist in the world. He is also referred to as Newton or Copernicus of cytology. He is the  one who discovered the chromosomal crossing over, which plays a major role in inheritance.

Arun Kumar Sharma



Arun  was an Indian cytogeneticist, cell biologist, and cytochemistry. He is also known as the father of Indian cytology. The Government of India has also awarded him with the third highest civilian honor, Padma Bhushan in 1983.



George N. Papanicolaou





George  is known as the father of modern cytology. He developed a method for early detection of cancer cells; his method has resulted in a 70% decrease in cervical and uterine cancer deaths in the last 50 years.

James Edward Rothman



James was an American biologist and cytologist. He discovered molecular machinery involved in vesicle building. He won the Nobel prize in 2013 for medicine.

Ralph M. Steinman 

Steinman was a Canadian immunologist and cytologist. He won the Nobel prize in 2011 for medicine. His work contributed in advancing of autoimmune diseases , cancer and graft rejection

full_content:
Career profile: Pathologist

Overview:

Who is a pathologist?

A pathologist is a health care practitioner who examines the body and tissues.

They  also carry out laboratory tests. A pathologist helps other health care providers get diagnostics and is a key member of the treatment team.

What is the career profile of a pathologist?

A pathologist's career consists in researching and analyzing various illnesses and their nature.

The branch of pathology comes from medicine that even engages laboratory exams of many organisms and tissues involved in a disease.

A pathologist works 9-12 hours in a day on an average of 5-6 working days of a business week.

Roles and responsibility of a pathologist:

Perform tests



      Pathologists allow the entry of new  drugs and medicine in the market, in order to make the medicines public they have to be well tested and not be dangerous. 

Make reports

      The reports made by pathologists help the doctor know the illness which has to be treated in a patient, their reports hold  scientific matters of the patients problems.

Perform autopsies 

      Experienced pathologists can also be medical examiners and can detect the cause of death.













Suggesting treatment options 

      Pathologists can examine and detect the problem after looking at the reports they themselves make and even suggest the best treatment option required for a particular problem.

Advice and support staff

      Experienced pathologists can often educate the juniors on learning more things and advise them into being better and being supportive throughout their journey.

Benefits for choosing pathologist as a career profile:

Good paying salary 

Pathologist having an average annual salary of 10 lakhs proves to be above average and can offer a comfortable and lavish lifestyle. With the increase in demand in the coming day and growing days of pathology it is most likely that the profession will offer more salary.







Variety of work

Everyday brings out a different kind of work in pathologists work life, with so many charts and datas and tests the life of a pathologist is very busy and fascinating at the same time.

Always in demand

None of the physicians or medical providers can work without a test or a report of the disease or problem in a person, that makes pathologists and their work always stay in demand and they’re never off the board.

Rewarding career

Being a pathologist must bring a lot of opportunities to help the people in need on an everyday basis and also a lot of respect from serving so many ill and helpless people, that sure does make pathology a rewarding career.



Work life balance

Pathologists have very flexible working hours and their personal life is Rewarded by that, they can balance both their work and personal life with ease in that case, it’s truly beneficial for them.

How to become a pathologist?

STEP 1: Candidates should pass 12th% board examinations with a minimum of 50% with biology, physics, chemistry and mathematics as their mandatory subjects.

STEP 2: Candidate need to appear for entrance exams like NEET PG(National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (Postgraduate) in order to get inside a good university or college

STEP 3: Candidates need to complete their bachelor's degree in BSc ( bachelor of science) or MBBS (Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery) in order to be a well known pathologist.

STEP 4: after completing bachelors candidates need to complete a post bachelor's degree specializing in fields of pathology of their own choice.

STEP 5: Candidates should take an internship for one year or two, get a license and start working officially.

Eligibility and skills to become a pathologist:

Age limit:

Minimum age to become a pathologist is 15. 

Maximum age to study pathology and become a pathologist is 25.

Educational qualifications:

A candidate must’ve scored a minimum of 50% with physics, chemistry, biology and mathematics as their Mandatory subjects in 12th. 

MBBS degree and then a MD in pathology.

Bachelor’s of science (BSc) is the bachelor’s degree candidates needs in order to pursue masters in pathology.

Master’s of science (MSc) is the masters degree in order to practice pathology for the general public as a form or internship and getting a legal license for becoming a pathologist.

Skills requirements: 

Good eye sight



Pathologists are supposed To have a good eye for detailing and accuracy, the whole process and treatment is based on the reports a pathologist sends, and any mistakes would create a huge problem.

Good at data analysis

They have to get through a lot of reports and charts in order to get the accurate results, they’re expected to be very good at data analysis and interpretation to ace their field.

Problem-solving and decision making skills.

With reports pathologists are also helpers in suggesting correct treatments, and decision making, whereas making charts and graphs challenges their problem-solving skills.

Inquisitiveness and self motivation.

To find numerous solutions in their work life they need the inquisitive attitude towards learning new things on a daily basis and self motivation because their work is very detailed and can frustrate them often.









Good time management skill

Average working time for pathologists is 9-12 hours, which is almost half of the day which brings them very less personal time. They should know how to balance their work and personal life in order to enjoy both equally.

Working environment :

Most pathologists spend time in scientific or medical research laboratories Depending on their area, pathologists may also spend time in greenhouses, farms, hospitals or mortuaries.

The core workweek may last 40 hours. The hours are flexible, though, and often exceed forty hours a week. While pathologists may face rotating shifts, their hours are usually regular.

Types of specialization available for pathologist:

Anatomical Pathologist 

An Anatomical Pathologist specializes in diagnosing tissue samples from the organs of a human body.

The aim of the examination is to determine if cancer cells are present in the tissues.Such a pathologist plays a crucial role in accurate diagnosis.

Clinical pathologist

A clinical pathologist is another type of pathologist who conducts and supervises laboratory tests for bodily fluids like urine or blood samples.

The main objective of clinical pathologists is to identify the parasites, bacteria and viruses responsible for the disease.

Forensic Pathologist 

A medical examiner investigates evidence related to homicides and accidents.

The primary lines of evidence include samples of human hair, blood or tissue fibers to determine the cause of death. Forensic Medical Officers work closely with law enforcement.

Cytopathologist

Cytopathology is a branch of pathology which studies and diagnoses diseases at the cell scale.

A cytopathologist is a pathologist who identifies human diseases by studying cells obtained from body secretion and body fluids.

Histopathologist

Histopathologists examine organs, tissues, cells and genetics for diagnosis.

They examine the organs and tissues of patients with the naked eye and microscopically examine cell samples.

Pathologists can train the staff and upcoming generation of pathology by being lecturers, professors or even a senior worker. They can open their own labs by getting a license and train the students or aspirants, this way they expand their knowledge for good. 





Income

Pathologists are said to have an above average pay scale and earn a pretty decent living for themselves. The demands and value they carry is always going to be there and they’re always going to be the need of the hour. This way pathologists never run out of work and stay employed.

Demands and future scope:

Career in pathology is totally positive. There is tremendous growth in India and around the world.

From studying common conditions to searching for neurological disorders, a pathologist's career is incredibly diverse.

Considering all the job opportunities and growing rate of demand for pathologists, the profession is well respected and offers a lot of scope in India as well as overseas.

Top inspiring pathologists:

Fatima Carneiro

Fatima is an excellent and dedicated pathologist who made multiple discoveries in gastric cancer. She now works as a professor of Anatomic  Pathology.

Elizabeth Montgomery 

Elizabeth teaches anatomical pathology, clinical pathology and cytopathology as a professor in a school of medicine. She has also authored more than 15 books on “tumor fascicles”.

Adam Booth

Booth is well known for his social media influencing skills. He promotes and spreads awareness related to pathology as much as he can on all the current social media platforms. He was also the best pathologist of the year in 2018. 

Jo Martin

Jo specializes in neuromuscular disease of gut in renal pathology and is a well known histopathologist. She is a professor of various known and reputed pathology institutes. She also holds a leadership degree.

Adrian Newland 

Adrian is currently the Clinical Advisor in the reconfiguration of England’s Pathology Service. Adrian has spent years of his life contributing the leadership in his sector of pathology. He is praised by many for his good work.

title: pilot
summary: Pilots are highly trained professionals who learn in depth about flying different kinds of aircrafts. These trained professionals are also responsible for maintaining the internal mechanisms of an aircraft.
description:
Career profile-Pilot



Overview:

Pilots are highly trained professionals who learn in depth about flying different kinds of aircrafts. These trained professionals are also responsible for maintaining the internal mechanisms of an aircraft. 

The courses for becoming a pilot are according to the specialization in aircraft. There are various types of different aircrafts, and so are their pilots.

Goods pilots, fighter plane pilots, passenger pilots, sports pilots and private pilots are some of the different kinds of pilot. The specialization of a pilot completely depends on the stream and subjects they choose.

 If aircrafts and the sky mesmerizes you like anything, then maybe being a pilot is the best suited career profile for you! Read more to know everything about this Career profile. 

                           



Roles and Responsibilities

To perform aircraft inspections before and after the flight to ensure that the flight is safe and secure to fly. This includes checking the engine, Fuel and all. As safe landing is the responsibility of the pilot he needs to take care of all this.





                             



To Choose the route of the flight to be taken. They need to ensure that the route and safe and efficient. Also they need to check the  weather and everything on that route.



                              



To communicate with the other staff like the crew and airport staff about the situation and landing and everything. To take care of the operations of the flight.



                             

In case of emergency, pilots need to assure the crew, passengers and others are safe and need to update them. Keeping everyone calm and reacting appropriately is the main thing.



        





Benefits Of Choosing Pilot As A Career Option:

Flexible work schedule: Pilots do not have to work 9 to 5 everyday; instead they have a well-defined flexible working schedule. They can choose to travel according to their own requirements and schedule. 



                   



Non-monetary benefits: along with a good pay scale, a pilot gets benefits of medical insurance, free meals, complimentary lodging and other allowances. 



                 



Good pay scale: pilots are one of the highest paid professionals in India as well as abroad. The pay scale of a pilot is handsome and enables them to live a comfortable life.



                         



Aviation has great scope: be it government or private, aviation has great scope in coming years. The aviation industry faces constant changes, ultimately providing greater job opportunities. 



                     



Job security: as the aviation industry is constantly changing, there is a huge demand for pilots.

                    



How To Become A Pilot?

The step-by-step procedure given below will guide you through the complete process of becoming a pilot right after class 12th. 



Eligibilities and Skill Requirements:

Age limit:

Candidates must be at least 17 years old to apply for the pilot courses.

The maximum age to become a pilot completely depends on the type of entrance exam or institute.

Educational qualifications:

A candidate applying for the pilot admission must have passed 10+2 with physics, chemistry and mathematics as compulsory subjects.

A candidate must have studied English at an Intermediate level till 10+2.

Candidates are required to have passed the qualifying exam with at least 50% aggregate.

If a candidate from the commerce background wants to pursue pilot courses then he/she can enroll with PCM subjects from an open state board.

Medical requirements:

All the candidates must have a medical certificate issued by the authorities. 

Skill Requirement To Become A Pilot:

Clear communication



Clear communication skills are required because pilots need to communicate with co-pilot , cabin crew, and airport staff for operations and updating everyone about the flight. If the communication is not done properly then it may create many problems. 

                                               



Environmental awareness



Environmental awareness is necessary for the pilot because the pilot needs to know about the weather forecast , climate, traffic and everything before the flight takes off. So analysis of the environment by the pilot is very important and that needs to be accurate.

                                         



Team working skills



Pilots need to work with staff and co-pilot so they need to have team working skills for efficiency and good communication. If anything happens , pilots need to work in a team with the cabin crew and others.

                                        

Aircraft awareness



Pilot needs to have awareness about the condition of the aircraft, that is, the fuel level, the engine efficiency, and other equipment and other related things. Pilot should have knowledge about all the equipment of the aircraft and what to do in emergencies.

The working environment of pilots is in airport and aircraft because there work either is to fly while they have a fly or they have to wait or have training for them.

Entrance Exams To Become A Pilot: 

If you wish to build your career as a pilot in the aviation industry then you need to qualify some entrance exams. Cargo Pilots

Charter Pilots

Military Pilots

Fire fighter pilots

description:
Career profile: Veterinarian

 

 

Overview:

 

Who is a veterinarian? 

A veterinarian is a person who provides animal veterinary medicine or medical treatment. They are doctors for animals.

There are many kind of vets, some work with small animals, large farm animals or even wild animals. 

Veterinary surgeons learn to treat all animals, but sometimes work with a particular type.

 

What kind of career profile is veterinarian? 



Veterinarians take care of animal health (animal refuge) and work to enhance public health (animal welfare).

They diagnose, treat and conduct research on diseases in pets, cattle and other animals.

Vets usually work 8 hours in 5 days of a business week on an average, they may have to take over time to respond to emergencies as well.

 

Roles and responsibilities of a veterinarian: 





Test animals for health and diagnose s.

Like humans animals have tests too, stool tests and many more which help the vet examine and detect the issue in the pets.



Treat and dress their wounds

Often pets get into situations which can only be fixed by bandages, its safe and best to take the pet to the vet instead of performing home surgeries.



Perform surgeries on animals.



In some or few cases pets might need surgery too, vets are trained to precisely use their tools in situations like these.





Prescribe suited medication 

It is important to let the client know the correct detailed medication for their pet to get healthy sooner.



Provide advice to pet owners on general care, medical conditions and treatments.



Vets have to sympathise with the pet owner or make them feel safe because to some owners it might just not be a pet but a family member.



Test and immunize against illnesses.



     Vaccines and proper tests can treat the animals even faster for their recovery.





















 

Benefits for choosing veterinarian as a career profile: 

High paying salary





With years of specialisation and high numbers of humankind being more affectionate to animals and desiring for healthy pets a vet can make good money in its career. 



Can work with pets 



If you’re a person who loves spending time with animals or being around them being a vet is going to be a dream job as you spend most of your time with animals.





























Making a difference in the lives of animals 





Every day will bring new challenges so you will never get bored in your work environment.



Variety of learning 





You will get a lot of knowledge throughout the years through experience. You will learn better animal handling skills. 

 

How to become a veterinarian?

To become a veterinarian, you must follow the career path given below 

STEP 1: complete 10+2 with physics chemistry and biology as mandatory subjects with 60%

STEP 2: After class 12th you can also pursue a veterinary diploma course. 

STEP 3: Apply and qualify NEET( national eligibility cum entrance test) 

STEP 4: Get admission into a veterinary college for a bachelors degree course in veterinary science and animal husbandry (B.V.S.c& AH.) 

STEP 5: get a master degree in veterinary sciences or related course. Master of Science (M.Sc), Master of Veterinary Science (M.V.Sc) or a Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) degree might help in further vet career. 

STEP 6: receive a license that permits to practice veterinary for general public.

 

Eligibility and skills requirement to become a veterinarian 

 

Age limit :

Minimum age limit to be a veterinarian is 17 years 

Maximum age for a veterinarian to retire is 65 years 

 

Educational qualifications : 

A candidate must have scored at least 60% in 12th standard with physics biology and chemistry as their mandatory subjects.

Bachelor of Veterinary Science (BVSc) is the degree you need to study to become a veterinary doctor in India.

for further studies an MD in veterinary science or it’s related specialisation should be completed to become a certified veterinarian.

A student must clear NEET (national eligibility cum entrance test) in order to get into a government college for veterinary education.

Compassion

Veterinarians have the risk of being harmed by the animal they are treating. It is important for them to understand the stressed , scared or any kind of signs given by the animal in order to be safe and respect animal boundaries.



Manual dexterity



Veterinary surgeons require fine motor skills for many of their tasks like in situations where they have to examine the pet unconsciously, a pet is awake, he must be able to work quickly and precisely in case of distress.

Types of specialisation available for a veterinarian:

 

Companion-Animal Veterinarians.





This is the most common type of veterinarian. These are the veterans who treat our beloved pets, most commonly dogs and cats.

companion- animal vets advise on general care of animals; diagnose illness, administer vaccines, treat minor injuries and many more.

 

 

Food-Animal Veterinarians











Veterinarians specialising in farm animals work with farm animals raised for meat and dairy. animals like cows, pigs, chickens, and sheep.

Their main  responsibility is assuring the overall health of farm animals. This includes immunization, testing and treating diseases.



Research Veterinarians









Each type of veterinarian is important, but research veterinarians play a critical role in the health of all animals, including our valuable pets.

Research veterinarians examine previous results and look for better ways to diagnose, treat and prevent animal diseases. They mostly  work in laboratories.

 

Food Safety & Inspection Veterinarians



food safety & inspection vets spend most of their time on farms and travel a lot. these vets worry about human safety more than animal health. 

They inspect on-farm hygiene standards to ensure animal products are fit for human consumption.

Promotions and upgrades of a veterinarian:



Specialisation

Veterinarians must obtain a Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) from an approved veterinary college. At this stage, some veterinarians choose to specialize as a means of separating from the pack and increasing their chances of progressing professionally.

 

Education

Veterinarians who wish to teach have the opportunity to take on more senior roles, such as leading veterinary departments in teaching hospitals and medical schools.

 

Income

Veterinarians can make a lot more money by opening their own work domains and clinics, or switching specialties

Demand and future scope:



This is the only profession where the scope is multidimensional, multi-disciplinary, and multidirectional.

vet may enter into various career options, as:

Government veterinary officers, private practice, scientists, corporate jobs and the list goes on.

Considering all these aspects and the future of our nation I think being a veterinarian offers a lot of scope and needs good talents in this profession.

 

Top inspiring veterinarians:

 

Claude Bourgelat



He founded the first veterinary school in lyons, france, in 1761 after years of  work to establish it.

 

Bernhard lauritz frederik bang 



He discovered the type of bacteria that causes brucellosis, which is responsible for miscarriages in cows and flu-like illness in humans.

 

James Herriot



Dr.james is known for the book written by him is responsible for advancing the veterinary profession.

 

Mary Knight Dunlap



She ran a house call zoo, as well as worked for a toxicology department and authored many publications.

 

Elinor McGrath





She was the first woman to widely practice veterinary practice all over the world, and devoted her whole life nursing the animals of chicago.
